### Caroline Cooper — 2012-06-15T17:37:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-31340>

**Some History

I have been playing around with **cold thermogenesis since about January 15, 2012. I don't really have any weight to lose and my health is good. I have been on the **SCD/GAPS or a modified paleo diet for about six years. Changing my diet transformed my health and cured my health problems which included: asthma, allergies, chronic sinus infections, yeast infections, osteoarthritis and epilepsy. Because I have been using the SCD/GAPS or modified paleo diet for a long time, I am a good candidate for seeing what cold thermogenesis can do.

I do have some sort of endocrine problem. I haven't been tested in about five years but the last testing I did my adrenals were very tired. I do have a low body temperature which points to a dysfunction in my adrenal-thyroid-pituitary axis. After reading Dr Krase's blog posts I have decided to try using CT to reboot my adrenals-thyroid-pituitary axis. From what I have read, people like myself are slow to respond to CT.

Getting into that cold tub is very hard for me especially in the morning when my temperature is the lowest of the day. Even though I have been off coffee for over a year, I am going to try using coffee "medicinally" to reboot my system. Or that is the hope. I am going to do this trail for thirty days. I hope this journal will be of assistance to other people with endocrine problems. I hope you can learn from my mistakes; I know I will make a lot.

Some Changes I have made since reading Dr Kruse's website:**

1\. I turn off the most of the breakers in the house except for the fridge and freezers at sundown.

2\. I have tried to be more ketogenic with my already fairly low carbohydrate diet.

Results of CT Experiment After 30 Days**

These are some of the things I noticed over the last 30 days of CT. Do note I started CT in the middle of my last cycle when my temperatures were their "hottest":

1\. I had a shorter cycle than normal, only 25 days.

2\. I had a longer period than normal, 6 days rather than 4-5 days. Normally, my periods start and stop without the nonsense of spotting.

3\. I had some spotting on day 9 which normally never happens. There was big and black clots of blood. Sorry, for the graphic detail.

4\. I found it easier to do the tub if my temperature getting into the tub was over 36.5C. This means the luteal phase (after ovulation) of my cycle is easier for CT.

5\. The follicular phase (before ovulation) is harder to do CT because my temperatures are naturally lower during that time.

6\. My follicular phase appears to have a more "stable" temperature profile from day-to-day. Normally, my temperatures can be all over the place.

7\. I had 10 days in my follicular phase under 36.3C which according to Katie Singer would mean I have a "cold uterus". (More than 3 days under 36.3C means infertility.)

8\. I don't tend to weigh myself more than once a week. I have gained 3 pounds this month but that really doesn't mean much. I never did my measurements during the month so I can't say if my body composition has changed. I can say this. I have two sizes of pants size 8 and 10. Size 10 are really loose and size 8 are comfortable. My husbands says I look firmer, but he's a really nice guy!

9\. I am more tolerate to cold. I am willing to into the local lakes and rivers; I would not have been willing to do so in the past.

It might be helpful to outline what I was hoping CT would do for me:**

1\. I hoped it would increase my overall body temperature.

2\. I hoped my cycles would be a few days longer. In particular my luteal phase which would mean my progesterone ratio is better.

3\. I hoped I would have a better temperature transition - a nice snappy rise in one day - from my follicular phase to my luteal phase.

4\. I hoped it would "fix" my hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis without the use of bio-identical hormones or "manually" messing with my endocrine system.

**Results of CT Experiment After 60 Days

What I have noticed over the 60 days of CT.

1\. My second cycle was even shorter at 24 days.

2\. My second period was more normal. Four days with not spotting.

3\. My follicular phase did not have the normal up and down of daily temperature. All my days in the follicular phase was 36.3C or under that temperature which means I have a "cold" uterus and I am infertile. I had a more even temperature throughout my follicular phase. (This is considered a healthier pattern. This month I looked like I have thyroid problem, low average temperatures but stable. Normally I have low, fluctuating temperatures which point to adrenal problem.)

4\. I seem to be able to increase or keep my body temperature the same in the cold tub after ovulation and during the luteal phase. In the follicular phase I cannot increase my body temperature and my body temperature can drop quite low.

5\. I started bio-identical 20mg of progesterone on day 10 of my third cycle. I started taking 25mg of progesterone on day 17.

6\. My weight is back down to 130 pounds which is normal.

7\. I have noticed my feet are tougher. I can walk bare foot with the ouch, ouch, ouch of every step.

8\. I like doing CT in the lake with my daughter. Being in the water with her has enriched my life.

9\. I have not seen much improvement in my endocrine health over the last 60 days of doing CT. CT may help in the long run but it isn't a quick fix for people like me. I am wondering if CT works better for men without hormonal cycling that women face?

10\. Choosing to take bio-identical progesterone feels like a "failure" to me. I am sad that the CT did not have a more positive effect. I would hope that I would not have to take progesterone long term and could use it to re-balance my system and then be able to go off the hormone.

11\. I will continue for another 30 days of CT for a total of 90 days for this experiment. I wonder how many people would be willing to have "faith" in this method if there isn't some signs of improvement?

12\. Saliva Test Results from Day 55**

The test results were no surprise to me, just validation:

Estradiol: 3.1pg/ml

Progesterone: 49pg/ml

Ratio Pg/E2: 16

Testosterone: 43pg/ml

DHEAS: 18.7ng/ml

Cortisol Morning: 12.2ng/ml

Cortisol Noon: 1.6ng/ml

Cortisol Evening: 1.5ng/ml

Cortisol Night: 0.7ng/ml

1\. The estradiol is at the top quadrant (estrogen dominant) while my progesterone is low quadrant pointing to anovulatory cycle or luteal phase failure. I have tried all of the other ways to increase my progesterone over the last 40 cycles without much success. Supplementation seems to be my only choice or do nothing and accept the situation.

2\. Testosterone in top quadrant but have symptoms of deficiency. May point to poor tissue response.???

3\. DHEAS way above top quadrant for my age.??? Normally, would mean I am taking DHEA supplements, adrenal adaptogens or a well trained athlete. I am not an athlete, nor have I recently taken supplements or adaptogens.

4\. Morning cortisol is very high. Gee, no surprise there! Yeah, I'm stressed out of my mind, but who the hell isn't in our modern world. I was really happy to see a normal pattern of decline over the day. That was a big change over six years ago when I was a flat-line and it was surprising I could get out of bed in the morning. I will continue to work with my mind on stress management.

**August 17, 2012: I have decided not to continue with the journal. I realized Day 90 would be well into the start of my home schooling year. I need to focus on other tasks right now.

**Day 1**

Waking Temperature: 35.7C (day 11 of cycle)

After coffee temperature: 36.7C

15 minutes

After tub temperature: 36.0C

Ring finger tip on left hand white and numb. Still not hungry but will eat soon.

(**Tub Note: I reuse the tub water for about a week. I alternate with epsom salts (magnesium) and cider vinegar in tub water. I use the tub as a heat sink so the tub is basically room temperature.)

Supplements**

I don't normally supplement with nutraceuticals, I use "superfoods", but during this "reboot" I am going to supplement. The coffee is going to put pressure on my adrenals and stress my system. I am hoping the extra supplementation will reduce this stress.

Life Extension Mix Powder: 1 scoop

Life Extension Booster Softgels: 1 softgel

Life Extension Super Omega 3: 2 softgels

Life Extension Rhodiola Extract: 250mg 1 capsule

Life Extension Resverstrol: 100mg 1 capsule

Life Extension N-Acetyl Cysteine: 600mg 1 capsule

AOR Cogni-Q PQQ: 320mg 1 capsule

Exercise/Play**

2.5 hours of very easy bicycling with family. Had a snack of coconut cocoa bars and raw milk after ride. Wasn't hungry, just being sociable.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/31871=242-coconut-cocoa-bar.jpg)

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-06-29T21:37:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-34434>

**Day 11 (June 25, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.5C (Day 21 of cycle)

1c Coffee with about 1/2c raw cream and 2tsp organic butter

**

Breakfast

Cold roast beef, squash salad, raw milk and raw cheese. Ate breakfast on the MV Frances Barkley on the way to Sechart Lodge, just outside the Broken Group Islands.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34593=267-frances-barkley-breakfast.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34593=268-lunch-kit-1.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34593=269-lunch-kit-2.jpg)

**CT: Exercise/Play

About 4.0 hours of canoeing through the inner BG. Collected oysters. Tried to stay uncovered while paddling. Had lots of goosebumps.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34593=270-picking-oysters.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34593=271-oyster-bed-westcoast.jpg)

Lunch**

Cold roast beef, squash salad, raw cheese, raw milk and homemade beef jerky.

CT: Ocean**

Suntanned today and paddled around in ocean. Water felt surprising warm to me. Slept in tent. Don't know the nighttime temperature or ocean temperature.

Dinner**

Beef jerky and two cooked oysters.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-06-29T21:49:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-34436>

**Day 12 (June 26, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.5C (Day 22 of cycle)

Matcha and herb tea

**Breakfast

Salad, homemade dressing, raw cheese and 1/2 can sockeye salmon. Our stove is not working properly. Had two raw oysters; much better than the cooked ones last night.

**CT: Exercise/Play

About 3.0 hours of canoeing through the outer BG. Felt tired today after yesterday's hard paddle. Tried to stay uncovered while paddling. Had lots of goosebumps. Got cold and had to put a jacket on.

**Lunch

Started with homemade beef jerky and raw cheese. Finally got the stove working again and ate 1/2 pound of fresh prawns. The prawns are loaded with roe.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34595=273-prawns-msr.jpg)

Dinner**

1/2 pound of prawns in butter loaded with roe. Gave up on stove and built a "rocket stove" of stones on the beach. Ate more of the oysters raw. Very delicious meal. Some of the other kayakers were looking enviously at our meal! (They were eating packaged mac and cheese. Poor buggers.)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34595=274-stone-rocket-stove-small.jpg)

CT: Ocean**

Suntanned and did over 20 minutes in ocean today. Water felt surprising warm to me. Slept in tent. Don't know the nighttime temperature or ocean temperature. I got really cold in the night. I had a series of shivering all night long. The shivering focused on my kidney area and made my sleep difficult. I slept with my jacket on but still was cold.

After ocean temperature:** 34.5C (Yes, that is right.)

Dr Kruse's explanation for shivering fit focused on Kidney area:

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?2221-Shivering-fit-aroung-kidney-area](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?2221-Shivering-fit-aroung-kidney-area)

Campsite Pictures**

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34595=275-campsite-day-2.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34595=276-massive-conifer-2.jpg)

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-06-29T21:57:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-34438>

**Day 13 (June 27, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.7C (Day 23 of cycle)

Matcha and herb tea

**Breakfast

Three raw oysters, one lamb chop, salad and homemade dressing. My salad wasn't looking that great today. Cooked everything on the stone rocket stove. Cooking with the rocket stove was much faster than our malfunctioning MSR stove.

CT: Exercise/Play**

About 3.0 hours of canoeing through the inner BG. Needed to catch the MV Frances Barkley today at Sechart Lodge. Felt rested after yesterday's easy day. Tried to stay uncovered while paddling. Had lots of goosebumps and got too much sun. Collected more oysters in the Pickerton Islands before returning to the lodge.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34597=277-too-small-rockcod.jpg)

Lunch/Dinner**

Wasn't very hungry today just ate homemade beef jerky.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-06-29T22:04:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-34442>

**Day 14 (June 28, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.3C (Day 24 of cycle)

3c coffee and commercial cream (nasty additives)

**Breakfast

Wasn't hungry so didn't eat breakfast.

**Lunch

Snacked on homemade beef jerky and raw cheese for the drive back home. Traffic was insane on main highway so we took highway 1 through the Fraser Canyon and got to see all the flooding.

Dinner**

Shared some French fries and vinegar with Erika at an old-fashioned burger place in Cache Creek. The French fries are made with real potatoes and I am sure cooked in some nasty vegetable oil. I wonder if we will ever see French fries in tallow again?

Native Food Fishery**

There was a sockeye salmon opening at Port Alberni. When we returned, the cash buyers had bought all the fish. Our prawn fisherman was gone for parts too. We were very disappointed. But as we were leaving Vancouver Island we saw a sign for sockeye on the highway and picked up ten whole fish at the side of the road!

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34601=281-native-food-fishery.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34601=282-whole-sockeye-salmon.jpg)

Flooding Pictures**

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34601=278-boiling-fraser-river.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34601=279-flooding-thompson.jpg)

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-06-30T03:52:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-34515>

**Day 15 (June 29, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.6C (Day 25 of cycle)

4c coffee, raw milk, cream, and 4tsp pastured butter

**Tub

15 minutes at 17.2C

Felt good in the tub. Very easy.

After tub temperature: 36.9C

**Breakfast**

I helped clean 10 sockeye salmon into steaks and fillets and salted the roe of 7 females. Breakfast was grilled sockeye salmon bones, which is the part left over from filleting, and garden salad greens and homemade dressing.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34674=280-grilled-salmon-bones.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34674=286-salmon-roe-salt.jpg)

Lunch/Dinner**

Had the remainder of the grilled sockeye salmon bones with garden salad greens and homemade dressing. I had a raw oyster for dessert. I have grilled the heads but I was too full to eat one. I guess for breakfast tomorrow.

After dinner temperature: 36.9C

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34674=284-salmon-bones-oysters.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34674=285-raw-oyster-dessert.jpg)

Had three dried figs later in the day.

Spot Ice**

Block ice: 60 minutes (lower abdomen)

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-06-30T20:11:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-34637>

**Day 16 (June 30, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.3C (Day 26 of cycle)

Sudden wake up 5:00am this morning. Our cows were in the neighbors yard. Had to run about in the rain a chase cows.

3c coffee, raw cream, and 3tsp pastured butter

**Breakfast

Breakfast was the grilled sockeye salmon heads warmed up in pastured butter with garden salad greens and homemade dressing. After the two raw oysters I could only eat one head.

After breakfast temperature: 35.9C

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34797=283-salmon-heads-oysters.jpg)

**Tub

15 minutes at 17.2C

Tub was later in the morning today. I had a really tough time in the tub. It felt really cold today. I was shivering almost from the first minute in the tub until I left the tub. The tub temperature did not change overnight. My cold tolerant did for some reason.

After tub temperature: 36.4C

**Spot Ice**

Block ice: 60 minutes (lower abdomen)

Lunch/Dinner**

Enjoyed a dinner of pork shank, buttered squash with garden salad and homemade dressing. I had a half glass of raw milk. Had a earlier snack of two small bowls of dried coconut. I am not a big drinker and I can't tolerate most alcoholic products because of the additives. I enjoyed a very small amount of Taboo Absinthe** which is locally made in Vernon, BC:

[http://tabooabsinthe.com/taboo-absinthe.html](http://http://tabooabsinthe.com/taboo-absinthe.html)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34797=294-pork-shank-squash-salad.jpg)

End of Cycle**

Started period in the afternoon. I have had a very short cycle this month. I have been told this is a sign of low progesterone levels.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-01T19:23:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-34823>

**Day 17 (July 1, 2012) (Weight 131 pounds)

Waking Temperature: 36.4C (Day 2 of cycle)

3c coffee, raw mik, and 3tsp pastured butter

After coffee temperature: 36.5C

**Tub

15 minutes at 17.2C

Tub was easier today then yesterday. I didn't shiver at all but I was feeling cold about half way through. When it gets hard to stay in the water I use Wim Hof's words: _"The cold is my friend."_ Seems to work for me.

After tub temperature: 35.9C

**Breakfast

Not hungry this morning. All the rich food the last few days is catching up with me. I have zero appetite. Ate just after 11:00am. Wasn't hungry but wanted to go biking and not bring any snacks so ate before going. I had another fish head and some extra fins with lettuce cooked in butter. I had a raw oyster and some of the salted salmon roe. I wasn't that pleased with the salted roe. I wish I had just eaten it fresh and froze the rest.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34983=295-fishhead-roe-oyster-lettuce.jpg)

Exercise/Play**

Easy family bicycle ride for about 1.0 hour. Beautiful day. Rode along the emergency flooding dikes for the Thompson River. Erika wasn't feeling well after visiting Grandma's place.

Spot Ice**

Block ice: 60 minutes (lower abdomen)

Lunch/Dinner**

Not hungry for dinner. Had a few pieces of the roasted fatty parts of some buffalo. My husband processed the lean buffalo meat into jerky. More thirsty today so drank 2c raw milk.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-02T18:50:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-35051>

**Day 18 (July 2, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.2C (Day 3 of cycle)

3c coffee, raw cream, and 3tsp pastured butter

After coffee temperature: 36.9C

**Tub

15 minutes at 17.2C

Tub was easier today. I had no shivering in the tub, just goosebumps. I still feel cold after getting out. It seems that if my temperature entering the tub is above about 36.5C the tub is okay. Below that temperature the tub is tough.

After tub temperature: 36.1C

**Breakfast

Not hungry again this morning. I will just feast at mid-day. I spent time yesterday making a SCD/GAPS or modified paleo birthday cheesecake and chocolate mousse so there is a birthday to celebrate today. I will make some raw milk ice cream too. I have the last of the lamb ribs to prepare.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/35211=298-cheesecake-chocolate-mousse.jpg)

**Exercise/Play**

Nice easy 1.0 hour walk with Shaen and Sonja. We wanted to take some pictures of the emergency flooding dikes in Riverside Park after the Canada Day celebrations.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/35211=301-riverside-flood-protection.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/35211=302-end-sand-dike.jpg)

Had a second easy 1.0 hour walk with Shaen. Got to enjoy the hot pine smell at the end of the day.

Lunch/Dinner**

Had a nice birthday meal of roasted lamb ribs and Caeser salad followed by cheesecake and chocolate mousse. I enjoyed a very small amount of Taboo Absinthe after the dessert course.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/35211=300-caeser-lamb-ribs.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/35211=299-birthday-cheesecake.jpg)

Spot Ice**

Block ice: 60 minutes (lower abdomen)

Supplements**

I found I really can't tolerate these supplements everyday. I find they bother my digestion. I don't know what it is but I am very sensitive to industrial additives and coloring. I think I will try to take them every second or third day.

Life Extension Mix Powder: 1 scoop

Life Extension Booster Softgels: 1 softgel

Life Extension Super Omega 3: 2 softgels

Life Extension Rhodiola Extract: 250mg 1 capsule

Life Extension Resverstrol: 100mg 1 capsule

Life Extension N-Acetyl Cysteine: 600mg 1 capsule

AOR Cogni-Q PQQ: 320mg 1 capsule

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-03T17:45:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-35287>

**Day 19 (July 3, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.0C (Day 4 of cycle)

2c coffee, raw cream, and 3tsp pastured butter

After coffee temperature: 36.5C

I am going into the coldest part of my cycle which occurs at the end of my period, usually around day 5 to day 7. I usually will drop into the high or mid 35.0C first thing in the morning.

**Tub

15 minutes at 16.7C

Tub was hard today. I shivered once half way through. Just goosebumps like normal. Can feel that icy feeling in my mid-back just above the kidney area. Very happy to get out of the tub today. Brrr.

After tub temperature: 36.0C

**Breakfast

Not hungry again this morning. Drinking all those calories in cream and butter sure makes me not want to eat. Ate a bit later in the morning. I had some oysters cooked in butter, leftover lamb ribs and garden salad. I can't believe the growth from my salad garden this year. Kamloops is normally very dry but this year is not normal.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/35447=304-garden-greens-bitters.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/35447=305-ribs-oysters-salad.jpg)

Exercise/Play**

Had a nice walk 2.5 hour in the rain with Shaen. I find the flooding very interesting and I am amazed at observing the power of the river. Had a nice nap after the walk too.

Lunch/Dinner**

I had more leftover lamb ribs, buttered squash and garden salad with homemade dressing.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/35447=306-ribs-squash-salad.jpg)

Spot Ice**

Block ice: 60 minutes (lower abdomen)

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-04T17:51:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-35478>

**Day 20 (July 4, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.1C (Day 5 of cycle)

3c coffee, raw cream, and 3tsp pastured butter

After coffee temperature: 36.5C

**Tub

15 minutes at 15.5C

Tub was hard again today. I shivered once or twice half way through. Normal goosebumps during and after tub. I was very happy to get out of the tub. Today was tub cleaning day. I refilled the tub with water and added cider vinegar for this week. I feel better about reusing my tub water for about a week. Partly it is just be frugal and partly it's not wanting to waste water in a desert/grassland area like Kamloops. I am thinking of a water of using my waste grey-water in the garden. I think I have figured out a way to do it for next week. Here is my morning's torture device being refilled:

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/35641=307-moonsoaker-tub.jpg)

After tub temperature: 35.9C

If you like my tub and are building a bathroom, here is the information:

[http://www.venco.ca/bathware-products/moonsoaker.php](http://http://www.venco.ca/bathware-products/moonsoaker.php)

**Breakfast

Not hungry again this morning. I wanted to go for a walk this morning with Shaen so I just ate to get it out of the way. We shared a breakfast of the last of the oysters cooked in butter with a hash of onions, pork and mushrooms. I can't believe how wonderful the garden salad greens are this year. To top off a fantastic breakfast a friend brought over some kombucha. I had about 1/2c. Very delicious brew!

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/35641=308-oysters-hash-salad.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/35641=310-kombucha-gift.jpg)

**Exercise/Play**

Had a nice walk 1.0 hour in with Shaen. We walked by a swamp with some very strange looking birds. I have to get out my bird book and try to find out which bird we saw. Spent the day at the lake suntanning and enjoying the day. Erika fished. Shaen and Sonja rode bicycles. I got into that cold lake and dried off in the sun, when there was sun. The temperature was all over the place. When the sun was out it was 34.4C; when the sun was behind the clouds it was 18.3C. We all went for a very short 0.45 hour canoe paddle.

CT: Lake**

The water was 16.1C. I stayed in for 13 minutes and tried to air dry. The sun was gone and it was cold but I did my best. My temperature after about 10 minutes of air drying was 35.2C.

Lunch/Dinner**

We had paleo burgers wrapped in lettuce leaves cooked on the rocket stove. I just love my rocket stove.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/35641=309-rocket-stove-burgers.jpg)

If you are interested, here is more information about rocket stoves:

[http://eatkamloops.org/rocket-stoves/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/rocket-stoves/)

Snack**

Made some coconut candy. I ate coconut candy and jerky.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-05T19:17:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-35668>

**Day 21 (July 5, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.1C (Day 6 of cycle)

2c coffee, raw cream, and 2tsp pastured butter

After coffee temperature: 35.4C

**Tub

15 minutes at 16.7C

I was surprised that the tub wasn't hard today with such a low temperature going in. Actually, the cold water felt good on my slightly pink areas from suntanning yesterday. I was wondering what my temperature would be like coming out. I think my cycle seems to have a very strong effect on my CT. Also, I don't necessarily "feel" cold when my temperature is cold. I don't know what all that means but I will continue to just observe my experiment.

After tub temperature: 36.5C

**Breakfast

I was not hungry again this morning. I have to work today so just ate to get it out of the way. I had leftover hash, squash and salad.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/35831=315-hash-squash-salad.jpg)

**Snack**

I had about 3c of matcha tea with some coconut candy.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/35831=316-coconut-candy.jpg)

Lunch/Dinner****

Wasn't hungry for dinner but Shaen made a delicious roast beef. I ate some to be social but the wonderful smell gave me appetite. I poured the beef juice over the squash. Of course, I enjoyed some garden greens.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/35831=317-beef-roast-juice.jpg)

Spot Ice**

Block ice: 60 minutes (lower abdomen)

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-06T18:08:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-35864>

**Day 22 (July 6, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.0C (Day 7 of cycle)

3c coffee, raw cream, and 3tsp pastured butter

After coffee temperature: 36.6C

**Consult with Dr Kruse

I was to have a consult with Dr Kruse today at 7:00amPST. Unfortunately, I was the only one there on the conference call. I stayed for until 7:30amPST, had my fill of telephone music, and finally hung up. I have put a note in "Ask Jack" about rescheduling the consult for another time and send him a private email. I was disappointed but stuff does happen. I am also having problems with my Karnivore membership. I still do not have access to the new webinar. Misty has been working on it this week. I am feeling impatient.

**Tub

15 minutes at 16.7C

Tub was easy today. I am still face dunking throughout the time I am in the tub.

After tub temperature: 36.0C

Breakfast**

I was not hungry again this morning. Will eat later. I had a nice breakfast of eggs, oysters, squash and salad. My dill is up and I added it to my homemade dressing.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/36028=320-eggs-oysters-dill-salad.jpg)

Spot Ice**

Block ice: 60 minutes (lower abdomen)

Lunch/Dinner**

Had to work late tonight. Ate a bit of coconut candy and matcha to keep going. I had a delicious meal of prime rib roast and Caesar salad. This is one of the benefits of getting the whole animal. If I had to buy prime rib it would be about $60 per roast. I would never do so but when you buy the whole animal you get the prime cuts along with the cheaper cuts.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/36028=321-prime-rib-caesar.jpg)

### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-06T18:34:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-35875>

and Dr K answered you......in ask Jack.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-07T17:04:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-36079>

**Day 23 (July 7, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.0C (Day 8 of cycle)

3c coffee, raw cream, and 3tsp pastured butter

**Breakfast

I have an extremely long day today. Shaen is off on a long-distance delivery and I have to run the show. It is days like today I see just how hard my husband works for his family. My breakfast was mushroom, onion and cheese omelette with buttered swiss chard. Off to do his gardening and farming chores.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/36245=322-chard-omelette.jpg)

**Thank you Misty!

I have been having problems accessing the Karnivore membership materials for over a week. Misty has been working on it but nothing that is done seems to change my lack of access. Nevertheless, after many attempts to get me access to the membership materials, Misty emailed me the new webinar on hormones. Thank you, Misty. I will listen to it now.

**Gardening and Farming Chores**

I spent about a hour this morning milking the cow, feeding the chickens and hogs, collecting the eggs, and processing the milk and eggs. I spent 30 minutes watering the gardens in the morning and then again in the evening. It wasn't hard work just took some time.

Lunch/Dinner**

A very long day again today. I had a paleo burger with cheese and onions on a bed of garden greens with dressing. Everything was topped with homemade mayo. I also had a glass of raw milk.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/36245=323-paleo-burger-milk.jpg)

Tub**

15 minutes at 18.3C

Before tub temperature: 36.4C

I "felt" really hot today but my temperature wasn't that hot. I found the tub refreshing after a hot day in the office. Started thinking about ways to "cool" down my tub as I go into the hot part of summer. I have found two 15kg honey pails. I have filled them 2/3 full of water and have put them in the deep freezer for later use.

After tub temperature: 36.2C

Spot Ice**

Right after the tub I spot iced. The spot ice felt colder after the tub.

Block ice: 30 minutes (lower abdomen)

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-08T20:17:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-36213>

**Day 24 (July 8, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.2C (Day 9 of cycle)

3c coffee, raw cream, and 3tsp pastured butter

**Gardening and Farming Chores

I spent about a hour this morning milking the cow, feeding the cattle, chickens and hogs, and processing the milk. I spent 30 minutes watering the gardens. It wasn't hard work just took some time.

Temperature after chores: 36.0C

This is typical of me to get "cold" after light exercise not heat up like most people.

**Tub

15 minutes at 19.4C

Before tub temperature: 36.0C

I "felt" really hot after my chores. Right after finishing chores I jumped into the tub. Felt cool and refreshing for the first five minutes. Okay after that; the tub temperature is up today. This is the coldest time of my cycle so I don't expect it to be easy.

After tub temperature: 36.2C

**Breakfast**

Not hungry yet. I will eat later. I am thinking about taking the girls to the lake so I will look around for some picnic foods. I am working on a new raw cookie recipe. Still haven't had any luck with it. Finally got the "dough" right on the cookies using coconut cream and coconut oil. Here is what I made; a bit of a fussy recipe. It is a "raw" Hamantashen cookie. I will post the recipe on eatkamloops.org at I get the bugs out of the recipe.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/36379=325-hamans-hat-cookies.jpg)

Lunch/Dinner**

Finally got somewhat hungry about 1:15pm so I had leftovers of prime rib and fried onions with garden salad and homemade mayo.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/36379=324-mayo-prime-rib-onions.jpg)

Suntanning/Tub**

After suntanning temperature: 37.4C

15 minutes at 19.4C

I decided to try some suntanning and then jumping into the tub. That was a wild experience. I stay in the sun as long as I could, checked my temperature, and got into the tub. The tub is still about 19.4C. It felt good for the first minute then I started to feel really dizzy. I was wondering if I should get out. I didn't want to black-out in the tub and drown. I decided to just be careful and stay in the tub. In about four minutes the dizziness had stopped and I started seeing the surface of the water start to move like in an earthquake. I also had this strange feeling in my mid and upper back. I can't describe it. The earthquake was me starting to uncontrollably shake. I shivered hard, the whole rest of the time in the tub. Was I getting hot or cold? I couldn't tell. I had to hold the thermometer to keep it in my mouth, I was shaking so hard. Well, I was getting cold. Just call me lizard woman!

After tub temperature: 35.9C

Snack**

Not really hungry but my girls loved the cookies and wanted me to try them. I had two to be sociable.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-09T18:05:20+0300

The tub was very easy today. I emptied my tub and refilled it with fresh water and epsom salts. I didn't have time to siphon the water from my tub into the garden. It makes me unhappy that we cannot legally plumb in grey-water systems especially in a dry area like Kamloops.

After tub temperature: 36.0C

**Breakfast**

Suntanning/CT lake**

Lunch/Dinner**

Good appetite after the day of suntanning and lake dipping. I ate a Hamen's Hat Cookie before dinner. I found the mouth texture creamy just like butter cookies. I can see why the girls liked them. I had another paleo burger with cheese, mushrooms and onions on a bed of garden greens. It was too hot to cook in the house so we got out the rocket stove and made dinner outside.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-13T18:18:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-37489>

**Day 29 (July 13, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.6C (Day 14 of cycle)

3c coffee, raw cream, raw milk and 3tsp pastured butter

By the way, our neighbors at the trailer park found our hogs and kept them safe all night. The hogs slept under the porch with their dogs. They where nice enough to put the hose on so the hogs had a nice cool wallow. They also stopped the bylaw enforcers from taking the hogs away and giving us a nice big fine for not containing our livestock properly. We were very grateful for their help.

After coffee temperature and reading the new blog: 36.6C

**Infection

I have an ear infection which made last night's sleep difficult. I have a weakness with my right ear, swimmer's ear. I have to use an ear plug in that ear if I want to go underwater. Unfortunately, my daughter Erika really wanted to dive underwater with me. Stupidly, I did so. The infection is in the inner ear and fluid is passing out of the ear. Not a great sign. I have tried a solution of hydrogen peroxide. Hopefully that will be enough. I'm okay with topical antibiotics but I only use them as a last resort.

By mid-day my ear was very sore and I was finding it hard to work and to hear. Unfortunately, I don't have one of those jobs that I can get time-off to be sick. I started a topical antibiotic.

**Tub

15 minutes at 21.2C

Before tub temperature: 36.6C

I will have to start using the ice pails for my tub dips. The internal temperature of the house is not going down very much at night so my heat sink tub is 21.1C(70F). I will try later today if I get a chance.

After tub temperature: 36.2C

**Breakfast

Not hungry yet. Had my breakfast at 9:45am. Shaen and made grilled chuck steak, and butter mushrooms, onions, and zucchini. I had a garden salad with homemade dressing.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/37656=349-chuck-zucchini-salad.jpg)

Research**

I am doing research on testing I can purchase myself. I am interested in blood tests for vitamin D levels and hsCRP. Also saliva tests for female and adrenal hormones. I live in Canada, otherwise I would just use Life Extension Foundation's** tests:

[http://www.lef.org/Vitamins-Supplements/Blood-Tests/Blood-Tests.htm](http://http://www.lef.org/Vitamins-Supplements/Blood-Tests/Blood-Tests.htm)

Since I live in Canada I need a different source of testing. I have found Rocky Mountain Analytical** but they have been recently taken over by ZRT**. This is the company that Dr Kruse mentioned in his hormone webinar:

[http://www.rmalab.com/](http://http://www.rmalab.com/)

[http://www.zrtlab.com/](http://http://www.zrtlab.com/)

I went to talk to the two compounding pharmacists in Kamloops about doctors that can do hormone testing in the area. One place recommends a doctor in Kelowna which is a three hour drive from Kamloops. One place uses ZRT and the pharmacist helps with the assessment of the tests. Of course, I would still need a doctor to write the script for any bio-identical hormones I might need, if I choose to go that way.

I know I want the testing for a baseline. Knowing my vitamin D statue and my hsCRP is very important information. Knowing my female hormone statue would be useful to know if any of my "interventions" are actually doing anything useful. Of course, there is the "cost" of knowing these numbers and the temptation to "do" something about those numbers. I have to assume, I, or someone I hire, is wiser than my body's internal wisdom. I'm not sure how I stand on bio-identical hormones. I would not have a problem with short-term use but I feel uncomfortable about long-term use. I guess that was what attracted me to Dr Kruse's system. The idea of "curing" my hormone problems without drugs or bio-identical hormones.

The money "cost" of these test depends on how often I would do them and who I use. If I use my local compounding pharmacists, the hsCRP and vitamin D could be done twice a year for a cost of $165 each time. The female hormone panel could be done three times a year for $275 each time. (Dr Kruse recommends four times a year.) For each year the testing would cost $1155. This cost is before any treatment.

Even though medical care is free in Canada, I don't have a local doctor. For the last three years I have completely avoided the medical system. I try to handle everything myself. I actually like it and I have learned a lot about basic medical care. BUT I do wonder what I am missing. I wonder if I could be better if I could find someone that really knows this stuff and was willing to help me. I am full of doubt and I don't know how I am to proceed. I wonder if the money is well spent on these kind of tests. I wonder if I should just say no to testing and do the best I can with my intuition and home testing methods.

**Snack

I had four small pieces of fig bar. I am working on adding more coconut cream to a recipe on eatkamloops.org:

[http://eatkamloops.org/raw-fig-bars/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/raw-fig-bars/)

**Suntanning/Tub**

15 minutes at 21.2C

Before tub temperature: 37.2C

Had a very short period to suntan today. I tried my ice pails in the tub. It didn't make much of a difference in the temperature. It seemed cold as the time went on but that is normal anyways. The surface of the water got a bit colder.

After tub temperature: 36.2C

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/37656=355-whole-ice-pail-tub.jpg)

Dinner**

I had cold sockeye with homemade mayo and a salad on the side. I got full before starting the second sockeye steak.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/37656=350-mayo-cold-sockeye.jpg)

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-14T16:29:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-37743>

**Day 30 (July 14, 2012) (weight 134lbs)

Waking Temperature: 36.5C (Day 15 of cycle)

3c coffee, raw cream, raw milk and 3tsp pastured butter

**Infection

I am completely out of pain this morning. Topical antibiotics sure work wonders. I will take the topical preparation for another day.

**CT Experiment

Here are my observations from the last 30 Days. If anyone has any commons please feel free to enlighten me! I am wondering if there are things I should be doing differently or if I should just stop this experiment.

These are some of the things I noticed over the last 30 days of CT. Do note I started CT in the middle of my last cycle when my temperatures were their "hottest":

1\. I had a shorter cycle than normal, only 25 days.

2\. I had a longer period than normal, 6 days rather than 4-5 days. Normally, my periods start and stop without the nonsense of spotting.

3\. I had some spotting on day 9 which normally never happens. There was big and black clots of blood. Sorry, for the graphic detail.

4\. I found it easier to do the tub if my temperature getting into the tub was over 36.5C. This means the luteal phase (after ovulation) of my cycle is easier for CT.

5\. The follicular phase (before ovulation) is harder to do CT because my temperatures are naturally lower during that time.

6\. My follicular phase appears to have a more "stable" temperature profile from day-to-day. Normally, my temperatures can be all over the place.

7\. I had 10 days in my follicular phase under 36.3C which according to Katie Singer would mean I have a "cold uterus". (More than 3 days under 36.3C means infertility.)

8\. I don't tend to weigh myself more than once a week. I have gained 3 pounds this month but that really doesn't mean much. I never did my measurements during the month so I can't say if my body composition has changed. I can say this. I have two sizes of pants size 8 and 10. Size 10 are really loose and size 8 are comfortable. My husbands says I look firmer, but he's a really nice guy!

9\. I am more tolerate to cold. I am willing to into the local lakes and rivers; I would not have been willing to do so in the past.

Tub**

15 minutes at tub temperature: 22.2C

Temperature before tub: 36.5C

Tub water felt down right hot today. The house internal temperature hit 29.4C. I think the heatwave will end today though. Nevertheless, my temperature dropped again. Changed my tub water today and added epsom salts.

Temperature after tub: 35.9C

Breakfast**

I had two eggs, beef and onion hash, and salad with dressing.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/37910=351-eggs-beef-hash.jpg)

Tub**

15 minutes at tub temperature: 22.2-18.3C

Temperature before tub: 36.8C

I have tried using a pail filled with frozen water to get the temperature of my tub down. It didn't really work so today I decided to dump the ice right into the tub. That brought the tub temperature down. It was sort of fun feeling the different thermoclines in the tub. Cold on the top. Cold on the bottom and warm in between. The ice was 2/3th gone in 15 minutes. I refilled the pail with the remaining ice and refilled with cold water. I hope my freezer can keep up with this level of ice production.

Temperature after tub: 35.5C

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/37910=352-ice-pail-deep-freezer.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/37910=353-ice-in-tub.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/37910=354-remaining-ice.jpg)

**Dinner**

I was hungry for dinner tonight. I had a garden salad and homemade dressing. I had some chuck steak, mushrooms and onion hash. Delicious leftovers. I don't think I will be taking anymore pictures of meals. I think after a month of pictures the type of meals I have are clear.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-14T23:49:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-37850>

I have comments!

Caroline - I think I have a little "perspective" now, a bigger picture if you will. I did a solid 3 months of CT ending in some pretty regular deep CT. I took time off to get testing and then decided to do a round of HCG, so I've not done CT for the summer.

I did not pay attention to cycle stuff, as I was feeling like I had to force it anyway. . some days were harder, some were easier so I just plowed through in the hopes of faster results.

I had some typical Detox symptoms, but for the most part they didn't bother me. . .then after the 2nd month I had some MAJOR cycle disturbances and crazy lady things. . . I thought I was hitting menopause and full of fibroids and I was bleeding 14 days a month for two months. I had some MAJOR clotting - I won't go into details here but suffice it to say if I had been 'active' in the bedroom I would have swore I was having a miscarriage.

This spurred me to get moving on getting the labs/tests/doctor visits going - turns out there was absolutely nothing wrong down there. . . but I did notice the month AFTER I stopped the ct, the cycle got back to normal and all calmed down.

My thoughts all along were "this is just estrogen dumping from all the CT", and in retrospect I'm sure that was detox stuff.

My take away, and suggestion for you is not to be afraid to back it off and let your body work through the detox when it happens. If I had gone slower - say 3X a week at 50 degrees, or didn't do the Deep CT as often - I probably would have been better off.

I saw so many great things from the CT, I can't wait to get back to it. Plus, isn't the detox actually proof that it's working?

So, I say when the detox gets too severe - back off! Take your time and enjoy the ride.

3 lbds weight change can't really tell ya much can it? One thing I didn't get was weight loss from it. I did lots of CT plus the Leptin RX - but my health was headed in the right direction and that's the point.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-15T01:31:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-37877>

> **Caroline Cooper;38044 wrote:** Hi Cavemam,
>
>
>
> So you think I am having an estrogen dump? My charts do seem to show that I am estrogen dominant. I have been wondering if I should do some bio-identical progesterone. I was hoping that the CT would solve my hormone problems and low temperature problems.
>
>
>
> I just don't know what to do. I feel very confused and full of doubt. I have been researching blood and saliva testing this last week. (Yesterday I posted about my confusion and my doubts.) I wonder if I should even go down that road or just live with the way I am.

When you mentioned the clotting, it did make me think you are having an estrogen dump. Seems like others reported that type of thing too. Overall, there is much to be gained from the CT - and the negatives can be managed. If the detox for you right now is the estrogen thing, perhaps back off - see how you do the next month. It's hard not to worry "something is wrong" when your body goes a little wonky.

The low temps may point to some general trend, and I think the thing for women I've noticed is the "time frame" seems very different for women than men. My observation of just reading all these posts since about February is that women don't start to turn around for MONTHS. There are examples of those that see positives sooner, but my experience was a longer time frame and if you DO have any underlying corrections to make it very well can take a while with just CT.

BUT, are there not any positives right now with the CT? I noticed within the first month the calmness, good sleep, better skin (when it wasn't worse from detox). . and other things.

What are you positives? Hormonal dumps also explain emotional rollercoaster things.

Hell, if I am dumping estrogen I don't want to stop CT. The question is "if" I am dumping estrogen. How would I know? I wouldn't say I am on an emotional roller-coaster. I just feel full of indecision about if I should spend my family's resources on testing. Another question is what would I do with the information? If I decided to use the information found in the testing, would I be able to make a wise decision about something as complicated as my endocrine system? Or could I find someone wise enough to work with? Just because I "can do" something doesn't mean I "should do" something.

The biggest positive of CT is being more comfortable with the idea of going into cold water. I can enjoy the lake with my girls. I am not "scared" of the cold and have convinced myself that even though I have an aversion to the cold, the cold is "good" for me. Of course, that statement may not be factual.

Thank you for your help. Please continue with your observations. It is helping me clarify my concerns.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-15T02:41:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-37892>

> The biggest positive of CT is being more comfortable with the idea of going into cold water. I can enjoy the lake with my girls. I am not "scared" of the cold and have convinced myself that even though I have an aversion to the cold, the cold is "good" for me. Of course, that statement may not be factual.
>
>
>
> Thank you for your help. Please continue with your observations. It is helping me clarify my concerns.

Hi Caroline. Not sure if this will help, but I'd say getting at least your P and E tested would be helpful. If you are E dominant, you can pretty much guess that you've been dumping E. But you also need to help support that process because just releasing E into your system via CT doesn't mean it's clearing your system, especially if you have a history of a leaky gut. Did you hear the BHRT webinar? It would probably be super helpful to you. Also, when you're E dominant you increase your SHBG which then binds up needed T3 so you end up with hypothyroid symptoms (hence feeling cold/intolerant to cold)... another reason to clear that E. I have no idea how expensive the basic P:E ratio test is, but it's pretty simple and can be a blood test, so I'm guessing it wouldn't break the bank at all to self-pay if you have to. Good luck!

I am not "scared" of the cold and have convinced myself that even though I have an aversion to the cold, the cold is "good" for me. Of course, that statement may not be factual.
>

>
> Thank you for your help. Please continue with your observations. It is helping me clarify my concerns.

I have comments too. It would be hard to know if for sure it is an estrogen dump unless you tested before and after. I can tell you I did go thru an estrogen dump and have been tested and my results show me that. I was very e dominant so much so that Dr. K. chimed into one of my posts and gave me his thoughts. I had no idea how bad I was. I can't remember if I had the e dump 1 or 2 months after starting. But, for 2 weeks it was hell. I had 1 terrible week of pms and then another week of TOM. I don't normally have a tom cuz I had thermal ablation done around 12 yrs or so ago. So to go from not having one to having spotting for 6 days was crazy. I got thru it and I haven't had anything since. I don't even get the nasty breast tenderness that use to plague me for 2 weeks every month. I believe in CT and the benefits for sure. There were a few times I had a headache and then did my CT and it took it away. I wouldn't say quit now especially if you are having negative things result from it. I believe that means you are detoxing and that is a good thing. I also look at this as when a doctor tells you to ice something as it reduces inflammation. This is doing exactly that as far as I know. I hope you sort this out and keep posting.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-15T03:43:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-37908>

> **vkiernan;38063 wrote:** I have comments too. It would be hard to know if for sure it is an estrogen dump unless you tested before and after. I can tell you I did go thru an estrogen dump and have been tested and my results show me that. I was very e dominant so much so that Dr. K. chimed into one of my posts and gave me his thoughts. I had no idea how bad I was. I can't remember if I had the e dump 1 or 2 months after starting. But, for 2 weeks it was hell. I had 1 terrible week of pms and then another week of TOM. I don't normally have a tom cuz I had thermal ablation done around 12 yrs or so ago. So to go from not having one to having spotting for 6 days was crazy. I got thru it and I haven't had anything since. I don't even get the nasty breast tenderness that use to plague me for 2 weeks every month. I believe in CT and the benefits for sure. There were a few times I had a headache and then did my CT and it took it away. I wouldn't say quit now especially if you are having negative things result from it. I believe that means you are detoxing and that is a good thing. I also look at this as when a doctor tells you to ice something as it reduces inflammation. This is doing exactly that as far as I know. I hope you sort this out and keep posting.

Hi vkiernan,

What is TOM? I don't think I have PMS. Or if I do, it isn't very bad. I have heard horror stories.

How long did you do CT before you really felt you had gone out of it what CT has to offer?

Maybe it would be helpful to outline what I was hoping CT would do for me:

1\. I hoped it would increase my overall body temperature.

2\. I hoped my cycles would be a few days longer. In particular my luteal phase which would mean my progesterone ratio is better.

3\. I hoped I would have a better temperature transition - a nice snappy rise in one day - from my follicular phase to my luteal phase.

4\. I hoped it would "fix" my hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis without the use of bio-identical hormones or "manually" messing with my endocrine system.

I got so excited about Dr Kruse's words about "fixing" adrenal or thyroid problems on the brain level. (The ancient pathway.) That even if your thyroid was in a surgeon's pan in some operating room it wouldn't matter. I am disappointed that more hasn't happened and my "numbers" are actually going the wrong direction. Rats.

Maybe I also feel dis-empowered by the whole "testing process". I so like home testing where I am in control and understand the process. Handing it over to the specialists just doesn't sit well with me. I guess I am scared I wont be able to stop/control the process if I put myself in the hands of doctors.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-15T03:54:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-37910>

The hormone panel is the ultimate mirror to your brain health........if you do not see that via the Brain Gut series.......I am failing. Your case is a screaming obvious case of what is wrong. Its time that you begin to put it together.......Miss Vickie was in despair all last yr at MDA........and she is slowly slaying her dragons......once you the know the enemy the battle is just then attrition. Who has the most staying power wins. For some people on here they cant handle that.........that is on them. It is your decision what camp you are in. Do I think you can win...........doubt I would be here even posting a comment if I thought you were a lost cause.

Get serious about testing and learning. It's time to step up your game or wallow in mediocrity. Your choice.

I am disappointed that more hasn't happened and my "numbers" are actually going the wrong direction. Rats.

Sorry, TOM = time of month. Very positive that you don't have pms. I envy you but e-dom. and my age I'm sure play a part. I have to believe CT has increased my body temp. I am warm all the time now and people at work are running around with sweaters and I say OMG it's hot in here. My hubby said I am way warmer too. As for the adrenals, I have AF and it will be along road to fix them. I think I will be at it for a bit. I do believe I am making progress there though. I would like to take another 4x salivary cortisol test to see where I am at. I was on bio-identical hydrocortisone for the adrenals but stopped cuz I didn't want to take it anymore due to weight gain (5 lbs in less than 2 months) and I needed a few more tests and couldn't be on it any longer. For some other positive changes, I am waiting for some more test results to come back. My FT3 is over the range and before I never made it to the middle of the range. I am on Armour but have now backed off the dose; I have been on this same dose since the end of January. If my RT3 comes back low, then I will back off more on the Armour and that means things are really starting to change for the better. If I am actually getting T3 into my cells, I will be one happy lady. This totally excites me about the possibility.

As for CT, I had detox after 2 weeks and then later when I had the estrogen issue. I have been doing it since mid to late February. Some weeks a lot and other weeks not so much. Just depends on my work schedule and how I feel. I am sure I am not done gaining benefits from it and will continue. Since I have gone thru 2 detox's, I feel I need it. My digestion has not worked my whole life and I'm sure I have a lot of detoxing still to do. My opinion is this all does not happen overnight or in a couple of months. Dr. K. talks about certain things happening 18 to 24 months out. That seems like a long time but I have been doing the LRx or over a year now so for me I can wait and keep on a going. I am getting better, not worse. 3 years ago I was in such a dark place and just couldn't stand it anymore. A lot has changed since then and I am on the road to recovery. Unfortunately it doesn't happen as fast as we'd like.

Hi Dr Kruse,

I get the sense that you are trying to encourage me with the comment: _"It's time to step up your game or wallow in mediocrity."_ But all I feel is a deep sadness. Maybe if you knew my history you wouldn't take that kind of tack with me. I have a learning disability, dyslexia. All my young life I was labelled as a C grade student. I have been called mediocre before because I didn't learn as fast as my peers nor could I do simple things that they found so easy.

I have also lived in a family with one genus. It can't be easy being the smartest guy in the room and wondering why everyone just doesn't get-it. I know it made him very unhappy to never have anyone at his level to converse with.

I will try to just hear the goodness you are trying to send me and not let my historical pain cloud how I hear your words.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-15T06:50:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-37990>

> **Caroline Cooper;38056 wrote:** Hell, if I am dumping estrogen I don't want to stop CT. The question is "if" I am dumping estrogen. How would I know? I wouldn't say I am on an emotional roller-coaster. I just feel full of indecision about if I should spend my family's resources on testing. Another question is what would I do with the information? If I decided to use the information found in the testing, would I be able to make a wise decision about something as complicated as my endocrine system? Or could I find someone wise enough to work with? Just because I "can do" something doesn't mean I "should do" something.
>
>
>
> The biggest positive of CT is being more comfortable with the idea of going into cold water. I can enjoy the lake with my girls. I am not "scared" of the cold and have convinced myself that even though I have an aversion to the cold, the cold is "good" for me. Of course, that statement may not be factual.
>
>
>
> Thank you for your help. Please continue with your observations. It is helping me clarify my concerns.

I think the thought process for this one is to reverse-engineer it. I love testing, and also hate not having real results - but I'm fortunate to be in a position where that doesn't strain my family resources. I've been there though, and sometimes you have to make the best of it in your circumstance and do a cost/benefit analysis.

Here are the things I can tell you that I think are relevant:

1\. 1 month of CT will help with things, but its really just getting started. I think you ARE experiencing detox and that is good!

2\. Your "reasons" all seem more like adjusting symptoms to underlying things rather than accomplishing a real health change in your body. I am by no means all that up on cycle stuff. . the first time I tracked mine in my entire life was the last two months - but I think what you mean is you are using those signs as a tracker for if your hormones are operating well. It sounds like you know what you are talking about. If those are reasonable ways to end run around an expensive lab report - then so be it, they are telling you something.

3\. Your real concern seems to be "what I'm doing isn't working". I think it's too early in the CT process to dismiss it. Nobody is fixing hormone issues in a month. Your diet and lifestyle seem very directionally positive. Changing is always full of failures and things that don't work. Two steps forward, one step back and all that.

Also, the experience of finding a doc to get all of this done is not always a minor hill to climb. I have spent waaaaaay too many hours and quite a bit of money and STILL haven't found a doc to work with me or help with ordering these things. Yes, I could just go order and pay out of pocket but there I would be with a ton of info I didn't quite know what to do with. Sometimes the best path for YOU is just that, and doesn't make it a mediocre path - it just makes it your path.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-15T17:59:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-38056>

> **KiwiLauren;38060 wrote:** Hi Caroline. Not sure if this will help, but I'd say getting at least your P and E tested would be helpful. If you are E dominant, you can pretty much guess that you've been dumping E. But you also need to help support that process because just releasing E into your system via CT doesn't mean it's clearing your system, especially if you have a history of a leaky gut. Did you hear the BHRT webinar? It would probably be super helpful to you. Also, when you're E dominant you increase your SHBG which then binds up needed T3 so you end up with hypothyroid symptoms (hence feeling cold/intolerant to cold)... another reason to clear that E. I have no idea how expensive the basic P:E ratio test is, but it's pretty simple and can be a blood test, so I'm guessing it wouldn't break the bank at all to self-pay if you have to. Good luck!

KiviLauren,

Thank you for your comment. I think that is good advance. Yes, I have listened to the webinar on hormones.

I have a few days to make the decision if I do the testing this month or wait another month or longer. I must remember I am in control of this experiment and how fast I process. Do I truly believe my body can heal itself? If I do, I can wait a few months. I have been working on this project for about two years. I have tried so many things over that two years. I guess I am getting impatient for "positive results".

You know, I haven't hurt myself nor have I given up my power and let someone else make the decisions. I have so many things to be grateful for. I am healthy. I am whole. I am in good balance with my emotional and mental space. Sure I'm sensitive which is a very natural womanly state. Thank you for your kind suggestions.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-15T19:39:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-38087>

**Day 31 (July 15, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.6C (Day 16 of cycle)

3c coffee, raw cream, and 3tsp pastured butter

I am thinking about stopping the coffee. My 23andMe.com results state I am a "fast metabolizer" for caffeine. But just because I can doesn't mean I should. My intuition tells me I have used caffeine for years to self-medicate. It makes me felt "better" in the short term but I can't help but wonder what it does to me on the long-term.

**Infection

I am on the last day of topical antibiotics for my ear infection. I have apologized to my ear canal flora about doing a "slash-a-burn" on their population. The good and the bad die equally in a fire. I have asked my good bacteria to forgive me. I just don't have a better way.

**Tub/10kg Ice Block

15 minutes at tub temperature: 19.4-18.3C

Temperature before tub: 37.2C

The heat-wave ended last night. I could see the light from the dry lightening strikes throughout the night, flashing like a camera. The rain storm came early in the morning and broke the heat. The house temperature should naturally cool now.

Temperature after tub: 36.4C

**Breakfast**

I am not hungry yet. We are making some plans to go up to Sunpeaks and up the chair-lift to go hiking the wild-flower meadows. Finally, I ate around 10:30am. I had the last of the grilled chuck steak with sauted onions. I had a garden salad with fresh cilantro and homemade dressing.

The mosquitoes have been ravenous the last few weeks. Shaen cut down the undergrowth so going into the garden doesn't mean getting carried-off by the insect world. We are pretty sure the Barn Swallows that have just recently move into the area have finished their nesting activities so less mosquitoes wont be a problem for them.

[http://www.allaboutbirds.org/guide/tree\_swallow/id](http://http://www.allaboutbirds.org/guide/tree_swallow/id)

Play/Exercise**

The girls did not want to go to the alpine meadows to see the wildflowers so Shaen and I left them at home. We took Sunburst Express chair-lift to Mid-Mountain at 1850m and hiked from there to Top of the World at 2080m. We continued a bit farther up Skunk before heading back. The wildflowers have just started. It was a wonderful walk! The round trip was about 2.0 hours. Here is a picture of me doing the trail and another doing some CT in the snow.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/38256=357-climb-totw.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/38256=356-ct-snow.jpg)

If you have never heard of Sunpeaks, it is a one of the many jewels of BC:

[http://www.sunpeaksresort.com/summer/interactive-maps/hiking](http://http://www.sunpeaksresort.com/summer/interactive-maps/hiking)

Snack**

We brought some homemade buffalo jerky for our hike. I had two small fig bars before doing my CT.

Tub/10kg Ice Block**

15 minutes at tub temperature: 18.3-16.7C

Temperature before tub: 37.0C

The water was wonderful at first but Sonja came in and started playing with the block of ice in the tub. I told her she was adding to the suffering of her poor old mother, but she just laughed at me. She thinks I'm a weirdo to get into a tub with ice.

Temperature after tub: 36.7C

**Dinner**

Didn't really feel like dinner. I guess all that snacking earlier today. Shaen made a beautiful Sunday Dinner of pork roast, squash and potatoes. I had some squash and butter. I made some homemade raw ice cream for dessert. The girls ran outside to get some raspberries from the garden for topping. I am so full now!

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/38256=358-vanilla-ice-cream-raspberries.jpg)

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-15T23:43:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-38125>

> **Caroline Cooper;38230 wrote: **Thank you all for taking the time out of your busy day to comment. I feel very supported and heard. I will commit to another month of my CT journal.
>
>
>
> Next Tuesday and Wednesday are important days. Will I do the ZRT blood and female hormone and adrenal saliva panel?** A base line would be very useful for my experiment but it takes me away from home testing and complete control. If I bring in others and their expertise on what I "should do" will I have the strength to continue to go my own way or will I give up my power to those authorities?** As you can see my autonomy is very important to my well-being but I know I will be influenced and feel the pressure of their opinions.

My gut feeling (just based on my short journey with this thus far) is that this will enhance your autonomy, not compromise it. You will have your labs to analyse and think about and react to as you see fit. You can put them in the recycling bin if you decide this was a bad move. But you can also gain some amazing insights from them. I was amazed at how 'OK' I could convince myself I was feeling until I saw my labs. It was like a mirror that allowed me to really get in touch with just how not-OK I actually felt underneath. I've been holding on for most of my life, trying to figure out how to heal myself and bumping into walls left, right and centre. The labs (and this community and Dr K in particular) didn't take away my autonomy. Instead, I feel it's brought me back to life! And I feel, for the first time, that I have a real shot at being healthy, integrated and really engaged in all that is around me. I wish you the best and know you'll figure out how to make this work for you.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-16T18:26:32+0300

> **Jack Kruse;38124 wrote:** Why you feel you do is buried in BG 4 and 5. When you understand how to unlock the humanness of your brain out.......you will get the meaning. Its there.......and you have buried it with epigenetic decisions. You need to soak in cold water and you need to become a Barcuccuda in British Columbia........that is the Epi paleo Rx for what ails you.

Hi Dr Kruse,

Sorry, for taking so much time to respond to the your comment. I needed to do some research on species Barcuccuda because it is a southern fish and not found normally in the northern Pacific Ocean. (There is one Barcuccuda species, the California Barcuccuda, that is native in the Puget Sound area.) I have to assume that such a strange comment means you are talking metaphorically. In many Native traditions they use "animal spirits" or "animal protectors" to help make important life transitions.

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barracuda](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barracuda)

My area does have visits from Barcuccuda during El Nino years when the waters of the Pacific Ocean heats up. Unfortunately, these are not good years for the fishermen or marine animals because the Barcuccuda are ravenous and eat all the salmon fry. I don't think I would want to be a BC Barcuccuda or take that fish on as a "totem animal". I don't want to destroy my environment and head south in the wake of the destruction:

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/El\_Ni%C3%B1o-Southern\_Oscillation](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/El_Ni%C3%B1o-Southern_Oscillation)

Honestly, I don't know what to make of your comment. Maybe you need to be a bit less cryptic if you actually want to communicate with me.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-17T18:05:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-38728>

**Day 33 (July 17, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.7C (Day 18 of cycle)

**ZRT Saliva Testing: Four samples to be taken today.

2c organic matcha

I have stopped the coffee. I hope I will not have too many withdrawal symptoms.

**Tub/10kg Ice Block

15 minutes at tub temperature: 19.4-18.3C

Temperature before tub: 37.1C

The water felt icy today.

Temperature after tub: 36.6C

**Breakfast

Not hungry yet. Ate at about 10:30am and had leftover pork rib roast, squash and onions. I did not like those onions roasted. Tasted nasty. I also had two fig bar and a glass of raw milk with some fermented cod liver oil and butter oil. (I was feeling a bit down the last few days.)

**About Book Yesterday and Problems with Testing

I did some good thinking last night while I slept - I do my best thinking while sleeping - and realized what my problem is. The problem with **What Your Doctor May Not Tell You about Premenopause** is not the book itself. It is clearly written and engaging. What troubles me is how "wrong headed" the direction suggested might be**. All through the book they state that xenoestrogens caused by our modern lifestyles are causing hormone imbalances in animals and humans. Then they state we should take bio-identical progesterone and other hormones to balance for these xenohormones in the environment. On one level it makes perfect rational sense. On the other level it is crazy. Let me explain. If I put put bio-identical hormones on my body, at least some of the hormone will wash off my body or be excreted in my urine that will make its way into the water systems of the great environment. In the end I will worsen the xenohormone problem in the greater environment. Yes, maybe I save myself and reduce my health problems but at what costs to the future and to other people? I am having a ethical quandary. Do I make an action the might** help me in the short and long-term but at the cost of adding these hormones into the environment and potentially worsening the situation for future generations? What price am I willing to let others pay, so I can be "optimal"? Will future generations curse me for my selfishness?

Exercise/Play**

Had a nice slow 1.5 hour walk in the park with Shaen. We picked up our books at the library and braved the mid-day valley heat. My calves are sore after that climb on Sunday.

Dinner**

I had some more sweet potato fries and homemade ketchup. I had some cider vinegar on top. Wonderful meal. I just read this really interesting post about consumption and knowing what is "enough". I loved it when she says: "I’m a capitalist who is anti-consumerism".

[http://grassfedcooking.com/2012/sidestepping-the-upsell/](http://http://grassfedcooking.com/2012/sidestepping-the-upsell/)

Tub/10kg Ice Block**

15 minutes at tub temperature: 19.4-18.3C

Temperature before tub: 37.4C

The water felt icy. I can't seem to see an pattern in why the tub will feel icy one day and tolerable the next even though the temperature isn't changing.

Temperature after tub: 36.6C

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-18T01:31:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-38866>

> **Caroline Cooper;38906 wrote: **Day 32 (July 17, 2012)
>
> Waking Temperature: 36.7C (Day 18 of cycle)
>
> **ZRT Saliva Testing: Four samples today
>
> 2c organic matcha
>
> I have stopped the coffee. I hope I will not have too many withdrawal symptoms.

>
> 15 minutes at tub temperature: 19.4-18.3C
>
> Temperature before tub: 37.1C
>
> The water felt icy today.
>
> Temperature after tub: 36.6C
>

> **Breakfast**
>
> Not hungry yet.
>
>
>
> About Book Yesterday and Problems with Testing**
>
> I did some good thinking last night while I slept - I do my best thinking while sleeping - and realized what my problem is. The problem with What Your Doctor May Not Tell You about Premenopause** is not the book itself. It is clearly written and engaging. What troubles me is how "wrong headed" the direction suggested might be**. All through the book they state that xenoestrogens caused by our modern lifestyles are causing hormone imbalances in animals and humans. Then they state we should take bio-identical progesterone and other hormones to balance for these xenohormones in the environment. On one level it makes perfect rational sense. On the other level it is crazy. Let me explain. If I put put bio-identical hormones on my body, at least some of the hormone will wash off my body or be excreted in my urine that will make its way into the water systems of the great environment. In the end I will worsen the xenohormone problem in the greater environment. Yes, maybe I save myself and reduce my health problems but at what costs to the future and to other people? I am having a ethical quandary. Do I make an action the might** help me in the short and long-term but at the cost of adding these hormones into the environment and potentially worsening the situation for future generations? What price am I willing to let others pay, so I can be "optimal"? Will future generations cure me for my selfishness?

I think that this is why many who advocate for BHRT advocate for creams. It goes directly to your hormone levels (not through the liver) and therefore right to your system. My sense from reading these docs is that this is a very different process than other ways of supplementation and also VERY different to synthetic hormones. If you are testing and getting your hormones balanced in a bio-identical way that means you are excreting nothing that would be different than if you were a 22 year old with perfectly balanced hormones without intervention. In other words, get the dosing, form, and application right, and your body uses them all. This is a very different scenario than our drinking waters being polluted by BPAs and birth control pills. (Like Janagram, I am not 100% sure of any of this, but I am pretty certain the comparison you are making is apples and oranges (or should I say, epi keto paleo and safe starches?)

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-18T03:54:39+0300
