Hi KiwiLauren,

I hear what you are saying. In the book **What Your Doctor May Not Tell You about Premenopause**, they repeat over and over again about the minute amounts that make endocrine changes not only in humans but other animals. In the book they talk about alligators with dis-functional reproductive systems. It makes me wonder.

I don't know if you have ever seen this poster before. It is called Fish Pharm** and shows what drugs have been found in fish near Chicago:

[http://visual.ly/fish-pharm](http://http://visual.ly/fish-pharm)

I am sorry. I don't have any easy answers. I just have lots of questions.

Hi janagram,

Sometimes I wish I could just stop asking troublesome questions. I haven't had an easy life because of my constant questioning. It can drive people mad!

A friend of mind here in Kamloops recommended a movie. Maybe you haven't seen it. It is called I AM**. The movie asks important questions like: "What is wrong with the world?" and "How could we make the world better?"

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qpVEH-Bpdes\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;qpVEH-Bpdes\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qpVEH-Bpdes\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;qpVEH-Bpdes)

I would like my actions to make the world a better place. It is just so hard to know what action really is right. Here is a Sioux saying: _"The longest journey you’ll ever make is from your head to your heart."_ I hope that some day I will complete that journey. My mind will be full of acceptance and peace. My heart will be full of love.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-18T15:37:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-38984>

**Day 34 (July 18, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.6C (Day 19 of cycle) (Woke very early 4:00am.)

I woke this morning to bed-spins. It was like I had been drinking all night, something I haven't done in decades. I also was really hungry and dizzy. I guess two dinners of sweet potatoes did not agree with me. It could be simply too many carbohydrates in one meal pushed me into a hypoglycemic state. Or it could be something else. Just a bit of background information. **Sweet potatoes are not allowed on the SCD and GAPS because it is thought that people like myself cannot digestion complex starches very well. These undigested starches go into the large intestine and become food for the bacteria in my gut. Depending on the type of bacteria in the gut different "symptoms" will occur in the people. The bacteria do what bacteria do and consume and ferment the undigested starches. The biological end products of the fermentation are reabsorbed by the large intestine and come into my body causing all kinds of mischief. This process has been called autointoxication. In my case it can be headaches, numb hands, bed-spins, allergies, asthma, sore joints, seizures(?), etc for other people I can be much more serious like sending them into a psychotic state or making them autistic. It depends completely on the person's strains of gut flora. For more information:

[http://www.breakingtheviciouscycle.info/](http://http://www.breakingtheviciouscycle.info/)

Nevertheless, I got up and made myself a very early morning breakfast. I almost feel okay now. After I ate I had diarrhea. In a way I am grateful for my symptoms. It is easy for me to see a food does not agree with me. I thought sweet potatoes was one of my very few "safe starches". Unfortunately, I was wrong or I just went over some magical tolerance amount. Gee, I'm wondering about those saliva tests. Will those numbers be a good baseline or will my cortisol and other hormones be completely out of sorts?

**Breakfast

I ate four buttered scrambled eggs and a garden salad with dressing. I had 2c of organic matcha.

**New Post at eatkamloops.org

Please read if you are interesting in how I home process milk and eggs:

[http://eatkamloops.org/morning-chores-home-processing-of-milk-and-eggs/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/morning-chores-home-processing-of-milk-and-eggs/)

Chores/Play**

It is raspberry picking season and I had planned on starting picking today. Unfortunately, I couldn't bend over without almost falling over. I had a morning nap, and a afternoon nap, and I was able to pick some raspberries for freezing. I try to freeze at least 100 pounds a year. This supply has to last my family until next season.

Saliva Test**

I brought my saliva test into Glover's Pharmacy** here in Kamloops. Considering my dizzy state I had my husband drive. One interesting thing happened at Glover's. The pharmacist asked for my BC Care Card but I didn't have it on me since I now** rarely access the formal medical system. It didn't matter because here in Canada all our records are online. He accessed my last script back in early 2008 for a topical antibiotic nasal spray. It was for a sinus infection. That was the last time I saw a doctor. Before I even went to the doctor I had consulted with the pharmacist because I was completely unwilling to take another course of oral antibiotics. Oral antibiotics would be the normal treatment the doctor would give me for a sinus infection. In the end I never used the nasal spray and stopped the infection with serrapeptase. I have made a promise to my gut flora that I will never take an oral antibiotic unless my life is at stake. Even then I have instructed my husband that it must be by IV.

**Lunch

I was hungry again at noon. That is strange for me. I ate leftover lamb shoulder roast, squash and garden salad with homemade dressing. I make some ice cream with homemade vanilla extract. Sonja really likes the ice cream when I open a vanilla pod and squeeze out the seeds and liquid from inside the pod.

**Tub**

15 minutes at tub temperature: 19.4C

Temperature before tub: 37.1C

I decided not to do the CT this morning. I really wasn't sure if my system could take it. After two naps I felt like I could give it a go but I decided to be kind to myself and skip the ice.

Temperature after tub: 37.0C

Dinner**

I decided to have dinner tonight. I had a small amount of roasted lamb, zucchini, and garden salad with homemade dressing. I had 1/2 of water kefir with fermented cod liver oil, butter oil and some Custom Probiotic six strain. I hope that will settle my digestion.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-18T16:07:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-38986>

> **ATL\_Paleo;38647 wrote:** I believe Dr K was saying you need to eat lots of fish ... the Epi paleo Rx.

Hi ATL\_Paleo,

I see you are a newcomer. Welcome to the forum!

Yes, I am aware that he likely meant I should eat a lot of fish. If Dr Kruse did mean just that, why not just say so? I did find the comment cryptic and confusing. I am on this forum because I am looking for answers from people more experienced with CT then I have. When we communication in person we can get away with much more complex communication because we can see the others body language and expressions. Communication in a written form on the internet is a difficult exercise. Miscommunication happens all the time. Just read my words with a kind and compassionate voice or an anger voice. My written words will sound different.

My intention when I write is to speak in a kind and compassionate way but the reader's mood can effect how we interpret a given passage and the intention of the writer.

Please come back for another visit. I hope you can share how CT is working for you.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-07-18T17:14:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-39001>

Hi Caroline,

I just wanted to let you know that I am in Canada too. I used Rocky Mountain Labs when I finally decided to get some testing done. I did the hormone panel with the morning cortisol. I went through a local compounding pharmacy here and the pharmacist was very helpful. It was very enlightening to see just how low my hormones are. It explains a lot! The pharmacist who often recommends BHRT thought I would benefit more from supplements. Maybe because he has a difficult time getting some our local doctors to actually write the scripts. I don’t know. Anyway, I was more than happy to give the supplements a try but I’ve only been taking them for a few weeks. He said I should notice an improvement within the first month and more to come in the future months. It’s hard to tell but I feel like the brain fog is becoming less dense and my mood has been fairly stable. This I like!!!

I’m glad you did the testing and hopefully you will feel more empowered with the results. Good luck. I’ve been reading your journal ever since I read your coffee recipe!

Did you ever have your consult with Dr. Kruse? I look forward to booking one with him. Hopefully sooner than later?

**

Hi paulalynn,

From your picture you look too young to have low hormone levels. Maybe you are older than you look. Through my work with the Weston A Price Foundation** I had also seen many very young women with hormonal and fertility problems. Are you having those kind of problems?

I have found pharmacist to be very helpful and they know far more about drugs than any doctor I have met. Have you looked at the a4m.com or lef.org? The can sometimes be of help finding doctors that can do bio-identical hormone stuff. I think trying the herbal supplements first is a very good idea. What kind of supplements are you taking for what hoped outcome?

[http://www.a4m.com/directory.html](http://http://www.a4m.com/directory.html)

[http://www.lef.org/Health-Wellness/InnovativeDoctors/](http://http://www.lef.org/Health-Wellness/InnovativeDoctors/)

I just know hormone regulation is very complex. Manually messing with someone's endocrine system is a difficult job. Our bodies are wonderful self-regulating systems and they do the best they can with the materials available. I wonder if I or someone I hire could do a better job then my body's internal wisdom. Also, I have worked so hard over the years to get off all medication to change my mind and just take something seems somehow going... backwards. As you can see I am not confident that it would be a good decision for me. I have a good idea of what the tests may show, I just don't know if I have the internal conviction to avoid taking the offered "solution".

No, the consult with Dr Kruse did not happen. I was there at the scheduled time but he wasn't. I haven't rescheduled yet and will likely just wait for the saliva test results before booking. I want to do my own processing of the test results before looking to others for answers. I guess, this is my Independence Day!

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-19T17:43:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-39352>

**Day 35 (July 19, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.8C (Day 20 of cycle)

2c organic matcha

**Tub

15 minutes at tub temperature: 19.4C

Temperature before tub: 37.2C

I skipped the ice again this morning. Being kind to myself. I know from experience I can have up to 72 hours of discomfort after having a food reactions.

Temperature after tub: 36.5C

**Breakfast

I ate four buttered scrambled eggs and a garden salad with dressing. My stomach is still a bit upset and I have gas but I am through the worst of it.

**Work/Play

I did about 1.5 hours of walking in the local neighborhood doing fliers. I spent the rest of the day working on #$@&\*%! marketing copy. I just don't have my heart into writing copy to make people buy stuff they don't need. I don't want to _"create needs". Nor do I want to be like some marketing shill who recently stated: _"that I just needed to get greedy and take the business away from my competitors."_ I don't want to advertise because I don't want to add to the toxic visual waste we all have to look at everyday. I just want to offer a good product and help my community make better decisions. I don't know why that isn't enough. I loved it when Shannon Hayes_** _of Sap Bush Hollow Farm_** _says: "I’m a capitalist who is anti-consumerism"._

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shill](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shill)

Dinner**

I had leftover lamb shoulder roast and garden salad with homemade dressing. I had 1/2c of water kefir with fermented cod liver oil, butter oil and probiotics. (I take those three when I am not doing well.) I use the unflavored FCLO and BO and use the powder form of the probiotic to avoid additives in supplements that tend to give me trouble:

[http://www.greenpasture.org/public/Products/CodLiverOil/index.cfm](http://http://www.greenpasture.org/public/Products/CodLiverOil/index.cfm)

[http://www.greenpasture.org/public/Products/ButterOil/index.cfm](http://http://www.greenpasture.org/public/Products/ButterOil/index.cfm)

[http://www.customprobiotics.com/custom-probiotics-six-cultures.htm](http://http://www.customprobiotics.com/custom-probiotics-six-cultures.htm)

Suntanning/Tub/10kg Ice**

15 minutes at tub temperature: 21.1C-19.4C

Temperature before tub: 37.6C

I suntanned for about an hour and after seeing my temperature I thought I could handle the ice.

Temperature after tub: 37.1C

HI Caroline,

I am 42 years old. I suspect my hormones have been low for a number of years. Of course I didn’t know until I came across Dr. Kruse that hormones could have such a profound effect. Then I read “What Your Doctor May Not Tell You About Menopause”. Then I got tested. I think it explains a lot for me. I may have other issues as well but it’s the brain fog, water retention & weight gain with the inability to loss any significant amount of weight that finally got me thinking about testing my hormones.

These are the supplements that the pharmacist recommended I take:

Douglas Labs Progesto-Mend 90 caps is a combination of phytoprogesterones, progestogenic adaptogens and other herbs that support optimal progesterone function by maintaining the health of progesterone producing glands and by supporting the healthy function of progesterone responsive tissues. Progesto-Mend may be a useful dietary supplement for individuals wishing to support healthy progesterone production and function.

Progestomend Herbal 2,100 mg (Proprietary Blend):

Rehmannia (Root), Bupleurum (root), Passion flower (aerial parts), Peony (root), Coleus forskiohlii (root, standardized to 10% forskohlii), Dioscorea vilosa (root) and Chasteberry extract(fruit).

Douglas Labs Adreno-Mend 120c is a synergistic combination of ten highly valued herbal extracts with adaptogenic properties designed to support healthy, balanced adrenal gland function. These plant adaptogens include Schisandra chinensis, Bacopa monnieri, Rhodiola rosea, Eleutherococcus senticosus, Magnolia officinalis, Rhemannia glutinosa, Bupleurum falcatum, Panax ginseng, Coleus forskohlii and Withania sominfera.

I plant to continue on these for a few months & see how I feel. Keep us posted on you!

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-19T18:45:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-39380>

> **Caroline Cooper;39450 wrote:**
>
>
>
> I just know hormone regulation is very complex. Manually messing with someone's endocrine system is a difficult job. Our bodies are wonderful self-regulating systems and they do the best they can with the materials available. I wonder if I or someone I hire could do a better job then my body's internal wisdom. Also, I have worked so hard over the years to get off all medication to change my mind and just take something seems somehow going... backwards. As you can see I am not confident that it would be a good decision for me. I have a good idea of what the tests may show, I just don't know if I have the internal conviction to avoid taking the offered "solution".

I have some of those feelings about BHRT. I'm choosing to do as much as I can via epi-paleo and I don't mind doing things outside the box for that matter. I realize that in the end that might not be enough due to my previous life and that will be okay. I will cross that bridge once I get there (well, I'm already taking DHEA and preg).

There's another way to think of it in terms of providing the raw material that your body is too broken to make. You're not trying to regulate the hormone balance.... you're just providing substrates. B/c hormones are needed for, like, everything, in your body to function correctly.

I do not agree with you on your concerns about polluting the environment via BHRT. I think humanity needs it desperately and that is the wrong end to approach the problem. Stop the birth control pills, the plastics, all the other xeno-estrogens.... and what we do to fix our bodies will help humanity, not hurt.

**

Hi Souldanzer,

I hear what you are saying. I completely agree with you that we need to clean up our environment but most of that is completely out of my personal control. In our household, I try to never have plastic come in contact with my family's food. We have gone completely organic but I wish I had gone organic years ago. I am concerned about my daughter's being born and living in this world but again that is completely out of my control. I have tried everything I know to protect their growing bodies.

I think we all need to make a personal decision if we are comfortable with bio-identical hormone replacement, or not. More information might make me more comfortable. I just want you to know I am very grateful these treatment are available to us. It is a personal choice. I would never want to stop anyone from having the choice to use these hormone tools.

You know the old saying: _"As long as you're breathing, there is more right with you than wrong with you."_ When I think of my body I try to see it as a "whole and healthy". I don't like the image of "being broken". I'm not broken. My body has the most amazing ability to heal itself. I have seen it. All I have to do is learn how to listen to its non-verbal communication. I have to be patient. The journey to health is not always straight. I am actually glad I got sick. If I hadn't I would have never changed my family's diet. I just can't image the state my youngest daughter would be in right now if I hadn't got sick first.

We feed our bodies in many ways. Ancient societies knew our thoughts are what bring in our reality. Dr Kruse would say it's epigenetics! Maybe both are right. We need to be very careful the thoughts we "feed" our bodies. Having gratitude and saying thanks for all the work our bodies do for us everyday is a good exercise. When was the last time you said a big thank you to your gut biota for all its good works?

I would like to share with you a book I just found a few months ago. It has had a profound effect on myself and to my great surprise my husband. The book has been like a salve for what ails him. I hope you will enjoy this book as much as I have.

A Guide to the Good Life: The Ancient Art of Stoic Joy** by William Braxton Irvine

### janagram — 2012-07-20T19:01:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-39690>

wow. Book looks very interesting.

Thank you, I read a review of it...looks good.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-20T19:41:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-39700>

**Day 36 (July 20, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.4C (Day 21 of cycle)

2c organic matcha

**By the way, that is a low morning temperature for the luteal phase of anyone's cycle. I had this strange thought. Why not use caffeine if I have a morning temperature of 36.4C or less during the luteal phase as a little hormonal boot-in-the-pants? Of course, I didn't really "trust" this thought. Maybe it is coming out of my caffeine addictive brain wanting its fix, not out of some wise internal messaging system. Well, guess who won out!

2c coffee, raw cream and 2tsp butter

**Breakfast

I was hungry this morning and ate buttered scrambled eggs and garden salad with homemade dressing.

**Tub/10kg Ice

15 minutes at tub temperature: 19.4C-18.3C

Temperature before tub: 37.2C

Wow. I am roaring after those two cups of coffee. That is one powerful drug. Remember how I am trying to see some pattern with the feeling of "cold" in the tub and actually being cold. Well, I do have something to report. I am finding to my great surprise I find the tubs with the ice easier than the water at room temperature. Actually, I am starting to "like" the ice tub routine. Maybe that's just my drugged brain taking to you. **Just because I can doesn't mean I should.** I feel GREAT using coffee in the short-term but should I drink coffee in the long-term? Could I actually only use coffee as medicine or will it just progress into addiction?

Temperature after tub: 36.5C

Dinner**

I got so focused on my work today I looked up and it was after 5:00pm. I am glad Shaen got dinner ready because I completely forgot about it. He made a pork roast, baked potatoes and squash. I just ate the some pork roast and squash with butter.

Tub/10kg Ice**

15 minutes at tub temperature: 18.3-16.7C

Temperature before tub: 37.0C

I found the tub cold and was just on the point of shivering.

Temperature after tub: 36.7C

Holiday**

We are going to have a holiday on Vancouver Island. I will start packing tonight. We will leave after milking on Sunday. Our milking cow Olivia is pregnant so the bull is going to the butcher's on Saturday night. I'm sure our relief milkers will be happy to see him gone. For such a little guy, he's mean, but I am sure he will be delicious.

Beautiful Conversation**

I did some packing for the trip to Vancouver Island. Part of the trip to VI is dropping off Sonja at Grandma Sarah's place. They are going to England for three weeks. I helped Sonja get ready for her first intercontinental trip. She had a list and everything was organized. She has done research on the internet about the maximum size of carry-on luggage and the new "safety" restrictions. She knew more then I did. As she packed, I gave her passport, English pounds, letter of permission, and travel insurance papers. I warned her of the importance of these papers. It was at this point I saw the building of her anxiety. She is only twelve. After I turned off the house breakers that night, we had a long conversation. I stroked her hair as she lay her head on my lap. We talked through the setting sun and into the dark. By the end of the conversation I could not see her features well. It was a lovely conversation. One of the best of my year. I wondered if this was what life used to be like when people had relationships not distracted by modern life which can take us away from the really important moments. I'm sure I wouldn't have had that conversation if the lights were on and the computer singing its siren's song.

### janagram — 2012-07-20T20:59:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-39735>

um, its a drug...I am addicted also at this time...need to cut back then move to tea.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-20T22:26:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-39779>

> Could I actually only use coffee as medicine or will it just progress into addiction?

If you are using coffee to wake up/function/elevate your BT then you are not using it as medicine. If you need coffee that badly, you need to stop drinking it. This is a huge sign that your cortisol/thyroid is off.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-21T01:34:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-39845>

> **Souldanzer;39966 wrote: If you are using coffee to wake up/function/elevate your BT then you are not using it as medicine. If you need coffee that badly, you need to stop drinking it. This is a huge sign that your cortisol/thyroid is off.**

**

Hi Souldanzer,

I quit coffee over a year ago. It was really hard to quit but I did. I restarted the coffee for this experiment. Dr Kruse says yes to coffee for people with a healthy gut. If someone has a leaky gut Dr Kruse says coffee is out. I guess I see myself with a somewhat healthy gut but after the sweet potato situation I am not so sure.

I agree with you. I am likely better off without the caffeine. That is the recommendation of the Weston A Price Foundation**. No coffee. No chocolate. The WAPF thinks heavy caffeine use is the major reason why women in North America have such a rough time with menopause. During menopause the ovaries give up production of female sexual hormones and the adrenal taken over. Many women have adrenal exhaustion so the adrenals in these women have nothing to give. The result is bad menopause with lots of problems.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-21T01:41:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-39847>

a leaky gut is a reason not to drink coffee. bad cortisol is another. If you "need" coffee, you need to stop it and probably all other caffeine with it (that includes green tea). If you don't care if you have it or not, then you can drink it. Doesn't make sense at first look, I know. But if you think about it, it does. I know it's hard to quit. Done myself several times.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-21T01:44:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-39850>

> **janagram;39922 wrote:** um, its a drug...I am addicted also at this time...need to cut back then move to tea.

Yes, caffeine is a powerful drug. But the state loves what it does for its workers! Makes us all energetic and ready to buy, buy, buy! Sorry, I'm still high as a kit. I just got a project done in half the time. Wow. Too bad caffeine burns most of us out and makes us irritable.

If your really a druggy like me you could try getting off coffee by replacing it with matcha. Here is where I get my matcha:

If I had been "sad" with the higher temperature I would be thinking addiction. (If I decide to use the temperatures of 36.4C during my luteal phase to "treat" with caffeine, what temperature during the follicular phase?)

**Breakfast

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-28T02:09:20+0300

**Day 43 (July 27, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.3C (Day 4 of cycle)

3c matcha

It was good to be home. I was glad the house wasn't too hot. I did wake with numb hands this morning. Two days of potatoes will do that to me.

**Tub/Cold Water Added

15 minutes at tub temperature: 18.3-16.7C

Temperature before tub: 36.7C

I got my tub filled last night and added some epsom salts. I added cold water to the tub to get it up to the right level.

Temperature after tub: 36.2C

Breakfast**

I had pork hash with onions and zucchini. I had some leftover clams and garden salad with homemade dressing.

Dinner**

I had pork hash with onions and lard. I had some garden salad and homemade dressing. I was just about to get into the tub when my husband needed a talk. It was a three glass of wine talk so more important than a cold bath. What do you do when you have been taught a pack of lies? How do you transform your life into something that is sane and righteous? I don't mean righteous in a religious sort of way, thought that could be a starting point, but a "rightwise" way of life: a just, ethical or right way to live.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-28T19:01:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-41680>

**Day 44 (July 28, 2012)(132lbs.)

Waking Temperature: 36.3C (Day 5 of cycle)

3c matcha

**Breakfast

I had three raw oysters for an appetizer. I had pork hash with onions with garden salad and homemade dressing.

**Tub/Ice

15 minutes at tub temperature: 19.4-18.3C

Temperature before tub: 36.5C

It is funny getting into a cold tub with an ice block floating around in it, hoping that this strange action will help my endocrine system. Regardless of if my actions work for the original purpose, sitting in cold water is a good Stoic exercise. I can think about all the different sensations of cold and warm and how I feel about discomfort. I can ponder the emotions that make "being cold" more unpleasant or the reverse how to make an unpleasant sensation more pleasant by using the powers found in my mind. I can think about my hopes and striving for endocrine health and how acceptance could bring the happiness I'm really looking for.

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZCojflXcjGo\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;ZCojflXcjGo\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZCojflXcjGo\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;ZCojflXcjGo)

Temperature after tub: 36.3C

**Epi-Paleo Rx Post and Webinar

I have just finished listening to the webinar and read the post and comments. Presently, I am reading The Art and Science of Low Carbohydrate Living** by Jeff Volek. In the book he recommends people on ketogenic diets consume five grams of salt a day: three in food and two in broth. All of these sources got me thinking about broths.

Martin Luther** said: _"Why should a man die when sage grows in his garden?" Of course, he died in the 1540s. Nevertheless, food can be medicine. Special broths can have a healing effect when drank on a regular basis. Here is just one healing broth that will be especially good for the Epi-Paleo Rx_**_. You can choose any mushrooms or seaweed that you fancy and think might help your condition. Even if you hate seaweed you will like it in this mixture. You add the powder to bone broth, seafood broth or water. (I have this powder in my emergency food kit.) I prefer to get the whole mushrooms and seaweed not the powders. I know this adds extra work but I feel better doing the processing myself.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/41905=368-making-broth.jpg)

3 parts dried mushrooms

3 parts dried organic sage

1 part sea salt

1 part seaweed

My choice of mushroom is Shiitake and Maitake. My choice of seaweed is Bladderwrack.

[http://www.mountainroseherbs.com/learn/\*\*\*\*ake.php](http://http://www.mountainroseherbs.com/learn/****ake.php)

[http://www.mountainroseherbs.com/learn/maitake.php](http://http://www.mountainroseherbs.com/learn/maitake.php)

[http://www.mountainroseherbs.com/learn/bladderwrack.php](http://http://www.mountainroseherbs.com/learn/bladderwrack.php)

This is what Dr Kruse thinks of Bladderwrack. You will see why I picked it after reading the description:

"Bladderwrack is loaded with vitamin K and it is an excellent adrenal adaptogen and it helps sensitize us to insulin to because of its K2 like effects. It is often used today by Native American cultures broths and in sauna/steam baths for degenerative arthritis and inflammatory joint conditions."_

Dinner**

I had a cup of broth as a snack. For dinner I had four raw oysters to start. I had some pork and onion hash cooked in lard. I had garden salad and homemade dressing on the side. That's Dulse on my hash. My daughter Erika brought me one perfectly beautiful strawberry from the garden. The flavor was so rich and intense. While I made ice cream for dessert, Erika picked some fresh raspberries from the garden.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/41905=369-macs-oysters.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/41905=370-salad-hash-dulse.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/41905=371-seasonal-raspberries-ice-cream.jpg)

**Tub/Ice**

15 minutes at tub temperature: 18.3-16.7C

Temperature before tub: 37.1C

Temperature after tub: 36.6C

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-29T19:39:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-41816>

**Day 45 (July 29, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.2C (Day 6 of cycle)

2c matcha

**Breakfast

I had six raw oysters before getting in the tub. The webinar reminded me why it is important to try to eat within 0.5 hours of waking to help balance circadian cycles with hormone cycles. I don't find this easy because I am not hungry upon just waking. After the tub I had sockeye salmon steak with zucchini and cheese. I put some dulse on top. I had garden salad with homemade dressing. The white flowers in my salad are cilantro which is in bloom right now.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/42049=372-sockeye-dulse-zic.jpg)

**Tub/Ice

15 minutes at tub temperature: 19.4-18.3C

Temperature before tub: 36.5C

I was pondering the reason why hot baths are considered pleasant and cold baths are considered harsh or unpleasant. What makes a hot bath pleasant and a cold bath unpleasant? I tried to be very present for the cold, the cold does that anyway, trying to see if I could use my mind to transform the cold into a pleasant experience. OR even answer the question why hot is pleasant and cold is not.

Temperature after tub: 36.3C

Snack**

I had a snack of mushroom seaweed broth. I had an idea to add some raw oyster nectar to the broth. I have away found oyster nectar repelling for some reason and will actually gag if I try to drink it like my husband does. The raw nectar was delicious in the broth and I drank it up without any issues.

Suntanning/Lake CT**

Three times at 15 minutes at lake temperature: -+18.3C

Went to the lake today. It is a beautiful day without a cloud in the sky. Erika taught be how to dance in the water. It's wonderful watching how children can take the cold so much better than adults. How much is physical and how much is mental?

Dinner**

I had leftover sockeye salmon steaks with zucchini and cheese. I sprinkled some dulse on top. I had a garden salad with homemade dressing. I have made some coconut candy and will likely have a few pieces tonight for dessert.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-08-02T00:00:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-42354>

> **paulalynn;42582 wrote:** Your ice cream looks delicious! I'm afraid to ask for your recipe. I used to binge on ice cream (store bought ice cream). You are doing great with getting so much seafood in your diet. I must start doing the same!

Hi paulalynn,

I wouldn't want to encourage destructive behavior but the way I look at homemade ice cream, assuming one is lactose tolerant, it's the best dessert in the world. True ice cream, made from raw cream and raw pastured egg yolks, would be hard to overeat after your body was full of the good fats found in these foods. Here is the recipe:

[http://eatkamloops.org/cream-cream-and-more-ice-cream-recipes/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/cream-cream-and-more-ice-cream-recipes/)

I think food processors put addictive additives in industrial "ice cream" to make people eat it compulsively.

My girls ate 2L of the ice cream each day for about 60 days before they filled up on the fats. After that it wasn't like a hoard of locust when I made ice cream. With Olivia, I had to change the recipe because she just doesn't have the same amount of butterfat the Patty did. Now, I just add extra raw egg yolks to get the fat intake up. I have added up to six egg yolks to this recipe. The ice will have this incredible yellow color:

[http://eatkamloops.org/walnut-maple-ice/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/walnut-maple-ice/)

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-08-02T00:09:50+0300

> **Souldanzer;42535 wrote:** That happened with a batch of shrimp I bought the other day!!! Took me a while to realize it was roe! Every single shrimp had a load of roe at the tail end!! I figured it was a gift from the universe.... to my hormone panel

Hi Souldanzer,

Yeah, I felt really happy when I went back to Port Alberni and found the fisherman still had those prawns loaded with roe. Funny though. The roe might make it difficult to sell because some people see the roe as yucky. I had to encourage my daughter to eat the roe last night. I told her the roe was the best part and I would take away her prawns if she didn't eat them! She gave me "the stink-eye" and sucked up the roe. She liked it after trying it. But the idea of the roe grossed her out.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-08-02T16:42:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-42494>

**Day 49 (August 2, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.2C (Day 10 of cycle)

3c coffee, raw milk and 3tsp butter

**Update on Bio-identical Hormone Replacement Research

I have been doing a far bit of reading the last few weeks on this issue. I found the way that our drugs and hormones wont get into the greater environment. Unfortunately, it is not standard practice at this point. We would have to take our city's sewage and long compost (12-18 months) the material. Here in Kamloops we do short composting (2-6 months) of sewage. Long composting activates fungus which degrade the problem chemicals and hormones. Short composting doesn't complete this process. The Kamloops composting facility would have to be ten times the present size to accommodate long composting. I don't think that will be happening anytime soon. I don't know if this is completely correct but we are a long way from processing our sewage in a safe way. Each of us need to think about our waste streams to help correct this serious problem. **Don't throw your unused medication down the toilet!

I have been reading Dr John Lee's books **What Your Doctor May Not Tell You About Menopause and **What Your Doctor May Not Tell You About Premenopause. There is a lot of the same material in both books. It appears to be a reasonable approach to bio-identical HRT.

I am also rereading TS Wiley's books **Lights Out** and Sex, Lies and Menopause**. I read Lights Out many years ago because of reading Suzanne Somers book on hormone replacement. It was the first place I had heard about the issue of light and hormone health. I did find her bio-indentical HRT to be scary high doses. (But who the hell am I to even have an opinion?) I do think there was some good research in the book but I know TS Wiley has got into hot water because of some of her stated personal credentials and the actual hormone program itself. (Many people have had big problems on the program.) Here is 20/20 and Suzanne Somers author of Ageless**:

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2W9i6Oi5DvI\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;2W9i6Oi5DvI\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2W9i6Oi5DvI\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;2W9i6Oi5DvI)

So, what does all this mean to me? I haven't got my saliva test back yet though I already know I have what Dr Lee would call luteal phase failure**. I may also be having anovulatory cycles**; that is a bit less clear to me. So, what should I do? Coffee isn't a magic endocrine bullet even used in a pulse-like method after a low morning temperature. I could just wait and do CT for months without a positive result. In the short-term CT has not had a fast positive result. Actually, the experiment has made most of my numbers negative. (Yes, I am aware of the three steps back, two steps forward theory of health. Don't irritate me with it. I'm drinking coffee and I might not be responsible for my reaction. Be wary! If you do comment, please be nice. I am a sensitive person.)

I have decide to give the bio-identical protesterone a go. (I would have to start on day 10-12, which is today.) I'm not sure for how long. In my family of origin with five members, three have developed cancer, and one has died of cancer. Messing with my endocrine system is not something I do without concern. I don't care that people say its "safe" and "natural". What does that mean anyway?

By the way, the Life-Flow Progesta-Care** has a Proposition 65 cancer warning on the package. I called the company and got a number for more information that didn't work. I called back and asked which ingredient got the cancer warning. It was the bio-identical progesterone. The state of California does not recognize that bio-identical progesterone is different then progestin, thus the cancer warning on the label. The question that comes to mind is: who do I trust?

[http://www.oehha.ca.gov/prop65/p65faq.html](http://http://www.oehha.ca.gov/prop65/p65faq.html)

[http://google2.fda.gov/search?q=natural+progesterone&client=FDAgov&site=FDAgov&lr=&proxystylesheet=FDAgov&output=xml\_no\_dtd&getfields=\*](http://http://google2.fda.gov/search?q=natural+progesterone&client=FDAgov&site=FDAgov&lr=&proxystylesheet=FDAgov&output=xml_no_dtd&getfields=*)

**Tub/Ice

15 minutes at tub temperature: 18.3-16.7C

Temperature before tub: 36.8C

I had time to contemplate that new information. I have always said, I prefer a ugly truth to a beautiful fantasy, any day.

Temperature after tub: 36.3C

**Breakfast

I had an omelette with onions, mushrooms and cheese. I had a garden salad with homemade dressing and squash with butter.

**Snack**

I had some spicy beef jerky.

Dinner**

I had a lamb shoulder roast with squash and butter. I had garden salad and homemade dressing.

Tub/Ice**

15 minutes at tub temperature: 16.7-15-6C

Temperature before tub: 36.8C

I think this was the coldest tub temperature I have done. The house temperature is finally coming down at night and the ice is starting to get the water colder.

Temperature after tub: 36.3C

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-08-03T17:59:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-42686>

**Day 50 (August 3, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.1C (Day 11 of cycle)

3c coffee, raw cream and 3tsp butter

**Tub/Ice

15 minutes at tub temperature: 16.7-15-6C

Temperature before tub: 36.4C

Gee, that was cold today.

Temperature after tub: 35.9C

**Breakfast

I had the last of the oysters cooked in butter. I had prawns in butter and garden fresh Chinese greens in butter. I am collecting prawn shells in the freezer after each meal. I will be baking and drying some of the shells and grinding them into a powder. For others I will make a seafood broth.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/42965=387-oysters-prawns-garden-greens.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/42965=388-prawn-shells.jpg)

**Happy Vagina Formula

I have been working on a formula for a "happy vagina" after all that talk about sex lubes and vaginal dryness associated with menopause. I still think organic coconut oil is an excellent sex lube and most people have it in their kitchens.

I haven't work everything out yet but I will post it on [www.eatkamloops.org](http://http://www.eatkamloops.org) in the Healthy Household** section once I have worked out the problems. Here's the recipe for a "poor-woman's" menopausal formulation. If you're not poor you can buy the commercial formulation:

1/2c organic shea butter

1/4c organic shea nut oil

1/4c organic primrose oil

2T organic vitamin E oil (7500iu per oz) (optional)

Gently heat the shea butter in a 2c Pyrex glass in hot water. Let cool to at least body temperature. Add the other oils. Always store formulation in the fridge. (Primrose oil and vitamin E is very unstable at room temperature.) When ready to use take 1/2tsp from jar and roll into a small ball. Insert the small ball into upper vagina like a tampon. Remember to wipe any extra formulation on any area of your body that has dry skin. Each batch will make 96, 1/2tsp small balls and each small ball will give 78iu of vitamin E.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/42965=384-compound-happy-vagina.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/42965=386-melt-shea-butter.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/42965=385-homemade-happy-vagina.jpg)

Snack**

I had 3c matcha and some coconut candy made with Brazil nuts for my selenium hit. I don't really like marzipan but the coconut mixed with Brazil nuts tasted like that to me.

Tub/Ice**

15 minutes at tub temperature: 16.7-15-6C

Temperature before tub: 36.7C

I found I just had to stay still for the first five minutes. At the point I could face dunk but I started shivering.

Temperature after tub: 36.2C

Dinner**

We are having beef burgers on lettuce leaves.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-08-04T18:10:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-42840>

**Day 51 (August 4, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.1C (Day 12 of cycle)

2c coffee, raw cream and 1tsp butter

**Tub/Ice

15 minutes at tub temperature: 18.3-16.7C

Temperature before tub: 36.3C

The tub felt cold today. As I have said before getting in the tub at that body temperature is hard. I did not face dunk but used my mind to accept the cold and discomfort. A cold tub is a good Stoic's exercise. I had one of those unusual days where my body temperature went up in the tub, not down. I will ovulation today or tomorrow. While I was in the tub, I was thinking about the human condition and how women have to learn to ride the rhythms of the universe in a way men do not. It makes women "weaker" in some ways and "stronger" in others.

Temperature after tub: 36.6C

**Breakfast

I am not hungry yet. I find all that butter and cream in the coffee really makes eating breakfast hard. It would be really easy to do Intermittent fasting (IF) during these coffee days. The question that comes to mind is what would the coffee and IF do to my insulin levels? I would have to get a glucose meter to find out.

I had an omelette with zucchini, mushrooms, onions and cheese. I have buttered zucchini and garden salad on the side. I know many people wonder how to figure out how to manage carbohydrate load seasonally. Having a garden and eating out of it will teach the most committed urbanite about eating locally and eating seasonally.

Snack**

I had some Brazil Coconut Candy and 2c matcha.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/43119=392-brazil-coconut-candy.jpg)

Tub/Ice**

15 minutes at tub temperature: 18.3-16.7C

Temperature before tub: 36.7C

It was a cold tub again. I couldn't bring myself to face dunk. Just sat there and tried to be comfortable. I could hear the grasshoppers outside.

Temperature after tub: 36.8C

Dinner**

I had two small sockeye salmon steaks marinated in dill with zucchini cooked with lard. I picked another garden salad with dill, beet tops, and parsley. My lettuce are starting to bolt. I cut off the tops but that wont stop the natural course of things. I made some homemade ice cream. I am so full. A great meal.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-08-05T18:20:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-43070>

**Day 52 (August 5, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.4C (Day 13 of cycle)

I am on my two day rise towards my luteal phase of my cycle. (A healthy cycle would make a sharp one day rise.)

3c coffee, raw cream and 3tsp butter

**Tub/Ice

20 minutes at tub temperature: 22.2-19.4C

Temperature before tub: 36.9C

I changed my tub water yesterday. I wasn't careful about making sure the water going into the tub was as cold as possible so my tub this morning was warmer than normal. It felt so warm. Also, I am on my two day rise towards my luteal phase of my cycle which appears to make me more tolerant to the cold. I decided to give myself an extra five minutes. It was so "warm" that I grabbed the ice block and first tried to sit on it. My shoulders were out of the water so I took the block and placed it on my stomach area for as long as it was comfortable. The block of ice was about the size of my spread hand at the end of the bath. I just left the block in the tub to help the cooling process. I don't normally do this because I do not want to overwhelm my freezers with warm items because it will effect the quality of my frozen foods. Everything has a cost.

Temperature after tub: 37.0C

**Breakfast

I am not hungry yet. I ate a delicious Sunday breakfast of omelette with cheese, zucchini, onions and mushrooms. I had a garden salad of four types of lettuce, beet tops, dill and parsley with homemade dressing. I just love eating dill at this time of year fresh from the garden. We will be going to the lake today. I am sure I will be doing some lake CT and suntanning. We are bring the rocket stove and will cook paleo burgers on lettuce leaves for dinner. I can't wait!

**Suntanning/Lake CT**

Lake CT for three times for 15 minutes

What a beautiful day. I couldn't believe the number of people.

Lunch**

I had two paleo burgers with homemade mayo and ketchup on lettuce leaves. I had a small amount of milk. I had some Brazil coconut candy. Shaen cooked the burgers on the rocket stove in the middle of the park. Shaen was a bit nervous about the crowd and wondered if we would have any complaints. Actually, we made almost no smoke. A barbecue down the beach put out far more smoke and smell than our rocket stove. A neighbor came over to look at the stove and said it reminded him of being up past the Arctic Circle and seeing Norwegian torches used to land a plane. I think he was surprise I knew what a Norwegian torch was. If you are wondering what a Norwegian torch is, here is a video. They call it a Swedish Torch:

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vjQRLQTNsJo\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;vjQRLQTNsJo\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vjQRLQTNsJo\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;vjQRLQTNsJo)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/43354=396-busiest-day-of-year.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/43354=397-rocket-stove-lunch.jpg)

Tub/Ice**

Before tub temperature: 36.9C

15 minutes at tub temperature: 22.1-19.4C

The house was hot when we got home even though we had shut all the blinds on the west side of the house. The temperature was 29.4C so I wasn't that surprised my tub was warm again.

Temperature after tub: 37.1C

Snack**

I has some pumpkin seeds cooked in coconut oil and a very small glass of absinthe.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-05T18:31:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-43075>

Mine, too. I have a few lettuces that are shaded by some bigger kale I think those are going to make it a little longer. Oh well, time for zucchini and cucumbers and chard  I think I'll put some spring onions in their place I've been eating those like crazy. My radishes never really made it this year. Bolted before they could grow a radish. And my turnips are \*bitter\* yuck.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-08-05T19:24:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-43082>

> **Souldanzer;43359 wrote:** Mine, too. I have a few lettuces that are shaded by some bigger kale I think those are going to make it a little longer. Oh well, time for zucchini and cucumbers and chard  I think I'll put some spring onions in their place I've been eating those like crazy. My radishes never really made it this year. Bolted before they could grow a radish. And my turnips are \*bitter\* yuck.

You know I don't mind the bolt anymore. I have been trying to do more permaculture with my garden in the last few years. It's messy but a lot less work. I let the plants seed and watch what comes up next year. Even annuals and bi-annuals can work this way. We have a self-perpetuating potato patch and sweet beet area from last year. (You can eat the beet greens but the root was traditionally used as animal fodder. Cows love them. You wouldn't believe the number of seeds coming off those plants.) I am working on getting my cilantro and dill to come back without planting.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-05T21:13:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-43100>

I did the cilantro and dill.... unintended successful experiment. I just didn't clean out the bolted plants.... they came back. Dill is perennial me thinks in CO. I do eat beet greens... used to juice carrot tops but no more. Jerusalem artichokes are self-perpetuating if you like them.

Any ideas on what to do with bitter turnips....? I choked one down the other day, cooked, but it was no fun.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-08-06T04:32:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-43156>

> **Souldanzer;43384 wrote:** I did the cilantro and dill.... unintended successful experiment. I just didn't clean out the bolted plants.... they came back. Dill is perennial me thinks in CO. I do eat beet greens... used to juice carrot tops but no more. Jerusalem artichokes are self-perpetuating if you like them.
>
>
>
> Any ideas on what to do with bitter turnips....? I choked one down the other day, cooked, but it was no fun.

Yeah, I'm wondering about winter kill around here. Jerusalem artichokes grow well around here but I can't tolerate the starches. The potatoes in our garden are for other family members. I can't tolerate the starch in potatoes. I am still waking up with numb hands after eating those potato French fries almost two weeks ago.

If you have any livestock I would feed them the bitter turnips. You could try more salt and see if the bitterness goes away. If you like fermenting you could try that but you are likely wasting your time. Better to feed them to the animals or throw them in the compost. I am sure they will make wonderful soil in the end!

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-08-08T18:25:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-43676>

**Day 55 (August 8, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.5C (Day 16 of cycle)

2c coffee, raw cream and 2tsp butter

**Tub/Ice

15 minutes at tub temperature: 22.2-19.4C

Temperature before tub: 36.9C

It was nice having Shaen sit and talk with my while I was in the tub. I didn't seem to even feel the cold.

Temperature after tub: 37.2C

**Breakfast

I had an small piece of omelette with mushrooms , zucchini and cheese. I had salad and homemade dressing on the side. I was almost too stressed to eat.

**Suntanning/Lake CT**

15 minutes of lake CT

It was very windy today at the lake. Business intruded on my day off today. I had a very short period of time at the lake but it was wonderful nevertheless.

Dinner**

I had some tuna salad, raw cheese, lettuce and cold sockeye out of our lunch kit.

New post at eatkamloops.org for the Healthy Household series. It's about sex lubes and Happy Vagina Pearls. I hope you all like it:

[http://eatkamloops.org/heathy-household-sex-lubes-and-happy-vagina-pearls/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/heathy-household-sex-lubes-and-happy-vagina-pearls/)

Here's is the whole Healthy Household series if you are interested:

[http://eatkamloops.org/healthy-household-staying-clean-safely-and-saving-money/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/healthy-household-staying-clean-safely-and-saving-money/)

Snack**

I had a lamb chop and small glass of absinthe.

Saliva Test Results**

The test results were no surprise to me, just validation:

Estradiol: 3.1pg/ml

Progesterone: 49pg/ml

Ratio Pg/E2: 16

Testosterone: 43pg/ml

DHEAS: 18.7ng/ml

Cortisol Morning: 12.2ng/ml

Cortisol Noon: 1.6ng/ml

Cortisol Evening: 1.5ng/ml

Cortisol Night: 0.7ng/ml

1\. The estradiol is at the top quadrant (estrogen dominant) while my progesterone is low quadrant pointing to anovulatory cycle or luteal phase failure. I have tried all of the other ways to increase my progesterone over the last 40 cycles without much success. Supplementation seems to be my only choice or do nothing and accept the situation.

2\. Testosterone in top quadrant but have symptoms of deficiency. May point to poor tissue response.???

3\. DHEAS way above top quadrant for my age.??? Normally, would mean I am taking DHEA supplements, adrenal adaptogens or a well trained athlete. I am not an athlete, nor have I recently taken supplements or adaptogens.

4\. Morning cortisol is very high. Gee, no surprise there! Yeah, I'm stressed out of my mind, but who the hell isn't in our modern world. I was really happy to see a normal pattern of decline over the day. That was a big change over six years ago when I was a flat-line and it was surprising I could get out of bed in the morning. I will continue to work with my mind on stress management.

**

Hi Gladina,

Thank you for your kind comments. You gave me a smile on what has been a really tough day. When I have a "day-off" like today, I wonder if I will ever be free of my chains. All I can do on days like today is count my many blessings and work on the gift of forgiveness. I so want to have tranquility even when the storms of life batter my roof.

Yes, the salt thing is interesting. I don't control my salt intake. Of course, I have been programmed by my culture to "control" my salt intake even if I'm not consciously aware I am doing so. Maybe I do need more salt than my "trained" tastes "naturally" allow. I have been trying to add a bit more and remembering to add some seaweed that always sits on my table.

As for probiotics, I have had some success with therapeutic probiotics. Dr Natasha Campbell-McBride has some interesting ideas about getting probiotics in to your system. Her GAPS book outlines the methods. She uses probiotics orally and anally for really tough cases. I still think it is best to use fermented foods first** and then add therapeutic probiotics if required.

[http://eatkamloops.org/what-is-a-healthy-gut/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/what-is-a-healthy-gut/)

[http://www.customprobiotics.com/custom-probiotics-six-cultures.htm](http://http://www.customprobiotics.com/custom-probiotics-six-cultures.htm)

Here is what I know about colostrum. Find a local cow if you can. It will work much better:

[http://eatkamloops.org/vanilla-colostrum-shake/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/vanilla-colostrum-shake/)

It is really cutting edge stuff but many people think you can't replace the "mural" bacteria just the "transitory" bacteria. The "mural" bacteria is the bacteria on the wall of the intestine connected into the gut-brain. The "transitory" bacteria come and go with your food. I don't know if this is correct or not but I have seen great improvement in my condition:

1\. Low-carbohydrate or ketogenic diets help control the populations of "bad" bacteria.

2\. Fermented foods at every meal. (Make your own in your own kitchen to get the local "wild" bacteria. It will help you the most.)

3\. Eat the "vegetated" part of the plant to feed your "good" gut flora: lettuce, celery, herbs, beet tops, etc. Avoid root or high carbohydrate parts of the plant.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-08-10T02:25:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-44009>

**Day 56 (August 9, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.1C (Day 17 of cycle)

Wow. That is a low temperature for my luteal phase.

2c coffee, raw cream and 2tsp butter

**Breakfast

I had bowl of leftover tuna, celery and onions.

**Getting Progesterone

I decided to give it a try and see if I could get a local doctor at the clinic to write me a script for bio-identical progesterone. I went down to the clinic before 9:00am and waited in a line with over 40 people. I got a time to see a doctor at noon and went home to work. I was surprised how easy it was to get the script when I finally saw the doctor. He hardly even looked at the test results. I said the pharmacists next door said he thought I needed about 25mg of BHRT progesterone. The doctor just wrote the script for three months. I noted all the sick people in the line up today and asked for a refill for another three months. I didn't want to take up his valuable time when there are so many really sick people needing help. I didn't get the refill but he gave me three WHOLE months worth of hormone which works out to about a six month supply. I also showed the pharmacist the BHRT progesterone I already had and asked him if he thought it would be okay to use in an emergency. He thought the progesterone looked really good. This is the stuff I got. I was surprised it was easy to get over the border:

[http://www.life-flo.com/women-s-wellness/progesta-care-unscented-3.html](http://http://www.life-flo.com/women-s-wellness/progesta-care-unscented-3.html)

I was really shocked at how many really sick people were in line. I saw one man that had one of those walker-seats and couldn't stand and a woman pulling an oxygen cart. These very sick people didn't go to the front of the line but had to queue with the rest of us. Many people don't have a family doctor and can't get one. I was shocked and how the clinic system was dysfunctional because of the high numbers of sick people. I am the end of the baby-boom. When I see line ups like this all I can think is don't get sick and find ways to take care of your own health.

Snack**

I had three bowls of fresh local blueberries.

Tub/Ice**

15 minutes at tub temperature: 18.3-16.7C

Temperature before tub: 37.0C

The tub felt cold today.

Temperature after tub: 37.2C

Dinner**

I had part of a pork chop with onions cooked in lard. I had garden salad with celery tops and homemade dressing.

I also hope it continues and hope that my cycle will be regular again this month too! I hope that bbt (basal body temp.) begins to increase too over time. (Although I don' feel the cold...hmmm...).**

**

Hi Gladina,

If you have gut dysbiosis, constipation can be a real problem. When I went on the SCD/GAPS program the only thing that didn't go well for me was constipation. I was having type 1 and on good days type 2 bowel movement using the **Bristol Stool Chart**. It wasn't very pleasant:

[http://thewvsr.com/bristolstoolchart.htm](http://http://thewvsr.com/bristolstoolchart.htm)

Dr Natasha Campbell-McBride has a section on constipation in her book, Gut and Psychology Syndrome**. The sections starts on page 225. She gives detail instructions on how she does an enema and how to use probiotics. I know there can be a real yuck factor here and I wish I hadn't been so squeamish about trying it. I could have saved myself years of suffering. I guess it was thinking about my mom dying of bowel cancer that got me over the yuck factor.

The emema, the way she does it, seems to retrain the bowel to work properly again even if the constipation has been going on a long time. Well, it worked for me anyway. I now have type 4 or 5 most days. I now occasionally get constipated traveling but normally my bowel function is healthy.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-08-10T18:00:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-44138>

_I had someone from [www.350.org](http://http://www.350.org) ask me what I thought would work better than a carbon tax. Here is what I came up with. I just wanted to share it with you:

What would happen if everyone had there own garden in there front and back yard? What would happen if the family ate all summer long out of that garden and put some away for the winter? What would happen if people traded between households and helped the people they knew didn't have enough?

What would happen if we started building our houses out of local materials only, so the houses would look different all over the world because the local materials are different? What would happen if we powered these houses with local resources? What would happen if we slightly changed the way we lived to reduce our energy consumption? What would happen if we changed laws and bylaws so people could make these changes?

What would happen if we all started eating herbivores (cows, buffalo, etc) feed only on pasture? What would happen if only occasionally, we all ate omnivores (hogs, chickens, etc) mostly feed on pasture but needing additional grain and legume feed?

What would happen if we all started eating only organically produced foods and stopped buying industrially produced foods?

What would happen if we stopped buying stuff we don't need? What would happen if we stopped buying junk and only bought items that could last a lifetime of use and could be given to our children?

What would happen if we thought about everything that we don't produce ourselves? What would happen if we wondered about how an item is produced and what the true costs are to the people that produced it and the true costs to the environment?

What would happen if we started to think about our waste stream? What would happen if we became aware of how our "medicines" and "cosmetics" that we use and throw away poison other animals and ourselves? What would happen if people really had to deal with the garbage produced by their own household consumption?

What would happen if we could heal ourselves of most illnesses and not need the care of doctors and the medical system? What would if be like to not have 40 people standing outside a medical clinic hoping for help?

What would happen if we all lived near where we worked, and our work places were safe and healthy? What would happen if women that decided to have children could stay with their children instead of having to go back to work and ship their children off to be raised by strangers?

What would happen if people became aware of how much "raping and pillaging" of the environment and of oppressed peoples occurs so we can have a nice fat gain in our portfolios? What would happen if everyone realized their high paying job with all those benefits was causing suffering for others?

What would happen if we discovered that we were actually slaves of a system that was progressively destroying the world, while enriching the few? What would happen if people decided not to participate in the above?

That's what "we" could do but I would never want to participate in a system that would "force" these changes on my neighbors. If we are going to change the world it will be through raising the consciousness of people not through oppressive laws which have got us where we are now. Einstein said: "We can't solve problems by using the same kind of thinking we used when we created them."

_

Love, Caroline

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-08-12T21:06:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-44561>

**Day 59 (August 12, 2012) (130lbs)

Waking Temperature: 36.3C (Day 20 of cycle)

I forgot to take the 25mg of progesterone before bed last night. I took the dose as soon as I woke up. I am having some trouble integrating the progesterone cream into my normal routine.

2c coffee, raw cream and 2tsp butter

**Breakfast

I had a pork chop with onions cooked in lard. I had a salad on the side with homemade dressing. I was glad to see that Shaen got some more olive oil.

**Work

I spent 3.0 hours cleaning up my basement. I had a family member's stuff stored in there and it was getting to be a bit much to work around. I also was able to re-organize my stores and outdoors equipment; my youngest daughter has done some of her own "re-organizing' down there which I had to clean up. I know as a child I loved to go through my parent's storage trunks; I used to get in trouble for the mess I couldn't help but make. I hope to go to the lake today and do my CT there.

Lunch**

I had canned tuna salad on lettuces leaves with raw cheese. I enjoyed some cold raw milk. I couldn't help but look around and I could see just how far I have gone from the standard beach picnic.

Suntanning/Lake CT**

Three times at 15 minutes

I felt really renewed after my half day at the lake. I laid on the ground and looked up at the blue sky and fluffy white clouds. All the best things in the world are free of the taking.

Snack**

I ate some raspberries right off the plants. I tried the first sour cherries off the tree today. They are ready for picking tomorrow. I feel really bad because much of our raspberry harvest didn't get picked this year because of work related difficulties. We got a white fly infestation on the raspberries likely caused by lack of picking. I have been trying to encourage Erika to pick raspberries but unfortunately it's not working. I guess she will have to experience what it is like to not have enough before she can learn to pick fruit in season. Erika eats more fruit then everyone in the house combined. A shortage of fruit will impact her the most.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-08-14T05:10:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-44847>

**Day 60 (August 13, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.7C (Day 21 of cycle)

I took a double dose of progesterone yesterday. My temperature was much better today.

2c coffee, raw cream and 2tsp butter

**Breakfast

I had leftover lamb roast, squash and garden salad with homemade dressing. I had already worked about 3.0 hours on an empty stomach. Working without food is not a problem.

**Work

Today was my day off but I helped with 6.0 hours of cleaning and organizing. In Kamloops in summer it is best to start working about at 6:00am to avoid the heat. I got the freezers completed emptied, organized and clean out. I shut down our biggest freezer and move everything over to the other freezers. Our bull is ready for pick up and we needed to make space for him. The three of us got six goboxes ready for a "garage sale" on Saturday. We also got one gobox as a dump box of later processing. We will also have a "free" pile for the taking. Later in the day did some house cleaning and organizing. Shaen and I should be able to have tomorrow mostly off.

Tub/Ice**

15 minutes at tub temperature: 19.4-18.3C

Temperature before tub: 36.8C

I found the tub cold today. I was shivering again through most of it.

Temperature after tub: 36.8C

Getting My Hair Cut**

I got my hair cut today. I noticed all the gray as it was being cut. It has been a stress-fulled time. I went through a period like that this winter watching myself gray then go back to a more normal color. I am glad I don't color my hair. I can see my stress more fully this way.

Dinner**

I haven't been hungry yet though I have drank some red wine. I am feeling quite "warm" from the wine. My work environment has been, difficult. I think about Marcus Aurelius' words of advice: _"Begin each day telling yourself: Today I shall be meeting with interference, insolence, disloyalty, ill-will, and selfishness - all of them due to the offenders' ignorance of what is good and evil."_ Sounds terrible until you understand Stoic philosophy. His words help me keep my tranquility, peace and happiness. (Well, I'm not quite "there" because my hair has gone gray again!)

Late in the evening, I had some canned tuna with celery and onions.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-08-15T03:48:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-44984>

**Day 61 (August 14, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.8C (Day 22 of cycle)

It is good to see the higher temperature. I had a difficult night. I woke up at 2:00am to bed spins. This is the second time I have had this problem after drinking Shaen new homemade red wine. There must be something they put in the juice that is not on the label. I have learned not to trust industrial food. I felt sick to my stomach most of the day.

2c coffee, raw cream and 2tsp butter

**Breakfast

I had leftover lamb roast, squash and garden salad with homemade dressing. I did not feel well after eating.

Exercise/Play**

I had a very easy walk 1.0 hour in the alpine area at Sunpeaks. We had planned a hike but I was not in any shape to do so. We drove back home and picked up our bull from the butchers. I slept for about 2.0 hours in the afternoon. I still wasn't feeling that great.

Dinner**

I had a grilled rib steak from our bull with garden salad and homemade dressing. He was tender and delicious. We all love him better dead than alive. He was a very aggressive and dangerous animal considering how small he was. Got to go. The raw ice cream is ready!

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-08-15T17:02:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-45020>

**Day 62 (August 15, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.3C (Day 23 of cycle)

Another low temperature for luteal phase. As you can see my temperatures are all over the place. I have to read my journal for the last 30 days and try to assess my progress. I will try to get to it tomorrow. I am willing to do another 30 days for a total of 90 days for this n=1 experiment. I sure have not seen any "big" or "quick" improvements.

3c coffee, raw cream and 3tsp butter

**Breakfast

I had a T-bone steak and salad with homemade dressing. The bull is delicious!

**Exercise/Play**

I spent 1.0 hours riding a horse today. This will mean nothing to you but means a lot to me. The last and only time I was ever on a horse, I was about 7 years old. It was the most exciting day because I could get up on a horse at my uncle's ranch for the first time. Within a few minutes I became so sick. My eyes began to water, my nose started to run, and within about 15 minutes I could not breath. I was told I was allergen to horses. They removed me from the horse, sobbing. After my first reaction I could not even walk through a yard that had horses in it or I would break out in hives all over my skin and I would have extreme breathing difficulties within a few minutes.

Today my nose did run and I sneezed a few times. I could feel a bit of heaviness in my lungs but I could ignore the feeling. I don't think I could go on a multi-day trail ride but I could ride for short trips! I can't believe my first, and worst allergy, is almost gone!

Dinner**

I had leftover tuna, onions and celery.

New Post**

I had someone ask me what I thought would be better than the government's plans for a carbon tax. Here is my answer. I hope you enjoy it. In advance, I apologize for being an idealist and thinking so highly of my fellow man. Yes, people have told me what a stupid-head I am to believe such nonsense:

[http://eatkamloops.org/what-if/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/what-if/)

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-08-16T18:04:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-45209>

**Day 63 (August 16, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.5C (Day 24 of cycle)

2c coffee, raw cream and 2tsp butter

**Tub/Ice

15 minutes at tub temperature: 16.7C

Temperature before tub: 36.7C

The house has finally cooled off from the summer heat. Last night I needed my wool blanket and I was still cold. I have been using just a cotton sheet for the summer. The overnight temperature in the house is about 17.8C.

Temperature after tub: 36.6C

Breakfast**

Not hungry yet. I had beef liver and onions for breakfast. I had a few pieces of mock white chocolate coconut candy.

CT Experiment**

What I have noticed over the 60 days of CT.

1\. My second cycle was even shorter at 24 days.

2\. My second period was more normal. Four days with not spotting.

3\. My follicular phase did not have the normal up and down of daily temperature. All my days in the follicular phase was 36.3C or under that temperature which means I have a "cold" uterus and I am infertile. I had a more even temperature throughout my follicular phase. (This is considered a healthier pattern. This month I looked like I have thyroid problem, low average temperatures but stable. Normally I have low, fluctuating temperatures which point to adrenal problem.)

4\. I seem to be able to increase or keep my body temperature the same in the cold tub after ovulation and during the luteal phase. In the follicular phase I cannot increase my body temperature and my body temperature can drop quite low.

5\. I started bio-identical 20mg of progesterone on day 10 of my third cycle. I started taking 25mg of progesterone on day 17.

6\. My weight is back down to 130 pounds which is normal.

7\. I have noticed my feet are tougher. I can walk bare foot with the ouch, ouch, ouch of every step.

8\. I like doing CT in the lake with my daughter. Being in the water with her has enriched my life.

9\. I have not seen much improvement in my endocrine health over the last 60 days of doing CT. CT may help in the long run but it isn't a quick fix for people like me. I am wondering if CT works better for men without hormonal cycling that women face?

10\. Choosing to take bio-identical progesterone feels like a "failure" to me. I am sad that the CT did not have a more positive effect. I would hope that I would not have to take progesterone long term and could use it to re-balance my system and then be able to go off the hormone.

11\. I will continue for another 30 days of CT for a total of 90 days for this experiment. I wonder how many people would be willing to have "faith" in this method if there isn't some signs of improvement?

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-08-17T21:06:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-45402>

**Day 64 (August 17, 2012)

Waking Temperature: 36.5C (Day 25 of cycle)

3c coffee, raw cream and 3tsp butter

**Breakfast**

I had liver and onions cooked in lard. I had some sausage patties and zucchini on the side.

The End**

I have decided not to continue with this journal. I realized that Day 90 would be well into the beginning of my home schooling year. Even though this journal normally takes only about 30 minutes a day, I really should be focusing on other tasks that are not getting done. The journal has been an interesting experience. If you would like to chat come visit me at [www.eatkamloops.org](http://http://www.eatkamloops.org).

### Zorica Vuletic — 2012-08-20T04:58:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carolines-journal.1884/post-45800>

It's good that you kept the journal for 60 days. That's still pretty good. I can understand schooling and all the Fall activities that surely ensue.

I hope things improve for you. I have quite similar results as you regarding body temperature. My cycles are not short though...not at all. After last month July 21st which the previous one was May 7th and the one before that was March 18, you can see that my cycles are long. I'm really hoping that my cycle arrives any day now this month. Even if it's not regular this month, I feel more confident that over the next few months it WILL re-regulate itself for a number of reasons. After a ~month of heavy seafood consumption I can feel such a difference in mood and cognitive functioning. I feel physically energetic and I think my body composition is quite favourable. My skin seems very supple, soft and literally has a glow to it.

The thing about my cycle last month was that it was sudden (but spotting for first day) and it was very minimal cramping and pain. I did NOT need to take Advil, first time ever in my life. I also got rid of a day of ovulation from 7 (that's kind of excessive, yes?) to 6 days. Overall it was on the lighter side and seemed brighter red instead of ...idk. Just seemed more 'normal'.

Good luck, and if I have any questions or comments, I'll come visit you over at your site.

All the best!
