### [email redacted] — 2012-06-26T03:48:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-30-day-seafood-challenge-thread.1920/post-33570>

My favorite way to make white flakey fish that isn't expensive (like sea bass or halibut) is saute a boatload of onions till past translucent and starting to brown., and chopped tomato and garlic and whatever herbs/spices you like. Simmer that all together until you can't stand waiting. . .you want it brothy and juicy. Add chopped fish and put on a lid and cook till fish is done. Serve with a splash of good olive oil and herbs.

I used to get this at a favorite place in Boston - and they called it portuguese seafood. It's also great with lobster and scallops.

### AmyJ — 2012-06-26T04:03:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-30-day-seafood-challenge-thread.1920/post-33571>

Dinner Salmon.

Celebrating day 7--I don't think I've eaten seafood 7 times in a 3-month period before, let alone 7 days in a row!

Appreciating the recipes/meal ideas, thanks everyone!

### Glamazon — 2012-06-26T04:30:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-30-day-seafood-challenge-thread.1920/post-33576>

> **August;33469 wrote:** Question: how soon till you might be able to tell some difference in the way you feel . . or other physical difference . . I happened to have had a seafood meal for I think the 2 days before Glam posted something in the Cellulite vs CT thread that inspired this thread . . and prior to that I was probably averaging fish/seafood more like once a week . . I think I'm starting to feel different better in sort of a difficult to describe sort of way . . maybe my skin is a bit better/firmer/less cellulite/softer . . pretty subtle stuff . . . . . but it has not been very much time at all . . . so please tell me . . it what I think I'm feeling just my imagination . . it is too soon . . or it is possible . . .?

I was trying to put away a can of salmon a week, about 10 ounces. I wasnt always successful but most of the time I was because I always finished the can. That process was over about3 weeks. Then Dr. K started posting about the vital importance of cold seafood _and_ I noticed the skin changes. More softer. I said in that cellulite thread that I didn't have probs so much with cellulite, but since thne there is so much re-distribution my fat in my belly feels squishy and I am starting to see dimpling, alot of dimpling, in my thighs.

I began noticing changes after about two weeks in my skin. I spent most of my life in Florida and never had a problem with dry skin because of the humidity. Here in Colorado my skin does get drier. I do put Coconut oil on it and that helps but it seems with the salmon the coconut oil works even better. I was starting to get these patching feeling place on my cheek bones but those have gone away in the first couple of weeks even without me putting any CO on my skin. Also my pores look finer.

This is why I decided to start eating seafood for at least one meal a day.

### Inger — 2012-06-26T15:42:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-30-day-seafood-challenge-thread.1920/post-33644>

Thanks, Seahorse! I knid of started to like onions a lot after starting CT.. so I use them on almost everyrhing, raw onions.. so good. I do not know why really I crave them but I guess it might be that my body needs them then!

My challenge went not so good.

I did the fishpuree.. I ate it, two whole smallish fishes. But it was very yucky kind of because they smelled weird. I guess they was fished in a polluted water. They was prefrozen though so any parasites was dead for sure but I suppose that was no a good fish to eat. I am feeling kind of nauseated still and have minor abdominal discomfort. No way could I drink my wine, I just could not.. I hope this nausea passes soon. I ate some digestive herbs from the garden. I guess I will stay by my old good mackerel heads for now, the mackerel swims in clean waters and is a beautiful and nice fish. Never made my stomach sick eather!

### Glamazon — 2012-06-26T15:53:54+0300

Love salmon skin that has been fried in ghee.

### Inger — 2012-06-27T10:22:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-30-day-seafood-challenge-thread.1920/post-33894>

I would not eat canned food if I could get fresh. Fresh is always superior, or prefrozen.

In my world there comes no canned food no more. Also no sausages and such. I take the extra work gladely.. I know it pays back to me - better health... and my health is so much worth!

It takes me about max. 5 minutes to make my breakfast, lightly, in a small amount water cooked, fresh wildcaught fish.

Or raw. That needs also almost no prep. If you are real lazy eat everything raw and unprepared.  That will give you only health bonuses.

### nonchalant — 2012-06-27T15:54:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-30-day-seafood-challenge-thread.1920/post-33921>

I went to an asian market yesterday. I'd been there before, but now I was seeing it thru Kruse eyes. Wow the seafood section was bigger than the meat section! Incredible variety and good prices. Really looked fresh. You just have to walk through the asile of brightly packaged rice products to get there!

Seems that good food doesn't have fancy packaging, catchy names, or coupons available.

Since starting this challenge I've noticed that I'm able to resist food temptations quite easily. And I seem to be uninterested in my collection of dark chocolate.

### nonchalant — 2012-06-27T16:00:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-30-day-seafood-challenge-thread.1920/post-33924>

"In high mercury states you might notice that your balance is not good after an acute exposure. Any time I go to my dentist’s office for anything I do this test on myself. I also do it after I eat seafood to see if the fish might have been tainted. Try to do some yoga poses that require you to be on one leg. You will notice a big change and your balance will be decidedly off from normal. When this happens, I usually supplement immediately with Selenium and eat a lot of Cilantro the next two weeks. Why? It’s a natural mercury chelator and selenium is the antidote for mercury toxicity."

[http://jackkruse.com/what-can-you-find-about-your-own-health/](http://http://jackkruse.com/what-can-you-find-about-your-own-health/)

### Tizawee — 2012-06-27T16:16:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-30-day-seafood-challenge-thread.1920/post-33925>

Sea bass has a very delicate flavor. You might try something with a stronger flavor. For example, next time you have salmon use bacon grease on a small piece so you can try it. Or, it may be that your mouth just isn't into fish + bacon.

### Shijin13 — 2012-06-27T16:33:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-30-day-seafood-challenge-thread.1920/post-33928>

my favorite fish is Steelhead Trout. OMG Effing amazing. They've got limited seasons... and all Natives are catch and release.

The are both a fresh water and saltwater fish. the difference btwn them and salmon is they don't die after they spawn! occasionally my cousin will catch a BIG non-native up in Hell's Canyon and bring it home and we'll have it over the holidays. I'm hoping this year he gets several!

### vlynnb — 2012-06-27T23:01:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-30-day-seafood-challenge-thread.1920/post-34034>

OK, I am taking the challenge.

Shrimp for breakfast and wild caught flash frozen cod with zucchini and green onions sauteed in CO for lunch. Good. Not as good as Rocky Mountain Trout though but that will have to wait until I find time to go fishing.

Shijin, I would like to learn some steel head fishing tips from your brother-in-law. It is delicious but so far for the two years my in-laws have been going they haven't been very successful, only one or two catches. They have not gone to Hell's Canyon though, more around Challis and Salmon. I hope we all get better at it and can fill our freezers full.

Still have to fix my brain a little though. Those memories of my mom frying fresh caught small rainbow trout on a cold morning and serving them with good toasted bread are very ingrained (huh, wonder where that "ingrained" came from?).

Sockeye salmon with crisped skin, sauteed in ghee.

I ran a nutritional profile on salmon. 52 gms of protein in a cup. The skin is very, very rich in omega 3s, more so than the flesh. Almost a gm of potassium and other goodies also in the meat.

yummm.....

It seems the sockeye is running in abundance this year on a rebound. [http://news.yahoo.com/northwest-sees-record-returns-sockeye-salmon-214721088.html](http://http://news.yahoo.com/northwest-sees-record-returns-sockeye-salmon-214721088.html)

### LinD — 2012-07-01T16:54:59+0300

> **August;33643 wrote:** Seafood is a source of more than just protein and omega 3's . . . I think I remember elsewhere you saying that you made broth out of the exoskeletons of shrimp . . and that that was likely similar to the way the Inuit got some of their C . . . it is a bit surprising . . I always associated Scurvy with the sea/sailors . . also every since I learned that we were one of the few species that does not make our own . . I've sort of lamented the fact that we seemed to have lost that ability along the way . . . but I got from reading elsewhere in the book that Dr Cordain might think too much vitamin C has some drawbacks and generally C not as beneficial as we might have hoped . . I take vitamin C because I tend to really crave the C (sour stuff) . . and I thought it had some nice anti-inflammatory properties . . .
>
>
>
> Cod for breakfast was good . . .

I know taking lots of C made a strange bump on my palate go away. My dentist said I needed a root canal. I had no pain with this 'thing.'

Seafood has choline... very important combined with the incidental O3 for brain development. I think the shrimp exoskeleton broth was also about glucosamine/chondroitin (sp). Supplements are made from shellfish. Unfortunately, I can't be bothered peeling shrimp. Or I'd try the broth. I might try is just once. Have to remember to buy the unpeeled kind.

Chitin, comes from the exoskeleton.(C8H13O5N)n is a long-chain polymer of a N-acetylglucosamine, a derivative of glucose. It is used in surgical procedures all the time for patches/sealing and suturing. It dissoves into the body over time.

### bigknitwit — 2012-07-05T17:55:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-30-day-seafood-challenge-thread.1920/post-35633>

I haven't been posting to this thread, but I've been eating my seafood diligently. In the beginning, the fish meal didn't feel like I'd had enough "meat" in it. Now, a month or more later, it's my protein of choice and I'm eating it 1 - 3 X /day. I love salmon and cod and scallops. I love the salmon sauteed in coconut oil and the crispy skin. Then I take out the salmon and saute gr onions, raw coleslaw (shredded cabbage/carrots). It's fantastic! I keep going back to the store to buy more seafood lol And I'm losing weight too. Not sure if it's the seafood, the CT, the pregnenelone, the increased Armour dose, or all of the above, but I'll take it!

### nonchalant — 2012-07-12T22:50:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-30-day-seafood-challenge-thread.1920/post-37262>

This is starting to look like an addiction. I had a half pound of sockeye salmon in butter for breakfast, and lunch found me in the drive-thru line at a sandwich shop. I was going to order a lettuce-wrapped sandwich, but I just couldn't. In disbelief, I found myself driving over to the seafood restaurant, where they greeted me by name and set me up with a large Santorini shrimp cocktail. Yes, it's fabulous, chock full of shrimp, with olives and avocado, tomatoes onion and cilantro, but come on!

This is getting out of hand!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-14T21:36:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-30-day-seafood-challenge-thread.1920/post-37814>

WARNING

I had smoked oysters a couple of days ago from Crown Prince and have been on the search to find a bunch of them to stock up on. I hate raw oysters so this is a big find for me. No one has them in stock ANYWHERE!! Why? Because the FDA has recalled all shellfish from Korea because they believe it has been adulterated. Crown Prince Smoked Oysters is on that list!

[http://blog.consumerbell.com/2012/06/21/consumer-recall-news-fda-ban-on-korean-shellfish-leads-to-10-different-recalls/](http://http://blog.consumerbell.com/2012/06/21/consumer-recall-news-fda-ban-on-korean-shellfish-leads-to-10-different-recalls/)

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-14T22:08:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-30-day-seafood-challenge-thread.1920/post-37822>

Yes, I ate mine not knowing of the recall. I loved them so went to find more but every store I tried is out. TJs said they don't know why but they are going to a "seasonal" item. Then I found the recall on the FDA site: [http://www.fda.gov/Safety/Recalls/ucm306377.htm](http://http://www.fda.gov/Safety/Recalls/ucm306377.htm) and that other recall notice stating it may be contaminated with the norovirus. Check one of the two sites, maybe there is a product code you can compare your can to. Ate them on Tuesday....I'm fine (thanks for asking).

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-17T05:21:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-30-day-seafood-challenge-thread.1920/post-38625>

Remove the leafy part from the tough stems, then I chop it and fry it in a pan with coconut oil and garlic. Lots of people think bacon grease is great for this. Or you can leave the leaves in bigger pieces (without the stem), coat with oil of your choice, a bit of salt and any other spice you might like, and put in oven. Check frequently, the leaves will become crispy and yummy but make sure they don't burn.

### Martin — 2012-07-17T22:39:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-30-day-seafood-challenge-thread.1920/post-38828>

Went to local seafood shop up here in my little mountain town. It's run by a family that used to vacation by going deep sea fishing and started bring back fresh/frozen and sell here to finance their trip. Lucky for us!

They have so much and they say it is all US or Canadian sourced.

Started with Oysters. Picked fresh parsley I grew from seed, garlic from a friend's market garden, a dash of tobasco sauce, some lemon juice, fancy olive oil, and grass-fed butter(gobs!).

To make sauce, put oil and butter in skillet, hot, then added garlic, 30 seconds, threw in chopped stuff, turned off heat, set aside.

Quickly seared the oysters in CO oil, about minute and half each side, and put the little yummies in a small bowl and added set-aside said sauce.

Died.

Heaven.

### Huck — 2012-07-18T18:09:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-30-day-seafood-challenge-thread.1920/post-39025>

There is a little store just down the road from me that sells fresh produce, cage-free eggs, pastured beef, etc. He hooked me up with a quarter side of beef earlier this year at a good price. I was talking to the owner today about a good place to buy seafood and he hooked me up with a guy that drives to the shore to pick up fish/seafood every Thursday and delivers on Friday.

Just talked to the guy and he has both fresh and frozen at good prices. Ordered flounder, cod, and scallops for delivery this Friday.

The owner of the little store also offered to pick up seafood for me when he goes to Maine this fall.

If anyone is near Danville, VA and wants the names, pm me and I'll send you the contact info for the beef guy and the seafood guy.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-20T02:12:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-30-day-seafood-challenge-thread.1920/post-39527>

BG 5

> In 2005, Dr. Cunnane found that eating one kilogram (1/2 pound) of shellfish from mollusks and crustaceans is all that is needed to meet these requirements to fuel human brain growth.

There's a problem here.... one kilogram is about 2 pounds. In the comment section it was clarified that it should have read as about 1lb/day b/c of a conversion error. Thing is, one kilogram isn't 1lb either.

### Kiera2 — 2012-07-20T05:48:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-30-day-seafood-challenge-thread.1920/post-39603>

I have survived! 10 whole sardines (at least 15cm long), a fistful of parsley from the garden, juice of two small lemons, a tablespoon of ghee and a splash of iced water. I was so afraid of identifying any 'bits' I think I let the vitamix go for too long; it was a little warm. I'm not sure that chilled would have been easier though. It was a little green from the parsley, and very, very smooth. I used a straw and it was almost too thick. More water next time!

At first it tasted like I was drinking the ocean, but then I tasted the parsley and ghee. Not too bad.

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-08T14:16:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-30-day-seafood-challenge-thread.1920/post-69877>

MamaG - what's your primary seafood?

we've been hitting up the local international market for fish, shellfish, oysters, mussels & clams, scallops, squid, and octopus.

Have you tried Ingrid's MHS? I've got to say - after drinking it - I began to see a difference immediately.

here's how I do Ingrid's MHS - 2-3 lemons juiced and zested, a bunch of cilantro, and 2 frozen mackerel heads (you may need to add a bit of water as well) . blend up until smooth. drink through a straw - unless you're going to feed your brood - 2 head are sufficient...

### vkiernan — 2013-01-08T14:18:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-30-day-seafood-challenge-thread.1920/post-69878>

Soooooooo, 6 months later, I just now find this thread. I've been eating at least half a pound of seafood for the last four months and seen no changes from it. Anyone else? What does this indicate? Secondly, is the consensus that Jack meant to go with 1/2 kilo, and not 1/2 lb, so we should be eating 1lb daily?

I don't eat a lb. a day but we are all different. I would think you still have work to do on the gut. I am still not over the hump for the gut healing and what I mean by that is when I load up on seafood I get the big D still. Seems like it goes right thru me and is kinda like raw sewage too iykwim. When this happens, I have also noticed that I lose energy. Got to believe I am not absorbing much then either and probably a little dehydrated. I can eat a can of sardines for lunch for a few days and pretty soon, whoosh, there it goes and continues until I back off. Really amazing that it happens in the first place.

MG, what are you doing/done for fixing your gut? I know you eat fat and protein and seafood but do you do turmeric and black pepper, fermented foods, l-glutamine for leaky gut, etc. etc? I don't remember what your gi effects said about the status of your gut.

### MamaGrok — 2013-01-08T14:29:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-30-day-seafood-challenge-thread.1920/post-69882>

> here's how I do Ingrid's MHS - 2-3 lemons juiced and zested, a bunch of cilantro, and 2 frozen mackerel heads (you may need to add a bit of water as well) . blend up until smooth. drink through a straw - unless you're going to feed your brood - 2 head are sufficient...

Wild salmon, cod, sablefish, ahi tuna, shrimp, scallops, langostino, squid, occasionally canned oysters/sardines/herring/roe.

I haven't come across any whole mackerels, lol. Any reasons it's gotta be mackerel?

I usually saute fish in bacon grease till crisp on the outside and rare in the middle.

V, I use tons of black pepper & take curcumin (turmeric) daily. Ferments & bone broth have been a regular part of my life for five years. I don't have any pathogenic bacteria going on, but do have a seriously off-balance firmicute:bacteroidete ratio.

I have found that ox bile has ended nearly all the diarrhea and 100% of the cramps. !!! Something is wrong with my gallbladder, I think. It seems not to release bile slowly when needed, but all at once a few hours after a meal. Ox bile seems to prevent this entirely. My reading shows it may be a result either of Hashi's or of celiac's, or both! Or something else!

It seems not to release bile slowly when needed, but all at once a few hours after a meal. Ox bile seems to prevent this entirely. My reading shows it may be a result either of Hashi's or of celiac's, or both! Or something else!

I don't think it has to be a mackerel - its just what Ingrid had on hand. Just look for a small/fatty type of fish. even whole sardines (With heads) would work.
I'd search out your asian/international market - they have great turn over, as well as great prices, and offer a wider variety of fish - WFM in NoVa carries mackerel - but the international market has it all the time. the international market is actually one of the best fishmongers I've been too. I'd push more shellfish. we found a 1lb bag of frozen mixed seafood at our international market for $3.99/lb - its got mussels, squid, clams, shrimp, cuttle fish, and oysters in it. its become a weekly staple.
we can also get 5 raw oysters for $2. we've been getting 10 oysters/week and eating them raw - amazing fresh from the ocean/bay taste.
I'm usually eating seafood 2x/day sometimes more... I'd push it harder if you can... w/your thyroid and hashis you need all the raw natural packaging you can get. chiveche is a good way to get some tasty raw in as well... scallops, shrimps etc...

### MamaGrok — 2013-01-08T14:54:07+0200

I'd push more shellfish. we found a 1lb bag of frozen mixed seafood at our international market for $3.99/lb - its got mussels, squid, clams, shrimp, cuttle fish, and oysters in it. its become a weekly staple.
> we can also get 5 raw oysters for $2. we've been getting 10 oysters/week and eating them raw - amazing fresh from the ocean/bay taste.

I eat sardines whole out of a can. Any benefit to buying them whole & frozen? I think the canned ones have heads; but I might be crazy. I assume mackerel is small, and so you can eat the bones? When I fry up a whole red snapper, I always eat all the head meat & the eyes. And the fins. And the skin. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I am very hesitant to buy fish in random places b/c I absolutely cannot eat fishy fish. I'm perfectly willing to eat all kinds of gross stuff, raw stuff, whatever, so long as it's high quality, and ... I just can't eat farmed fish. I figure most of what's in the international markets is low-quality farmed crap from the disgusting polluted coasts of industrial China. Correct me if I'm wrong!

I did find great live oysters (or were they mussels?) in the natural foods market near me. It was very high quality. I can't take bad oysters.

I only two times a day, lol, and I can't eat just fish for breakfast. I tried, but even drowning it in animal fat, it still doesn't stave off my cravings. I have to eat at least 6oz of mammal flesh with my breakfast to keep cravings away (my #1 health issue, without a doubt, b/c when they come, I can't keep to any kind of dietary plan), and then I pile 8oz seafood on top of it ...and am barely hungry for dinner at 6pm, but I manage to get another bit of seafood & animal meat then. I don't think there's any room for more seafood, but I can try, lol. It's already at least half a pound each day, usually close to twice that.

I do want to branch out in variety. I need a regular source of something good, but that involves more trips to more stores, hard in my state of life. I'll do it if I see more results ... right now, there are none! Discouraging, at least in that one little aspect!

I certainly have no intentions of quitting the seafood - I've always known I should eat more, and I have no problem eating fish for breakfast now; why quit the habit?

### Charlotte — 2013-01-08T15:19:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-30-day-seafood-challenge-thread.1920/post-69890>

I asked Dr. Kruse in my consult, why MHS and he said they have loads of nutrients, especially behind the eyes and if I remember right lots of Q10.

# Thread: A HUGE thank you to doctor K
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-huge-thank-you-to-doctor-k.1923/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### msclman — 2012-06-18T00:00:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-huge-thank-you-to-doctor-k.1923/post-31823>

I don't know if this belongs here but I wanted to share my story with you all. My life has been going downhill for 7 years now. Energy, sex drive, motivation, mood, all were horrible. Not to mention putting on 50 pounds! I have seen 6 doctors over the last 7 years. Not one provided any sort of relief. These weren't your run of the mill doctors either, we are talking published best sellers under their belts. To make matters worse all every doctor I saw just wanted to do was push statins and other garbage on me. I had done enough research on my own to see the flaw here and it was so frustrating to constantly be printing up studies to show my own doctors, the people I was paying to treat me!! I'm sure I am not the only one here to be condescendingly laughed at by their doctor for going against medical dogma even after providing proof that the very same doctor could not explain her way out of. I am not a doctor after all so I have must have no idea what I am talking about I guess.

I found my way to a forum that led me here and things just started to click together for me. I knew I was onto something here. Having spent over $30k trying to get my issues solved I have become quite gun-shy when it comes to coughing up money for yet another doctor, but after reading thru several dozen blogs I was getting excited. Something inside of me knew the truth had finally revealed itself. I broke down and signed up for a phone consult. I wish you guys could have seen the look on my face when in the first 10 minutes Dr.K broke down EXACTLY what my issue was and told me EXACTLY how to fix it! I wanted to cry. After 7 long and painful years, someone had finally gotten it. Please keep in mind I have seen several renowned doctors, and I myself have a degree in biology and psychology with a minor in biochemical pharmacology. I have 6 doctors in my small family as well. Not my doctors, not my family who were md's or myself saw the info that was right in front of our faces. The evidence was screaming to us and no one heard but Dr. Kruse. I tell you it burns a little to have wasted enough money to buy a BMW just to get my issues solved for the price of a nice dinner w my wife!!!! I will give an update in the coming months and post blood work (my befores can be see on this site) as well so I can share how the therapy went. Again Jack I really want to thank you from the bottom of my heart and I highly recommend anyone struggling for answers to pick up the phone and give Dr.K a call. That call will change your life.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-18T03:04:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-huge-thank-you-to-doctor-k.1923/post-31857>

Thanks......for the kind words.....nice way to end my day. Now I have to go cook that seabass.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-06-18T04:28:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-huge-thank-you-to-doctor-k.1923/post-31875>

Thanks for sharing your story. Always inspiring to hear others successes!

### janagram — 2012-06-18T21:49:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-huge-thank-you-to-doctor-k.1923/post-31985>

So good to hear! I have just made an appt. with an integrative medicine Dr. who isn't aware of Dr. K., fine, but who also isn't totally on board with paleo, but kinda.......whoooha! what am I getting into? At least I'll get all the tests I want. But at the end of the day I may have to have a consult with Dr. K.

### msclman — 2012-06-19T00:15:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-huge-thank-you-to-doctor-k.1923/post-32014>

> **janagram;32094 wrote:** So good to hear! I have just made an appt. with an integrative medicine Dr. who isn't aware of Dr. K., fine, but who also isn't totally on board with paleo, but kinda.......whoooha! what am I getting into? At least I'll get all the tests I want. But at the end of the day I may have to have a consult with Dr. K.

well bst of luck to you! See if you feel better, if you don't i cant recommend Dr.K enough. Problem is they just arent teaching this stuff in medical school so 95% of the traditional doctors are just going to be a decade (probably much more to be honest) behind Kruse. I had a doctor 5 months ago refuse to prescribe me test gel because it was "proven to cause prostate cancer"!! But like you said its good to get someone who will play ball and get you the labs you want and if insurance pays for it even better. Also its good to have an open minded doc who will let you do your experiment, after all dr.k doesnt treat you but gives you an education. you will still need to find someone to treat you, so it is a good thing to have this integrative doctor around.

# Thread: Can we get a ranking of grains?

### Jennie747 — 2012-06-18T01:56:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-get-a-ranking-of-grains.1926/post-31842>

It's been tough getting my family members to give up _all_ grains. Are there things I can transition them to that are better than wheat, while I move them towards optimal?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-18T02:50:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-get-a-ranking-of-grains.1926/post-31849>

They all suck and are not fit for human consumption.......any questions?

### vwaggs — 2012-06-18T04:16:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-get-a-ranking-of-grains.1926/post-31868>

The book Wheat Belly really helped me see how each grain is harmful to us. Love that book!

### Glamazon — 2012-06-18T04:39:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-get-a-ranking-of-grains.1926/post-31877>

GRAINS ARE BAD! ALL BAD! THERE ARE NO SAFE GRAINS!

I understand but it is really as bad as Dr. K writes.

Grains are crack for the body. They work on the opiate receptors of the brain.

[http://www.sott.net/articles/show/244237-Wheat-is-an-Opiate](http://http://www.sott.net/articles/show/244237-Wheat-is-an-Opiate)

Your family will resist. You have a tribe of addicts. I can highly sympathize as I struggle also. It is a common addiction that can be as strong as an addiction to heroin but one can wean themselves off of them. They are such a "normal" part of modern life that people cannot understand how bad they are.

Go to Wheat Belly website and read.

Best of luck. It will be difficult. Learning to substitute things like coconut flour and nut flours for your family should help. There are some paleo mom blogs out there that address this subject.

Maybe someone can remix this so it says wheat. Maybe Dr. K can make one.

\[video\]\[video=youtube\_share;VCDnR6Px-co\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VCDnR6Px-co\[/video\]\[/video\]

### indigogirl — 2012-06-18T05:10:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-get-a-ranking-of-grains.1926/post-31880>

**From the comment section of Jack's latest blog, especially the last paragraph:**

Bob Smith Says:

June 17th, 2012 at 2:33 pm

@Paul- That’s interesting about YAP1. It provides a pathway from mRNA to nuclear DNA. Assessed, it helps answer one of many “how” questions about cancer. Other pathways are better for answering “Why”.

Recently a massive meta study showed what anyone watching already knew. It showed that people with type 2 diabetes are between 2 and 5 times as likely to have 24 of the 25 studied major types of cancer than the general population, a population which includes a large segment of type 2 diabetics.

Ballparked, the cancer incidence among type 2 diabetics is roughly 3.5 times as great as the cancer incidence among non-type 2 diabetics. That, my friends, is a smoking gun. If you want to develop cancer you should first try and develop type 2 diabetes. The rest should take care of itself.

Type 2 diabetes is largely the result of consuming volatile sugars along with proteins, flavonoids and opiates from foods like wheat. The pathways are pretty well nailed down. Sugar grows intestinal microorganisms. Opiates dull the immune system while, at the same time, mimicking the proteins displayed by the microorganisms. The immune system makes the intestinal walls porous. The partially digested opiates, sugars, flavonoids and proteins flow into the bloodstream, and wreck havoc all over the body.

Type 2 diabetes is characterized by insulin resistance. Opiates from wheat and other plant foods mimic insulin. They clog up insulin receptors, and prevent cells from consuming blood glucose. Cells starve. The blood increasingly fills with glucose, and elicits ever higher levels of insulin.

Cancer represents portions of the body ceasing their normal toil. Cells cease to consume food for the performance of specialized tasks, and instead consume food for the creation of useless tissue. Human cells perform this transformation when insulin resistance causes them to abandon their normal diet of glucose.

Cells are protected from assault by tissue transglutaminase (TTG), a protein in the extracellular matrix (ECM). Ingested flavonoids attack and destroy tissue transglutaminase, leaving cells unprotected. Unprotected cells, starved by insulin resistance, start importing and burning flavonoids for fuel. Unfortunately flavonoids do not lend themselves well to being burned for energy.

Glutamate is the most commonly consumed flavonoid. Glutamate is so essential to cancer transformation that many cancers produce their own glutamate. Wheat is roughly 1/4 glutamate.

PKM2 is a catalyst which tells cells why to burn fuel …..whether to burn it for energy or for the creation of tissue. As shown above type 2 diabetes fills the blood with glucose and makes cells starve for glucose. The glucose gradient across outer cell walls causes cells to redirect the aggregation of PKM2. The insulin-resistance form of PKM2 causes cells to start creating tissue.

Cells start creating randomly mutated tissue, combining human DNA with whatever proteins happen to be hanging around in the cytosol. From this point evolution takes over. In order to survive, mutated cells must feed themselves, reproduce, and avoid the body’s immune system. Unsuccessful cancer cells die. Successful cancer cells survive and form tumors.

In a nutshell …..When human cells use fuel for energy, they use mitochondria to make ATP. Then they burn the ATP. When cancer cells use glucose to create tissue, they ferment it directly. The cancer transformation starts with human cells in a glucose-charged environment. Because of insulin resistance the human cells cannot use the glucose. The cancer transformation replaces human cells with cells which can use the glucose.

There are very few differences in the “why” pathways from one type of cancer to another. For avoiding cancer our challenge is to end the insulin resistance and the glucose buildup. We do this by eliminating opiates from foods like grassy grains, dairy and legumes, eliminating flavonoids like glutamate and aspartate, eliminating fructose, and boosting our intake of meat and fats.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-06-19T06:09:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-get-a-ranking-of-grains.1926/post-32094>

> **Jennie747;31948 wrote: It's been tough getting my family members to give up _all_ grains. Are there things I can transition them to that are better than wheat, while I move them towards optimal?**

**

That is a really tough question. I am a **Weston A Price Foundation** member and most members believe that grains if properly prepared by fermenting, sprouting and fleshly grinding makes grains safe, for at least for some people. There is also a sub-sect of WAPF members that are on the SCD/GAPS or modified paleo diet** which do not consume any grains or grain products. I am one of these people. Grains are poisonous and addictive for me. Furthermore, I haven't found many "safe starches" either; they all make me "sick" sooner or later.

We are all different but everyone seems to benefit from getting off the industrial food and eating nourishing traditional whole foods. Focus on whole foods from a quality source, first. Your family will benefit from these changes.

In my family 50% need the SCD/GAPS diet and 50% do not. I have also noticed that the 50% that doesn't need the diet seems to have higher carbohydrate need and do well on a higher carbohydrate diet.

For the 50% that need the SCD/GAPS diet:

1\. I use nuts that I grind into flour for bread-like or cake-like products. I do not recommend buying nut flour because it is likely rancid and of poor quality. Shelled nuts should be stored in the freezer. WAPF recommends that you soak and dry your nuts before consumption. I use nuts for special occasions; I find them hard to digest even if soaked and dried prior to preparation. Nuts make delicious cooked or raw cakes and cookies. Nuts are a very small part of our diet.

2\. I do not use coconut flour. It is a strange industrial product and I do not understand the processing. I use coconut manna or creamed coconut which comes from the whole coconut meat. Coconut cream makes delicious raw desserts.

For the 50% that doesn't need the SCD/GAPS diet:

1\. My husband cooks up organic brown basmati rice.

2\. Potatoes are a good source of starch for the ones that can tolerate nightshade plants.

3\. Yams and sweet potatoes are good additions for most people.

4\. For special occasion eat "illegal" foods out of the house.

Good luck with your transition. Over the years, my husband has really noticed the benefits of eating this way. Experience having a healthy family is the best teacher.

### Jennie747 — 2012-06-19T12:59:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-get-a-ranking-of-grains.1926/post-32109>

Cereal is the biggest thing here. I rarely buy it anymore but sometimes give in to begging. Wondered if something oat based was maybe better than wheat based. Guess not! LOL

### thyme — 2012-06-19T14:06:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-get-a-ranking-of-grains.1926/post-32110>

cereal: Toast big coconut flakes/chips to very light brown. Put in bowl. Add fruit, if eating. Add milk or coconut milk, as per your diet. Enjoy!!

as for general grains...they might all suck, but I'm a non-custodial step-parent and radically changing the children's diet would be a pointless disaster, so I choose to use the lesser evils when needed. When the children are here, we eat potatoes with dinner most nights, but soaked white rice (or rice noodles, if we're coming to a rare pasta crisis) on enough other nights that they don't really notice that we're not eating wheat. And, since I live in Scotland, I have easy access to non-GE, uncontaminated, traditionally kiln dried and single-milled oats, so I make oatcakes when the kids are here so they don't notice (as much) the absolute lack of toast and bread and veg oils, etc.

Most men response positively to a high meat(protein and fat) focused diet. It's usually women that fight this change because of compulsive dieting and dieting propaganda.

These are some of my very old recipes that I used during my family's transition to more nourishing whole foods. There is a lot of preparation to make the granola but you could double or triple the recipe. Most people will not notice the difference:

### vlynnb — 2012-06-19T17:17:59+0300

### thyme — 2012-06-19T18:50:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-get-a-ranking-of-grains.1926/post-32166>

oh, I feed the kids eggs & sausage & avocados & meats and all the rest...but when I have them for one week every four months, and the 12 year old is already stressed over leaving her mother & her home & her television & her country & coming to my kitchen for only one week every four months or so, and she pushes her eggs & sausages around on the plate and then bursts into tears as soon as I've left the house because she likes me and doesn't want to hurt my feelings but she hates eggs and sausages and misses her toast and....my priority, in this situation, is to make her feel welcome and at home for the short stays in which we are lucky enough to have her. A small homemade oatcake with cheese makes the egg & bacon go down and keeps things familiar enough that food doesn't add to her alienation. Her diet with her custodial parent isn't going to change. Her older sister chooses to eat the way we do; she might, one day, or she might not. I'm not entering a power-struggle over it.

### vlynnb — 2012-06-20T00:11:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-get-a-ranking-of-grains.1926/post-32253>

thyme, I admire how you are handling the situation. It is better to not alienate her and just let her know how you eat but that you are sensitive to her need to feel more comfortable and have food that is more familiar and favorable to her when she is there so seldom.

I have a somewhat similar problem with my grandchildren who live in my basement. Their parents were highly ranked college athletes and SIL is a coach and they are very health conscious, they eat a healthy diet according to CW, only allowing sweets etc. on weekends and special occasions for the most part. The other grandmother (who has lupus( wants to be known as the grandmother who always brings donuts, a dozen at a time for four children. My daughter tosses what is left after grandma leaves. I have a good relationship with all three but one of them told me I was not like other grandmas because I don't bake cookies! Made me a little sad that that was the image of what a grandma does for her grandchildren (my image in the past too). Guess I will have to try some paleo cookies at least, though with my probable AI there are not many I could eat with them. I do serve them pancakes for sleep overs since that is a long standing tradition but I also serve eggs and a breakfast meat at least. It looks like this will be a slow process to convince family what is best for them. I hope to be known as the grandma who learned how to regain her health and helped them to learn.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-06-20T01:32:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-get-a-ranking-of-grains.1926/post-32275>

Hi Thyme,

Family and society pressures are a bugger. I wish we didn't live in such a toxic food environment. The pressure is bad for adults let alone children. So what can a mother or step-mother do? I guess, we have our love and respect for our children's evolving choices. (Autonomy is very important in my family.) We can do the best job we can do within the limitations the world gives us.

From my humble opinion homemade oatcakes with eggs and bacon sounds pretty good to me! Just compared that breakfast to corn flakes, skimmed milk and half a grapefruit with sugar on top. Or what about a Coke, twinkie and a bag of salt-and-vinegar chips!

Thyme, you are doing a great job within the limitations given by your situation. Give yourself a pat on the back and a hug to the lucky child that has you as a step-parent.

### kathylu — 2012-06-20T15:02:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-get-a-ranking-of-grains.1926/post-32378>

If you are struggling with having removed grains from your diet, try baking with coconut flour. I tend to use coconut flour for a treat a couple times per week and I make goodies for my 5 year old, especially to take to school so he doesn't feel deprived. Coconut flour is finely ground, dried, defatted coconut meat. It's a little high in fiber, which is not a bad thing for me as it increases regularity. There are lots of great recipes on the web, or you can get a copy of Bruce Fife's "Cooking with Coconut Flour". There's absolutely no reason to miss grains anymore...

From the Tropical Traditions website:

Tropical Traditions organic coconut flour is fiber from the coconut meat after most of the oil has been extracted to make Virgin Coconut Oil. It is certified organic, unsweetened, and has not been treated with sulfites. Tropical Traditions organic coconut flour is high in dietary fiber and protein, and is gluten-free. It has more fiber than gluten-based grains. Tropical Traditions organic coconut flour can be added to standard wheat-based recipes to add extra fiber, by substituting 10-30% of the grain-based flour with our coconut flour. Some recipes, such as muffins and quick breads, can be accomplished with 100% Tropical Traditions organic coconut flour and therefore be 100% gluten-free! (See some free recipes below.) Since organic coconut flour contains natural sugar from the coconut meat, baked goods need less sugar added. The organic coconut flour has a mildly sweet coconut taste!

Gluten Free Coconut Flour Banana Muffins recipe photoTropical Traditions organic coconut flour also contains over 19% non-gluten protein! Therefore, it makes an excellent addition to shakes and smoothies where fiber and protein are needed. Or simply dissolve a spoonful in water as a high-fiber drink with a coconut flavor! This organic coconut flour is very versatile and very tasty. Sprinkle it over your favorite dishes to add a wonderful coconut flavor, use it as a thickener in soups and sauces - the possibilities are endless!

Wow! It's crazy what CW does to those around it. I am in the process of eliminating things from my 12 yr old son's diet, starting with wheat. Although with the "teenage personality" coming through it is not going to be easy. One day he asked "Why do you keep learning this stuff"!! We are fortunate that he attends a Montessori school and the teachers are so open-minded. The kids eat if & when they are hungry. His teacher is going to make their classroom wheat free next year (she read Wheat Belly!) so it's definitely a step in the right direction.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-06-20T19:17:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-get-a-ranking-of-grains.1926/post-32443>

Yes, it is a cautionary tale, but that doesn't mean I want to give up. At the time I was in trenches with Social Services, PAC, school authorities, and the sometimes confused, and dirty looks, of my friends, family and neighbors. I am so grateful that I had the choice to pull Erika out of the school system. A generation ago that choice would have made my child into a truant. As a parent, I would have been breaking the law; I don't think I would have made the choice under those kind of pressures.

I am also very grateful to Dr Kruse and his work. I believe his work will bring down the walls. I have read almost every word on his website. Though, I likely have only understood about 50% of it. (Kind of like my grade point average in grade school!) I find Dr Kruse's work brilliant and I hope he can make inroads with the medical system. The system needs all the help we can give it, if we are going to save ourselves and our children.

I also believe in the power of Mothers to change the world by changing what happens in the household and in the neighborhoods. Motherhood's "power" is in transforming the dinner table, the garden, and the community we all share. That will take facing our worst fears and having courage to fight for our children's health:

[http://eatkamloops.org/dealing-with-children-societal-pressure-and-halloween/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/dealing-with-children-societal-pressure-and-halloween/)

[http://eatkamloops.org/industrial-food-sickness/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/industrial-food-sickness/)

**\- Breakfast w/ 50gm of protein Yes, 57.5 grams of chuck steak with 3 T CO.

**\- Breakfast w/i 30m of rising No, 40 minutes

**\- 5+ hrs between Breakfast and Lunch No lunch

**\- 5+ hrs between Lunch and Dinner No dinner

**\- Finished dinner by 7 No dinner

**\- no snacking** Check

\- 3.5+ hrs between dinner & bedtime** Yep

\- exposure to morning temp & sun** Slept in and got right on computer, so I missed the morning sun. Going to go get some Vit. D this afternoon while staining my deck

\- Darkening the house at sunset** I was still outside staining the deck when the sunset. I will do better tomorrow.

\-CT -** I did a hot epsom salt bath and did the croak method of adding cold to the tub water. Then, I did 2 hours of spot-CT with compression shorts.

\- 100% primal, whole 30 diet (no PUFAS)** Check

\-

>
>
> There are lots of people who offer great advice around here. I'm not really one of them yet, but hopefully I can get my butt in gear and be one of the success stories one day  Good luck with the detox, stay strong!!**

**

Yes, let's be detox buddies!!! I'm sorry you feel bloated and are struggling with binging, too. I'm not sensitive to bathroom talk; you're okay.  How about this for TMI -- I had to stop my husband from getting frisky with me because my stomach hurt to much to have him touch me! That was depressing. For someone who has struggled with libido issues for a long time, it's finally returning, and then I have to say no because I've binged? Sad...

Anyway, the angry cysts/zits on my face are a good reminder that gluten and dairy are not my friends. I dropped 5.2 lbs. of inflammation from yesterday's steak day. Quelsen has told me I know how to reduce (that's not my problem), but now I need to focus on eating the right kinds of foods in the correct quantities. And, if a binge comes my way, I'm going to take paleo pepper's advice and binge on a head of cabbage instead of a piece of cake and ice cream. LOL!

I'm really focusing on changing my mindset. I've also gotten rid of most non-paleo foods in my house. I was still accommodating to my husband, making him treats, but I believe having to look at these things day in and day out and say no everyday can be exhausting, so I will limit the mental mind games to when I'm out and about.

**\- Breakfast w/ 50gm of protein Yes, 64.1 grams of ground beef (8 oz) with 1 T CO.

**\- Breakfast w/i 30m of rising Yes

**\- 5+ hrs between Breakfast and Lunch No lunch. I will be in town getting a back massage over lunch and will have an early dinner.

**\- 5+ hrs between Lunch and Dinner Yep.

**\- Finished dinner by 7 Done eating by 5:30 pm I ate over a lb. of bacon along with my pork burger. I wouldn't call it a binge, but I was definitely wanting more. If I would have had carbs in the house, I would have grabbed them. I was craving pumpkin puree and coconut cream oddly.

\- no snacking** Check...the urge to snack on carbs and sugar really hits me in the evenings. It's like I feel like I will never get to eat again, so my brain tells me to buckle down and eat, eat, eat! I have that urge right now, so I'm going to go outside and do my cattle chores and hope for a nice distraction.

\- 3.5+ hrs between dinner & bedtime** Check.

\- exposure to morning temp & sun** Didn't get outside again for morning light. Need to improve in this area.

\- Darkening the house at sunset** Wore my blue blockers for the last hour I was up. I was sleeping by 11. I was still outside doing cattle stuff at sundown. I'm not sure how to get better at this, unless I totally ditch my husband with the work and get inside. Maybe I will wear the glasses outside. What a sight!

\-CT -** spot-CT for 1 hour this morning

\- 100% primal, whole 30 diet (no PUFAS)** Check, although my bacon had nitrates...so maybe not?

\-

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-19T22:43:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32242>

Just got back from a one-hour deep tissue massage...so relaxing. Going to spot CT and take a nap. I had some hunger pangs at about 10:30 am this morning, but I think it's the sugar cravings talking, not actual hunger. I ignored it and went to my appointment, ran some errands and plan on eating an early dinner of ground pork and CO.

Thanks, Sueville. I hope so. I've learned a lot from reading other's journals and realizing that many of my issues are similar to what other people are struggling with, too...so I hope that people will weigh in and help me out as I go down this path of recovery. I'm come so far...too far to give up now, so I'm going to keep plugging along and see how it goes.

BAB...fat and protein...CT...dark...sleepy....repeat

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-20T17:18:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32408>

**Breakfast w/ 50gm of protein Yes, 88 grams of ground pork and ground beef with 2 T of CO. I'm really amping up my BAB to see if it stops me from wanting to eat so much at dinner. I wouldn't call yesterday a binge, but I ate a TON of bacon on top of my dinner, so it was a lot of bulk and a lot of calories. I'm going to try to swap the trend and eat most of my food at breakfast.

**Breakfast w/i 30m of rising Yes, 20 minutes.

**5+ hrs between Breakfast and Lunch No lunch.

**5+ hrs between Lunch and Dinner

**Finished dinner by 7

**No snacking** Check

3.5+ hrs between dinner & bedtime**

Exposure to morning temp & sun** Again, how do I incorporate this in while taking my supplements and readying BAB? I also oil pull at this time, as well. Maybe I should start cooking my BAB over an open fire outside, LOL!

Darkening the house at sunset**

CT** Spot CT (I wear an ice pack on my stomach that wraps around and velcros on. I also shove ice packs in my compression tights and in my sports bra. I'm quite a sight!

100% primal, whole 30 diet (no PUFAS)**

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-20T17:28:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32411>

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-20T17:28:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32413>

Hi There

I'm also wondering about why we would crave/overeat protein and fat. I understand the biochemistry behind the carb/everything binges but the wanting to overeat protein and fat just really mystifies me. It just doesn't make sense..... we're made to eat this stuff and it doesn't really "set off" anything in the brain that I know of that would lead to uncontrolled eating.

I do this, too.... wanting to eat way too much protein and fat. It comes in phases, right now it's fat. Coconut oil in particular. Can't get enough of it. I buy bacon one pack at a time b/c I know I'll eat the whole darn thing in one day. It's a treat for every once in a while and I just plan on eating the whole thing in advance.

I'd say if you can at all, don't worry about overeating the good stuff right now and just focus on staying on AI.... one thing at a time. And I know that's hard if you're afraid of gaining weight from it. But then... the other side of fear isn't so bad I'm finding out.... I'm really liking it actually. So maybe time to let go of some fear.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-20T17:51:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32420>

> **Souldanzer;32527 wrote:** Hi There
>
>
>
> I'm also wondering about why we would crave/overeat protein and fat. I understand the biochemistry behind the carb/everything binges but the wanting to overeat protein and fat just really mystifies me. It just doesn't make sense..... we're made to eat this stuff and it doesn't really "set off" anything in the brain that I know of that would lead to uncontrolled eating.
>
>
>
> I do this, too.... wanting to eat way too much protein and fat. It comes in phases, right now it's fat. Coconut oil in particular. Can't get enough of it. I buy bacon one pack at a time b/c I know I'll eat the whole darn thing in one day. It's a treat for every once in a while and I just plan on eating the whole thing in advance.

And I know that's hard if you're afraid of gaining weight from it. But then... the other side of fear isn't so bad I'm finding out.... I'm really liking it actually. So maybe time to let go of some fear.

Yeah, I really need to dump my scale and my relationship with it. The bacon was a lot to eat, but you're right -- it didn't set off the crazy in the brain, I've got to eat more, more, more feeling that I get with carbs. Thanks for the reminder about AI. I was just pining away after a brownie recipe to take to the lake this weekend, but that would mean allowing eggs and almonds back in, and that, I cannot do.

I usually plan out what I'm going to eat the day before, and I ate everything I planned to eat all day for breakfast this morning. Maybe I will add something else for dinner, maybe not. I'm not sure how dangerous fasting is for someone with an ED...but it was a lot of protein and fat, so the cravings should stay away.

Sigh...sometimes I just wish I could be normal. Eat one gluten free treat without needing to eat the whole pan, and then not feeling guilty about that one bar and making my life miserable for days after to try to lose the weight.

Souldanzer, what was a turning point for you? I read that you made a remarkably fast recovery this time around. What do you think was different this time?

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-20T17:54:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32421>

> **Jennifer;32525 wrote:** Keep riding PaleoCowgirl!

Cute! Thanks, Jennifer! I read through parts of your journal and see TOM has returned for you! Congrats! I can't remember the last time I had TOM. I bet it was 2010. I got off birth control in Feb. and am hoping to see it's return soon. DO you think there was one element of your routine that helped you out? If so, care to share?

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-20T18:22:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32430>

> **PaleoCowgirl;32535 wrote:** Cute! Thanks, Jennifer! I read through parts of your journal and see TOM has returned for you! Congrats! I can't remember the last time I had TOM. I bet it was 2010. I got off birth control in Feb. and am hoping to see it's return soon. DO you think there was one element of your routine that helped you out? If so, care to share?

LOL..we're thinking alike. Well, I've been on the elmination diet since June 2, so I've been eating really clean - no coffee, no alcohol, no caffienne, no eggs, no pork, no red meat, no dairy. This is all for my leaky gut. I added in sweet potatoes. And I eat a lot of salmon now. I stopped weighing myself except maybe once a week. I've stopped sweating the small stuff. Added the pregnelone, 7 keto, Phosphatidyl Choline and l-carnitine. I take a lot of fish oil. And the red clover which I read in Dr Lee's hormone book. He also suggested Vitex herb for ovulation but I never found that one so I didn't buy it. And I switched to armour thryoid and did some spot CT on carotids. Or maybe the estrodial has finally kicked in???

### Lyndra — 2012-06-20T19:06:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32441>

> **PaleoCowgirl;32534 wrote:**
>
>
>
> Sigh...sometimes I just wish I could be normal. Eat one gluten free treat without needing to eat the whole pan, and then not feeling guilty about that one bar and making my life miserable for days after to try to lose the weight.

Maybe there is no "normal" behavior when it comes to junk food like that. How many people can look at a pan of brownies and not want to eat until they're stuffed or beyond? I gave in to GF treats a few weeks back and one begat another and another. Once I finally got them out of my system (brain & body), I'm not craving them anymore.

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-20T19:23:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32445>

> **PaleoCowgirl;32534 wrote:** Yeah, I really need to dump my scale and my relationship with it. The bacon was a lot to eat, but you're right -- it didn't set off the crazy in the brain, I've got to eat more, more, more feeling that I get with carbs. Thanks for the reminder about AI. I was just pining away after a brownie recipe to take to the lake this weekend, but that would mean allowing eggs and almonds back in, and that, I cannot do.
>
>
>
> I usually plan out what I'm going to eat the day before, and I ate everything I planned to eat all day for breakfast this morning. Maybe I will add something else for dinner, maybe not. I'm not sure how dangerous fasting is for someone with an ED...but it was a lot of protein and fat, so the cravings should stay away.
>
>
>
> Sigh...sometimes I just wish I could be normal. Eat one gluten free treat without needing to eat the whole pan, and then not feeling guilty about that one bar and making my life miserable for days after to try to lose the weight.
>
>
>
> Souldanzer, what was a turning point for you? I read that you made a remarkably fast recovery this time around. What do you think was different this time?

YES, dump the scale! I'm a lot happier now that I did. Of course, I gained and I'm not thrilled about that but I'm putting health first, so there you have it. I know that I've gained. My clothes can tell me that. Clothes are better than an actual number that you can use to berate yourself with.

And this may sound crazy, but just give yourself permission to eat the whole pan. See what happens...I gave in to all my cravings for about 10 days solid. I stopped trying to stop myself. Stopped beating myself up. And yes, I gained and it was ugly for awhile but now I seem to be able to stop myself. There are things that I still eat a bigger portion of than my super-uptight personality wants me to eat! But I can pull the plug. Even so, I always did the BAB and ate my fat and protein. It's the nuts that get me, every time. OH and the muffin cups. I could eat a lot of those.

I think another key is not to tell yourself anything that starts with "never" like "I can never have nuts again". At least for me, the more never something is, the more I want it. Set small goals. Like no bingeing this week. For some crazy reason, doing this elimination diet has been easy, even though I can't hardly eat anything. I think it's because I know it's just 28 days.

Also, I think realizing that you are normal! As normal as I am!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-20T19:31:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32446>

> **Lyndra;32555 wrote:** Maybe there is no "normal" behavior when it comes to junk food like that. How many people can look at a pan of brownies and not want to eat until they're stuffed or beyond? I gave in to GF treats a few weeks back and one begat another and another. Once I finally got them out of my system (brain & body), I'm not craving them anymore.

Agreed. For awhile, I felt I was too strict, and I needed to cut myself some slack, so I experimented with g-free treats and loved them, but I also noticed that I craved sweets more...I need to focus on just eating real food.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-20T19:33:31+0300

The bacon was a lot to eat, but you're right -- it didn't set off the crazy in the brain, I've got to eat more, more, more feeling that I get with carbs. Thanks for the reminder about AI. I'm not sure how dangerous fasting is for someone with an ED...but it was a lot of protein and fat, so the cravings should stay away.

>

> Souldanzer, what was a turning point for you? I read that you made a remarkably fast recovery this time around. What do you think was different this time?

I still have to eat when I get hungry pretty much. So if I ate everything in the AM for the day, if I was hungry for dinner, I would still eat. How do you even plan your food intake like that in advance? How can you predict how much and what your body is going to need exactly?

I made a very quick recovery from my last lapse for my own standards. If there is a next time, I will catch it even earlier and do the required corrections. I'm pretty sure this happened b/c I've been following AI and Lrx for long enough to get some pathways established in my brain. So while my AN pathways are still very functional and easy to slip into if I go off road with my program, if I correct the deviations, I think my new and improved pathways just kick back in quickly. It was like my body remembered what it was supposed to do. I'm not at my goal yet with my eating... still working on some things. I'm not afraid of relapsing any longer - b/c I know what to do to get out of it now.

### Lyndra — 2012-06-20T20:19:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32463>

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-20T21:22:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32476>

> **Souldanzer;32574 wrote:** I still have to eat when I get hungry pretty much. So if I ate everything in the AM for the day, if I was hungry for dinner, I would still eat. How do you even plan your food intake like that in advance? How can you predict how much and what your body is going to need exactly?
>
>
>
>
>
> I made a very quick recovery from my last lapse for my own standards. If there is a next time, I will catch it even earlier and do the required corrections. I'm pretty sure this happened b/c I've been following AI and Lrx for long enough to get some pathways established in my brain. So while my AN pathways are still very functional and easy to slip into if I go off road with my program, if I correct the deviations, I think my new and improved pathways just kick back in quickly. It was like my body remembered what it was supposed to do. I'm not at my goal yet with my eating... still working on some things. I'm not afraid of relapsing any longer - b/c I know what to do to get out of it now.

Well, I do adjust if I'm hungry and depending on my activity levels; it's not so rigid...but I feel failing to plan is planning to fail, and I chart everything; I even plug in what I eat on binges, so I can look at exactly what I'm putting in my mouth. My hungry/satiety signals are so far gone that I don't trust myself. For example, even though I ate over 1200 calories for BAB, I could have kept eating. I have no idea when I'm hungry and full really, until I'm way past the uncomfortable point. On the flip side, it can be really easy to eat too little because the hunger cues don't really come like the blood sugar drops on a SAD diet.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-20T21:23:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32477>

> **Souldanzer;32570 wrote:** maybe stop being blinded by the goal and start to enjoy the journey....

True. There is a certain anxiety that comes with not cycling, especially because one day I would like to have children. It plays tricks with my mind. What's wrong with me? Am I not fertile? Will I have problems getting pregnant? Miscarriage? Developmental issues in my baby? Maybe all women have these fears; I don't know. But, you're right...I need to relax and let TOM come when it comes.

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-20T21:26:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32478>

I'm the wrong person to ask about caloric intake.... but..... you ate 1200 calories for breakfast and think that you cannot eat anymore today?  You have plenty of room for dinner.

What's wrong with me? Am I not fertile? Will I have problems getting pregnant? Miscarriage? Developmental issues in my baby? Maybe all women have these fears; I don't know. But, you're right...I need to relax and let TOM come when it comes.

Think long and hard about when the right time is to have a baby. Or you might be doubling up on ED problems (and others) for the rest of your life and your child's life. If you have a child while you're still ED, you will set up your child to have the same problem.

This one is emotionally charged for me as my mom was AN right before she got pregnant with me. Same situation as yours... desperately wanted to get pregnant w/ HPA amenorrhea. If I was given the choice, I would have happily waited a few more years to join this world set up for success instead of the disaster that I have been so far. I keep wanting to say "it's not fair" and I know that won't change anything... what doesn't kill me makes me stronger.... but it's not a lot of fun, speaking as the future baby you might be having.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-20T21:36:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32482>

> **Souldanzer;32593 wrote:** I'm the wrong person to ask about caloric intake.... but..... you ate 1200 calories for breakfast and think that you cannot eat anymore today?  You have plenty of room for dinner.

You've got me there. I'm clearly still not out of my binge and restrict mentality...

I'm thawing out some venison steaks to have for supper. They are so lean though; they kind of turn me off. I'm really into my fatty cuts of pork and beef these days. I probably should add some fish once in awhile; I've been bad about that.

Yeah, I really need to get myself in shape before I bring a baby into this world. I read your journal entry about that and the point you made that people absolutely should not think about reproducing if they aren't optimal, and I cried to my husband about it. It's a really crappy feeling to think I shouldn't have a baby because I wouldn't be doing that child any favors. Then, I see my friends and cousins who have no problems with weight issues, have happy healthy babies and seem blissfully unaware that the cheerios, ice cream and other SAD goodies they give their babies are bad for them!

But, you're right...I would hate to be on binge/restrict mode while carrying a child. That's not fair to my unborn babies. And, it would be a terrible environment to develop.

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-20T21:51:18+0300

Enjoy the process and success!

### KiwiLauren — 2012-06-21T00:39:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32532>

PaleoCowgirl, I have been reading your thoughts and think you're pretty inspirational yourself. You've been so honest and I think so much of our 'cheating' and 'hiding' is about dishonesty (mostly trying to keep things from ourselves!). Here is what I'm wondering. How would you eat/live if you were pregnant. Right now. I think you should eat and live that way at this moment. My guess is you'd nourish yourself, mind body and spirit. You wouldn't eat crap because you'd be motivated by life not to do so. You wouldn't restrict your calories because you'd want to nourish a growing being. You would enjoy each moment and love the journey because you knew each moment was precious and never-to-be-repeated. Well all that is true right now. Maybe if you live it - NOW - you will not only move out of the stuck place but also end up giving birth to a new you. And a baby.

Wow, this is really powerful stuff. So true! I just sat down to have venison and wanted something sweet for dessert and realized I didn't have anything in the house I could have -- not a piece of fruit or scoop of coconut butter or nothing. I was just thinking about going to raid my mom's cupboard...that's becoming a habit during my binges since we live 15 minutes from town and a half mile from her kitchen...anyway, I read this note and realized, I don't WANT to hurt my body with sugary carbs. I don't want to binge in secret and have a hidden addiction. What you said is exactly how I should treat myself. Eat when I'm hungry, stop when I'm full, eat healthy, real foods, don't restrict and don't overdo it. I know it's easier said than done, but I need to find a place in my mind where this mantra actually sticks and works the next time I want a sugar high. Thanks so much for the advice!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-21T02:47:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32563>

> **Souldanzer;32650 wrote: I've seen people with ED's continue the detrimental behaviors throughout their pregnancies. Getting pregnant does not cure an ED. Many have tried.
>
>
>
>
>
> .... nobody needs a spare scale....**

**

I don't know why I've got baby on the brain so much right now. It started when my doc diagnosed me with PCOS and said I could have a hard time getting/staying pregnant. I've always been one to try that much harder when somebody tells me I can't do something. In reality, my husband and I don't want a baby for a few years yet. We have a land loan (cattle pasture) that will be paid off in 2015, so that gives us a lot more cushion and extra money for a new member of the family. In the meantime, if I stick to that goal, that means I have 3.5 years to get optimal.

**Between now and then, I want to:**

\- Get a regular TOM back

\- Settle at an appropriate weight for my height. I'm 5'3.5" I weighed 137 on my wedding day and everyone said I looked beautiful. To me, I looked fat...I know 109 is way too long, so I should settle somewhere in between and be happy about it.

\- Reduce my need for t3 and keep my hashis at bay

\- Heal my adrenals. I hate seeing my temps tank to 95 in the afternoon. No wonder I feel crappy!

\- Eliminate my cravings and urge to binge

\- Sleep through the night without waking up to go to the bathroom

\- Have a positive body image. Right now, when I look in the mirror, I see a skinny boy with a spare tire, thinning hair on my head and extra hair everywhere else (thanks PCOS) and acne (from my binges). Hot, huh?

\- Change the way I think about food. Food is medicine. It's not a treat. It's not a punishment. It's not a reward. It's fuel.

How am I going to do that?**

\- CT regularly. Get in the bathtub, pansy!

\- Eat fish regularly. Just because I live on a ranch and have a surplus of beef doesn't mean I can't eat more fish!

\- BAB. Fat and Protein. Fat and Protein.

\- Leptin reset. No snacking. No late-night eating. Plenty of space between meals.

I'm going through a lot of the same things you are right now (pretty much everything minus the baby brain) and I have many of the same goals. It kills me that it's so easy for so many people and so so hard for me. I've been a terrible detox buddy lately  I'll be back on the wagon again soon...I hope. Keep if up though, you're doing awesome and your journal is inspiring!

Thanks! Yeah the ED crazy brain is hard to understand unless you've experienced it. It's like a switch that can't be shut off...I know if I keep at the BAB and protein and fat and not restrict so much, the binging will go away. I just have to forget the scale for awhile, which is so hard because I DON'T want to be a fat girl again. 177 lbs. is way to much for this small frame of mine. Talk about crazy inflammation!

Why have you been a terrible detox buddy? Are you struggling? Binging? What's going on? You can get right back on the wagon right now. Forgive yourself of your slip-ups and be kind to your body. I always have to remind myself of that. The self-loathing after a binge can be almost more destructive than the binge itself.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-21T06:18:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32626>

I don't know why, but I ate bad foods again tonight. What's my problem? All these people on here motivating me and supporting me and wanting me to do well, and I mistreat my body again. And, again. And, again. Oh, the squeaky wheel. I'm getting to the point where I don't trust myself to eat anything because I don't know if I will ever stop. Yet, not eating anything only exacerbates the problem. What is a girl to do?

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-21T16:39:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32692>

> **PaleoCowgirl;32748 wrote:** I don't know why, but I ate bad foods again tonight. What's my problem? All these people on here motivating me and supporting me and wanting me to do well, and I mistreat my body again. And, again. And, again. Oh, the squeaky wheel. I'm getting to the point where I don't trust myself to eat anything because I don't know if I will ever stop. Yet, not eating anything only exacerbates the problem. What is a girl to do?

I know - I hate that when I do it. I feel like two people - one is wise and loving and knows what she needs to do to treat her body with respect. The other is eating. All I can say, is you do it until you don't. If I can paraphrase MomaG (from memory so I hope this is the gist of it) if you have a history of restricting or doing counter-measures to "make up" for bingeing, your body doesn't trust that it will be nourished. So if you binge, you still have to BAB and feed your body and not do any corrections. Your making connections in your brain, the behavior you want will follow. May I also suggest that you stop identifying yourself with the "fat girl" who has to watch her weight and scrutinize herself. If you want to be thin, act like a thin person! Be a thin person in your mind and it will ripple down. I like what Kiwi said, be that person now.

And if all else fails, check this out:

[http://fastpaleo.com/chocolate-covered-bacon/](http://http://fastpaleo.com/chocolate-covered-bacon/)
