### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-21T18:08:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32717>

**\- Breakfast w/ 50gm of protein Yes, I had a HUGE chuck steak

**\- Breakfast w/i 30m of rising Yes

**\- 5+ hrs between Breakfast and Lunch No lunch. I slept in until late and didn't get done with BAB until 10 am

**\- 5+ hrs between Lunch and Dinner No Lunch.

**\- Finished dinner by 7 Ate dinner at 4:30 pm

**\- no snacking** Check

\- 3.5+ hrs between dinner & bedtime** Yep

\- exposure to morning temp & sun** No...

\- Darkening the house at sunset** Put my blue blockers on at 7:45 pm. Already feeling sleepy...

\-CT** Cold shower, face dunking and spot CT twice

\- 100% primal, whole 30 diet (no PUFAS)** Check

\-

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-21T19:11:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32750>

This one is for you - i found it facinating and so explains why know that I have stopped trying to stop myself, I feel so much better!

[http://www.gnolls.org/2342/restrained-eating-willpower-and-why-diets-fail-why-are-we-hungry-part-3/](http://http://www.gnolls.org/2342/restrained-eating-willpower-and-why-diets-fail-why-are-we-hungry-part-3/)

Why Dieting Makes You Fat: You’re Depending On Willpower

All this seems like an obvious, common-sense result. We know intuitively that willpower is a limited resource: we only have so much tolerance for denying our own wants. Furthermore, life is far more pleasant when we’re not living in a constant state of self-denial (or self-flagellation).

But there are even more reasons to minimize the role of willpower in a diet. These titles say it all, and I don’t even have to quote abstracts:

Am J Clin Nutr. 2001 Jan;73(1):7-12.

Cognitive dietary restraint is associated with higher urinary cortisol excretion in healthy premenopausal women.

McLean JA, Barr SI, Prior JC.

J Gerontol A Biol Sci Med Sci. 2006 Jun;61(6):628-33.

High cognitive dietary restraint is associated with increased cortisol excretion in postmenopausal women.

Rideout CA, Linden W, Barr SI.

Psychosom Med. 2010 May; 72(4): 357–364.

Low Calorie Dieting Increases Cortisol

A. Janet Tomiyama, Ph.D.,a Traci Mann, Ph.D.,b Danielle Vinas, B.A.,c Jeffrey M. Hunger, B.A.,b Jill DeJager, MPH., RD,d and Shelley E. Taylor, Ph.D.c

**In other words, restraint—using our willpower—doesn’t just use energy. It causes stress.

A Brief Note On Cortisol

Cortisol is a glucocorticoid—a class of necessary regulatory hormones whose functions and interactions are far too complicated to discuss here. However, we know that persistently high cortisol levels are strongly associated with increased stress, and that administration of exogenous glucocorticoids (the fancy term for “giving them as drugs”) actually produces increased stress, along with a litany of terrible side effects.

“Side effects of oral corticosteroids that are used on a short-term basis include: an increase in appetite, weight gain, insomnia, fluid retention, and mood changes, such as feeling irritable, or anxious.

Side effects of oral corticosteroids used on a long-term basis (longer than three months) include: osteoporosis (fragile bones), hypertension (high blood pressure), diabetes, weight gain, increased vulnerability to infection, cataracts and glaucoma (eye disorders), thinning of the skin, bruising easily, and muscle weakness.”

\-UK National Health Service

Persistently high cortisol not only makes you feel stressed, with all the nervousness, sleep disruption, and ugly side effects that entails…

…it makes you eat!**

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-21T19:25:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32754>

Some of us bingers on here noticed that we can't even handle very small amounts of carbs without it triggering a binge. Maybe try to eliminate the fruit if it sets you off. You now know more. No fruit will set you up for success next time.

> Am J Clin Nutr. 2001 Jan;73(1):7-12.
>
> Cognitive dietary restraint is associated with higher urinary cortisol excretion in healthy premenopausal women.
>
> McLean JA, Barr SI, Prior JC.
>
>
>
> J Gerontol A Biol Sci Med Sci. 2006 Jun;61(6):628-33.
>
> High cognitive dietary restraint is associated with increased cortisol excretion in postmenopausal women.
>
> Rideout CA, Linden W, Barr SI.
>
>
>
> Psychosom Med. 2010 May; 72(4): 357â€“364.
>
> Low Calorie Dieting Increases Cortisol
>
> A. Janet Tomiyama, Ph.D.,a Traci Mann, Ph.D.,b Danielle Vinas, B.A.,c Jeffrey M. Hunger, B.A.,b Jill DeJager, MPH., RD,d and Shelley E. Taylor, Ph.D.c
>
> **In other words, restraintâ€”using our willpowerâ€”doesnâ€™t just use energy. It causes stress.**
>
> A Brief Note On Cortisol
>
> Cortisol is a glucocorticoidâ€”a class of necessary regulatory hormones whose functions and interactions are far too complicated to discuss here. However, we know that persistently high cortisol levels are strongly associated with increased stress, and that administration of exogenous glucocorticoids (the fancy term for â€œgiving them as drugsâ€) actually produces increased stress, along with a litany of terrible side effects.
>
>
>
> â€œSide effects of oral corticosteroids that are used on a short-term basis include: an increase in appetite, weight gain, insomnia, fluid retention, and mood changes, such as feeling irritable, or anxious.
>
>
>
> Side effects of oral corticosteroids used on a long-term basis (longer than three months) include: osteoporosis (fragile bones), hypertension (high blood pressure), diabetes, weight gain, increased vulnerability to infection, cataracts and glaucoma (eye disorders), thinning of the skin, bruising easily, and muscle weakness.â€
>
> \-UK National Health Service
>
>
>
> Persistently high cortisol not only makes you feel stressed, with all the nervousness, sleep disruption, and ugly side effects that entailsâ€¦
>
>
>
> â€¦it makes you eat!**

Love this! Thanks for sharing. It makes a lot of sense!

### allison09 — 2012-06-22T16:52:07+0300

Coconut manna gets me every time. It's like kryptonite. Can't keep the stuff in my house...but we're not the only ones. It seems like lots of bingers have trouble with it.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-22T19:40:30+0300

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-22T19:45:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32970>

**\- Breakfast w/ 50gm of protein 96 grams of ground beef (12 oz.) and 1 T CO -- Kill them cravings!

**\- Breakfast w/i 30m of rising Yes

**\- 5+ hrs between Breakfast and Lunch No lunch. I would hope I wouldn't need lunch after a breakfast like that!

**\- 5+ hrs between Lunch and Dinner No Lunch.

**\- Finished dinner by 7 I ate dinner at 4 pm

**\- no snacking** Check

\- 3.5+ hrs between dinner & bedtime**

\- exposure to morning temp & sun** Yes, I woke up and immediately threw open my curtain and looked at the sun before getting to my BAB. Then, I spent all morning outside.

\- Darkening the house at sunset**

\-CT**

\- 100% primal, whole 30 diet (no PUFAS)** Check

\-

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-22T19:56:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32972>

> **PaleoCowgirl;33099 wrote:** Which totally sucks because I LOVE that stuff...seriously. Mix it with Libby's pumpkin, a little stevia, cinnamon and coconut flakes on top...and it's my favorite dessert!

You can make your own - just throw some coconut flakes or meat of a coconut into the food processor. If you are lucky enough to have a Vitamix, it takes about 2 minutes. Otherwise it's a real PIA - you have to stop and scrape it off the side continuously. Although I just read that someone tilted their food process by using a book and then it works better. Then you can just make a little bit at a time and since it's such a PIA, you won't be tempted to make more! LOL

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-23T00:33:37+0300

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-23T18:31:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33166>

Good luck on your consult Cowgirl - wanted to let you know I am getting much out of your postings on all this. . . I look at your photo and see how cute (and tiny!) you look, and it just helps me deal with ME better seeing that even you have some of the same eating issues (can't get full!) as someone like me.

The more bricks that get put on the wall that prove it's not some kind of weak character help me believe it.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-23T18:42:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33172>

> **Cavemam;33298 wrote:** Good luck on your consult Cowgirl - wanted to let you know I am getting much out of your postings on all this. . . I look at your photo and see how cute (and tiny!) you look, and it just helps me deal with ME better seeing that even you have some of the same eating issues (can't get full!) as someone like me.
>
>
>
> The more bricks that get put on the wall that prove it's not some kind of weak character help me believe it.

Thanks for the kind words. I often relate to your posts, as well, so thanks for sharing yourself on the forum.

As far as my consult went....tough love from Dr. K but much needed. If anyone is doubting whether a consult is worth the money (I'm very conservative with my spending), just do it. No question, the best money I have spent on this journey so far.

Now, if I can only get my husband to understand what I'm going through. I tried to share with him some of Dr. K's thoughts, and we ended up getting in a fight. He thinks I get on the internet, read a bunch of voodoo and create health problems I don't have. I'm finally admitting to myself that I have an ED, and I tried to tell him I do and that's what I'm struggling with, and he doesn't see it. He says he sees me eat all the time. I'm so frustrated. Yet, I know if I can't get him on board to support me, I still have to do this fight for myself. And the first hurdle I have to get over is my weight. I need to gain weight; I don't want to. It's fun being this cute, tiny little girl, but at what cost? Not being able to have babies? Making my binge/restrict disorder worse? As Dr. K put it this morning via Skype, I'm my own worst enemy.

Next step: get some labs, narrow down my issues and eat ALOT of fat. Dr. K gave me permission to eat a whole stick of butter in a sitting. LOL!

Have a great weekend everyone! I'm off to a wedding.

### vkiernan — 2012-06-23T19:00:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33179>

Fighting w/dh is no fun. You do know you are doing the right thing and that's what is important. Nobody will take care of you but you. Perhaps when you can make some progress your dh will finally see or maybe not. No matter what, do what is best for you. Mine supports me but still doesn't get it as he still tries to feed me grains and doesn't do anything to keep my stress down. With my AF, he is slowly killing me and doesn't have a clue no matter how much I tell him. Stick up for yourself and do what you need to. You are worth it!!!!

Are you needing tests to see what is wrong or was he able to tell you from the labs you have? I wish my consult w/been longer. I choose the 1/2 hr and no one told me to write up a history. I feel like I was sorta short-changed. But on a positive note, I do have to have more labs so perhaps the 2nd consult will be much more enlightening and maybe a good thing it was only what is was. It was still good but certainly could have been better.

The thing with Jack's knowledge is - everything he has told me so far has been confirmed by a doctor here. HE IS THE REAL THING AND WE ARE SO LUCKY TO HAVE HIM. Perhaps your dh should have some labs and see where he stands. If he eats grains and sugar, he is probably not optimal either.

I hope you really really enjoy yourself today at the wedding.

### chocolate — 2012-06-23T19:03:02+0300

Maybe you could tell you husband that you are actually needing a tune up and everything needs retimed. That's the way I see it.

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-24T22:29:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33341>

sounds like some big thinking for you PaleoCowgirl, sending you a hug...I know your hubs is probably scared...I'll be praying he comes around for you! I guess being single has it's positives and negatives - I don't have to worry about anyone else but me right now. But I do take flak from friends and family about how restrictive my diet is right now when I'm "healthy." Well, they don't know what's going on with my hormones (except when I'm a bear)! And it was Dr K's blog on "avoiding the fates of Michael J Fox and Ronald Reagan" that got me. Only in my case, it's avoiding the fate of my maternal grandmother (Alzheimer's) and paternal grandfather (Parkinson's Disease). Yes, we "aren't that sick" right now, AND we're trying to keep it that way!

I know it must be hard to hear you need to gain weight. I know that I am very unhappy with the pounds that I put on BUT I got TOM again. I know in the end, we'll be able to have the figure we want and be healthy. But it might take some patience with our bodies in the mean time. I'm trying to be more loving and accepting of myself. If I can figure out my self-worth without being tied to skinny-minny me, I'll be all set! Keep going, PC!

>
> Are you needing tests to see what is wrong or was he able to tell you from the labs you have? I wish my consult w/been longer. I choose the 1/2 hr and no one told me to write up a history. I feel like I was sorta short-changed. But on a positive note, I do have to have more labs so perhaps the 2nd consult will be much more enlightening and maybe a good thing it was only what is was. It was still good but certainly could have been better.
>
>
>
> The thing with Jack's knowledge is - everything he has told me so far has been confirmed by a doctor here. HE IS THE REAL THING AND WE ARE SO LUCKY TO HAVE HIM. Perhaps your dh should have some labs and see where he stands. If he eats grains and sugar, he is probably not optimal either.
>
>
>
> I hope you really really enjoy yourself today at the wedding.

Thank you for the kind words! And, you're right...Dr. K is extremely knowledgeable, to the point, and the more info you can give him, the more help he will be.

The wedding was a lot of fun, and then we went to my grandparent's cabin today and tanned at the lake...so a good weekend all around!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-25T06:37:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33405>

To summarize my conversation with Dr. K:

1\. Do I have hashis officially? Antibodies present isn't enough to determine. **Officially test needed.

2\. How about PCOS? Just because my glucose test indicated elevated insulin doesn't mean I have it. An ultrasound would confirm yes or no.** However, because my periods are non-existent, not just inconsistent, it leads Dr. K to believe I have...

3\. HA (hypothalamic amenorrhea) Wtf is that? Dr. K sent me three sites to check out:

[http://noperiodbaby.blogspot.com/2009/06/hypothalamic-amenorrhea-recovery.html](http://http://noperiodbaby.blogspot.com/2009/06/hypothalamic-amenorrhea-recovery.html)

[http://halfasmanychances.blogspot.com/p/hypothalamic-ameno-what.html](http://http://halfasmanychances.blogspot.com/p/hypothalamic-ameno-what.html)

[http://forums.fertilitycommunity.com/unexplained-infertility/2020186789-need-support-hypothalamic-amenorrhea-anyone.html](http://http://forums.fertilitycommunity.com/unexplained-infertility/2020186789-need-support-hypothalamic-amenorrhea-anyone.html)

Basically, it's me in a nutshell. I busted my butt to get skinny, and in the end, it shut down my signaling to ovulate. I need to research this to further understand it, but I plan to visit with my primary caregiver this week about it.

4\. Direct quote from Dr. K, "You are one of the most messed up cases I've dealt with. This is serious \*\*\*\*. You may never be able to have a baby."

5\. His solution? Eat BAB...focusing more on fat than protein. Keto-paleo. No paleo treats. Absolutely no cheating, no gluten or sugar. Do for 12-18 months and see if period resumes. Period must be regular for 6 months before getting pregnant. I need to gain 10-15 lbs. So, basically my decision to get on hcg (which elevated my testosterone) is what helped me get thin but also caused me a whole lot of problems.

6\. Stop all supplements (except probiotics). He pretty much said it's like pouring buckets of water on a burning house.

7\. Beware of any doc who ever tries to get me on clomid.

How do I feel?

Angry. Depressed. Worried. How fat do I have to get? Will I ever be able to have a baby? If so, at what cost? The weekend was a bit rocky with foods, but I survived. It's time to get serious now about getting optimal.

Dr. K said I would be my own worst enemy on this journey, and I'm thinking he's probably right. I took notes from our meeting, and I need to dig into them deeper, but for now, these are preliminary notes.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-06-25T07:16:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33411>

Period must be regular for 6 months before getting pregnant. I need to gain 10-15 lbs. So, basically my decision to get on hcg (which elevated my testosterone) is what helped me get thin but also caused me a whole lot of problems.
>

>

> Dr. K said I would be my own worst enemy on this journey, and I'm thinking he's probably right. I took notes from our meeting, and I need to dig into them deeper, but for now, these are preliminary notes.

Wow, PaleoCowgirl. That's a BIG consultation with some BIG revelations. What I hear is that you feel he is spot on - 'that's me in a nutshell' and that how this happened is making sense. I think this is really important. Because it now means that the plan is your plan, not someone else's, and it's for your specific health, not for someone else's idea of health or for weight loss.

You ask 'how fat do I have to get?' and I think that is the 'worst-enemy voice' talking. You said Dr K suggested you gain 10-15 pound. One, that is NOT fat. Two, you know how much to gain. Seems like a bit of fat on your body is important, just like fat in your food. Fat is equating with health here. And goodness. And life. Fat nourishes your body, your hormones, your baby-making abilities. There is nothing to fear here. Hurray. (You will still be beautiful with 10 extra pounds on you!)

I have just paid for my first consult (no time scheduled yet) and I can feel the nervousness in me about what may be revealed. So I can only imagine how hard it is to hear all this really tough stuff and how much is at stake. I don't have any easy answers or words of wisdom in that department, except be kind to yourself. Always. Treat yourself with the same kindness that you dole out to everyone else on this forum. Self-care is the route that will get you to optimal, not beating yourself for having so far to go.

Hang in there.

### vkiernan — 2012-06-25T14:37:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33418>

> **PaleoCowgirl;33537 wrote:** How much did you have to gain when before TOM made an appearance? I know this sounds vain...but I've bought so many cute new clothes and jeans to fit my small new body, and most I'm going to have to give away as I become a lard \*ss. Ugh. My husband was pretty supportive when I told him I needed to gain weight, as we has been telling me the same thing for months. He suggested I start lifting weights, so I don't just get squishy as I gain.

I feel really bad for you cuz I'm sure you are devastated over the thought alone. I just gained 5 lbs from the HC I've been on and I know how bad that is making me feel. But, just a couple of thoughts: 1.) Sometimes one preceives themselves differently than others. You probably do need to gain. Having to gain weight might actually make you more attractive especially to your dh (a little fat in the right places is very feminine). I always thought I wanted to be super thin but my sister is soo tiny now that I NEVER, EVER want to look like her. Can't even hug her appropriately as there is nothing to hug. 2.) Gaining some weight doesn't mean you will be a fat \*ss. Did he tell you how much weight you need to gain?

If your dh is telling you to gain, then he sees you different than you do and you probably do need to gain. I do understand about the weight gain though. No one wants to. Go slow and enjoy the ride! I can tell you that I have missed out on a lot of enjoyment because "I" thought I was too fat to wear this or that and kept telling myself when I lose a few lbs. I will dress like this. I am not fat still today but still have that screwed up mind. Why do we think no one will love us if we gain a few? I'm sure if you gain a few lbs. no one will probably notice and as for the cute little clothes, 1 size larger will still be very cute and you will still be awesome! Try not to stress over this cuz you know it will make you gain for sure, that whole cortisol thing. This really is a chance for you to get to know your body too. You will know what makes you gain and how much and how fast.

### vkiernan — 2012-06-25T14:50:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33419>

Ok, PCG, I didn't read your whole recap from your consult. WOW! If the good doc is telling you 10-15 lbs to gain, that is serious. I would not be looking at this from a weight gain stand point but more of a HEALTH standpoint. Sounds like you need to get it in gear cuz optimal isn't coming your way if you don't and worse things might be coming your way. How tall are you and how much do you weigh now? I am picturing very very thin. Fix yourself now before you get old. It is bxxxx to do it later. Trust me on that one. Still after a year, I am struggling. Hell, I am struggling to even find a doc who can deal with everything I have going on. So very frustrating.

As for how much you will have to gain? I think your body will tell you when TOM returns and stays. You have a journey but can make it. I wish you the best.

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-25T15:48:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33426>

> **PaleoCowgirl;33537 wrote:** How much did you have to gain when before TOM made an appearance? I know this sounds vain...but I've bought so many cute new clothes and jeans to fit my small new body, and most I'm going to have to give away as I become a lard \*ss. Ugh. My husband was pretty supportive when I told him I needed to gain weight, as we has been telling me the same thing for months. He suggested I start lifting weights, so I don't just get squishy as I gain.

My advice is not to focus on the number. Just eat. Eat if you are hungry (Still do BAB and three meals max a day) but don't try to "diet" and do Leptin Rx. I know my head sometimes try to determine how much I should eat instead of my body. How it happened for me was I had an emotional trigger (my bff is my same age and told me she is pregnant with baby #5) and it sent me eating like crazy. Honestly, I couldn't tell you how much I gained because I stopped weighing, but it's in the 10-15 range. I def don't recommend a week long bender as your method to gain but eat. Eat fat and protein and fish and don't "push" yourself to do just two meals a day. If you need three, then eat three. I put the weight on in April. Finally got TOM in June. I could see signs of ovulation before TOM came, so figure that as a ballpark.

And I feel your pain. Believe me. I almost cry when I put on my new clothes and they are tight or I think I look like a blob.

Here is a great article on AM and it's written by that chick you like Paleo Pepper

[http://www.paleoforwomen.com/how-extremity-can-make-even-the-best-diet-fail/](http://http://www.paleoforwomen.com/how-extremity-can-make-even-the-best-diet-fail/)

Also, I decided to follow the bulletproof exec's wife's diet for being healthy for baby. Do it now and in three years you will have rockstar baby. I KNOW it. Also she recommended this exercise called T-Tapp. I'm trying it now and I'll let you know how it goes.

[http://www.betterbabybook.com/](http://http://www.betterbabybook.com/)

And no, sweetie, you won't be a lard-arse. Be KIND to yourself. K?

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-25T16:01:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33431>

> **PaleoCowgirl;33537 wrote:** How much did you have to gain when before TOM made an appearance? I know this sounds vain...but I've bought so many cute new clothes and jeans to fit my small new body, and most I'm going to have to give away as I become a lard \*ss. Ugh. My husband was pretty supportive when I told him I needed to gain weight, as we has been telling me the same thing for months. He suggested I start lifting weights, so I don't just get squishy as I gain.

You'll have to change your thinking if you want this to work out.

None of this really has to do with "needing to gain weight" or whether you are "too thin" or not. It's about normalizing your hunger signaling, eating patterns and hormones along the way. Once they are re-established, the weight will settle wherever it needs to be for your body to be healthy.

I suggest you focus on eating properly and becoming healthy instead of the scale going up.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-25T17:35:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33451>

Question? How do I respond with multi-quotes? I clicked on the "quote +" button, but then what?

Before I respond to all of your kind words, here are a few more gems from Dr. K:

1\. I'm up to 300 mcg of t3/day, and he said the reason I'm on such a high dose is because it's all going to Rt3. **I need to get this checked.** I didn't take my evening dose last night and woke up to a temp of 98.6. Since I've been tracking my temps, I have NEVER seen my temp go that high. Not sure what that means, and I'm also night sure WHY it's all going to Rt3?????

2\. No CT. I have really struggled to do it, and he said it is only stressing my body further, so don't worry about it.

3\. He did say to focus on circadian rhythms, so I have to get more religious about getting to bed and wearing my blue blockers.

4\. Because I've been on reduced energy reserves and increased cortisol from exercising my whole life, my body is just plain tuckered out.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-25T17:37:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33452>

Thank you for the kind words. I do need to wrap myself around the idea of gaining weight. I liked how I looked last summer weighing about 5 lbs. more than I weigh right now, and I could have happily stayed there if I would have ever stabilized. Last summer, people commented on how good I looked; this summer, they seem more concerned and less congratulatory about my weight...so I guess there's my answer.

By the way, it's okay to be nervous about your consult. Dr. K asked I was I was doing, and I said I was nervous, and he said, "Well, you should be. I have some serious sh\*t to tell you." YIKES! Anyway, he does a great job of explaining that serious sh\*t, so at least I have a plan of attack. Good luck with your consult!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-25T17:44:04+0300

Thanks for the kind words. Dr. K said to gain 10-15 lbs., which is frustrating because it's almost back to my pre-hcg weight...it's like, why did I mess with a good thing, and why did I think I was so fat? I wish I had never done it, if I had known then what I do now. The trouble wasn't the hcg though, it was the underlying problems I had before it that were made worse by the stress of losing so much weight in a year.

I know what you mean though about super skinny women. Two of my closest friends are size 0, and I was always the big, corn-fed, country girl around them, and FINALLY, I was the same size as them! It felt great. It's frustrating that they can be "healthy," or then again, maybe they aren't, and I have to gain weight and be larger again to be at my optimal... this is just me feeling sorry for myself though. I need to get over it!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-25T22:55:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33526>

Turns out when you eat poorly, it makes you feel like crap. The last two months have been plagued with repeat binges, and as I detox from my latest, my head pounds, my clarity is shot, I'm unproductive, crabby and bloated. Yuck. My first instinct, of course, is to fast and eat little until I feel better, but I have a new focus on BAB with lots of fat, and I'm not stepping on the scale or worrying about it right now. My temp was up this morning and I observed some watery CM, so maybe I've ovulated. If so, I would have TOM would be right around the corner. Maybe?

Also, random question, I have some cabbage and a ton of radishes from my garden...anybody have good suggestions for recipes?

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-25T23:03:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33528>

Look for the more egg white consistency to indicate ovulation. And great plan. To get different results, you gotta do something different.

As for the cabbage, ferment your own sauerkraut! So good for leaky gut! And radishes I like to use like potato. Either boiled or with eggs like hash browns (or in ground beef). [http://balancedbites.com/2012/01/easy-recipe-raw-sauerkraut-fermented-probiotic-food.html](http://http://balancedbites.com/2012/01/easy-recipe-raw-sauerkraut-fermented-probiotic-food.html)

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-25T23:54:29+0300

Excellent! Thank you! I've always wanted to try making my own sauerkraut...maybe now's the perfect time to try it!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-25T23:58:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33537>

My doc is great! I just called her up, and I'm going to get blood drawn to officially test for hashis, as well as checking my rt3 and iron levels. This should give me an idea of where I'm at and hopefully help me understand why I'm not absorbing the t3.

Once these results are in, I also want to see her and get an ultrasound to see if I have pcos or if its ha. I'm also looking at ordering blood spot/saliva labs to check my LH, FSH and a complete female hormone profile. Is there anything else I'm missing that might prove useful to know?

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-26T18:57:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33700>

Ugh...my jeans are tight....I'm puffy and squishy. I hope my efforts to gain weight result in TOM soon. I know I shouldn't expect instant results, but this gaining weight thing is no fun, especially when I've worked so hard to take it off.

Here's a real concern I have....what if my weight continues to climb? What if I get fat again? Like really fat? How do I stop at a happy medium?

I'm just really bummed out today. I feel like everything I've done has been for nothing. Sigh...

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-26T19:20:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33702>

You're focusing on the wrong thing. You need to normalize your eating patterns. That might or might not include weight gain. Your weight isn't the problem. Your eating is. And your eating is the result of many other things I'm sure Dr K explained to you. JMHO

Start searching for health instead.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-26T19:46:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33709>

I know in my heart you are right, Souldanzer. It's just a tough reality...and a bit depressing. But, if TOM comes, I know I'm on the right track. I actually want it to reappear more than I want to be a size 0. I need to focus on that.

I'm going grocery shopping tonight, as our pantry and fridge are completely empty. Part of me wants to leave it that way, so I have nothing to binge on, but part of me knows I need to get some veggies and make some decent meals around here. Anybody have any favorite paleo dishes? I think part of my issue is I'm bored with my food, so bad stuff sounds so much better.

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-26T22:08:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33751>

> **PaleoCowgirl;33847 wrote:** I know in my heart you are right, Souldanzer. It's just a tough reality...and a bit depressing. But, if TOM comes, I know I'm on the right track. I actually want it to reappear more than I want to be a size 0. I need to focus on that.
>
>
>
> I'm going grocery shopping tonight, as our pantry and fridge are completely empty. Part of me wants to leave it that way, so I have nothing to binge on, but part of me knows I need to get some veggies and make some decent meals around here. Anybody have any favorite paleo dishes? I think part of my issue is I'm bored with my food, so bad stuff sounds so much better.

I went through it too...the fear of "will I ever stop gaining" and I DID stop gaining but it came on pretty fast in the beginning. Sometimes you have to go backward to go forward on the right track.

Recipes...yes, I agree when I get into a food rut it leads to eating off program. Eating AI can be so yummy! Do you have Dr K's cookbook?

I know lately it's been hard to keep up with trying recipes because life is so rushed, but I have a few I go to a lot. I'm eating a lot of avocado right now. I blend it with all kinds of stuff to make gauc or ice cream or pudding treats.

[http://www.thrive-style.com/2012/03/dark-chocolate-avocado-pudding/](http://http://www.thrive-style.com/2012/03/dark-chocolate-avocado-pudding/)

I love mustard in avocado with lime juice and some kickin spices. Pair that with fish and good to go.

I like this recipe for coconut curry and it's very adaptable, I've done it stovetop with shrimp and kale and squash. Mix it up:

[http://paleopot.com/2011/12/easy-paleo-crock-pot-chicken-curry-with-peppers-and-cabbage/](http://http://paleopot.com/2011/12/easy-paleo-crock-pot-chicken-curry-with-peppers-and-cabbage/)

And I love this spicy salmon

[http://everydaypaleo.com/2010/05/31/grill-it-up-salmon-two-ways/](http://http://everydaypaleo.com/2010/05/31/grill-it-up-salmon-two-ways/)

and this is a great little recipe for tuna or salmon salad, especially if you can eat eggs!

[http://everydaypaleo.com/2012/04/21/two-minute-tuna-salad/#more-4323](http://http://everydaypaleo.com/2012/04/21/two-minute-tuna-salad/#more-4323)

I also did pulled pork in the crock pot with Zevia Root Beer. Super simple

Last night I ground up liver and made meatballs. I modified this recipe according to the spices I had on hand:

[http://primalonadime.com/2012/04/22/easy-beef-liver-meatballs/](http://http://primalonadime.com/2012/04/22/easy-beef-liver-meatballs/)

(and I used a lot less liver, more like 4:1 beef to liver). I put cinnamon in some of them and liked them the best, but I love cinnamon in everything...LOL

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-26T23:15:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33760>

> **PaleoCowgirl;33896 wrote:** I do have Dr. K's book, but most of them aren't AI protocol friendly. Are there some out of there that you have tried and liked? Thanks for the recipe ideas. I'm thinking avocados sound really good right now, so I'm definitely going to try your suggestions. I just ordered two paleo cookbooks, too, for inspiration, so we will see if that helps.
>
>
>
> You always offer really kind words of support, and I appreciate it, Jennifer. From the time you started gaining weight until TOM reappeared, can I ask how much you had to gain and how long it took? I know my experience won't be the same as yours, but hopefully it might be similar.

I know you're scared, so I will answer you but I don't think you should focus on a number. Let your body go where it needs to go for healing....I gained about 2-3 pounds a week for a month/5 weeks. That was April. So roughly two months after gaining 10-15. Can't tell you for sure because after Dr K said it was ruining my adernals, I stoped the scale. Even so, you know. Your pants are tight. It sucks. But your health is more important. I believe Dr K completely when he says becoming Leptin Sensitive trumps every thing else. Just be gentle with yourself. I'm kind of confused about what the heck is going on with me now that I'm cycling again. Weird appetite and cravings. I guess, for so long I was trying to MAKE my body do what I wanted and do what I wanted at any cost. Don't war with your body. Your body will win, so better to just let it! Good luck.

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-27T00:10:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33771>

HPA amenorrhea:

> Ghrelin and Agouti both stimulate hunger but in AN, they are uncoupled from their master controller leptin, while IL-6 levels are quite elevated and **fuel the conversion of T4 and T3 to make high levels of reverse T3. This biochemical maneuver completely uncouples the thyroid, and it can no longer work well. When this occurs it directly affects the pulsatile release of GnRH and shuts off the ability to have a menses. It also destroys the LH surge that is normally associated with oocyte release monthly.
>
>
> **The high cortisol also directly affects the Hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis too.** Cortisol directly blocks 5′ deiodinase enzyme that converts T4 to T3 (this occurs in the liver). Cortisol Releasing Hormone (CRH) (seen elevated in high cortisol production states) directly blocks TSH. The implication is huge because Thyroid Stimulating Hormone (TSH) and T3, the active thyroid hormone, are inhibited quickly in this process. Immediately, any excess T4 is then shunted to reverse T3. Reverse T3 is a COMPETITIVE inhibitor to T3, the active thyroid hormone.** This basically turns off the thyroid! (Youâ€™re welcome ladies.), This is a biological switch needed to shut metabolism off in starvation mode. This is precisely what happens in starvation or in anorexia. Once T3 is turned off, no fat burning can occur at the muscles with UCP3. Remember UCP3 activation requires T4, T3 and Leptin to be working well. With high cortisol it cannot. Shutting off these things could be good biologically, if youâ€™re badly starving or if youâ€™re morbidly obese.

Your level of T3 supplementation is suspect, I'm sorry to say. Plenty of people have abused thyroid meds in an attempt to lose weight. If you're dealing with HPA amenorrhea, I'm not sure there is a point in out-supplementing the underlying issue no matter how badly your thyroid is off.

Did your doc tell you to take that much T3? Or is this self-medicating?

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-27T01:50:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33802>

I'm so sorry you have to go through this PaleoCowgirl. I know how scary it might be to be told you have to gain weight, especially after putting so much effort in losing the weight. Once you'll gain a little healthy pounds, you'll feel better and probbaly won't have that urge to binge anymore. And best of all, your body will become baby ready. It's really true that our body knows best. It won't let you have a baby unless you are healthy enough. And once you'll be, you'll realize how silly it was to stress about 10 lbs or so.

I don't think you'll get fat with more fat and BAB. Sugar would make you fat, but good fats will help hormones and your brain rewire. Lack of fat in the diet often leads to sugar cravings...

I was talking with a friend of mine today about my journey with bulimia. I was so, so obsessed about my weight, I was sure I was FAT at 125 lbs (5'5''). I screwed myself up, and now almost 35 lbs later, I am really FAT. But I feel soooo much better. Ok, I'm not optimal, but I'm so much more healthier than I was, I'm free of binging (thank's God) and I don't have to fight against myself everytime I have food in front of me. The weight botters me, I won't lie to you, but I know it will come off at the right time. I was thinking of doing HCG, but reading your story and Jennifer's makes me a little scary.

You have to do this for yourself. And as V said, you better do it while your young, it's gonna be much easier even if it's gonna be pretty long as Dr K predicted.

And thank's for dharing your story, I hope you'll continue journaling as you go through this.

k

Seriously...thank you for this. I asked Dr. K to explain why everything was going to rt3, and he asked if I had read his blogs...which I have, but I've been re-reading trying to figure it out and tadaaaa....you provided the perfect info.

I've been upping my dose everytime I start to feel hypo, and that's how high I got without feeling ANYTHING. I dropped the dose in half and feel no difference. So, it's pretty much telling me none of it is getting utilized.

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-27T04:01:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33855>

I do hope that TOM returns fast for you but try not to focus on it or you'll scare it off with your cortisol! Lol. I've been seriously at this all year and I am on estradiol from my doc. Plus I just switched from synthroid to Armour and have been on an elimination diet! And have made drastic changes to how upset I let myself get about any thing. If I feel myself starting to stress, I think is it worth my adrenals getting hurt? The answer is usually no. Here's a little quote for you (and me):

Happiness (or TOM) is like a butterfly, the more you chase it, the more elusive it is. But if you turn your attention to other things, it will come softly and sit on your shoulder.

Remember, having fun is part of Dr K adrenal Rx!!

I love this! This is exactly what I needed, as I've gone from obsessing over the scale to now obsessing when TOM will return. I have to remember yours came when you least expected it!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-27T05:22:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33875>

I asked Stefani (Paleo Pepper) for some advice about what I'm going through, and I thought I would post what she had to say, so maybe her words could help someone else, too. She blogs at Paleo For Women and has gone through similar scenarios. I really encourage anyone struggling with ED to check it out.

....I think it's both PCOS and HA. I also think that 123 is a great weight for someone who is 5'4... even a few more pounds. Does that put you around a size 3-5? **Totally sexy. Totally!

The problem is: when women lose weight, they lose estrogen production. So your estrogen levels have dropped, and apparently they have dropped lower than your pituitary gland and ovaries are comfortable with. This means that your pituitary is not getting the estrogen signaling it needs to trigger menstruation, so you have stopped menstruating. But your body is still trying in fits and starts to make it happen, which has given you the cysts. At least, I think this is possibly what has happened to you. I've seen it a fair number of times before.

**One thing that will help you is reducing any kind of stress in your life. I mean this very, very seriously. And to recover and eat as many calories as your body is asking for-- as what you experienced is pretty dramatic weight loss, and very stressful to your metabolism. Another will be to make sure you include carbohydrates in your diet. I think you might find that if you eat a meal high in carbohydrate, you will feel a bit more sexual or fertile... however that might appear to your particular body (for example, having more vaginal discharge). This is because carbohydrates spike insulin which spikes leptin, which is the hormone our fat cells normally send to the hypothalamus to assure it that we are well fed. Eating carbohydrates "tricks" the body at least a tiny bit into thinking that it has more fat on it than it actually does.

But the thing about those carbs is that they are a short term fix. **What really is the only thing I have seen consistently work for women is putting on weight. Not a ton, mind you, but a few pounds here or there, and slowly. **If you can inch up your BMI and still be within the "normal" range-- as I expect you can-- this might be your most powerful fertility tool.** That's not easy for a lot of women to hear, but it is (possibly) your truth. It is, definitely, my truth. So I know what it feels like-- I know so well.

But putting on weight... that is also just a part of eating more calories and not being restrictive in general. I bet you have seen my post on fasting for women. It's a bit deal. Fasting convinces our bodies that we are starving. I would recommend to you, therefore, to eat more, smaller meals throughout the day. Definitely. I have read multiple times on hypothalamic amenorrhea forums that women need to eat consistently throughout the day in order to recover their fertility.

So: possibly put on weight, definitely eat more calories, don't exercise more than a couple of times each week, and eat carbohydrates, at least 100g/day.**

Those are my general prescriptions, in any case.

And finally, how to stop bingeing/restricting. Well. That would take a novel, and several years. But I can tell you what the most powerful tools are: forgiveness, and love, and and a focus on the future**. Let go of the idea of a 'perfect' body, and look in the mirror as little as possible. Recognize that your eating behavior is a demon that has happened to you-- not something that you have made happen yourself-- and that when you overeat, it's not your fault.** You must forgive yourself for eating. It's not your fault. So let it go. **Think of a binge as something hurtful that has happened to you, and move forward with nourishment and forgiveness and sympathy for yourself and love rather than anger and hate. And do not restrict after a binge. I know-- it's hard not to. Let yourself eat less if you feel like eating less. But don't if you don't. **The thing about bingeing and restricting is that restricting has to stop first. Restricting necessarily begets bingeing, so unless you stop restricting first, there's a fairly good chance they will persist in you longer than you would like.

The same for bingeing goes for cravings. Forgive yourself for having them, and let them roll off, or even laugh their way off, of your shoulders. Try transferring your cravings and overeating behavior to progressively healthier and more natural foods over time. If you normally binge on cake, for example, try moving first to fruits, and then to sweet potatoes, and then carrots. If you crave cake, think: "how would a few pounds of sweet potatoes go down instead?" and see if that satisfies you just as much. It's surely just a bandaid on the overeating problem, but it's helpful for women trying to navigate these complicated waters. One step at a time.

And focus on nourishment rather than deprivation. Think of food in terms of "I can have more. Do I want more?" rather than "Will I let myself have more?"** And think of your body as the great, beautiful thing that gives you life, and that deserves your love, rather than something that should be punished for not fitting into society's norms. That's silly. Society is dumb, but your body is brilliant.**

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-27T20:07:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33998>

> Ghrelin and Agouti both stimulate hunger but in AN, they are uncoupled from their master controller leptin, while IL-6 levels are quite elevated and fuel the conversion of T4 and T3 to make high levels of reverse T3. This biochemical maneuver completely uncouples the thyroid, and it can no longer work well. When this occurs it directly affects the pulsatile release of GnRH and shuts off the ability to have a menses. It also destroys the LH surge that is normally associated with oocyte release monthly.
>
> The high cortisol also directly affects the Hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis too. Cortisol directly blocks 5′ deiodinase enzyme that converts T4 to T3 (this occurs in the liver). Cortisol Releasing Hormone (CRH) (seen elevated in high cortisol production states) directly blocks TSH. The implication is huge because Thyroid Stimulating Hormone (TSH) and T3, the active thyroid hormone, are inhibited quickly in this process. Immediately, any excess T4 is then shunted to reverse T3. Reverse T3 is a COMPETITIVE inhibitor to T3, the active thyroid hormone. This basically turns off the thyroid! (Youâ€™re welcome ladies.), This is a biological switch needed to shut metabolism off in starvation mode. This is precisely what happens in starvation or in anorexia. Once T3 is turned off, no fat burning can occur at the muscles with UCP3. Remember UCP3 activation requires T4, T3 and Leptin to be working well. With high cortisol it cannot. Shutting off these things could be good biologically, if youâ€™re badly starving or if youâ€™re morbidly obese.
>
> > My brain isn't getting this 100% yet. But I am very interested in this since I know my T3 ratio is 6%, so I was interested in supplementing T3 but if its all gonna go to rT3...hmmm...testing,right?

### BJK77 — 2012-06-28T03:21:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34084>

> **PaleoCowgirl;34014 wrote:** I asked Stefani (Paleo Pepper) for some advice about what I'm going through, and I thought I would post what she had to say, so maybe her words could help someone else, too. She blogs at Paleo For Women and has gone through similar scenarios. I really encourage anyone struggling with ED to check it out.

PCG,

Thank you for sharing this with us. I just started reading about your journey and could see myself in your posts. It actually just brought me to tears. I'm not overweight (never have been), but I've always felt like I was never thin enough, never good enough, just never enough.

I never really dieted or exercised, but I got to my thinnest a few years ago before I crashed hard. I started eating a vegetarian diet and doing an extremely intense workout program when I suddenly started to develop all my food intolerances and my health just went downhill extremely fast and yet I was happy that I was so thin! Of course I was upset and scared about the new health issues I found myself dealing with, but ironically I finally felt like my body was perfect. I was 5'6" and only about 106 lbs!! Looking back on it now I can see that I was sick in so many ways - mentally, physically, emotionally. I wasn't restricting food really. I didn't go hungry, but I didn't necessarily eat what I knew I should have been eating.

I have gained weight since then and I'm trying to look at it as a blessing, that my body is strong enough to put on some weight now and I'm hopeful this is a sign of good things to come. I know I gain super easily if I eat more than 30-40 grams of carbs in a day, so I generally limit my carbs, but I always tell myself I'm doing it to be in line with the leptin rx. Truthfully it's probably more because I don't want the weight gain.

I had my consult with Dr. K last week and he believes my main problems are my adrenals and thyroid. I just ordered some T3 to start since I was on nature-throid / synthroid previously. If I'm being honest with myself, I'm hoping that the T3 will take off a little of the weight I've put on . . . I don't want to think that way, but it's constantly there! I had a friend tell me last week that I looked great. She confided in my that last year she feared for me because I "looked like a skeleton." We have such a skewed perception of our own reality. Scary stuff!

Sorry - I'm rambling now! Just wanted to share with you that you are so not alone. I hate that I would ever think of something as superficial as weight being more important than overall health, but that's my reality. I'm desperately trying to get over that and I think I'm making headway as I know you will too. Actually just writing this out has been so therapeutic!

You will be fine PCG! You will find your way to optimal

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-28T03:29:43+0300

If I'm being honest with myself, I'm hoping that the T3 will take off a little of the weight I've put on . . . I don't want to think that way, but it's constantly there! I hate that I would ever think of something as superficial as weight being more important than overall health, but that's my reality. I'm desperately trying to get over that and I think I'm making headway as I know you will too. Actually just writing this out has been so therapeutic!

> You will be fine PCG! You will find your way to optimal

I'm sorry for your struggles! Thanks for sharing your story. Losing weight is somewhat of an addiction I'm discovering. Since I've been big my whole life, I don't know any other way than to diet! Be careful with the t3. I lost 8 lbs. in a month on it, which is how I got to my crazy low at 109. You're taller than me; I have a feeling 118 is a nice spot to be. That's what I was weighing when Dr. K told me to gain 10-15 lbs. Please be strong and don't fall into that trap again.

I'm gaining weight now, and while I know it's for the best, part of me hates it and hates myself. I feel gross and puffy. I liked feeling my ribs at night and thinking, at least I'm thin. I know that sounds sick, and I guess it is, I was in denial for so long...but if I'm honest with myself, I didn't really look good at that weight.

I'm choosing health not skinny. It's a tough choice (actually it should be an easy one), but I'm going to conquer my beast, and so can you!

Cheers to our road to optimal! Thanks again for stopping by.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-28T03:41:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34088>

Advice from Abetterme:

Don't want to lose this!!!

It's still a work in progress, and I guess it will always be, but if I could tell you what helped me, it would be those points:

1) BAB. At first, it was 85g of protein to make sure I would have any cravings. Then it was 70-ish grams, and not it's somewhere between 50 and 60g, but sometimes higher.

2) Enough fat. I can feel it when I don't have enough. I can't find any lard here, and sometimes I'm just tired of CO. I do my own ghee too, and the BPC if the best way to get my fat. When I was still craving sweet at the beginning, I was having CO even if that wasn't meal time. It's better than sugar even if I was off with the schedule...

3) I can't have nuts, coconut manna in the house. Everytime I buy some telling myself I'll be fine this time, I'm not. I bought almonds last week-end and ate them in 2 sittings. I don't consider it as binging (because before I would have ate tons of food after without being able to stop myself), but I know it's a no-go. I'm getting better with dark chocolate. I don't feel the urge to eat it all when I have some. But if I do, I don't feel bad, it's sugar free and full of fat

4)I also have a difficult time with fruits and sweet potatoes. When I buy berries, I eat them all in no time. I bought cherries yesterday. I choose the smallest size to make sure, but they're all gone. Everytime I tell myself I shouldn't but any anymore. Same with sweet potatoes. If I start and have a few, I'll want more and more.

5)Over the last year and a half, I've learned to listen to satiety and fullness signs. This one is difficult and it takes times. Most of the time I still eat 3 meals a day, but I listen to myself especially at lunch time and dinner. Lunch is always pretty small (protein and avocado), but I have it anyway because if I get too hungry in the afternoon, I might eat whatever come in sight and eat beyond fullness. You're hungry when you feel it in your stomac, not when you want to eat. If you are not sure, you can have water and wait a little. If you are not hungry for a steak, you probably are not hungry, period. When you eat and food doesn't sound as tasty as it was when you began, it's a sign you are not hungry anymore. You have to listen carefully and not eat too fast.

6) I'm not as hard on myself as I used to be. That feels good I'm not perfect, and I don't have to be. Sometimes I eat stuff I shouldn't, but that's ok, I'll go back on track on the next meal. I've always had a hard time not eating at night when not sleeping. Those last 2 weeks, I can't go to sleep before 3 o'clock in the morning, and I get hungry at midnight or one. Sometimes I eat, sometimes I don't. I now I shouldn't, but I don't beat myself up with that and I get up with my BAB the next morning.

7) I stopped weighting myself. Scale is only a number, I don't want to focuss my life on that anymore. I want to be healthy and strong. I go by how I feel, and not by how I weight.

8) Learning that binging is not psychological or emotional, but physiological. If I want to binge, it's not because I'm stressed/sad/angry. It's because there is something unbalanced inside me. I wish I knew that before!! Usually if I want to binge it's because I lack sleep, I haven't had enough fat or I had sugar and I want more and more!! Understanding what is happening is helping me a lot!

9) Journaling. It help me a lot. And couting the binge-free days too when I started. I wa smotivated by the number going up and didn't want to go back to zero. Now I've lost count, I have to go to previous messages to do the count. I know I'm approching the 100 days mark, and I'm so proud of it. I know however that I'm still vulnerable to a binge, and I'll probably will be for a while, but seeing the progress is really great.

It's not vain to want to fit in tiny clothes or be as thin as your friends. I know you didn't chose that over health and having a baby. But I know it's a constant battle. When I was in that circle, I thought I was having the control on my body. But at the end, I was always losing. I wasn't happy even if I was thin, because I was obsessing with food, the scale, staying away from social events, isolating myslef from my friends and familly. I was always feeling tired, stressed, puffy and gross. Talk about having control! I was also ao angry at myself, I coudn't see the end of that. I just wanted to be normal again! I sometimes wonder where I would be now if I didn't start the leptin reset protocol. I would be in some miserable place beating myself up and praying for some help. If you change your goal from "being thin" to "being healthy", at some point the size of your clothes won't matter to you anymore because you'll feel good about yourself and your body.

Of not, maybe hormone?? Time will tell.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-28T18:26:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34181>

> **PaleoCowgirl;34298 wrote:** Could I be making a recovery so fast? The cravings are dead, dead. My energy is good...but I am super moody -- I mean really irritable. What's up with that?

I have no explanation for it but I have felt that way too, a few times. Althought the irritability is not nice, I feel like I'm on top of the world with having the cravings dead!!!!

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-28T19:04:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34188>

> **Jack Kruse;34300 wrote:** your thinking improves your body awfully fast when you let your cells heal your thought via your new actions..........cellular health dictates your thinking.......always.

Just read this over at Whole 9 - pretty much the same thing Dr K is saying - our gut flora is protective of our ability to deal with stress! BIOLOGY influencing psychology

Prevention of gut leakiness by a probiotic treatment leads to attenuated HPA response to an acute psychological stress.

Translation: Improving gut barrier integrity by improving the balance of gut microbiota (bacteria) leads to reduced reactivity when faced with psychological stress. In other words, a happy, intact gut improves your stress "resilience". The gut, the immune system, the neurological system, the adrenals... it's all the same story.

[http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0306453012001291](http://http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0306453012001291)

### nonchalant — 2012-06-28T22:51:31+0300

Hi PaleoCowgirl, glad you're making progress. My cravings have also taken a dive recently. I credit the seafood challenge. And I am losing some of the Spring-fruit pounds I gained recently.

I also became quite impatient recently. Extremely impatient. Almost viciously so. I backed off on the L-Tyrosine I was taking, and my mood evened out. I even find myself smiling at mistakes, seeing the humor again. Whew!

\-Sally

This is such a good reminder. They are going to be on Underground Wellness tonight. I plan on listening in!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-29T18:26:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34371>

Good morning, everyone! I'm battling some demons yet...I don't know why I'm so in denial about fruit, but I CAN'T have any, plain and simple. Same for carrots...it triggered my sweet tooth yesterday...go figure. I gotta stay away from stevia, too. And, I had requested coconut manna at my health store a long time ago, and they called me yesterday to let me know it was in. Yeah, I'm NOT going to go get that either, even though I love it, unless I love binging. Which, I don't. I've got to kill the sweet tooth and never invite it back into my life. It's trouble.

I've been feeling pretty down on life, not very impressed with the hand of cards God has dealt me. I've been depressed, crabby and not myself since my consult with Dr. K...probably because the light finally clicked on, and I finally listened to advice that has been given to me over and over again that I never wanted to hear. Anybody ever do insanity workouts or p90x with Shawn T? Shawn T is a motivator in the tough love kind of way, and it works for me....Dr. K is the Shawn T of the health world, and apparently I needed a kick in the butt.

I recently read the book, "Heaven is for real," and the Dad fights with God while his son was being operated on in a life or death scenario. He whined to God and yelled at him and asked why he deserved any of this, and the son told him that God heard him and answered his prayers. I've been wrestling with God this week, and while part of me wants to quit this fight completely, I know I can't.

So...I'm putting my boxing gloves on, and I've going to kick this battle square on its a\*\*. I've made some poor food decisions lately in my anguish, and that's probably why I'm so miserable...so it's back to keto-paleo....no messing around...but I'm not going to restrict either.

I want optimal, and it starts today.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-29T18:33:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34372>

you do P90X and you will hurt yourself and your brain.........you need to back off. Discuss this with your Reproductive Endo and I bet you'll get read the riot act.

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-29T18:36:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34373>

PCG: Nothing has changed about where you are in your life. You just see a little bit clearer now. Use that knowledge to your advantage. Anger can be a very powerful motivator.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-29T18:38:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34374>

I love the fact you all help each other.......I absolutely love it.

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-29T18:41:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34376>

I couldn't eat carrots either during certain parts of my recovery. Don't sweat it. You now know more. Keep collecting information.

Practice Ahimsa with yourself.... been reading and thinking about this today myself. Hard exercise qualifies as self-harm in your case. Gentle walking can sometimes take the edge off the need to move hard for me.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-29T19:05:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34387>

Good luck PCG! You do sound determined to knock the negative thinking & behaviour in the butt! You will do it. You mentioned p90x. I used to do it a couple of years ago & am reminded of it everytime I walk into my living room (hence the chin-up bar in the doorway)! I actually love the fact that I \*can't\* exercise during the reset. Absolutely love it!!! I hope when the time is right I'll \*want\* to again but for now I am savouring every moment of the Reset Rx that prescribes No Exercise!!!

As I think about it, some of my thinking has gradually been changing. Like I just mentioned, I love not exercising, love eating rib steak as often as I want, love eating my fatty BAB's, etc. I may not be seeing results on the outside or on the scale because there's probably a mess inside of me that's I'm not aware of yet but I know things are going in the right direction. I think the more you \*do\* the right things the more your thinking will align and vice versa. You will get there!!!

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-29T19:15:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34393>

> Shawn T is a motivator in the tough love kind of way, and it works for me....Dr. K is the Shawn T of the health world, and apparently I needed a kick in the butt.

Contemplate that you and your body might need the exact opposite.... gentleness. You don't feel well, you now know that you \*are not\* well.... maybe it's time to stop fighting and relieve the dissonance within yourself. Who are you really kicking/hurting when you put on those boxing gloves?

You've got to let it all go.... "going to pieces without falling apart".... surrender to what is..... start where you are. You don't need to fix it all in one day. You don't need to be perfect today. Just set one little goal that you know you can reach successfully. It takes a while to build a stable base to build your life on.

Be gentle. Learn to be in unison with your body again. It's giving you a whole lot of information about what it needs every day. Learn to listen to what it is telling you again. Gentle. You've already tried the forceful way and it didn't get you to where you want to be....

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-29T19:19:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34395>

I did p90x pre-paleo...like in 2010. The only workouts I do now are farm work.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-29T19:25:33+0300

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-29T19:35:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34407>

I think lifting weights (in my case...throwing square bales on the farm LOL) or walking are great forms of exercise that don't stress the body out.

Awe! I grew up on a dairy farm; a family farm type, not a factory farm kind! We drank lots of raw milk back then!!!

### MartiD — 2012-06-29T19:35:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34409>

Mandy, I know you can pull though all this! Find something that makes you happy or at peace. I'm so envious that you are on a cattle ranch. I've told you that I was raised on a cattle ranch in OK, and although I don't think I appreciated it at the time, looking back I know that is what helped me battle family issues. I would get on my horse or take a walk with my dog and wonder through acres and acres of our land. I would play with the baby calves, enjoy the outdoors, smell fresh (mostly!) air, play in the pond, etc. I never watched TV, mostly because we couldn't get any channels where we were so my best friends were my irish setter, my white horse and a goat named Billy! What I would give for those days again. What I would give to have my kids have that type of a life!

Everyone is right about letting go of the anger and disappointment. Re-direct your energy on something positive. You will heal much faster I believe. Stay strong girl!

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-29T19:37:18+0300

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34412>

Perhaps you're both right...I just need to relax and go with the flow...but what I'm fighting is the part of me that just wants to quit altogether, that tells myself that I'm not worth it, and I'm tired of dealing with all this. That part needs to go away.

I love how you're working through this instead of stuffing it down. May I suggest that you love all parts of yourself, not just the parts that cooperate? The part of you that wants to quit is like a little kid that wants love but instead is making a whole lotta ruckus. Think of how you would respond to this part of you if it was someone else, or even your own child. It's trying to tell you that you're tired of fighting. No more fighting. I know how it is. You want to DO something and sometimes there is nothing to do but BAB, protein and fat and wait for the healing to work things out. Kind of boring...LOL

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-30T01:02:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34469>

Perhaps you're both right...I just need to relax and go with the flow...but what I'm fighting is the part of me that just wants to quit altogether, that tells myself that I'm not worth it, and I'm tired of dealing with all this. That part needs to go away.

How about not fighting any part of you? Like Souldanzer says, the anger has intelligence. So does the part of you that wants to quit. No one 'part' of you has all the answers, but they all have a voice. When you fragment yourself and listen to only part of you, you end up in a mess (eg listening to the part that says being thin is the prize you're aiming for). You can allow any part of you to speak up. Then the greater you, the whole you, can then make a decision re: your next step. Personally, I don't think being perfect is ever the goal. Being whole and healthy and completely you is the goal - as soon as you are untrue to or unkind to yourself, you stop leading your life fully.

Dr K gave you the kick in the pants that actually pushed open the doors so you could get enough light to see the big picture. Seeing the whole thing - in its full chaotic glory - is scary. Which is why so many people would rather follow bad advice or have small goals. You've got bigger fish to fry. But you don't have to do it all at once.

Every choice you make - every one - either drains your energy or adds to it. Sometimes the simplest things are the biggest because they contain the blueprint of your whole life. I think it's easy to overlook that and think you must have some big plan all figured out. We're all muddling though finding our way. Hang in there.

A question I have, and maybe it's naive, but humor me: if I'm not cycling, what happens to all the build-up inside? If the eggs never flush, I assume I do get cysts, but what are the repercussions of these cysts, and how can they be flushed out?

### allison09 — 2012-06-30T23:16:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34676>

> **PaleoCowgirl;34753 wrote:**
>
>
>
> A question I have, and maybe it's naive, but humor me: if I'm not cycling, what happens to all the build-up inside? If the eggs never flush, I assume I do get cysts, but what are the repercussions of these cysts, and how can they be flushed out?

I'm no expert, but I haven't been cycling as well, for a while. I went to the Dr. a while ago and got a bunch of test done. She saw the ultra sound and told me that there was no build up. She then went on to tell me that because there was no build up, there's no reason to be concerned that I'm not cycling. I'm not sure I buy that, but maybe you're not accumulating any build up as well?

### Sem — 2012-07-01T01:36:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34696>

PCG - I've been a lurker here and just wanted to say hi. I can relate to some of your struggles. I've never had the best relationship with food myself and my hormones have been a mess for years. I did have every big shot fertility doctor in Beverly Hills tell me I would never get pregnant again after my first son was born due to premature ovarian dysfunction. I went to Accupuncture and took some DHEA ( totally willy nilly so not necessarily recommending it). I ended up pregnant the old fashioned way. When my doctor delivered my second son via c-section she called him a "miracle" due to the state of my ovaries. You are probably much younger than I was then (I was 37) so I feel really good that you can get it together for yourself and any future babies. Good luck.
