Go be nice to the clerk, go open doors for people, go smile and have a conversation with someone who needs it - enjoy the \*\*\*\* out of that damn yummie and get out of the cycle of beating you up for it. If you don't leave the farm - do something unexpected and nice for your husband. Neighbor. Dog - whatever. You learn to give to yourself by understanding the pleasures and joy of giving to others.

Thanks! I love your two pieces of advice! So true! What started my binge last Friday before my sister's shoot was stress. I had a speaking engagement that day, plus all these people coming to my house for the shoot, and my sister was acting like a diva. I was also invited to help can string beans with the Hutterite women who live close to us, and they offered me an iced coffee with whipped cream on top. I haven't had a coffee beverage like that in 6 months, so I was in heaven. (In their culture, it's really really disrespectful to turn down their gifts. They sent me home with rolls, fresh jelly and some beef...which of course, I slathered some jelly on the rolls and it all went down hill from there.) By the time the photo shoot came around, my stomach was distended, and I was miserable. So, I put on some spanx and sucked it up, watching other girls put on dresses that used to fit me and no longer did. Of course, I'm incredibly inflamed from the gluten. I checked the scale and saw I dropped 10 lbs. of water weight over night. Once I saw the pics from the shoot though, I was immediately depressed. I looked so puffy and gross. Now, I'm admitting I need to gain weight, but not the way I have. I recommitted myself to the reset over the weekend, with my husband to back me up, and once this inflammation is gone, I have a feeling I will have a whole new healthy outlook on life and a chance to put on weight the right way. I have a feeling that the dress for my wedding will fit too once I take away all these inches of bloat.

> **Jennifer;38197 wrote: Lots of stuff to really think about and digest. Funny there are a lot of metaphors for thinking related to food. Clue?
>
> Any way.
>
> The more you think about and try to control yourself , the worse it gets. My gut tells me your avoiding something and all this is a good distraction. Cavmam has good advice there. Time to clear out the BS voices in your head so you can hear the small voice inside of you that's trying to get your attention. I think it's great your hubby has stepped up. Your having a war of control with yourself. Maybe it's time to lean on him. Either way. I know how horrible it is to feel out of control. IMHO All you can do is hottie your knees and admit you can't do it alone. And fat and protein
>
>
>
> Xoxo
>
>
> Yeah, I'm not sure what I'm avoiding, but I do know this ED has made me completely irrational about social situations with friends. I had a heart to heart with one of my girlfriends to tell her what I was going through, and having had an ED herself, she really understood and suggested that we get together for a movie or something instead of going to eat. I reached out to another friend too, and we plan to go to the rodeo this weekend. I WILL NOT BAIL!
>
>
>
>
>
> **Jennifer;38198 wrote:** And eat more seafood!!**

**

Fresh-caught crappie is on the menu tonight. I've also got seafood around, and I'm going to try to eat it at least every other day, even though I would MUCH prefer pork and beef.

Souldanzer;38489 wrote:** Great step to remove the trigger foods from your home. My now ex-husband had to live with these challenging circumstances for a while. I also gave up all credit cards and cash for a year or so and periodically thereafter so I could not go to the store and buy junk.

None of this eliminated the binging but it established the right environment for me to take other measures to stop it. I had to think before I acted.

I still occasionally binge these days and I choose to focus on the good days I had in between instead. There is many more healthy days now than bad days and the bad days are starting to fade. Cold and seafood was the most important thing for me to stop the binging along with Leptin rx. Binging takes a while to stop. Make sure you keep eating right and when you do binge, it's helpful to try to stick to AI foods as to not mess up your gut again.

You won't fix this overnight. An ED takes 10 years to recover from for most. Hopefully, Dr K's work might speed this up a bit.

Yesterday I ate more than I probably should have, but it was all okay foods -- coconut flakes (which I threw out to make sure I didn't overindulge on them again) but also pork loin and bacon. I wouldn't call it a binge, but I did eat past the hunger signal. The fact that I didn't go over to my mom's and plow through her junk food though is a step in the right direction.

I ended up having a great weekend with my husband -- laughing, watching movies, holding hands, working in the garden together, etc. It was so good to reconnect and know I have someone to support me through it all. Even if he doesn't get it completely, he knows I've been hurting, and is finally starting to come around and listen.

Today, I'm grateful for the return of my libido, my great husband, the sunshine and my positive attitude.

I'm praying for a shot of rain to help the crops and the hay fields, strength in my food decisions and to continue to feel good about myself.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-20T01:34:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-39512>

I went to the doc today, and she wants to test me for diabetes. She also ordered a pelvic ultrasound to be done to confirm IF I actually have PCOS or not. If I do, she wants to put me on metformin for fertility and controlling my insulin levels. Anybody have experience with this?

Here are my latest labs. Hackers, can you take a look?

FEIBC Panel

Iron 53 (37-170 mcg/dL)

TIBC 401 (250-460 mcg/dL)

FE Sat% 13 LOW (15-40%)

TSH 0.00 LOW (0.49-4.67 uIU/mL)

FT4 0.60 LOW (0.7-1.85 ng/dL)

FT3 3.2 (2.4-4.2 pg/mL)

RT3 63 Low (90-350pg/mL)

Ratio .05079 (Doc says needs to be .2 or greater)

I was expecting my rt4 to be sky-high because I was taking 300 mcg of t3 with no ill effects, and dr. k indicated it was probably all going to rt3. Not sure what this all means.

Help?

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-20T16:41:55+0300

4 Days binge free. This week is going great. I think I've got a handle on things...seafood (which tastes more like medicine than food to me) is really changing my thinking about the fuel I put in my mouth.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-20T17:06:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-39674>

I'm thinking more about my doctor's appt. yesterday and wanted to also mention that I told her about my consult with Dr. K and how he suspected HA, not PCOS. She said she was going to read up on both the HA and Dr. K...I think this will be really good, if she can check out his stuff and see where I'm coming from.

On another note, she said I'm at a much better weight now to get my period back and conceive. This is positive reinforcement that I'm on the right track, even though I'm super bummed at the way I put the weight on. My fertility friend chart predicts my period will come on Monday...I'm not holding my breath, but here's hoping.

Also, I've been lifting 5 lb. dumbbells whenever I walk by them (I leave them on the dining room table, so I see them often) Anyway, I'm trying to regain some of the muscle I lost, and my husband saw me doing it, and he sat down with me and showed me all kinds of lifts from his high school days. It's fun to be eating with him and now "working out" together. It's nice to have a teammate.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-24T01:22:01+0300

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-24T17:28:58+0300

I would always choose optimal for baby...not sure what it means that I'm willing to be less than optimal when it's just me.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-24T22:18:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-40817>

I wonder if it's my ED that caues me to be so emotionally insane or what it is. When I'm on a binge, even my family notices that I'm on a sugar high...lots of energy, lots of bubbling personality...then the crash comes. And, I'm in a deep depression, nothing matters, I don't want to go anywhere or do anything. I'm in that funk right now; my husband asked me today when I'm going to get my zeal for life again.

Then, when I'm firmly in ketosis, I have no emotions at all. Not happy, not sad, just there. Is that the way to live? Yet, as soon as I add carbs into my diet, I get cravings and want more, more, more. I'm to the point where I don't even trust a little vegetable like cucumbers. How lame.

What is the solution to this? I'm reading the Mood Cure right now, but I'm not keen on trying to fix my problems with tons of supplements. I've been supplementing like crazy the last year, and it seems like there has got to be a more natural solution...I know epi-paleo, but that still leaves me emotionally flat. Anyone else experience this?

>

>
> Then, when I'm firmly in ketosis, I have no emotions at all. Not happy, not sad, just there. Is that the way to live? Yet, as soon as I add carbs into my diet, I get cravings and want more, more, more. I'm to the point where I don't even trust a little vegetable like cucumbers. How lame.
>
>
>
> What is the solution to this? I'm reading the Mood Cure right now, but I'm not keen on trying to fix my problems with tons of supplements. Anyone else experience this?

I totally know exactly what you are talking about with the emotionally flat thing. I guess after years of crazy yo yo emotions, I sort of welcomed it. I finally feel sane, large and in charge! >

I'm just sick of relating my self-worth to the scale. I'm in a wedding this weekend, so it's crunch time to fit into that dumb dress. I'm hoping I don't fall into AN tendencies this week in preparation for it, but on Friday I binged and purged again and haven't eaten since, so it's fair to say I'm in ED mode again, which certainly isn't going to help me get my cycle back, help me get pregnant, help me heal this issue I have...sigh.....I should really go eat a bucket of shrimp and get over it. Even though I'm having some trouble s though, I do feel better at a higher weight. I've accepted it and am starting to be okay when I look at myself in the mirror. I'm working on confidence issues, but I will get there.

Haha! I do enjoy reading about your boy adventures! I live vicariously through you single ladies. I know a lot of cowboys if you're interested in the ranch life....unlimited access to beef, lots of grassy pastures, no neighbors, Wrangler jeans... ;

Affirmations: This is exactly what I've been working on. Trying to find what's beautiful...what I like about myself, the great things about my body, the fact that I'm getting stronger...

I do a little photography on the side, and I took maternity pictures this morning of a couple. It's exciting for them, but there is still this fear in the back of my mind that it's not in the cards for me. Let's hope I'm wrong. I WILL have a healthy baby someday. I WILL get my period again. I WILL maintain a healthy weight that allows my body to be at optimal. REPEAT. REPEAT.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-31T22:19:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-42106>

For some people A1c goes up after going very low carb for a while. Mine is at 5.4 and I do consider myself pre/diabetic. It appears though that with continued following of Kruse's program, my fasting BG has finally come down (last was 76! very proud of myself!!) this will eventually go away. It's called physiological insulin resistance.

> **Jennifer;42366 wrote:** any idea what your fasting blood glucose is, Mandy?

I still have the saliva test for adrenals and fh and lsh, but I've been eating so poorly lately, I don't really think it's worth measuring right now. I'm going to wait until I've been working at optimal for awhile, with no mess-ups before I measure those.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-31T23:04:25+0300

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-08-01T21:47:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-42333>

Holy smokes! Talk about hormone imbalance. When my thyroid was not functioning, I would get so angry and upset at the little things. I would throw a temper tantrum and cry. I would get so mad I could hardly see straight. Thankfully, I haven't had that in a really, really long time...until today. We were outside working with cattle, and my sister let a calf back that she wasn't supposed to. I immediately got so mad I was seeing stars, and I started bawling and had to leave. Of course, I realize it's ridiculous behavior, but jeeze, where did that come from? I think this happens when I don't have enough fat in my diet. Better increase it. That and fish. Blech, wish I liked it as much as I like my beef, pork and lamb.

As I sip some ice tea and try to relax (and get my work done today), I thought I would write today's goals:

1\. Laugh. Seriously. I need to lighten up. I hardly smile or joke around or enjoy myself anymore. I have put in an old video -- Blue Collar Comedy Tour. Maybe that will make me chuckle. Anybody have any secrets to laughing? Any good movies that just crack you up?

2\. Do yoga. I want to be flexible, strong and lean again. I don't want to be pudgy. I want to be at a healthy weight, but I also want muscles. I've got Jillian Michael's yoga DVD, and I'm determined to do it.

If I can accomplish laughter, relaxation, increased fat and yoga today, I'm going to call it a win.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-02T03:27:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-42412>

> **Jennifer;42668 wrote:** Full moon is wacking me out too! I'm all over the place. Hang in. Like ur list

You think it's the full moon? Haha! Honestly, I think what's whacking me out is restricting my eating this week. I'm about 2 inches away from zipping my dress up all the way for this dumb wedding. I should just go get the bridesmaid dress altered, but I'm really looking forward to just eating and not worrying about it and let my weight fall where it may. This wedding is causing too much stress, which is really dumb.

On a plus note, my yoga session was sooo relaxing! And, the redneck jokes made me laugh. ANDDDDD......it's RAINING! This drought is killing our crops and pastures! HURRAY for rain!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-08-03T23:18:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-42761>

I'm 5 days binge free!

I'm on day 3 of yoga, and it feels great to stretch, meditate and relax. I'm going to keep doing it several times each week.

I stocked up on some fertility supps: maca, red clover, red raspberry leave...I plan to take those supps with a B complex, vitex, DHA and probiotics. I've increased by prog. and estriol creams, too.

I'm adding a lot more fish to my diet. I just bought scallops, shrimp, salmon and a treat...lobster tail!

My bridesmaid dress ZIPS up for tomorrow. Yippee! Here's hoping I don't crash and burn tonight at the rehearsal supper or anytime tomorrow. Going to get back on track once I'm home from this event. Wish me luck!

It appears though that with continued following of Kruse's program, my fasting BG has finally come down (last was 76! very proud of myself!!) this will eventually go away. It's called physiological insulin resistance.

Robb Wolf talked about this...apparently when you are keto/paleo, your blood cells do not die off as quickly like they do when you eat a lot of carbs and sugar...so the A1C actually goes up as those blood cells are not dying off...so you have to look at other things like fasting blood glucose etc to know where you are at....

# Thread: showering after ct
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/showering-after-ct.1939/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### msclman — 2012-06-19T00:21:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/showering-after-ct.1939/post-32019>

did 80 minutes in 56 degree water yesterday. my wife used to be on the zone and we have all those reusable ice packs so i loaded up the tub with those and all the ice in my freezer. water got down to 55.6. i tried holding the packs against my stomach but that was a little brutal

anyway when i get done is it bad to take a hot shower after? just feels so good. if i get out without doing that it takes forever to get my temp up again.

### kathylu — 2012-06-19T02:27:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/showering-after-ct.1939/post-32049>

> **msclman;32128 wrote:** did 80 minutes in 56 degree water yesterday. my wife used to be on the zone and we have all those reusable ice packs so i loaded up the tub with those and all the ice in my freezer. water got down to 55.6. i tried holding the packs against my stomach but that was a little brutal
>
>
>
> anyway when i get done is it bad to take a hot shower after? just feels so good. if i get out without doing that it takes forever to get my temp up again.

Believe it or not, I use the time after the bath to do a cold shower! You are already acclimated, so it actually feels comfortably warm. That way I can extend my cold exposure a bit. Call me crazy.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-19T02:29:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/showering-after-ct.1939/post-32050>

crazy smart is more like it

### msclman — 2012-06-19T04:00:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/showering-after-ct.1939/post-32061>

> **kathylu;32158 wrote:** Believe it or not, I use the time after the bath to do a cold shower! You are already acclimated, so it actually feels comfortably warm. That way I can extend my cold exposure a bit. Call me crazy.

how long have u been ct'ing?? i dont dip my head in the tub so a cold shower would be tough but i will try it. and report back

### msclman — 2012-06-19T05:58:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/showering-after-ct.1939/post-32092>

so i did a 65 minute in 57 degree tonight and took a cold shower after. not as bad as i though it would be.

### TerrierMom — 2012-06-26T08:20:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/showering-after-ct.1939/post-33617>

You get to the point where it just feels great to be in a cold shower. Like drinking a nice cold glass of water feels on a hot day, but on the outside all over you, like aaaaahhhhh (in a good refreshing way, not a holy smokes that's cold kind of way!)

Cold water on my head to wash my hair always felt good before, but my skins couldn't take it running down my back until I worked at cold-adapting. Now I don't enjoy hot showers anymore, but oh how I enjoy cold ones!!

### MartiD — 2012-06-26T19:46:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/showering-after-ct.1939/post-33708>

I usually start with a cold shower, wash my body/hair, etc and then slip into a cold bath. I find it much easier that way! I have been putting in frozen 2 ltr bottles to help lower water in bath. After I get settled I usually hold the bottle in between my legs for a few minutes at a time. It's really cold being directly on skin so I don't leave long but I figure it might help with inner thigh fat!

### youthfuliz — 2012-06-26T21:22:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/showering-after-ct.1939/post-33734>

> **MartiD;33846 wrote:** I usually start with a cold shower, wash my body/hair, etc and then slip into a cold bath. I find it much easier that way! I have been putting in frozen 2 ltr bottles to help lower water in bath. After I get settled I usually hold the bottle in between my legs for a few minutes at a time. It's really cold being directly on skin so I don't leave long but I figure it might help with inner thigh fat!

Good idea! I'll try doing that... I have bottles of water frozen waiting in my freezer...

As to cold shower, that's the only kind of shower I can do now. Can't tolerate warm showers any more. Right now the tap water comes out at 62F. In March and April it was 52F. Not sure about Jan. Will find out this coming winter. Cold shower is very good for facial skin... if you want another incentive to do it.

### msclman — 2012-06-27T07:01:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/showering-after-ct.1939/post-33885>

# Thread: 7 Months Pregnant and doing Leptin RX-Am I crazy?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-months-pregnant-and-doing-leptin-rx-am-i-crazy.1940/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### walkers345 — 2012-06-19T01:02:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-months-pregnant-and-doing-leptin-rx-am-i-crazy.1940/post-32031>

I'll be 28 weeks on Wednesday. About two years ago, I started seeing a naturopath and had my labs done including sex hormones. They showed that I had very low in Free T3,T4, high TSH, Low Vitamin D, and slightly elevated CRP. I started Paleo/Primal 80/20 a few months after that, along with Vit D supplements, Naturothyroid 1.5grains, Omega 3, and Vitamin C. My next set of labs 6 months later were GREAT. Everything within normal limits, EXCEPT Thyroid. I was still too low, so we upped my naturothyroid to 2 grains. The following labs were even BETTER. I felt wonderful, but still suffered from major sugar benders and then...I got pregnant.

My first trimester I ate EVERYTHING! I was binging on SUGAR and GRAINS 3x a week. Gained 12 lbs in the first three months. I have always fought sugar binges and I think I just let loose and gave myself some sort of free pass! So DUMB..But I moved on. About 4 months in, I began Paleo/Primal again.. Started feeling so much better. Anyways, I started the Leptin RX 2 weeks ago. I definitely had a few days of "carb flu" but it only lasted a few days. The problem is, I just had my labs done one week into my reset. I have never had a problem with Cholesterol, but my total cholesterol is 273, triglycerides 164, free T3 1.7 (low) Free T4 .5 (low), CRP 1.9 (Higher than ever). My non-fasting glucose was 79, with A1C 5.4. Another random low number was Bilirubin at .1 (Low). My question is, should I be doing the LR now? I just want what's best for my unborn baby girl and both or our long term health. Thanks for your input. My blood pressure 105/65 or within 5 or that.

Stephanie

### walkers345 — 2012-06-19T01:10:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-months-pregnant-and-doing-leptin-rx-am-i-crazy.1940/post-32033>

I also forgot to mention that my WBC were high-11.2 (normal 4-11).

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-19T02:26:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-months-pregnant-and-doing-leptin-rx-am-i-crazy.1940/post-32048>

You need to elimate all grains and all sugar......now. The last trimester is where your baby gains its weight to support its brain. Make sure your Vitamin D level and progesterone are solid......this determines how good or bad your babies synapse are.......and my best advice. Wear seafood out until you child is 2 and make sure they are connected to the tit for a long time to offset that first 28 week maternal diet.

### walkers345 — 2012-06-19T07:20:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-months-pregnant-and-doing-leptin-rx-am-i-crazy.1940/post-32103>

Thank you Dr. K! I upped my Vit D to 5000 today. Grains have been out for about 2 months. Honey has been out for only 2 weeks. Does this mean I should lower my fat intake at my BAB? I have been doing Bacon, eggs, sausage which has kept me full until 1 or 2pm when eating at 6 or 7am. I definitely plan on breastfeeding as long as I can(1 + year, hopefully).

Question about seafood. I love it, but am wary. I had my mercury levels tested about 1.5 years ago, and they were elevated. I am a dentist and was questioning amalgam exposure. It was elevated, so I cut out fish, added lots of Vit C and fish oils, and got into a low range. Those were the only changes I made. I don't think amalgam was the culprit, I think it was the amazing sushi diet I was eating once a week. I have added in Salmon/tuna during my pregnancy sparingly, but am afraid to go full throttle..what are your thoughts? Thanks so much.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-19T14:46:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-months-pregnant-and-doing-leptin-rx-am-i-crazy.1940/post-32115>

Eat the fish....not to worry.....and eat the fat. Iw ould back off on the bacon for the baby.......the baby needs DHA fat first and foremost

### walkers345 — 2012-06-19T19:06:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-months-pregnant-and-doing-leptin-rx-am-i-crazy.1940/post-32174>

Thank you Dr. K and Shijin, I will back off on the bacon. I am cooking my eggs in coconut oil, and taking DHA and Omega 3's. I will up the fish. I love it! Still doing great with a BAB. Will have to change it up to keep full for as long as I am and keep the cravings down. Maybe, by just upping the coconut oil, adding some tuna and taking out the Bacon and sausage, I can still be satisfied.

I do notice even if I have 1/2 a sweet potato, like last night, I am hungry after dinner. Should I go above 50 carbs during this last trimester so as to not be in ketosis? Is is bad for the baby?

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-19T22:31:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-months-pregnant-and-doing-leptin-rx-am-i-crazy.1940/post-32237>

I'm loving this thread! Thanks for asking such great questions, Walker. Although I'm not pregnant yet, I'm hoping making a family is in my future in the next year or two. So, I appreciate you asking these tough questions; it's great to learn!

Congrats on your baby and best of luck in the remaining months of your pregnancy.

### nonchalant — 2012-06-19T22:37:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-months-pregnant-and-doing-leptin-rx-am-i-crazy.1940/post-32238>

Walkers, do you like brazil nuts? They are high in selenium to offset the mercury.

### walkers345 — 2012-06-20T00:28:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-months-pregnant-and-doing-leptin-rx-am-i-crazy.1940/post-32259>

Thanks Cowgirl! It's funny how things get way more serious when you're pregnant. For a year I was 80/20 paleo, but would have binge days where I would eat whatever I wanted. I started out about 2 years ago doing 4hour body(which allowed one free day a week) I used it to the nth degree. I've tapered this by finding MDA and realizing lots of ways 4hb is unhealthy. I also was not fixing my BED, just allowing it one day a week. (more in my first trimester! and around the holidays). I was working out 4x a week doing spin and cardio kickboxing and weights. It's harder now because I have less energy and can only do spin/weights. ANYWAYS, my point is, when it comes to a baby, things get REAL! That one day binge I knew was hard on my heart and all my other organs. I just don't want this to affect my unborn child. So better late than never I guess. I just hope this is a long term solution and I have faith it is. I am doing well so far, and I think the key is the BAB. I also can NOT eat my trigger foods, including almond butter, and honey! I hope sometime in the future I will be able to without it triggering my sweet tooth, but maybe not!

Nonchalant, I was just reading about Brazil nuts and looked for them at Fresh and Easy, but couldn't find them. I'll have to go to Sprouts because they may have more options. I've heard one nut a week is sufficient? Forgot where I read that one. I had no idea they could offset the mercury. THANKS!

### nonchalant — 2012-06-21T17:55:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-months-pregnant-and-doing-leptin-rx-am-i-crazy.1940/post-32713>

Stephanie, Dr. K recommends one brazil nut every other day, but I usually eat one per day. I sometimes buy them at Sprouts, but you have to smell them first. Often they are a bit rancid, sitting in those large buckets at room temp. I've had good luck at Walmart, who sells them in a small sealed bag. Keep them in the fridge.

Also you might want to check into cilantro. Evidently it will help "escort" the selenium/mercury combo out of your body. Jack recommends cooking it as little as possible. I sprinkle it in my scrambled eggs.

Welcome to the forum!

\-Sally

# Thread: light and circadian
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-and-circadian.1941/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### msclman — 2012-06-19T01:21:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-and-circadian.1941/post-32034>

so im trying to get my circadian adjusted but how extreme do you have to go? i mean is sitting in the living room with the lights on at 8pm going to throw you off??

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-19T01:23:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-and-circadian.1941/post-32035>

If you look outside and it's daylight, then you're fine. If you look outside and it's dark, then you're throwing yourself off.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-06-19T01:31:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-and-circadian.1941/post-32036>

I have an iPhone app that tells you what time the sun sets. I use this as my guide. It's called Sun & Moon, I think. I've had it for about 3 years and have used it for other things but it's great for this. Especially when you are out of town and the sunsets at a different time. You can look ahead on the calender and plan accordingly.

### msclman — 2012-06-19T02:03:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-and-circadian.1941/post-32043>

what im saying is , r ur house lights bad after dark? laptops? tv?

### Melinda — 2012-06-19T02:04:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-and-circadian.1941/post-32044>

> **msclman;32143 wrote:** so im trying to get my circadian adjusted but how extreme do you have to go? i mean is sitting in the living room with the lights on at 8pm going to throw you off??

When the sunlight fades I put on my orange glasses to block all blue light. We all wear them, kids too. We also put 25w bulbs everywhere. The refrigerator is too bright when we're going after ice for the ice bags so I'm glad to have the blue light blocking glasses. It's made an amazing difference: it makes me sleepy earlier and improved the quality of my sleep.

### Melinda — 2012-06-19T02:11:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-and-circadian.1941/post-32046>

> **msclman;32152 wrote:** what im saying is , r ur house lights bad after dark? laptops? tv?

Yes, they will interfere with your circadian sleep cycle. I recommend you install f.lux.com on your computer, it changes the screen to be less blue at night and reverses it during the daytime.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-06-19T02:15:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-and-circadian.1941/post-32047>

Sorry I misread your question.....still love the Sun/Moon app though. Helps to plan the sunset viewings too!

I go to the back room, turn all the lights off, and put on candles after the sun goes down. If I watch a movie I wear the orange "blue blocker" glasses and I too, have f.lux on the computer.

### chocolate — 2012-07-07T03:32:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-and-circadian.1941/post-36020>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21802437](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21802437)

> Seasonal variation of salivary testosterone in men, normally cycling women, and women using hormonal contraceptives.
>
> Stanton SJ, Mullette-Gillman OA, Huettel SA.
>
> Source
>
>
>
> Department of Psychology and Neuroscience, Center for Cognitive Neuroscience, Duke University, Durham, NC 27708, USA. [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted])
>
> Abstract
>
>
>
> Humans' endogenous testosterone concentrations vary over a number of temporal scales, with little known about variation longer than monthly cycles. Past studies of seasonal or circannual variation have principally used male participants and have produced inconsistent results. Thus, little is known about how testosterone concentrations fluctuate throughout the year, whether such variation differs between men and women, and whether there are influences of hormonal contraceptive use. The present study collected saliva samples from a large sample (N=718) of men and women, each collected at one time point within a relatively uniform distribution over a full calendar year. Both men and normally-cycling women displayed seasonal variation in salivary testosterone concentrations, such that testosterone concentrations are maximal in the fall and minimal in the summer. **Notably, normally-cycling women had testosterone concentrations that were over 100% greater at their maximum in fall compared to their minimum in summer.** Women using hormonal contraceptives not only had consistently lower endogenous testosterone concentrations, but also showed a flatter seasonal testosterone profile. The implications for studies of psychology and human behavioral endocrinology are discussed.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-07T03:44:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-and-circadian.1941/post-36023>

Mike...........total blackness. Tough out in NYC bro........but that is how we are designed.

# Thread: Cavemam on TV,  completely unrelated to optimal

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-19T03:53:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cavemam-on-tv-completely-unrelated-to-optimal.1944/post-32060>

In case you are interested. . .

Dateline NBC with Lester Holt has been following the Sister Carmen Community Center, here in Colorado, for about 7 months. They are doing a story on "the new poor", folks that were formerly middle class who are now struggling at or near poverty levels. I may be in various points of the program pointing to toilets and other fine real estate agenting. Then again I may not!

I will be watching it for the first time with everyone else so I can't tell you for sure. Just a little nervewracking.  I have no idea if or how I shall be seen. It should be a compelling piece and the emmy award winning producer was amazing to work with. It was such an honor to be a part of this project. At any rate, it was filmed right here in Colorado â€“ and you may be interested to see life here.

It will be on this Sunday evening. It is titled America Now: Lost in Suburbia on Dateline NBC. I believe 7:00 Colorado time. So, check it out if you like and feel free to forward to others who might be interested.

The most interesting part is when they started filming, A client of mine was featured â€“ and me helping him with the â€œAmerican Dreamâ€ of home ownership. Turns out he was interesting enough that they bumped his full story to a 1-hour feature on Rock Center with Brian Williams (also NBC) later this summer. We are set to close on my clients house next week â€“ and cameras will be in tow to capture the joyous moment. So, we donâ€™t know at this point if he was cut from the Dateline episode completely. So, if you donâ€™t see me on Dateline â€“ you will get more than enough (I know I did!) of me in a one hour special date TBD.

As how this may relate to optimal? The first shots taken of me were right as I started the LeptingRX + CT. Last filming was about 2 weeks ago. So I was filmed weekly for about 10 weeks.

Also, it's not over - so when we film next, I will be down and even lighter as I am trying an HCG round. I personally am scared to death about what I'm going to look like on camera!!! SO - I actually hope I'm cut!

Have no idea if my client got cut completely from Dateline - or if they are going to show some of his story in BOTH shows. His name is DAN and he's a single dad of three children.

# Thread: If you're considering a consult with Dr. K,
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-considering-a-consult-with-dr-k.1945/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2012-06-19T04:24:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-considering-a-consult-with-dr-k.1945/post-32067>

Just do it  Seriously! I had my consult this evening and not only did it give me hope that I am actually going to get well and regain my health and life, it's just very reassuring to have someone actually GET it for once. I feel like I have a solid path forward now and know exactly what I need to do. After fighting for 2+ years and feeling like I'm getting nowhere, it's unbelievably refreshing and uplifting to see a light at the end of the tunnel.

I know I have my work cut out for me both in being a "bulldog" to get my doctor's buy-in on my treatment plan and also to aggressively tackle my issues, but having it all spelled out so clearly for me makes it feel like it's truly within reach. I had read some of Dr. K's blogs over and over, but I just couldn't comprehend everything by reading it alone. Having him explain things based on my own lab results and then going back and re-reading the blogs after our talk just made it all click for me. I see my doctor on Thursday and have printed out several pages and written down extensive notes to make sure I get what I want when I walk into that office!

So if you're contemplating a consult and just aren't sure if it's worth it or what you'll get out of it, I would urge you to just go for it. Dr. K was amazing and so very helpful. For the first time in a very long time I have hope

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-19T04:44:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-considering-a-consult-with-dr-k.1945/post-32077>

Thanks for the kind words!

Outward life is not as important as what happens inside you now. Create your future by directing your present, magnificently. Never settle as you question everything.

# Thread: looking for a sensitive word for fat.... leptin resistant is an automatic...but means
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-sensitive-word-for-fat-leptin-resistant-is-an-automatic-but-means.1946/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-06-19T04:26:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-sensitive-word-for-fat-leptin-resistant-is-an-automatic-but-means.1946/post-32070>

many different things.

1\. First make sure you really are Leptin resistant (LR) to begin with.

**The easiest way to do this if you are heavy is to look in the mirror. **If youâ€™re overweight you definitely are Leptin resistant.** If you still have a large appetite and crave carbohydrates, especially at night, these are also signs that you are likely Leptin resistant. If you are fit or in decent shape and not sure based upon the above symptoms, I would tell you to go get a blood test and check your reverse T3. It will be elevated. I also recommend simultaneously checking a salivary cortisol level. With LR, you will always see higher cortisol levels later in the day.

I live in a world where weight is very relevant. I don't feel safe when I see cars with a payload of 500 hauling five very leptin resistant/fat people. I don't think we should pretend it is safe. It is, in fact, cruel for a leptin resistant/fat person to ride a horse. No apologies to the fat person/s showing up to ride the horse. Plus the reliability of the saddle on the horse.... you can only cinch one so tight. If we are supposed to pretend that fat isn't fat... please give me the pretend word**. Leptin resistant means more than or other than fat, also. We are all struggling with the same thing on some level.

We are just now getting into the fact that these foods are opioids and the addiction is real. The evidence is in how unhappy folks are with the way they look, and don't actually feel that great. Stuffing stuff in your piehole when you regret it as you are doing it is addiction. I find it incomprehensible that there isn't value in that knowledge.

# Thread: Hunger VS Hunger Smackdown!

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-19T07:15:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hunger-vs-hunger-smackdown.1947/post-32102>

For those of you who've gone a few rounds with HCG (har har) could you perhaps describe the hunger feelings?

I feel what I think is hungry - but it's different than cravings/eat the baby kind of hunger described with too big a dose.

It's more like my actual stomach is cramping up? I am excited when eating time rolls around - I wolf it down with relish, but it's not unbearable.

I just wonder if I could do something different to make it less uncomfortable.

Of course, I did try eating upon waking this a.m. trying to stick to leptin rx - but I wasn't hungry.

So perhaps I just go with the flow and eat lunch and then a dinner.

I'm down 11 lbds total, and 6 from my pre-load weight. Already can see a difference. Totally stoked.

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-20T17:44:17+0300

> **debbiemikus;32409 wrote:** That is great Cavemam. I have a question for anyone with experience with HCG, Colleen or anyone. Is it a requirement to wait three weeks doing P3 and then 3weeks on P4 after first round? I have been P3 for two weeks and have been hovering at the same weight with a massive amount of calories. I usually have more than 2000 with ratios of 50-70% fat 20-30% protein and 10-20 carbs. Can I move ahead? Thanks and sorry if this was not on topic. I haven't been hungry.....lol

you should finish out the 3 weeks of no starch and no sugar, whether you proceed to P4 (add in starch and sugar) is up to you. if you are asking whether you can go back onto P2 then definitely better to have at least 3 weeks inbetween rounds, 4+ better in my opinion as it gives your body time to restock and rest before you put it back into P2

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-20T21:42:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hunger-vs-hunger-smackdown.1947/post-32486>

Hola All,

Just had to share. Adjusted dose down yesterday and again today. . .at about 125-135, I think it's the sweet spot! No hunger today  No major tummy empty feeling so far, I had zero problem waiting till about 12:30 for lunch - and I'm not hungry for it in the same way I was prior days.

Losses are happening, I was unable to use the scale this am - but I think everything is in sync and running smoothly.

My version is:

300ish grams of protien a day, with 1 meal seafood - somedays both are seafood. Jack's seafood mantra is in mind.

1 fruit

2 veggies.

I've been doing different veggies/protien for each meal - but I'm preparing for a 5 day vacation and cooking freezing extra portions for that. We are driving so I'm gonna get some dry ice and a cooler and just bring all the food along.

Do you know if having the same veggie/protein for both Lunch and Dinner is something that messes up the process - or is it just unwise because the variety gives you a better nutrient profile?

### colleencoble — 2012-06-21T06:00:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hunger-vs-hunger-smackdown.1947/post-32622>

Awesome, Cavemam! I was about to tell you that you needed to adjust downward.  Sorry I've been out of pocket. Been in meetings every day at my publishing house. It's my 10th anniversary with Thomas Nelson and it's been crazy busy every minute.

### colleencoble — 2012-06-21T16:38:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hunger-vs-hunger-smackdown.1947/post-32690>

She's doing 300 grams of weight in protein. 100 extra grams to compensate for the 1 fruit she's eliminated.  It's totally bad to have hunger on hcg and always indicates a problem. In fact, when I can't get someone to stop having hunger, I tell them to stop. You don't want to lose muscle and the wrong kind of fat because you can mess up your metabolism more.

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-21T18:23:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hunger-vs-hunger-smackdown.1947/post-32728>

YUP! 300 grams weighed raw. I'm doing chicken, shrimp, white fish, and beef (2x a week only).

I'm not doing any fancy cooking or trying to make it palatable. It's like a supplement. I have been dieting for about 35 yrs so I'm real good managing the boredom and emotions involved with not eating w the world.

Of course with daily losses it's easy to stay motivated.

Today is day 7 and I'm down 10 lbs from my PRE load weight.

I think having a set time frame (40 injections) really makes a mental difference.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-23T01:03:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hunger-vs-hunger-smackdown.1947/post-33055>

That's great. I've just ordered my hcg from that place in Canada mentioned in another thread. Just need to get some bac water and a couple of larger syringes for mixing ... found the water at Amazon, big surprise. I'm so excited Cavemam to see the success you're having.

When I tried it 3 years ago it took me 8 months to drop 20 pounds. Long story. After 18 months of low carb, and 9 months grain free, 8 months leptin RX I'm hoping for a different story.

Are you eating anything other than protein? How about fat intake. Have you dropped it to zero?

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-23T01:51:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hunger-vs-hunger-smackdown.1947/post-33067>

Darleen,

I'm on day 10 now, still going strong. I have dropped almost 13 lbds from my pre-load weight (18 total). I know people like to count the after loaddays weight in losses, but I don't get that!

I'm as zero fat as one can be. I'm doing 300-350 grams per day. (meat weighed raw) of either chicken, shrimp, white fish. I did one beef day, and will do another this week. Don't know if that is a neutral or negative for me since I've only had it once towards the beginning.

I'm trying to do as much seafood as I can stand.

I'm doing one fruit a day and alternating between an apple, strawberries and orange.

I'm having a smallish serving of veggie.

I'm eating a full 500 calories a day, and filling in gaps in calories with protien.

No grains.

I tried doing protien in the a.m. ala reset, but I got too hungry later in the day. For me it's easy to eat lunch/dinner rather than bfast dinner.

I'm going with that.

I've been making lots of dose adjustments. . don't know if that is wise or not.

I got my TOM on day 3 but just kept going. . .and I'm happy so far!

# Thread: Children and Circadian Rhythm
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-and-circadian-rhythm.1948/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tweety — 2012-06-19T14:57:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-and-circadian-rhythm.1948/post-32120>

Spinoff from Cavemam's question to Dr. Kruse

So Dr. K answered kids should always follow the light. I am very curious about this one. My kids seem to do this naturally, because they are always up early in the summer and tend to sleep in for the winter months. Now mine are 9 and soon to be.... and a bedtime of 8:30 or 9pm is alright with me. But what about winter? It gets dark around 5:30 or 6 for a while, and I can't see them going to bed so early this coming winter.

I do manage to get them in bed closer to 7:30, but as they get older I think that will be more challenging. I know I have read about some of you going by candlelight once the sun goes down...

What are your plans or experiences? Kids ages? How do they respond?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-19T15:22:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-and-circadian-rhythm.1948/post-32123>

Sleep for children is all about habit........this is why they invented Teddy Bears. Put them down and give no options.

### tweety — 2012-06-19T22:16:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-and-circadian-rhythm.1948/post-32232>

Mine dont really have options! I am just thinking about winter time. We homeschool, so our schedule is usually up to us. However, I am finding the older they get, the later their activities get scheduled. Not by my doing, but by the establishments and studios that provide these activities. Our schedule is more and more being dictated by the outside world.

In fact, my kids are usually in bed in their room and we'll hear our neighbors kids outside until 10 or 11pm throughout the year. Its crazy to me, but I always wonder what my children think about going to sleep listening to their friends outside screaming and running around! They never say anything though, so it clearly doesn't bother them too much.

Actually, my kids have always followed this rhythm, just because we don't usually have anything to be ready for. And I will agree that their dispositions, and moods, and behaviors in general are awesome when we do this. When its interrupted for a late night or super early morning, they become bears!

### nonchalant — 2012-06-20T00:26:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-and-circadian-rhythm.1948/post-32258>

I think running around screaming outside is nicer in the mornings, when the mosquitos aren't as bad.

(Not to say I run around and scream outside... Well, I'd like to sometimes.)

### bigknitwit — 2012-06-20T00:36:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-and-circadian-rhythm.1948/post-32261>

My kids really follow the sun as well. But in the dead of winter it is dark here (in NH) around 4 - 4:30pm. There's no way they'll be in bed at that time (they are 11, 9, 6, and 3). I may need to invest in the yellow glasses, but it's not like my 6 year old will be wearing them during her team gymnastics practices. I don't really see a way around it, without withdrawing from their activities.

### Shijin13 — 2012-06-20T00:38:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-and-circadian-rhythm.1948/post-32262>

my youngest is up at 5/530 every day... oldest... we can put her to bed at 8pm, and she'll lie awake and talk until 10. I know its not good for her...just haven't figured out the optimal schedule for both of them yet!!! of course If I go back to work on site w/a client - they'll both be getting up at 5/530 every day!

### KiwiLauren — 2012-06-20T03:43:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-and-circadian-rhythm.1948/post-32312>

We're in the middle of winter and it's dark at 5pm. I don't put my kids to bed at 5pm but we don't use any artificial lighting. It's candlelight only (and there is one salt lamp in the hallway). They do their homework by candlelight, brush their teeth and change clothes by candlelight, play games by candlelight, etc. We have dinner much earlier (usually by 4pm) and they are in bed by 7:30 or 8:00 at latest. My kids are 8 and 11. They both wake, on their own without alarms, just before sunrise. They know that come summer they will be able to stay up later if they wish and we'll have longer hours of light. My son has one evening of rugby practice per week - it lasts until 5:30pm. It's outdoors without artificial light. I whisk him home immediately he comes home to a house lit only by candlelight.

As I said on another thread, they complained in the beginning, but we've been doing this for many months now and it's just normal. They both sleep much better, fall asleep quickly and sleep solidly. We have not used the glasses - all screens go off at nightfall and it's strictly candles. They think I'm nuts and don't want their friends to sleep over until it's summertime, but they're going with it. I've also noticed that our evenings are much calmer, which is a bonus.

They think I'm nuts and don't want their friends to sleep over until it's summertime, but they're going with it. I've also noticed that our evenings are much calmer, which is a bonus.

### kathylu — 2012-06-20T14:39:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-and-circadian-rhythm.1948/post-32374>

Bedtime for our 5 year old is in bed no later than 8 pm year round and he's usually asleep by 8:15. He tends to get up shortly after sunrise for between 10 and 11 hours of sleep. He feels so much better (and we do too!) than when his sleep is irregular. He was still demanding a nightlight to keep the monsters away, so I gave in. However, I go in and turn it off shortly after he falls asleep. Once I started doing that, he almost immediately started sleeping an extras 45 minutes to 1 hour each night. Amazing how 4 watts of light can affect your sleep cycle!

### Shijin13 — 2012-06-20T15:12:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-and-circadian-rhythm.1948/post-32382>

Kathylu

thats funny dd has to have a nightlight... ds will wake up in the middle of the night and turn it off...she doesn't notice, unless she wakes up to go potty... kinda funny...

### tweety — 2012-06-20T20:16:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-and-circadian-rhythm.1948/post-32461>

I never thought about the nightlight! My kids share a room. My youngest falls asleep immediately and sleeps late. My oldest like Shijin said, goes to bed at 8ish, but sings or talks for a long time. But its still benefical because at least she's in a dark room, not in a room with artificial lights.

I posed your idea KiwiLauren about the candlelights for winter. I got more interest than I thought I would! I think I will have to try this out. We cut out TV a long time ago and their computer usage has always been daytime use, so its just letting go of our household lights.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-06-20T23:20:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-and-circadian-rhythm.1948/post-32505>

> **tweety;32576 wrote:**
>
> I posed your idea KiwiLauren about the candlelights for winter. I got more interest than I thought I would! I think I will have to try this out. We cut out TV a long time ago and their computer usage has always been daytime use, so its just letting go of our household lights.

Definitely not my idea but I was surprised, too, at how relatively easy the switch to no-lightbulbs actually was. I confess that I spend a LOT of time feeling incompetent about how to handle all the dietary issues and potential health concerns I have for both me and my family. I found the circadian cycles thing to be an area that was basically easy to implement, not at all confusing to understand/know what to do, and quick to see benefit from. No supplements need to be purchased, no convincing anyone to get in a 50 degree bathtub, and straightforward. My cup of tea!

# Thread: The Leptin RX is exactly like training a dog..... The Guide Dog Training is the same
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-leptin-rx-is-exactly-like-training-a-dog-the-guide-dog-training-is-the-same.1949/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 31
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-06-19T15:28:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-leptin-rx-is-exactly-like-training-a-dog-the-guide-dog-training-is-the-same.1949/post-32124>

I finally figured out my confusion with everyone's difficulty.

The eating happens on schedule, the addictions get acknowledged and suffered through. You go to restaurants and have only club soda. You drink water only all day long. You go to bed at night and sleep. You take a couple of long perambulations.

Dogs can do it. People train them all the time. It's not that difficult. You just follow the rules.

Or you are dumber than a dog.

The suffering is just a given, knowing there is going to be die off and opioid withdrawals.

I finally realized why I don't understand. I still don't understand. Maybe folks can help me understand.

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-19T18:06:26+0300

Food. Food ........ Food..... Did I sniff some food? I think someone's in the kitchen..... FOOD!!!

Was it mindset? Did he do something differently from what you are telling us works?

I figure it is like to retrain an untrained or poorly trained dog and you need to separate the situation. The foods are identifiable addictives... Its not like the old days where no one knew what to put in their mouths. Now figure out what an addict does for opioids and alcohol... I know b vitamins are needed for dt's... The opioids are just a nasty stretch, from what I read and was going through getting off wheat.

Its the primitive brain that is needing retrained. Isn't that what the leptin reset is all about? then when there are training difficulties... seek experience.

Please explain the difference?

The whole point of the body image training not working, is because of it being food addiction. Not because they look in the mirror and think they will never be fit. its just like any other addiction. It is scary. I love the renewed commitments and one day at a time. The fat is just side effect unless the hormones are trashed. There are no fat people in pow camps, hashis, bad hormones, or not. Fasting will start ketosis. What will stop it?

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-19T18:50:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-leptin-rx-is-exactly-like-training-a-dog-the-guide-dog-training-is-the-same.1949/post-32165>

I think what chocolate is trying to get at is what slows the Leptin reset. Simply reducing calories does not always equal weight loss. Read Quelsens's journal and you can see that. We are a system. Changing one thing changes everything. How do you come to your own personal optimal homeostasis when you cannot feasible have labwork every three months? It's trial and error. Paying attention. Taking notes. Praying. And no humans are not as easily trained as dogs because we don't always know what our main motivation is! A dog is motivated by food, to please its owner. Pretty simple. Is it memory? A dog has no ability to make any associations other than immediate. It's it our ability to reason and remember the past that trips us up?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-19T20:02:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-leptin-rx-is-exactly-like-training-a-dog-the-guide-dog-training-is-the-same.1949/post-32195>

> **Jennifer;32278 wrote:** I think what chocolate is trying to get at is what slows the Leptin reset. Simply reducing calories does not always equal weight loss. Read Quelsens's journal and you can see that. We are a system. Changing one thing changes everything. How do you come to your own personal optimal homeostasis when you cannot feasible have labwork every three months? It's trial and error. Paying attention. Taking notes. Praying. And no humans are not as easily trained as dogs because we don't always know what our main motivation is! A dog is motivated by food, to please its owner. Pretty simple. Is it memory? A dog has no ability to make any associations other than immediate. It's it our ability to reason and remember the past that trips us up?

That is the definition of environmental mismatch.

### janagram — 2012-06-19T20:20:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-leptin-rx-is-exactly-like-training-a-dog-the-guide-dog-training-is-the-same.1949/post-32209>

most of us are made up of many parts...there is an animal part of us that is not so easy to train after running wild through fields of cheetohs all these years, and there is an emotional child part who says,"why can't I ever have dessert?" and there is a wild adolescent part who just needs a few more drinks to really party down, etc., etc, and I think that is why the mental part can be convinced of the right way to live, but getting all the other parts on board is another story. Not letting us off the hook, though...we do still have to get everyone on board. Wine and chocolate, now and then, helps.

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-19T20:56:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-leptin-rx-is-exactly-like-training-a-dog-the-guide-dog-training-is-the-same.1949/post-32221>

> **Jack Kruse;32306 wrote:** Not trying to get into this mess but how does one access tone on the internet? Am I missing something?

I think you may be, which is good for you though - probably helped you develop into the kind of person who can brush off, get up, and start over - even when people are throwing stones. This isn't a bad thing - but the question might give you some insight into those times when you are misunderstood.

As a highly empathetic person, I evaluate the written word to detect meaning/emotions. It's served me extremely well in this text/email environment for business. Rare is the person that says what they are actually feeling/meaning - so most also tend to not take others at their literal word and interpret based on word choice, quickness of response, punctuation, capitalization, etc.

Having read all of Chocolates posts - I think there is a strong judgemental current (I don't think she would disagree, judging doesn't make an assessment wrong). It's not a negative about chocolate, many people both love and hate that environment.

A placement of word can easily turn the perception of comment from informative to bitchy.

Tailoring what you say, to a particular audience might serve you in some circumstances. It serves me everyday.

One thing I have noted - when Alpha males (JK, lookin at you) do the literal and brutal honesty, it comes across to most as strong and confident - people are drawn to it. Women for sure, but men too.

When it's a woman doing it, it's perceived by both men and women as something entirely different (bitchy etc).

I learned as time went on, that it's not being dishonest to communicate to others in a way that they are MOST likely to understand. Particularly in a one on one situation.

For some (Chocolate I suspect), there is too much else going on and little need to make that adjustment so why bother. I love the internets for that.

But Jack - don't dismiss that people DO read all kinds of tone/emotions into the written words here and everywhere. For everyone (lizard brain?) it's all about THEM, not what you meant.

### Ray — 2012-06-19T22:15:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-leptin-rx-is-exactly-like-training-a-dog-the-guide-dog-training-is-the-same.1949/post-32231>

> **chocolate;32236 wrote:** ...Dogs can do it. People train them all the time. It's not that difficult. You just follow the rules.
>
>
>
> ... I still don't understand. Maybe folks can help me understand.

Why don't people follow rules? A couple of observations

We are AMAZING at rationalizing, and many (most?) of us have had little recent practice adhering to discipline of any sort. Perhaps military training helps. Perhaps some are just good at trying new things... not just dabbling, but carefully, completely and accurately doing that thing. Anyhow, we all have our baggage that may help or hinder our effort.

Most of us have had a lifetime of improper training as to when, where, how, and why to eat. These old habits die hard. For example, the idea that there are "healthy" foods perhaps leads one to think that more of that good thing is better. Hence, you read on these forums about people are consuming surprising amounts of coconut oil daily or putting a half-stick of butter in their coffee, and wondering why weight loss is elusive. For what its worth, most people can easily disregard satiety cues and "push through the fullness" no matter what they are consuming.

Not everyone has the same ability to evaluate their own behavior. If you are not getting results, perhaps you should find a coach.

Finally, our bodies are in various states of disrepair. This results in different outcomes for various individuals doing the EXACT SAME THING.

best wishes to all...

### chocolate — 2012-06-20T00:29:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-leptin-rx-is-exactly-like-training-a-dog-the-guide-dog-training-is-the-same.1949/post-32260>

Its not about sticking to the rules.... its just that those are the rules and until you follow them you can't expect certain things. Addiction is absolutely exhausting to me..... but why pretend that it isn't addiction. You a can't even get started until you acknowledge addiction. And then to say fat is the new porn.... you can look at it but don't talk about it.... It makes me nuts. Those 15 passenger vans that keep crashing and killing kids is because no one is talking about weight. In a car, each seat belt is rated for 150 lbs. That's usually the payload. Why can you call out a heroin addict or meth addict or alcoholic for lying or endangering others... why do you think its dangerous to drive sleepy? We have an on board distillery that needs carbs and lots of them to make ethanol.... just because you are consuming alcohol indirectly, its the exact same addiction. I have dealt with nicotine addiction. 30 years ago, a beautiful 300 lb woman told me I was lucky, at least no one could tell by looking at me. I couldn't believe it was possible that she was addicted to food. When at the airport, a lot of folks would go to Vegas, standing in front of me shaking trying not to buy the ticket. One guy thought I was his lucky charm... how bad does that suck? anyway, I saw him one Friday with a bad tooth and I always enjoyed his smile, it was so beautiful. The next Friday the tooth was gone and he was headed to Vegas, trembling and shaking and buying his ticket....he didn't have the cash for a return ticket. I never saw him again. I hoped it was his rock bottom.

I have a house near Griffith Park in LA and one of my tenants' boss has a mission of getting mentally ill and unhelped folks help. My tenant uses his horse trailer and truck to distribute things and he's been all over LA for years. He knows a lot of folks. They show up at our house sometimes when they see the truck. Its definitely a challenge. Some folks hate themselves for being addicts. Then there are folks that are clingy and addicted to being addicts, that way little can be expected of them. Those are the ones that cost the effort of a dozen and will gladly jump line to get it. I guess its the victim mentality rather than the fight that wears on me. I love a good fight and love happy endings. I guess I landed amongst a different sort. Maybe things aren't easier for me, maybe I'm a harder fighter. I guess that makes you normal and me not. How does that make everyone feel? Is that a tone? lol...

And by the way, dogs are motivated by much more than food. And they love and have a sense of humor. Or at least mine do.

### chocolate — 2012-06-20T00:41:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-leptin-rx-is-exactly-like-training-a-dog-the-guide-dog-training-is-the-same.1949/post-32263>

I reread everything in this thread about the dogs.

We socialized a guide dog puppy and trained her. I honestly believe people should have a dog to train before they have kids. The world would be a better place. Its really true, you can go to a restaurant and the dog will behave better than folks kids. It makes me wonder how much time they actually spend with them. I have been in restaurants where people make those remarks. The dog would eat the same time twice a day the food bowl comes up after eating and water was down always. The dog was motivated by much more than food. When we would go places the ziploc of food was in my purse and she drank from a sport top bottle. Maybe someone that has experience should speak up here. The training manual is the leptin reset. The dog isn't hungry, it just want to chew. The dog needs to walk at the same time everyday. There were even puppy pool parties where the puppies went in and the kids didn't.

### chocolate — 2012-06-20T04:04:46+0300

No AN, not even ED.... that was the puzzler... 123 and me helped. She had a good doctor, just a preexisting misdiagnosis and no labs to imply otherwise... Born with oddball Asian innerds and we never questioned our organ status.... who would?

I don't think its much harder than pushing away from the table.

It isn't distaste .... There is an addiction involved and the evidence is the appearance... the mirror test reflects what everyone else sees. Year after year after year there is definitely something. And everything emerging about the human micro-biome implies that it doesn't take long to balance out (two weeks, just like ct). ( think that's coming up soon... which kinds of gut flora flourish under what diet kind of a thing, a hint was mentioned) It takes discipline and a prepared environment. After recognizing addiction. Or if it isn't that... what is it when you eat food you profess to not want or desire the consequences of?

[http://www.psypost.org/2012/06/this-is-your-brain-on-no-self-control-12307](http://http://www.psypost.org/2012/06/this-is-your-brain-on-no-self-control-12307)

> However, the dorsolateral prefrontal cortex (DLPFC)—the part of the brain that manages self-control and says, “I really want to do the dumb thing, but I should overcome that impulse and do the smart thing”—fires with less intensity after prior exertion of self-control.
>
>
>
> He said that loss of activity in the DLPFC might be the person’s self-control draining away. The stable activity in the ACC suggests people have no problem recognizing a temptation. Although they keep fighting, they have a harder and harder time not giving in.
>
>
>
> **Which would explain why someone who works very hard not to take seconds of lasagna at dinner winds up taking two pieces of cake at desert. The study could also modify previous thinking that considered self-control to be like a muscle. Hedgcock says his images seem to suggest that it’s like a pool that can be drained by use then replenished through time in a lower conflict environment, away from temptations that require its use.
>
>
> Hedgcock says the study is an important step in trying to determine a clearer definition of self-control and to figure out why people do things they know aren’t good for them. One possible implication is crafting better programs to help people who are trying to break addictions to things like food, shopping, drugs, or alcohol. Some therapies now help people break addictions by focusing at the conflict recognition stage and encouraging the person to avoid situations where that conflict arises. For instance, an alcoholic should stay away from places where alcohol is served.**

What's the not simple part? fighting yourself? or forgiving yourself? I'm pity partied out, self or otherwise. Call it what it is, then you can fix it. Addictions are addictions.

dorsolateral prefrontal cortex is the last part of brain to myelinate.....your going to learn that soon......and guess why many humans have trouble with that? Because ketones are how we myelinate postnatally......so if your eating carbs......you never mature and myelinate......this is what happens in many ED and other mental illness and organic brain injury......how do we tell......hypoperfusion or signal change in T2 MRI scans.......

### Patty Cakes — 2012-06-19T19:27:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-labs-from-5-25-2012.1951/post-32185>

Let's start with your thyroid since it's the only thing I think I can help you with. You TSH is too high, FT4 should be higher and FT3 is definitely too low. You need to be on more meds. The half of grain of Armour is a starting dose for most but goes up from there. Since your rT3 ratio is bad I would definitely go for the T3. But this begs the question of why the ratio is bad: adrenals, low iron, something else? I would go back and test those things, if not now, then in two months to see why the rT3 issue is there. It takes approximately 12 weeks of straight T3 doing to clear a rT3 problem. However, it will just come back if you go back on Armour and haven't solved the originating issue of what caused it in the first place.

I love the T3 (just finished week 12) but don't want to stay on it forever so continue to work on my adrenals (the original cause of my bad rT3 ratio) and will start adding in the NP Thyroid (same as Armour) maybe as soon as next month!

Something is causing the rT3 ratio to go down if you were up nearing 20 in Jan. Have you done a saliva cortisol test lately? Adrenals seem to be tricky little buggers!

I upped my NP thyroid until my labs were almost perfect. I was on 2 1/2 grains (150 mg) and felt great! I just got the rT3 tested and it was 14.3. It's all about working on the correct time to take it in the, early before wake up, morning and how much. Plus doing all the other things for adrenals which I think you have already done most of these: morning sun in eyes, blue blockers at night, adrenal supps, etc.
