### Shijin13 — 2012-06-19T20:27:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-labs-from-5-25-2012.1951/post-32211>

JAn

my Dzugan updates

**DzLogic Update #1 20 March 2012

29 Feb had phone consult w/previous hormone doc - who recommended I back off of pregnenolone, frome 200mg (This was a dosing I chose based upon what I felt I needed - following my gut instincts & based upon my labs - and its how I've always managed my own supplementation) to 100mg, and eliminate 100mg of 7-keto DHEA. w/in 3 days saw a drop in sex drive - from my new \*normal\* to non-existent. I felt better on the 200mg of the pregnenolone, than the 100mg of pregnenolone. I can't say if the 7-Keto DHEA was helping me; but I know the pregnenolone was b/c when I was on 200mg my sex drive followed my cycle in sync - very predictable, and enjoyable

Previous hormone doctor also took me off 100 mg Ubiquinol.

Received BHRT (progesterone, tri-est, and liothyronine sr) on 10 march - and began them on 10 March - saw no immediate change in hormones.

On 14 March I woke up w/a Migraine, cycle day 25 ~ 5 days before menses should start. Menses began on 17 March, 3 days early. Horrible cramping, low grade migraine, fatigue and crankiness, began w/onset of cycle. Cycle still on going, migraine getting better - but not painful enough to require medication - just annoying.

Through out this period (29 Feb - today) I've been very HOT. almost like my body has turned on its furnace - Its not always constant, comes and goes - I'm taking cold showers/baths to combat the heat - the heat is radiating from the inside out - I've never been this warm in my life - I've always been cold. even my kids are telling me I'm too hot!

Over all energy remains the same - though I've definitely been a bit more tired in the am - this cycle.

Additional medication on 28 Feb - I began a parasite detox using ablenza - and I had massive die off for about 1 week. Now follow up w/herbal parasite cleanse and candida cleans.

ON 13 March - had a follow up appointment w/PCP Dr Jasmine Moghissi - to discuss the CIMT she had performed on me due to Familial Hyper-Cholesterol - CIMT showed inflammation in my arteries, but no plaque - due to inflammation she wanted me to start taking 100mg of Ubiquinol again, and Krill oil, in addition to EPA-DHA O3 fish oil. I've attached a copy of the CIMT.

On 15 March I received my supplements from previous hormone plan of the advanced greens, magna-calm magnesium, SAM-e (which was to keep on hold and not take yet), Selenium and Zinc gluconate. I took the advanced greens and magna calm as directed and ended up w/severe intestinal distress. When I read the lables the supplements above either had wheat as an ingredient, or modified food starch (which can include wheat byproducts). my reaction was a typical reaction to eating wheat or gluten, or a product contaminated w/wheat or gluten.

I know it wasn't related to the magna-calm magneisum - b/c the dose is less than what I normally consume - which is 1000mg of mag malate at night. This was a 100% reaction to wheat/ gluten consumption.

I returned the products to the previous hormone plan, for a refund. Then I went out and found Selenium and Zinc gluconate, from a brand of supplements I knew I would be able to take due to their policy of keeping the top 10 allergens out of their products.

DzLogic Update #2 28 March 2012**

I can tell I need to make some adjustments w/the hormones... as my sleep has suddenly gotten crazy - since last weekend I've been waking up at 1am - and unable to fall back to sleep until 3am. Sex drive isn't as high as it’s been in a past - have to work a bit more to get into the mood.

Had a migraine through out menses, low grade ~ 4days

2nd migraine on CD 12, 28 March.I also have my 2nd monthly migraine... 2 down, one to go...

My intuition is telling me I need btwn .3 - .6 P For 10days post totm, instead of the .1ml of P for 10 days. & btwn .6 - .8 P from then on out to next totm. I think the .2ml of the Tri-est is good. As well as 200mg pregnenolone, instead of 100mg & 100mg of 7keto dhea, or consider straight DHEA. Since dropping the Pregnenolone, to 100mg and eliminating the 7Keto DHEA - its getting harder and harder to wake up easily. As for the Liothyronnine sr - I don't feel any different on 10 mg... and I think an increase is necessary as well - intuition is telling me btwn 30mcc- 50mcc/day.

**DzLogic Update #3 8 May 2012**

4/10-I'm a total HOT MESS of HORMONES!! I have no clue if this is the adjustment to the hormones, or the chaos of PMS! Last night DH and I had a total blow out, feelings were hurt on both sides, and I was so mad I left and went for a drive - which freaked DH out b/c he knew he messed up. Eventually I came back and we ended up talking through everything - laying both of our issues out, and trying to figure out what we each needed to do to fix what we both broke. Fortunately, even though we're both bad at communication, and telling the other person what's wrong - when we have a blow out - the results are for the better of our relationship.

4/11 - it took us talking until about 3am to get everything out on the table, and get over our anger at each other, and hurt feelings... but at least we worked it out. ugghhh the end result to all this was a freaking migraine due to lack of sleep, and all the emotional stress... And here I was doing so well w/o a migraine... so Now I've had a migraine - but I don't know if it’s the result of hormones (I don't think it is - based upon what happened last night) or just stress from the blow out - and the ensuing late night talk session to get it worked out... Damn... marriage is hard freaking work! One thing I can say is I'm glad the kids were asleep while we were fighting...I wasn't the best person I could have been last night...

4/12 My Hormones are a freaking mess... my cycle this month was 26days! WTH??? Completely explains the total meltdown 2nights ago! So I'm guessing the migraine I had yesterday was both stress and hormonally induced.

Remainder of this cycle was good – no migraines in the middle of the cycle or prior to the start of next menses; however, this cycle ended up being on 24days long! WTH? Menses began on 5/6. No migraine or cramping or crazy hormonal chaos w/menses – so I’ll call that a win! I was so startled by the sudden appearance of menses that I forgot to use the .2ml of Progesterone that evening! I didn’t have any problems w/my missed does either!

Currently fighting a horrible cold/allergies not sure which as I’ve got sinus drainage and congestion, chest cough and general malaise.

In general I feel really good. I think the armour is helping more than the liothyronine for my thyroid. I actually no longer feel cold, and I feel like some of my thyroid symptoms are disappearing. I’ve also seen some weight loss about 3-5lbs. I attribute this to both the change in the thyroid medication, and the increase of Progesterone. I’ve also seen my waist measurement drop from 35” to 34”!

Supplements: I’ve discovered I do better taking the 7-Keto DHEA, DHEA and Pregnenolone 1st thing in the am, if I split the dose as recommended I’ve found It keeps me awake! I also discovered w/the increase of Progesterone – I no longer need the melatonin to fall asleep. I haven’t used it in 3 weeks; I fall asleep and stay asleep until I naturally wake up between 530-630 in the am well rested.

I haven’t taken the Vitamin A, as I’m not comfortable taking it since I eat Liver and other offal meats 3x/week. Also not comfortable taking the High Gamma Vitamin E, since I’m taking omega 3 EPA/DHA 2:1 ratio fish oils, krill oil, and CoQ10, as directed by my PCP to address arterial inflammation.

Also in the past week felt the need to increase magnesium malate from 500mg, to 750mg at night, as well as increasing zinc from 25mg to 100mg. I also added DIM, about 3days before my cycle; intuition drove me toward this, and I think it helped the transition with my cycle.

I’d like to figure out why my cycle keeps shortening? Prior to staring the BHRT, my cycle day average was 30 days. The first month on BHRT, my cycle was 27 days. The 2nd month on BHRT my cycle was 26 days. The 3rd cycle on BHRT was 24 days. While this past cycle I was migraine free, I’m wondering if there’s still an imbalance causing this? Is this the PCOS? or is my body just trying to figure out its cycle, now that it has the hormones it needs?

**DzLogicUpdate #4**

I am Updating because I woke up w/a migraine today. The migraine isn’t the most painful migraine I’ve had, however I’m very sensitive to light and feel nauseated. I haven’t had a migraine accompanied by nausea in almost 6mths. I’m currently on CD 3 of menses. On 1 June, CD 27 of previous cycle, in the morning I woke up feeling like I might get a migraine , but it didn’t happen. This continued for CD 28, CD29, CD30 of the previous cycle, and into CD1, and CD2 of menses. C3 Migraine. On cycle day 29, Cystic Acne began forming on scalp. On set of menses was painless, no cramping. Flow is light.

Sleeping well, I fall asleep easily, within 5-10mins of lying down, and feel refreshed when I wake up. I’m waking up between 430-5am every day w/o an alarm clock. I take the armour then go back to sleep for another hour and get up btwn 630-7am. I feel like I’m responding really well to the armor for thyroid. I’m no longer cold, even when temperatures dip into the low 40’s at night. I’ve been able to sleep in shorts and a tank top, and usually do not use the covers at night. I’m also not hot, when it gets hot outside. I feel like my body temperature is finally regulating properly!

Supplements that I’m taking:

200mg Pregnenolone, 100mg 7Keto Dhea, 75mg DHEA, 160mg Saw Palmeto, 4000mg Jigsaw Vitamin C; 5000 IU D3, 100mg Zinc, 750mg Jigsaw Mg Malate, 1250mg Everest Nutrition Krill oil, 1600mg: 800mg Jarrow Optimal EPAHA Omega 3’s, 100mg CoQ10 Ubiquinol, Now Foods Prenatal Vitamins, 2 capsules of B-complex w/metafolin & Intrinsic factor; 2000mg MSM.

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-20T01:18:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-labs-from-5-25-2012.1951/post-32272>

I know nothing about this stuff mostly but if you've got inflammation that won't budge, I'd say AI protocol, keto, and hardcore CT. Would probably also help the FBG and A1c. I like to stick with the "easy" stuff.... I also don't quite understand why you are taking omega 3 supplements when your last test showed a good ratio (no matter if that ratio is Jan or Dr K approved). I like to keep it simple..... you're not starting out, you're in good territory with eating gf meat and some seafood.... omega 3 supplements can get you the opposite of what you're going for b/c of oxidation. Eat more fish and forget about those?

I also thought one of the "optimal" goals was to get off as many supplements as possible in the end.... some people might need supps for the rest of their life, I understand.... but isn't the goal for you to also get off the BHRT? Eventually, that is.

Like I said, I just like to read these posts to learn more - don't really know anything

>
> eggs don't spike the BG - but that doesn't mean its not good for me....

From your labs it looks like you are definitely Leptin Resistant. My guess is that it's LR at the liver, but that doesn't matter. What matters is that you get your inflammation under control i.e. become Leptin Sensitive and your BG issues will fall by the way side. You stated that your diet is very clean and you may be correct. Make sure you have absolutely no fructose at all in your diet. My guess where your inflammation is coming from is all of the supplementation that you are doing with fish oil/krill oil. If your diet is truly Paleo clean, then your omega 6/3 ration should be 4/1 or better. With all of the omega 3 supplementation you are taking you very well may be pushing up your inflammation by not getting enough omega 6 i.e. your O ratio may be way off with more omega 3s which is inflammatory. JanSz recommended you get your omega levels tested, if you can afford that you should.

As expensive as fish oil/krill oil supplements are, what you save on not buying those, you can put towards your consult with Dr. K., just a thought.

I've tested my O6:O3 ratios and they're 2:1. The supplementation of the O3's is where I have some questions b/c My PCP is a big believer in both O3's and Krill oil, especially w/regards to arterial inflammation.

I do know that I am Mg deficient, and through some additional research I've been reading that Mg deficiency can cause inflammation. I'm leaning that direction, b/c Mg deficiency can show up as arterial inflammation, even though you don't have plaque, and have only patter a LDL. I'm trying right now to have my PCP order an exact test to determine where my Mg really stands...

Also waiting on my 23&me - which may shed additional light on the entire thing I have going on.

Intuition tells me I should be on an AI paleo protocal... when I follow my intuition/gut on foods, supplements etc- my body responds faster. And right now that intuition is telling me to test FBG/PBG, AI paleo Protocol & CT.

# Thread: Weaning off of HC - looking for feedback
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning-off-of-hc-looking-for-feedback.1953/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### vkiernan — 2012-06-19T21:00:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning-off-of-hc-looking-for-feedback.1953/post-32222>

I am weaning off of my HC so I can have some tests run to see why I have adrenal fatigue in the first place. I am wondering what sort of problems I might run into and looking for feedback.

I am trying to wean off of 32.5 mg per day after having taken it for 2.5 months. This is the start of my second week and I am a little tired yesterday and today. But, I physically worked pretty hard this weekend and that usually takes its toll. My weekend was energetic though and that is after removing 12.5 mg. every day last week.

I think CT helping my adrenals. I wake up every day with the light and am tired by 9 every night. This is pretty consistent and my sleep is getting better and better.

Thanks all.

### bigknitwit — 2012-06-19T22:05:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning-off-of-hc-looking-for-feedback.1953/post-32230>

I weaned off 15mg HC starting in January. I had been taking it for a couple of years, and decided it was time to see if I still needed it. The advice I read was to drop the dose slowly, like 2.5mg/week, and watch for symptoms of adrenal problems along the way. I did it much faster - I took 5 weeks to get off. I never noticed a problem. I waited 2 weeks and then did the adrenal stress index saliva test. My results looked reasonable, not great, but better than where they were. I was pleased, and started with DHEA and rhodiola to support them.

However, Shortly after that I ran into massive thyroid dosing problems that I am still dealing with. If I remember correctly, you take Armour as well, right? My Armour dose was not sufficient, but upping the dose brought on adrenal fatigue symptoms (low blood pressure, high heart rate, orthostatic hypotension, feeling awful all the time). It took me a good long while to get the tests run and figure out what was going on. I had been taking 2 grains of Armour, and now I am up to 3.5. My doctor told me that with continued DHEA, and possibly pregnenolone supplementation, I may be able to drop the Armour dose a little. We'll see. It's difficult for me to tell if the fast heartrate is due to adrenal fatigue, or too much Armour. It is getting better though, with continued DHEA. I don't know if this helps any, but I thought I would share...

### freshveggies — 2012-06-19T22:20:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning-off-of-hc-looking-for-feedback.1953/post-32234>

I might have missed something , but what test can show you why you have adrenal fatigue? I know saliva testing can show you the cortisol curve. I am just interested as I have never had a doctor tell me that they can show me why I have adrenal fatigue.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-19T22:59:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning-off-of-hc-looking-for-feedback.1953/post-32244>

adrenal stress index

### vkiernan — 2012-06-19T23:23:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning-off-of-hc-looking-for-feedback.1953/post-32246>

> **freshveggies;32347 wrote:** I might have missed something , but what test can show you why you have adrenal fatigue? I know saliva testing can show you the cortisol curve. I am just interested as I have never had a doctor tell me that they can show me why I have adrenal fatigue.

You are correct. Jack is talking about the test to confirm adrenal fatigue. I don't believe there is a test persay to know exactly what is causing it but a lot of things one can rule out or know that is contributing. In my case, it could be just too much stress over the course of my life. I also read where if your mother is stressed, that can pass off to you as a baby. I was baby #6 and my mother was stressed so who knows. She is checking my pituitary and ACTH. I am also doing the GI Effects test for my gut. Dr. Kharasastan (sp.) has a great book on adrenals and the thyroid. He talks about the gut usually being the issue for dragging down the adrenals which in turn drag down the thyroid. In my consult with Dr. K., he told me to check the gut as well to see if that was culprit. I am digging at this point shall we say. Just making sure there aren't other things going on that I don't know about.

I highly recommend that book. I think it is called "Why do I still have thyroid symptoms when my lab results are normal." I will verify tonight and edit if need be. It really puts things into perspective for me. Why everything happens and lots of examples. Not sure where Jack stands on it but it does similate a lot of what he says; just puts it into laymen's terms for people like me.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keep-stressing-over-high-crp-when-feeling-healthy.1954/post-32250>

My CRP labs are consistently in the red warning light range and have only gone up now as I've implemented many of the principals I have learned here.

10/24/11 - 3.54

3/15/12 - 3.34

6/14/12 - 6.93

I have expressed this concern with doctor. He also just ran Fibrinogen and Homocysteine:

Fibrinogen - 329 (240-450)

Homocysteine - 10 (4.0-15.4)

I have minimal knowledge of these labs, but understand elevated homocysteine is associated with atherosclerosis (heart health is a concern of mine based on family history) and fibrinogen is a blood clotting factor associated with heart attack.

Is there a genetic component here as I feel very healthy right now. What gives?

I will do reasonable testing to try solve this, but doing boat loads of testing that my doctor doesn't even understand because of a hypothetically bad number on a piece of paper based on research that is probably corrupt while diverting these resources from my family who I care more about than myself while I feel fine so others can say just do everything better just seems over the top.

\- I am a believer in a quantified self and don't mean to be as snarky as that sounds

I thought the CRP might be associated with the high reverse T3 problem I learned of in October 2011. However, I have cleared this out and lowered thyroglobulin antibody and throid peroxidase by removing T4 from my thyroid treatment (first diagnosed hypothyroid at age 7).

I have been tracking some other lab values - but 90% of the bagel eating, canola oil drinking Joe Schmo's off the street probably don't have CRP near this high from what I gather.

Well, I looked at her site and she looks close enough. She's not as anti grain as me, but she can't make me eat them. I need help with the whole sleep thing and hormones. she can deal with that. **Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-going-to-a-new-functional-medicine-doc-thur.1956/post-32377>

Good luck Janagram. If you suspect adrenal issues, be sure to get a 4x salivary cortisol test. You usually have to pay out of pocket but it is worth the money and will tell you what your cortisol pattern is like thruout the day. You can also get one with the hormones and that tells you a lot too.

### janagram — 2012-06-22T18:50:23+0300

### Destiny — 2012-06-22T22:28:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-going-to-a-new-functional-medicine-doc-thur.1956/post-33016>

> **janagram;33091 wrote:** Oh boy did I pay out of pocket.....! But, fine. I've got a packet of tests to go have done now. She took an extensive inventory of my physical health and diet, etc....so I feel like I won't be just flying by the seat of my pants so much, tho, she actually altered my supps but little. She told me to go get "activated" B supplements, and to add a carotene supp.....and not to take mag malate before bed (apparently it's energizing, which I don't need..) and she will zero in on my thyroid after tests.

Good luck with the doctor, hopefully, it is the one!

I heard that mag malate should be taken before bed because it makes you sleepy. According to what I found, it is OK to take it anytime.

How to take magnesium

Take your first dose of magnesium when you wake up in the morning and the last dose at bedtime. Magnesium is most deficient in the early morning and late afternoon. Most people find magnesium as good as a sleepin pill to help them get a good night’s rest.

Magnesium malate combines magnisium with malic acid, a weak organic acid found in vegetables and fruit, especially apples. The weak bond with magnesium makes it readily soluble in the body. Malic acid is a key component of several energy making chemical reactions in the body. Researchers have used magnesium malate succesfully to treat the chronic fatigue, pain and imsonia of fibromyalgia. Dimagnesium malate increases the amount of magnesium available to the body; it has the same properties as magnesium malate.

Magnesium can be taken with or without meals, but it is preferable to take it between meals for better absorption. Magnesium requires stomach acid to be absorbed. After a full meal, your stomach acid is busy digesting food and may not be avaialable to helpl absorb magnesium. Also magnesium is an alkaline mineral and acts like an antiacid: taken with meals, it may neutralize stomach acid and impair digestion.

# Thread: Hearing news with new ears....I know it's not just me,what about you guys?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hearing-news-with-new-ears-i-know-its-not-just-me-what-about-you-guys.1957/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TerrierMom — 2012-06-20T02:30:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hearing-news-with-new-ears-i-know-its-not-just-me-what-about-you-guys.1957/post-32292>

When you hear a friend has newly diagnosed AI, or thyroid, or gastric issues.... Or when you hear about celebrities on the news (today they talked about Sharon Osbourne breaking down over her sons MS diagnosis...I live in LA so celebrities ARE the news, no escaping it)....

Do you just wanna send them all to Jacks site??!? If it's someone I know and they are open, I do. I feel almost like, I would never forgive myself for not saying something when I think there are answers here. I even sent one of my favorite bloggers here, hoping she finds some hope and help (if you read the hilarious bloggess.com posts you will know about the multiple AI and depression/anxiety wackiness she has going on, it is in there interspersed with the hilarity). She is the only person I don't know personally that I sent here so far but honestly... If people are looking for answers and come here and don't find anything to help them, they may be brain dead (or not really looking)

The other thing is reading those ladies magazine you get in the super Arlen, each with a diet of the week or amazing weight loss story. This week one ran a story about the wonders of magnesium. It got some stuff right and left other stuff out.... Everything I see or hear or read about health and diet just pops out at me now. That, and anything anthropological or KT related.... I'm taping things off science and history channel like crazy, still need to catch up on one about theropods and feathered dinosaurs.....

I know I can't be the only one seeing the world through an entirely different lens... What do YOU do with all this knowledge you are soaking up here...we can't keep it to ourselves! I'd like to hear how you approach folks and where you point them to begin, based on the issues they have.... Kinda like "start here if you have adrenal issues" or "MS" or...

It's so hard because it's all so interconnected (the quilt). The info here is mind blowing

### PrimalPam — 2012-06-20T02:51:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hearing-news-with-new-ears-i-know-its-not-just-me-what-about-you-guys.1957/post-32297>

Oh, this happens to me all the time now!

Unfortunately, starting with my parents, no one wants to listen. My dad's cardiologist (dad has had quadruple bypass and multiple stents) continues to tell him to eat a "heart-healthy diet of whole grains and low fat". So my dad is "doing his best" by switching to whole wheat pasta and 5 days a week heart-pounding stairmastering. My mom, the diabetic, insists that "Diabetics NEED carbs", so she eats her 5 meals a day "to keep her metabolism going" with whole-grain crackers. Oh wait, now she's on NutriSystem, so she's eating their packaged garbage. But she's lost 15 lbs!! she will say. Sheesh.

I'm a single woman, and I recently had a date with a guy who turned out to be an Endocrinology professor at our medical school. He told me that his sister has MS so bad she can no longer work. I wanted so badly to point him to Dr. Terry Wahls' video and to Dr. Kruse's site, but I didn't want to appear first-date snarky.  I could tell, when I started to bring things up, he was not interested.

But here's some good news: Today, I saw a new OB/GYN from PaleoPhysiciansNetwork.com. A-w-e-s-o-m-e. She GETS it!!! Half her staff went to PaleoFX (I was there!!), and she started naming off all the tests she wants to run...basically a Dr. Kruse list! I almost cried.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-06-20T05:34:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hearing-news-with-new-ears-i-know-its-not-just-me-what-about-you-guys.1957/post-32347>

I get it! I have told three friends of mine about Dr. Kruse and shared his site. One looked at it but has a comprehension problem so she couldn't understand the blogs. Plus she's a ultra marathon athlete (college scholarship) and at 52 looks 60 in the face, 30 in her body and feels like crap but she wasn't interested. Another I never heard back from and the third who has had a lumpectomy for breast cancer and is easily 50# overweight said it wasn't for her. I feel saddened by the ignorance of people but realize it is not their path, at least not right now....maybe later.

I have told numerous strangers too but who knows if anyone comes on board. I will keep trying to spread the message and if people are interested they will search on their own. For now I have one convert and my BF has another. His started the LRx and is doing CT! He is totally into it. I'm happy that at least some sharing has turned into success.

### TerrierMom — 2012-06-20T13:05:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hearing-news-with-new-ears-i-know-its-not-just-me-what-about-you-guys.1957/post-32364>

>
> But here's some good news: Today, I saw a new OB/GYN from PaleoPhysiciansNetwork.com. A-w-e-s-o-m-e. She GETS it!!! Half her staff went to PaleoFX (I was there!!), and she started naming off all the tests she wants to run...basically a Dr. Kruse list! I almost cried.

Well that's awesome!!! I'm lucky that so far hubby is on board and my parents are kinda sorta following due to our results.... Mom says her dr tells her she will never be able to stop her statins....I know that's not true but not entirely sure how to get her there. She's got Kaiser HMO as her plan (in her 70's) so it will likely be very difficult to get her the right testing through them!!

Frustrating to see people pass it by but as we all know this requires radical change in thinking and beliefs and some people are not there yet...I suspect JK has been feeling this way for years now so we just gotta keep working with those who are open and hope for the best for the rest

### kathylu — 2012-06-20T14:11:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hearing-news-with-new-ears-i-know-its-not-just-me-what-about-you-guys.1957/post-32371>

I'm always amazed by the amount of discipline people have when they latch onto something that works! Keep sharing your results and the message will spread, even if more slowly than we would like. I keep telling my sister-in-law about the ice baths...of course she thinks I'm crazy! However, the other day she mentioned that she had heard people talking about cold baths and icing on the radio, so maybe there is something to it? I said "Seems so..." Baby steps.

### Susan M. — 2012-06-20T18:52:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hearing-news-with-new-ears-i-know-its-not-just-me-what-about-you-guys.1957/post-32436>

I also have trouble with comprehension. Sometimes I have to read a post a couple of times. I used to read all of the comments that are posted as well, but being as it is summer where I live, I am outside more than I am indoors for the time being so my education (via this site) has slowed down a little bit. I have had no luck at all in persuading anyone to look into this at all. None of my children or their spouses are interested, and one has a degree in genetics and another degree in biology. They all just kind of think I am a bit "wacked". But I am just mom, the one who never finished college. I have improved my health immensely and have lost 46 pounds but I still have absolutely no sway.

My aunt and uncle finally believe me that I have an intolerance to wheat because they heard it on The O'Reilly Show! Sigh.

I really love this site because it fully engages my desire to learn. I never in a million years thought I would be interested in bio-chemistry and anthropology though!

Susan M.

### Inger — 2012-06-21T11:05:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hearing-news-with-new-ears-i-know-its-not-just-me-what-about-you-guys.1957/post-32637>

I see you.. I see you!!!! Crazy, I have to tell all people I meet about it! LOL

I do it carefully though not to offend, and if I feel the other have no interest I don't go on. But my parents and my brother that lives here too wants to do all this! They do too, partly, but they have so weak willpower and are so connected to their addictions and habits its is hard to break for them - it is far from perfect. They kind of need me to do all for them but I cannot - I have to care for myself first. But when I have the possibilities, I cook and do things like shutting off the lights at night and light candles instead.. and they love it all! It is kind of beeing in childcare.. haha..They also love to hear when I tell the news I read on Kruses blog for sure. They have no doubt it is all true! But then - it is also totally logical!

### SeaHorse — 2012-06-21T14:22:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hearing-news-with-new-ears-i-know-its-not-just-me-what-about-you-guys.1957/post-32646>

I struggle with this a bit, because there is a real desire to reach out and help...but no one can be helped who isn't ready. The lead by example is what I aim for...but I'm also giving information about the website if asked and sometimes I just succumb and start raving a bit....that doesn't get me very far. I have one friend who is really interested and I trust she will open her mind and reap all the benefits.

Part of becoming healthier for me is to discern the difference between "fixing" and "showing." One leads to unhealthy worry and wasted energy and the other is how I want to teach.

### Inger — 2012-06-21T14:35:56+0300

### PrimalPam — 2012-06-22T00:08:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hearing-news-with-new-ears-i-know-its-not-just-me-what-about-you-guys.1957/post-32816>

At my new doctor this week, she said it's all about telling one person at a time and living by example.

She's 48 and looks 38. She used to be an extreme runner (marathons), but she cut way back on the running and now does HIIT's instead. People thought she was crazy. She hacks herself all the time and adjusts her micronutrients accordingly. She said that when you look better than others your age, people will ask you what you are doing.

I told her about Dr. Kruse's opening PaleoFX speech: We are each a candle, and we need to light the other candles. She loved it.

Unfortunately still, most look at me like I'm nuts when I tell them how much fat I eat (sat. & MCT). I can even show them 5 years of cholesterol, showing lower tri's, higher HDL's, and they are still like...but aren't your arteries hardening? Makes me sigh.

I started CT around the middle of Feb w/spot CTing... by the end of March I had moved up to CT Baths 2-3x/week.. took most of the last 1/2 of may off...until this week (work/life/job got too crazy for me so I had to back off a few things)

Jack will have to answer the question if you can normalize your P:E Ratio by doing CT; I'm guessing some women can, though it will take time. Not everyone is going to get there w/o assistance....it really depends on what you want out of your health.

Considering I've had migraines since I was 10, and I've probably had PCOS since I hit puberty - my P:E ratio has most likely been low for the entier time of my menstural life. Its a wonder I was able to get pregnant, and carry two kids to term w/my ratios.

BHRT is just one bullet in my arsenal... as is CT, epi-paleo, paying attention to light cycles, and seasonal eating.

### kathylu — 2012-06-20T14:21:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-hack-my-labs.1962/post-32373>

From wikipedia: Osteocalcin is secreted solely by osteoblasts and thought to play a role in the body's metabolic regulation and is pro-osteoblastic, or bone-building, by nature.\[3\] It is also implicated in bone mineralization and calcium ion homeostasis. Osteocalcin acts as a hormone in the body, causing beta cells in the pancreas to release more insulin, and at the same time directing fat cells to release the hormone adiponectin, which increases sensitivity to insulin.\[3\]

Your iron looks high, you have a leaky gut (cholesterol numbers) and OMG...do something about your vitamin D level fast. I'll let others with more experience talk about thyroid function.

### vkiernan — 2012-06-20T14:42:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-hack-my-labs.1962/post-32376>

As Kathylu said, your cholesterol is bad due to your low hdl and trigs. Those numbers should be reversed. Probably pregnenolone steal going on. Your trigs are that of a carb eating person from what I know. Your D is extremely low - take D3 10000 plus K2 with a mk4 subfraction and A. They all work synergistically. Read Dr. K's blog on supplements. Your FT3 is too low. Looks like your testosterone is high for a female but don't quote me on that one. Was your CRP a highly sensitive one? It is very high too. You have a lot of inflammation going on. I don't see an RT3; did you get one? I am betting it is high too. You are probably not converting T4 to T3 but to RT3 instead. I would think you don't have an amazing amount of energy either.

Are you doing CT? Eating a lot of coconut oil? Upping your 03's and laying off the 06's? Staying off of carbs? Following the circadian rhythm and getting good sleep? These things will go along way to help you with those numbers. It doesn't happen overnight but it does happen. Good luck on your journey Joann.

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-20T16:27:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-hack-my-labs.1962/post-32396>

Vitamin D real low, your cholesterol might be high b/c of your weight loss yet I wouldn't like the combination of high LDL, low HDL, high triglycerides and real high hs CRP.... that doesn't sound like a good combination. Yes to leaky gut.

Osteocalcin is a measure to assess bone formation status..... haven't found any ranges for that except for "in the teens". I'm pretty sure this one depends on your history and whether you have osteoporosis or are at danger of it. I think K2 gets it higher for more bone building.

### Owl — 2012-06-22T09:03:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-hack-my-labs.1962/post-32906>

From Mayo:

### Owl — 2012-06-23T08:42:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-hack-my-labs.1962/post-33123>

Yes, they expect older people to lose more bone, hence the higher limit.

Same Mayo article: "Osteocalcin levels are increased in metabolic bone diseases with increased bone or osteoid formation including osteoporosis, osteomalacia, rickets, hyperparathyroidism, renal osteodystrophy, thyrotoxicosis, and in individuals with fractures, acromegaly, and bone metastasis."

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-05T16:57:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-hack-my-labs.1962/post-49588>

> **Joann;50092 wrote:** Anyone know about Alkaline Phosphatase?
>
>
>
> Just had a blood test from gyn which was over range. 33-130 mine is 137 from Quest labs
>
> Previous test in June was 131 but range was 25-165 from Labcorp
>
> doc had me come in for abnormal test....freaked me out. but then said to redo in 3 months ????. thinking it something with liver. wondering if antibiotics could have something to do with Alkaline Phosphatase....2 months ago.
>
> Thanks from a nervous wreck
>
>
> [http://labtestsonline.org/understanding/analytes/alp/tab/test?gclid=CLTj8dzLnrICFSVgMgodiEwAig](http://http://labtestsonline.org/understanding/analytes/alp/tab/test?gclid=CLTj8dzLnrICFSVgMgodiEwAig)
>
>
>
> The alkaline phosphatase test (ALP) is used to help detect liver disease or bone disorders. In conditions affecting the liver, damaged liver cells release increased amounts of ALP into the blood. This test is often used to detect blocked bile ducts because ALP is especially high in the edges of cells that join to form bile ducts. If one or more of them are obstructed, for example by a tumor, then blood levels of ALP will often be high.
>
>
>
> Any condition that affects bone growth or causes increased activity of bone cells can affect ALP levels in the blood. An ALP test may be used, for example, to detect cancers that have spread to the bone or to help diagnose Paget's disease. This test may also sometimes be used to monitor treatment of Paget's disease or other bone conditions, such as vitamin D deficiency.
>
>
>
> If ALP results are increased but it is not clear whether this is due to liver or bone disease, then tests for ALP isoenzyme tests may be done to determine the cause. A GGT test and/or a test for 5'-nucleotidase may also be done to differentiate between liver and bone disease. GGT and 5'-nucleotidase levels are increased in liver disease but not bone disorders.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-13T02:29:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-hack-my-labs.1962/post-51306>

desaturases compete for omega 3's and 6's. The biggest issue is the proper amounts in a dietary context. This is why the optimal range is tight for a human with a big brain......3:1 or 6:1 you go over or under you aint optimal. This is why Wolf's book fish oil calculator was a big problem for me when the book came out. But you get a lot of bad press when you point out the mistakes in paleo land.

>

### JanSz — 2012-09-19T16:49:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-hack-my-labs.1962/post-52686>

The Fatty Acid Profile: Dietary Intake and Beyond

[http://www.metametrix.com/files/learning-center/articles/The-Fatty-Acid-Profile-Dietary-Intake-and-Beyond.pdf](http://http://www.metametrix.com/files/learning-center/articles/The-Fatty-Acid-Profile-Dietary-Intake-and-Beyond.pdf)

Figure 4. This pattern was found in a 54 year old male who, prior to specimen collection, had been eating **salmon 4-5 times per week

for 1.5 months plus supplementing with 2,000 mg of fish oil and 600 mg of flax oil per day. All four members of omega-3 fatty acids are

found in the fifth quintile. Fifth quintile linoleic acid with arachidonic acid in the first quintile shows a significant suppression of delta-6

desaturation. The AA/EPA ratio is in the first quintile, but not yet 2 standard deviations from the mean. However, continuing this level of

omega-3 oil supplementation can further suppress AA formation to generate a very low AA/EPA ratio. There are no clear adverse clinical

consequences at this stage, but the data provides a warning of the potential for crossing into clinically suppressed** AA status and various

consequences of sustained high peroxisome status, so supplementation of omega-3 enriched oils should be monitored.

> **JanSz;53312 wrote: The Fatty Acid Profile: Dietary Intake and Beyond
>
>
>
>
>
> [http://www.metametrix.com/files/learning-center/articles/The-Fatty-Acid-Profile-Dietary-Intake-and-Beyond.pdf](http://http://www.metametrix.com/files/learning-center/articles/The-Fatty-Acid-Profile-Dietary-Intake-and-Beyond.pdf)
>
>
>
> Figure 4. This pattern was found in a 54 year old male who, prior to specimen collection, had been eating **salmon 4-5 times per week
>
> for 1.5 months plus supplementing with 2,000 mg of fish oil and 600 mg of flax oil per day.** All four members of omega-3 fatty acids are
>
> found in the fifth quintile. Fifth quintile linoleic acid with arachidonic acid in the first quintile shows a significant suppression of delta-6
>
> desaturation. The AA/EPA ratio is in the first quintile, but not yet 2 standard deviations from the mean. However, continuing this level of
>
> omega-3 oil supplementation can further suppress AA formation to generate a very low AA/EPA ratio. There are no clear adverse clinical
>
> consequences at this stage, but the data provides a warning of the potential for crossing into clinically suppressed** AA status and various
>
> consequences of sustained high peroxisome status, so supplementation of omega-3 enriched oils should be monitored.

### CarolMorris — 2012-06-20T21:14:07+0300

I'm such a lurker, I can't get enough of this. Thank you all for your input, I wannabee one of you, first I have to figure out where to put my postsI didn't even know the difference between a blog and a forum--yikes. I see most of you are so young, I'm 61 female, menopausal for about 18 months.

Sorry this is so looong, I have so much to learn...

Before Leptin Rx:

Desperate for sleep

Food sensitivities up the gazoo, dairy, eggs, pork, nitrates, nitrites, red dye #40, yellow dye #5..on and on

(Anaphylactic to shellfish, mollusks and swordfish)

Inflammation, swollen hands and feet, bloating, skin, welts and itching

leaky gut, this took me awhile to figure out, I had no â€œtypicalâ€ symptomsâ€”never any pain or diarrhea

30-35 lbs overweight, skinny kid, within 10lbs of ideal â€˜til menopause, but always thought that was â€œfatâ€ (Twiggy generation)

Intense sweet and carb cravings as long as I can remember

Several borderline glucose tests 109-111â€”I refuse to get diabetes!!!

On metformin for 7 years for Insulin Resistance(supposedly as a preventative)

Always told I donâ€™t eat enough(avg. 1200-1400 cal)â€”I rarely ever over ate on proteins & fats, with sweets once I ate a bite there was never enough

Fat phobic(dietary)â€”seriously scared to eat any fats!!

Could not get my waist back or lose more than 10 lbs even for my daughterâ€™s wedding!

fatigue

10 weeks into Leptin Rx:

Have lost 4 Inches in my waist, 2 on leptin reset!!!

Maintained 12lb weight loss( on WB prior to LeptinRxâ€”thought I would gain on reset, but am maintaining).

NO cravings of any kind & eat lots of fat!! First time in my life that I can remember!!

Skip lunch most days or have bone broth or coffee with 5TBS coconut oil

Off metformin since 4/15 fasting bg in 80â€™s, non fasting rarely above 90 â€“yeah!!! I think the metformin may have contributed to lousy b-12

swollen hands and feet, bloating, skin, welts and itching---gone!

leaky gutâ€”I think is healing, was able to increase coconut oil to 15tbs a day with no negative effects

sweat pattern changing-not so sure about this, but I am warm

still desperate for sleep, although I have had a few nights of 6-8 hours

fatigue mostly gone except for really bad sleep nights

I was not taking any supplements. Iâ€™ve added:

TESTOSTERONE……..23ng/dl(2-45)

VIT.D (25-HYD)………...45(>30 ng/mL)

DHEA-S………………..106 ug/dL (246-911)

\------------

Supplement with DHEA

goal Women 275-400 μg/dL

Likely your pregnenolone is very low, measure it at LabCorp.

When you establish that your pregnenolone is actually low, consider supplementing with pregnenolone

and

lower your prometrium=micronized progesterone

Prometrium comes in 100mg gelcaps

Ideally you would replace all prometrium with pregnenolone.

I am talking pregnenolone Micrinized Lipid Matrix 150mg tabs, scored from Nutricology.

Dose needs to be titrated but expect (150mg-1200)mg/day

The idea is that

you have low cortisol.

Cortisol is made pregnenolone---->progesterone---xxxx-->cortisol

You are supplementing progesterone-----but----supplemental progesterone, even it is called bio-identical,

have difficulties down metabolizing (to cortisol)

If you supplement with pregnenolone

it should convert to progesterone

but

this timeit is actual progesterone that your body made

should have no problem to raise your cortisol.

When this works (like in my case it does)

I adjust pregnenone dose to get my cortisol=(20-22)

look up where is my progesterone ---->then tweak estradiol, estrone, total Estrogen

When/if you do all this, (and it is successful)

your will want lower progesterone (because current level may be too much) (goal 15-20-25)

and

to balance P/E you will lower also E2 supplementation.

\---------------

increase Vit D, B12, Folate

\---------------

Get IgE and IgG4 alergy tests,

E-95 Food Allergy Panel (Basic)

A-95 Food Allergy Panel(Extended)

Total 190 foods checked for IgE and IgG4 alergy tests

\-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

Review and use information in Post #1

# Thread: Fat-Free and Low-Fat Dressings Don't Unlock the True Potential of Salad
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-free-and-low-fat-dressings-dont-unlock-the-true-potential-of-salad.1967/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-06-20T22:40:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-free-and-low-fat-dressings-dont-unlock-the-true-potential-of-salad.1967/post-32500>

[http://www.medicaldaily.com/news/20120619/10367/salad-fat-dressing-nutrients.htm](http://http://www.medicaldaily.com/news/20120619/10367/salad-fat-dressing-nutrients.htm)

Fat-free and low-fat salad dressings may not be as good for you as regular salad. It's not because the dressings are not as healthy as they claim but because they don't unlock the full nutrient potential of salads.

In the case of salad dressings, some fat may be a good thing. Olive oil and canola oil-based dressings may help you get the most of your salad without ruining your diet. The reason for this is that there are many nutrients in salads that are fat soluble nutrients that won't be absorbed by your body if you choose fat-free or low-fat dressings.

salad

The study was led by Mario Ferruzzi, PhD, associate professor of food science at Purdue University. Researchers tested human absorption of carotenoids when using different types of fat-based dressings. The trial included 29 people being fed salad that was topped with either saturated, monounsaturated or polyunsaturated-based dressings and having blood samples taken to measure carotenoid levels. The salad dressings were topped with three, eight or 20 grams of fat from the dressing.

Carotenoids such as lutein, lycopene, beta-carotene and zeaxanthin can help reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease, cancer and macular degeneration. These nutrients dissolve in fat and when humans consume fat-free or low-fat dressings, there will be fewer calories consumed but also less carotenoids that can be absorbed by humans.

**Certainly too much fat in dressing is a bad thing and that's why researchers factored in dosage. In the 29 participants, monounsaturated fat-based dressings required the lowest dose of fat, three grams, to have the participants gain the most carotenoid absorption from the salad. Three grams of fat from the monounsaturated fat-based dressing absorbed as much carotenoids as 20 grams of fat.

Both the saturated fat-based, such as butter, and polyunsaturated-based, such as soybean oil, dressings required more fat to get more carotenoids from the salad.

**

While watching how many calories and how much fat you consume is important in daily diet choices, understanding nutrient absorption is just as important. With just a little bit of fat from using canola oil or olive oil-based dressings you'll be getting even more value from that salad thanks to all the nutrients you'll be absorbing.

### janagram — 2012-06-20T23:17:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-free-and-low-fat-dressings-dont-unlock-the-true-potential-of-salad.1967/post-32504>

Finally they get it! I hope I don't have to eat another salad with lowfat dressing at my friends'.

### chocolate — 2012-07-03T00:23:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-free-and-low-fat-dressings-dont-unlock-the-true-potential-of-salad.1967/post-35144>

[http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1046/j.1463-1326.2002.00195.x/abstract](http://http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1046/j.1463-1326.2002.00195.x/abstract)

Effects of arachidonic acid plus zinc on glucose disposal in genetically diabetic (ob/ob) mice

> Keywords:
>
>
>
> diabetes mellitus;
>
> plasma insulin;
>
> water intake;
>
> food intake
>
>
>
> Aim: The present study is designed to determine whether arachidonic acid (AA) plus zinc improves clinical signs of diabetes in genetically diabetic ob/ob mice.
>
>
>
> Methods: In the first study, effects of acute administration of AA plus zinc on glucose disposal were determined in ob/ob and lean mice (n = 6 each). In the second study, ob/ob and lean mice were treated with increasing doses of AA plus zinc for 2 weeks (n = 5 each). Postprandial and fasting blood glucose concentrations, three-hour-area-average above fasting glucose concentration (TAFGC), water and food intake, body weight and plasma insulin concentrations were measured.
>
>
>
> Results: Acute administration of AA plus zinc significantly increased glucose disposal in ob/ob mice. In the second study, postprandial and fasting blood glucose concentrations, TAFGC, and water and food intake in ob/ob mice treated with AA plus zinc for 2 weeks were significantly decreased compared with those in mice given no AA. **Plasma insulin concentrations in both lean and ob/ob mice were not changed by AA treatment in drinking water.**
>
>
>
> Conclusions: AA plus zinc in drinking water is effective in decreasing blood glucose levels in obese mice. These results indicate that use of these compounds should be considered as a dietary supplement to control hyperglycaemia in patients with type II diabetes.

I guess zinc is a cofactor with AA (olive oil, safflower oil) in warm adapted rats.

### nonchalant — 2012-07-03T00:41:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-free-and-low-fat-dressings-dont-unlock-the-true-potential-of-salad.1967/post-35147>

If there is no meat or cheese on the salad, I don't indulge. Skip the dressings.

### chocolate — 2012-07-03T01:03:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-free-and-low-fat-dressings-dont-unlock-the-true-potential-of-salad.1967/post-35153>

I read a study that showed just a teaspoon and a half made a difference.

Meat is zinc... Yesterday, I ate a duck salad and it was very duck poor. I was hoping for at least some sesame or olive oil to get me where I wanted to be, none. The place had just changed owners yesterday. I just came straight home and had tea with fat. Its tough.

### allendbrook — 2012-07-04T08:42:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-free-and-low-fat-dressings-dont-unlock-the-true-potential-of-salad.1967/post-35441>

Fat-free and low-fat salad dressings may not be as good for you as regular salad. It's not because the dressings are not as healthy as they claim but because they don't unlock the full nutrient potential of salads.

In the case of salad dressings, some fat may be a good thing. Olive oil and canola oil-based dressings may help you get the most of your salad without ruining your diet. The reason for this is that there are many nutrients in salads that are fat soluble nutrients that won't be absorbed by your body if you choose fat-free or low-fat dressings.

[**instaflex**](http://http://www.jointhealthmagazine.com/instaflex.html)

### janagram — 2012-07-06T01:52:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-free-and-low-fat-dressings-dont-unlock-the-true-potential-of-salad.1967/post-35734>

ditch the canola oil, but evoo is great!

### chocolate — 2012-06-21T01:53:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whole-foods-local-producer-loan.1971/post-32544>

[http://www.wholefoodsmarket.com/values/local-producer-loan-program.php](http://http://www.wholefoodsmarket.com/values/local-producer-loan-program.php)

Local Producer Loan Program

Whole Foods Market's Local Producer Loan Program (LPLP) provides up to $10 million in low-interest loans to small, local producers. Why? Because we believe in supporting local farmers and producers. We want to make it easier for them to grow their businesses and bring more local products to market. Thatâ€™s good for us and itâ€™s good for you.

Loans range from $1,000 to $100,000 and can be for things like purchasing more animals, investing in new equipment or converting to organic production. We minimize the fees, interest rates and paperwork that can often get in the way of a small local farm or business taking the next step to expand its operations.

# Thread: Endless Appetite Frustration

### bigknitwit — 2012-06-21T02:31:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endless-appetite-frustration.1972/post-32554>

I have been leptin Resetting for 14 weeks now. I have been doing CT for 10 weeks. I am still struggling with endless appetite. This morning I ate 1.2 pounds of salmon with cabbage, sauteed in 1/3 cup coconut oil, and would have continued eating. It's always this way. I am always sad when meal time finishes. This process would be much easier if I actually felt satiety after a meal. What's going wrong that's preventing this? I have dropped weight on the reset, and I'm within 7 pounds of my ideal weight, but it can change very quickly when eating the wrong foods (eggs, dairy, nuts, etc, which I haven't touched).

### Patty Cakes — 2012-06-21T04:17:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endless-appetite-frustration.1972/post-32596>

I wonder if you are still hungry as your body processes the hormones (CT driven). My appetite rose when I started CT and then after 3 1/2 months finally started to subside. Now I eat just two meals a day and that is plenty. My body took a while to process the excess estrogen.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-21T04:19:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endless-appetite-frustration.1972/post-32599>

This morning I ate 1.2 pounds of salmon with cabbage, sauteed in 1/3 cup coconut oil, and would have continued eating. It's always this way. I am always sad when meal time finishes. This process would be much easier if I actually felt satiety after a meal. What's going wrong that's preventing this? I have dropped weight on the reset, and I'm within 7 pounds of my ideal weight, but it can change very quickly when eating the wrong foods (eggs, dairy, nuts, etc, which I haven't touched).

FWIW I didn't start eating the Post LR until close to 9mths after being on the LR. after my last sets of labs, specifically my HIGH BG?insulin/HbA1C readings - Its pretty evident I still need to eat following the LR. It takes time. you will get there. Consider how long it took you to get to a point where you wanted to make a change - for me it took a serious medical reaction to drugs i was on, for me to start looking for alternatives - I went gluten free in 2004, it took me another 6yrs, to go 100% grain free. guess what I've got close to 36 yrs that I need to reverse. It won't take me 12wks to reverse this... it will take a couple of years - I'm OK w/that... be patient, let your body heal itself. trust it... it works...

# Thread: Freezed my fat first time since CT.. :-/
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezed-my-fat-first-time-since-ct.1974/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 39
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Inger — 2012-06-21T10:49:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezed-my-fat-first-time-since-ct.1974/post-32636>

Yesterday I laid in the sun on my stomach and had an gel-icepack under my abdomen.

After a while I just intuitionally checked how the skin looked on the iced area.. and I got a shock. It was partly, in long thin stripes, totally frozen, white and hard. I got so scared I run downstairs and tried to shower my abnominal area with warm water but it hurted real bad so I quit. I decided to just warm my skin up in the sun instead. After a while the frozen parts thawed and got red and swollen and hurted by touch. I then went to work. When home again I noticed it was less swollen already but still red. Today the day after, nothing more! My skin is totally healed, nothing to see!!! Wow that was a fast recovery (maybe my raw mackerel-head-smoothie helped the healing.. HA). Thanks God. I was so scared my stomach will look awful for days and weeks and I love to use my bikini all the time..

Inger

Food is for our healing.. so very important too!

### Cowboy — 2012-06-21T17:16:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezed-my-fat-first-time-since-ct.1974/post-32702>

I started CT with cold packs and this freaked me out the first time it happened to me. Only on the belly, never anyplace else, but it is a very odd feeling to find your fat stiff and frozen. I had no pain or rash. It may not be as controlled as Zeltiq, but then again Zeltiq sounds like it is a bit of "flying by the seat of the pants" also. Just because they charge a pile for it doesn't mean it is foolproof. Google Zeltiq problems and you will find lots of unhappy customers.

### Inger — 2012-06-23T08:59:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezed-my-fat-first-time-since-ct.1974/post-33124>

Ohh.. THANK YOU!!!!

It feel so very good to be with people that have the same view as I.. it is hard because mostly - also in the paleo-word - people do not want to see the benefits of eating certain body parts that are not the norm in our society.

But yes, raw organs and fisheads are nothing short of amazing when it comes to make you feel great. They kind of makes me fly, really!!

I eat the mackerel-head-smoothie only a few time / month, because they have whole mackerels that seldom here. Sad but true.

But always when they have them, I do the smoothie! I eat the roe and the fishmilk too.

I ate the brain of one of my parents lambs too. Raw. That tasted great BTW. Creamy and mild. I did not needed to hold my nose then ;-).

It amazed me how focused I was the time after eating the brain, I had a huge test in school and I had no time to learn before. But sitting there with the papers in front of me.. the answers just popped up.. everything I needed!!!

I wish I had access to fresh lambsbrain more often but it is aganst the law to sell here sadly. But - mackerel-heads will do it too!

Yes, I just toss the heads in a blender with water, enough to make it drinkable.

Thats it! Very convenient.

### AKMan — 2012-06-23T20:43:10+0300

### SeaHorse — 2012-06-26T00:20:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezed-my-fat-first-time-since-ct.1974/post-33538>

I seem to be the only one of my sisters who inherited my mother's taste for innards. She and I would fight over kidneys, hearts, liver and brains and would be sitting at the table chewing on the ends of bones long after everyone else had finished their meal. I kind of held back for a few decades when I was in my teens and twenties from fear of ridicule....but now I'm proud to make the cashiers roll their eyes when I unload my cart and my own family just gives me all their bones without comment.

This summer I will try a trout smoothie, fresh from our pond. That's a promise! I can't believe I just wrote that....

### Inger — 2012-06-26T07:13:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezed-my-fat-first-time-since-ct.1974/post-33607>

Souldancer,

put the raw heads in a blender (you can cut them smaller with a skissor before if your blender is not very powerful), add water enough to make it drinkable.. blend.. and strain if you want! Then drink it all. I close my nose. You can too if the taste is too strong for you.

I usually do 3 heds for one smoothie.

### Inger — 2012-06-26T17:52:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezed-my-fat-first-time-since-ct.1974/post-33683>

Oh no, I think you want need that with mackerel! I eat not frozen mackerel heads every time! It is as safe as sashimi in a sushiplace.

The parasites you can get from fish are not really dangerous eather, you get rid of them easy with a pill if you get an issue. I never had and I have been eating raw fish for about 7 years! I have eaten fish that had maggots too, but I just realized it after I ate the fish.. so too late. I often look in the toilet to check if there are anything moving, but nothing there. Also I feel great. So I think everything is fine!

I would only worry if the fish is from lakes (not saltwater) or very polluted areas. Also such fish I have eaten (that was a mistake) raw and have had no issues after that to this day.

Mackerel is just great! Do not worry Souldancer! Eat your heads as soon as possible, they are best fresh! But a few days in the fridge is fine for sure.

# Thread: Losing weight with CT when that is not ideal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-weight-with-ct-when-that-is-not-ideal.1978/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### earlyriser — 2012-06-21T16:54:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-weight-with-ct-when-that-is-not-ideal.1978/post-32698>

I am an athlete that needs to gain some weight for my sport (10-12 lbs) however since starting CT just over one month ago I have been losing weight despite eating more food. My CT consists of 30-45 mins ice baths @50-55F at least 4 times per week, cold showers daily and wearing only a shirt during winter.\*

I eat 90% primal which includes a lot of sweet potatoes + vegetables and grass fed animal products (including butter + eggs). My weight was stable before starting CT. I try to take advantage of the post workout window to eat most of my carbs for the day and am eating at least 20% more carbs than I was pre-CT. I lift 4 times per week and do sport specific training 4 times per week. Any help is much appreciated.

### Barry — 2012-06-22T12:24:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-weight-with-ct-when-that-is-not-ideal.1978/post-32909>

> **earlyriser;32824 wrote:** I am an athlete that needs to gain some weight for my sport (10-12 lbs) however since starting CT just over one month ago I have been losing weight despite eating more food. My CT consists of 30-45 mins ice baths @50-55F at least 4 times per week, cold showers daily and wearing only a shirt during winter.\*
>
>
>
> I eat 90% primal which includes a lot of sweet potatoes + vegetables and grass fed animal products (including butter + eggs). My weight was stable before starting CT. I try to take advantage of the post workout window to eat most of my carbs for the day and am eating at least 20% more carbs than I was pre-CT. I lift 4 times per week and do sport specific training 4 times per week. Any help is much appreciated.

First you need a body comp to find out the type of weight you have lost. Then work out tissue specifics... gain 10-12lbs of muscle or fat ? or lose fat, gain 10-12lbs of muscle ?

Has CT improved performance ? If not, consider stopping.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: What do you feed your kids for lunch?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-feed-your-kids-for-lunch.1980/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dan in Utah — 2012-06-21T17:46:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-feed-your-kids-for-lunch.1980/post-32710>

I can deal with a salad and sardines for lunch, but my kids...not so much.

I need lunch ideas for my kids. Please?

### skywaykate — 2012-06-21T18:10:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-feed-your-kids-for-lunch.1980/post-32719>

Would love ideas, especially getting them used to the idea, and replacements for fast foods (snacks, not McDonalds...but now that I say that, grab n go from the grocery store instead of McDonalds ideas would be appreciated too!).

What do you keep in hand that is fast?

sent from my phone while wrangling at least 1 toddler...expect typos, smile when you see them...the toddlers or the typos

### Sem — 2012-06-21T18:25:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-feed-your-kids-for-lunch.1980/post-32730>

I have tried to cut way back on snacks but what I do have is things like dried fruit, baby carrots, apples (baked or fresh), kale chips. I make banana bread an apple muffins made from coconut flour. There's lots of recipes floating around out there. For lunch, we have veggies cooked in bone broth, hamburger patties, chicken legs. Brown rice pasta sometimes with butter. Things like that. My kids are allergic to nuts but, if yours aren't, then there's a whole other world of nut flours, nut butters, etc.

### tweety — 2012-06-21T19:51:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-feed-your-kids-for-lunch.1980/post-32761>

we do a lot of leftovers, grassfed burgers made ahead of time, grass fed hot dogs, salami... always a couple of veggies. usually homemade yogurt or coconut smoothies, one type of fruit, nuts...

in the winter its so much easier. lots of soup, leftovers, broth. I bake with coconut flour then too, or occassionally almond flour. Its too hot to do any of that, so I am taking advantage of the heat to further wean them off of baked goods, rice and other starches that arent essential.

I try to limit their fruits to one per meal or less, and try to get their veggies to two per meal or more. Eggs are easy, hard boiled is quick to grab. And a recent fave- prosciutto wrapped around avocado. My daughter calls it meat sushi. They love them! I've been known to make them sushi rolls from tuna/canned salmon with avocado wrapped in nori.

I think the easiest thing to do is to always make extras when cooking.

### kathylu — 2012-06-21T21:05:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-feed-your-kids-for-lunch.1980/post-32772>

We have "breakfast for lunch" days often. Scrambled eggs, sausage, broiled tomatoes are favorites. Leftovers, or apple and raw milk cheese chunks. Occasionally our version of a PB&J...almond butter and raw honey on gluten free bread or a coconut wrap (I make my own). Paleo pancakes made from coconut flour, covered with cinnamon no sugar applesauce. Lettuce wraps (think tacos), chef's salad.

Check out paleoparents.com My 5 year old really digs making recipes from their book "Eat Like A Dinosaur".

### BJK77 — 2012-06-22T01:47:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-feed-your-kids-for-lunch.1980/post-32845>

Lunch is probably our most challenging meal, but I think we do pretty well. I don't allow snacks anymore, so I make sure the kids eat a full meal that will hold them over until next mealtime. It's difficult during the school year because it seems like teachers always push snacks anymore . . . so frustrating! Lunches are more difficult then too, but now that they're home for the summer I'm finding it much easier.

We do things like zucchini pizzas, burgers, meatballs with zucchini noodles or just plain meatballs, turkey patties, salmon patties, salmon/tuna salad, hard boiled eggs, meatloaf, homemade chicken nuggets, stuff like that. I try to make a few meals on the weekends and freeze for them throughout the week.

### skywaykate — 2012-06-22T05:22:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-feed-your-kids-for-lunch.1980/post-32889>

> **kathylu;32900 wrote:** We have "breakfast for lunch" days often. Scrambled eggs, sausage, broiled tomatoes are favorites. Leftovers, or apple and raw milk cheese chunks. Occasionally our version of a PB&J...almond butter and raw honey on gluten free bread or a coconut wrap (I make my own). Paleo pancakes made from coconut flour, covered with cinnamon no sugar applesauce. Lettuce wraps (think tacos), chef's salad.
>
>
>
> Check out paleoparents.com My 5 year old really digs making recipes from their book "Eat Like A Dinosaur".

Where did you find good recipes for the coconut flour pancakes and wrap? Would love links, please!

My daughters love stuff from Eat Like a Dinosaur, just don't always find it fast a lot of the time, but part of it is learning the recipes.

sent from my phone while wrangling at least 1 toddler...expect typos, smile when you see them...the toddlers or the typos

### LisaAPB — 2012-06-22T06:57:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-feed-your-kids-for-lunch.1980/post-32896>

meatballs, shish kebab, greek yogurt berry parfaits, bacon wraps, eggs, stuffed celery/tomatoes/peppers/mushrooms, veggies, lettuce "boats", olives, fruit, soups, some rice & potato on occasion, veggie lasagna

### tweety — 2012-06-22T16:39:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-feed-your-kids-for-lunch.1980/post-32930>

I struggle with the snacks. For them I doubt its actual hunger, because their meals are huge! They just snack because they like the idea of food any time of day. I am fairly strict about no snacks, but my husband and other people are a target for them! Especially the youngest. She seems to sense the weakness! She knows people will give in if they think she is starving.

And my mother in law seems to live by the idea that you should eat all day long. She eats very small meals, and then is always chomping on something. She came over the other day with a "smoothie" to share with them. I think it had several types of fruits in it, plus some sort of protein mix. It was like liquid sugar! But because its all fruit (even if six or seven all in one sitting) she thinks its a great option. Its hard to explain why I think fruits in that case might not be as great as they seem.

So despite my giant breakfast I prepared for them, they then had a smoothie snack 30 min later, and when I came back two hours later were eating more snacks of dried fruit and nuts. Then they ask for lunch right after that! I am just thankful they are young and their livers are functioning well so they can handle this. It's still much better than processed/soy/HFCS junk... but I want optimal for them too!

### Sem — 2012-06-22T18:57:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-feed-your-kids-for-lunch.1980/post-32962>

Other people are always trying to undermine me with the kids. This included my own parents. (who live across the street) and my mother in law (who lives with me). My husband is pretty good about it. But everyone else acts like I am torturing my children because I don't let them eat Doritos and Donuts all day. It's truly bizarre. When I took them off of gluten, my mother in law kept saying how she felt so "sorry" for them. Why? A blood test actually came back positive for allergies to gluten. And they had horrible rashes that cleared up within a week of going off gluten. What is there to feel sorry about? I also love how they will complain about the boys behavior but refuse to see the direct cause and effect of the food they just gave them.

### BJK77 — 2012-06-22T23:37:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-feed-your-kids-for-lunch.1980/post-33028>

> **tweety;33060 wrote: I struggle with the snacks. For them I doubt its actual hunger, because their meals are huge! They just snack because they like the idea of food any time of day. I am fairly strict about no snacks, but my husband and other people are a target for them! Especially the youngest. She seems to sense the weakness! She knows people will give in if they think she is starving.
>
>
>
> And my mother in law seems to live by the idea that you should eat all day long. She eats very small meals, and then is always chomping on something. She came over the other day with a "smoothie" to share with them. I think it had several types of fruits in it, plus some sort of protein mix. It was like liquid sugar! But because its all fruit (even if six or seven all in one sitting) she thinks its a great option. Its hard to explain why I think fruits in that case might not be as great as they seem.
>
>
>
> So despite my giant breakfast I prepared for them, they then had a smoothie snack 30 min later, and when I came back two hours later were eating more snacks of dried fruit and nuts. Then they ask for lunch right after that! I am just thankful they are young and their livers are functioning well so they can handle this. It's still much better than processed/soy/HFCS junk... but I want optimal for them too!
>
>
> I struggle with this as well. My rule is no snacks and the kids do great with it . . . until someone interferes! Sadly, it's usually family and they generally think what they're doing is ok. My mom has at least improved in the snacks she offers them, but I can't get her to just STOP the snacks. I swear it's like people think kids need to eat 24/7 or they'll waste away! She used to always push pretzels and crackers on them and thankfully she does at least understand the gluten issue so now it's constantly fruit. I allow the kids 1 piece of fruit per day, not even one per meal, but when they're at her house they are basically fruitarians
>
>
>
> Sem;33092 wrote:** Other people are always trying to undermine me with the kids. This included my own parents. (who live across the street) and my mother in law (who lives with me). My husband is pretty good about it. But everyone else acts like I am torturing my children because I don't let them eat Doritos and Donuts all day. It's truly bizarre. When I took them off of gluten, my mother in law kept saying how she felt so "sorry" for them. Why? A blood test actually came back positive for allergies to gluten. And they had horrible rashes that cleared up within a week of going off gluten. What is there to feel sorry about? I also love how they will complain about the boys behavior but refuse to see the direct cause and effect of the food they just gave them.

It is bizarre, but that's the world we live in. I've been told several times that people feel sorry for my children and that I'm depriving them. "Every child needs to have donuts for breakfast and bedtime snacks of oreos and ice cream!" I don't think there's a single person around me that understands what I'm doing and that's fine. I've learned to accept it, but I am getting quite the short fuse when it comes to dealing with them! If they want to be responsible for causing their children and themselves ill health, so be it; I refuse to be a part of that.

### tweety — 2012-06-23T01:57:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-feed-your-kids-for-lunch.1980/post-33069>

Well I am sorry that you both have the same experiences, but its nice that I'm not alone. I used to make my own toothpaste for the family because I wanted to avoid chemical ingredients. My mother also commented she felt so bad watching them "have to brush their teeth and not like the taste of it." I thought, this is silly- it's toothpaste! Its not food, it doesn't have to taste like candy or sweets. I still make it occassionally, and the kids don't love it but they tolerate it.

But yes, people think kids should be eating all the time. I actually am worried sometimes that my kids don't know the difference between eating out of hunger vs. eating because there is something yummy in front of you. Mine eat at any opportunity given to them. The upside is that now my oldest will pretty much eat ANYthing put in front of her. Sardines, oysters, turnips, onions... all the stuff that she used to reject!

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-23T02:10:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-feed-your-kids-for-lunch.1980/post-33070>

I make little sandwiches with cucumbers or zuch as the outside and stick a variety of things on the inside. Thin sliced beef with a vinegary mayo goes great on cucumbers. I like this alot too.

I do raw milk, so he's always up for a glass of "chocolate", by which I use 99% chocolate, tumeric, cinnamon, vanilla and a tiny bit of honey or maple syrup.

My husband is the master of almond flour baking, and so we have some "bread" at the ready for now and then when I am short on time. He's got one that he makes with a hard parm like raw cheese that is AWESOME. My son will eat a slice of that with a boatload of butter.

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-23T02:22:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-feed-your-kids-for-lunch.1980/post-33071>

Coconut Popcorn is a great one. . . .

Get the biggest flakes of coconut you can find, toast it in a pan with as much butter as you can, and salt liberally! When it cools its crunch and a tad sweet/salty.

I also like it with the Kerrygold garlic herb butter.

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-23T02:28:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-feed-your-kids-for-lunch.1980/post-33073>

Ditto on the well meaning sabotage. "Just a taste" or "Just a little bit" of whatever crap gets fed to children seems to happen when he's with others for play dates or with a babysitter friend.

I know I should man up (Jack up?) enough to tow the line - but I already feel like he feels negatively about being the odd man out in group settings.

Why don't you all move to Colorado!

### KiwiLauren — 2012-06-24T01:14:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-feed-your-kids-for-lunch.1980/post-33226>
