### Rolando567 — 2012-06-25T11:51:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-feed-your-kids-for-lunch.1980/post-33413>

Ask them what they liked to have at school for lunch and throw a few of those into the mix.

What about cheesy breadsticks and marinara to dip, spaghetti or fettuccine alfredo, grilled cheese, chicken salad pitas,tortilla wrap sandwiches, frozen grapes, mini pizzas, all beef hot dogs with chips and dip?

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

### XeniaMom — 2012-07-26T00:56:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-feed-your-kids-for-lunch.1980/post-41105>

Salad with albacore tuna on top. Olive oil and vinegar dressing with lots of fresh herbs. Greek yogurt, grass fed hamburgers.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-22T17:57:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-feed-your-kids-for-lunch.1980/post-46319>

I make soups most days (often served chilled), generally a salad and some cold meats, eggs or smoked fish (luckily my lot love smoked mackerel, smoked trout and smoked salmon, and we live near the coast so I know it's fresh) and, if they feel they need a pudding, some frozen berries, a drizzle of honey and some coconut milk or double/heavy cream makes a quick and easy one.

### Dan in Utah — 2012-06-21T18:00:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-32714>

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KkdFkPxxDG8&feature=related\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;KkdFkPxxDG8\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KkdFkPxxDG8&feature=related\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;KkdFkPxxDG8) Dr Stephen Phinney, MD, PhD

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-06-22T15:39:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-32925>

> **Dan in Utah;32840 wrote:** [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KkdFkPxxDG8&feature=related\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;KkdFkPxxDG8\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KkdFkPxxDG8&feature=related\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;KkdFkPxxDG8) Dr Stephen Phinney, MD, PhD

Hi Dan,

That was a very interesting video. I decided to look up his book and reserved a copy from the library. I sat wondering about the cover of his book which has a picture of vegetable oil being poured on a salad. What? You have to wonder what type of marketing editor came up with that picture. Where's the hunk of fat and meat?

After learning as much as I have about industrial vegetables oils I would skip the recommended safflower oil or canola oil. Ugh. Stick with an organic virgin olive oil.

I have been wondering about the traditional ketogenic diet used for treating epilepsy, before the modern use of vegetable oils. Before we had anti-convulsants the ketogenic diet was used to treat children and adults with seizure disorders. I would love to see some long term studies of ketogenic diets without vegetable oils. I am sure it has already been done. Do you know anything about the long term use of a ketogenic diet with children with epilepsy?

### Michael — 2012-06-24T18:39:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-33300>

> **WannaBeOptimal;33154 wrote:** It's the seed oil issue, Ray. When he recommended canola oil, I had a bird.

I don't think that's what Jack meant.

Steve Phinney did say he thought Canola Oil acceptable. So have plenty of others -- Loren Cordain, for example. Cordain has now withdrawn the recommendation, having got what looks like a lot of of pushback from Paleo people coming out a WAPF background. (Mary Enig, the fats expert at the WAPF, thinks Canola Oil pretty bad, especially because while it may be mostly monounsaturated fats, which is better than polyunsaturates, there's erucic acid in it (although they tried to breed it out).) It's a side issue.

I think Jack simply meant that he thinks that very low carb diets work best in cold environments.

### Michael — 2012-06-24T19:08:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-33310>

> **Caroline Cooper;33123 wrote:** I have been interested in the topic for some time. It is hard to find information about ketogenic diets before modern vegetable oils.
>
>
> There are probably some 19th-century books gathering dust in libraries, if they haven't been thrown out.
>
>
>
> I'd suspect they'd have made extensive use of animal carcase fats. I say that, because in those days everyone did. In England, for example:
>
>
>
> Dripping from beef has always been approved for cooking, and until the arrival of margarine (coinciding with the banishment of the cottager's pig), most children ate bread and dripping instead of bread and butter. Even in the opulent houses of late Victorian days, dripping cakes and dripping toast were considered excellent, whereas the mutton fast was the cooks's perquisite.

I have a cookery book on Norman Cuisine (from the 1980s, I think) and that has a recipe for Graisse Normand:

[http://www.amazon.com/The-Norman-Table-Traditional-Normandy/dp/0684183196/](http://http://www.amazon.com/The-Norman-Table-Traditional-Normandy/dp/0684183196/)

That's a cooking-fat you make with beef and pork fat and flavoring vegetables. The text seems to suggest that, although we associate Normandy with dairy products, and although most recipes in the book do use butter, extensive use of butter is part of a relatively recent gentrification of Norman cooking.

The chapter in _TSLCL_ is by Eric Kossoff. I don't know what fats and oils he's using. He is also using what they call a Modified Atkins Diet, which has been around since 2002, where possible. This is because the children suffer fewer side-effects on that than on the ketogenic diet. In Boston they've used a low-glycemic index diet, and apparently that has worked, too.

Kossoff has got a book out, and he's been interviewed here;

[http://www.thelivinlowcarbshow.com/shownotes/1972/dr-eric-kosoff-treats-epilepsy-with-ketosis-episode-367/](http://http://www.thelivinlowcarbshow.com/shownotes/1972/dr-eric-kosoff-treats-epilepsy-with-ketosis-episode-367/)

### jonnyh — 2012-06-25T14:59:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-33423>

Jack

Not sure if this has ben asked elsewhere, apologies if it has, but whats your view on V & P's theory that if your between 50g(or enough carbs to NOT be in ketosis), and 150g carbs, your not supplying your brain enough glucose?

Thanks

### Michael — 2012-06-25T15:47:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-33425>

> **jonnyh;33559 wrote:** Jack
>
>
>
> Not sure if this has ben asked elsewhere, apologies if it has, but whats your view on V & P's theory that if your between 50g(or enough carbs to NOT be in ketosis), and 150g carbs, your not supplying your brain enough glucose?
>
> Thanks

What's your reference for that, Jonny?

I've got a copy of the _Art and Science of Low Carbohydrate Living and nowhere do I recall their saying that.

I do recall their saying that the brain requires approximately 600 kcal per day. Furthermore, that it could run on either glucose or ketones. But where_ the brain might get 600 kcal of glucose seems to me be another matter. There's gluconeogenesis. Also, there could be other demands on glucose, depending on what you're doing and how efficient you are at using different substrates.

In practice, there are, of course, people eating, say, 70 g or 100 g or 120 g of dietary carbohydrate a day, and their brains don't close down ... although they rmight prompt their owners to go get a snickers bar in certain circumstances!

### jonnyh — 2012-06-25T16:01:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-33430>

> **Michael;33561 wrote:** What's your reference for that, Jonny?
>
>
>
> I've got a copy of the _Art and Science of Low Carbohydrate Living and nowhere do I recall their saying that.
>
>
>
> I do recall their saying that the brain requires approximately 600 kcal per day. Furthermore, that it could run on either glucose or ketones. But where_ the brain might get 600 kcal of glucose seems to me be another matter. There's gluconeogenesis. Also, there could be other demands on glucose, depending on what you're doing and how efficient you are at using different substrates.
>
>
>
> In practice, there are, of course, people eating, say, 70 g or 100 g or 120 g of dietary carbohydrate a day, and their brains don't close down ... although they rmight prompt their owners to go get a snickers bar in certain circumstances!

Hi Michael, it's in the performance book.

Just found this, it is the exact quote from the performance book, I copied this from a review I read:

'If you eat a reduced carb diet of less than 150 grams but more than 50 grams, you body does not become Keto-adapted and you are starving your brain of both glucose and ketones. End result – the body converts protein to glucose or - you go on a carb binge'

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-25T20:49:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-33490>

> **jonnyh;33559 wrote:** Jack
>
>
>
> Not sure if this has ben asked elsewhere, apologies if it has, but whats your view on V & P's theory that if your between 50g(or enough carbs to NOT be in ketosis), and 150g carbs, your not supplying your brain enough glucose?
>
> Thanks

Oooh, I've never heard this theory - interesting. I wonder if the switching from keytones/carbs can actually be regulated by the body very swiftly - or perhaps that is part of why sleep is so important at those levels. You need that time during sleep to generate the keytones.

### Michael — 2012-06-25T21:08:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-33496>

> **jonnyh;33566 wrote: this ...is the exact quote from the performance book, I copied this from a review I read:
>
>
>
> 'If you eat a reduced carb diet of less than 150 grams but more than 50 grams, you body does not become Keto-adapted and you are starving your brain of both glucose and ketones. End result – the body converts protein to glucose or - you go on a carb binge'**

**

Thanks. Good to have the authors' words.

So that would be from this book:

[http://www.amazon.com/The-Art-Science-Carbohydrate-Performance/dp/0983490716/](http://http://www.amazon.com/The-Art-Science-Carbohydrate-Performance/dp/0983490716/)

I don't think that says quite the same. But we have exactly what they said now, so thanks.

I guess their point there is that an athlete - this is from the book written for athletes - is likely to pretty soon burn through 600 kcals of dietary carbohydrate (even if the activity is mostly anaerobic). So, if he hasn't got ketones, since his brain requires the one fuel or the other, he must either get glucose from gluconeogenesis or "go on a carb binge" ... or, presumably, collapse outright.

It doesn't seem all that different to what was said in the book for a more general audience. The difference, I guess, is that they say this will** happen to the athlete. The person on the high-carb, low-calorie diet (1200 kcal's, 50% of which is carbs) that they gave as an example can, of course, just cut the exercise, which is not an option for a professional athlete.

I can't see there's a point of contention there. ...

... Unless it's the suggestion they make that keto-adaptation just will not happen unless you get below 50 g of carbohydrate a day, so that there is no smooth transition.

Is that the issue you were thinking of?

Mark Sisson _seems_ to speak as if he thinks it's a seamless process where the level of the one goes down and the other up. I say "seems" - I don't want to put words in his mouth, but that seems to be where he's coming from. Here he is taking about ketosis:

[http://www.askthelowcarbexperts.com/2012/02/5-ketosis-devil-or-angel-mark-sisson/](http://http://www.askthelowcarbexperts.com/2012/02/5-ketosis-devil-or-angel-mark-sisson/)

i don't know. I should think V & P have worked with enough patients, and also subjects in clinical settings, and taken enough measurements while doing so to know whether keto-adaptation is a smooth or discontinuous process.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-25T21:19:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-33499>

> **jonnyh;33559 wrote:** Jack
>
>
>
> Not sure if this has ben asked elsewhere, apologies if it has, but whats your view on V & P's theory that if your between 50g(or enough carbs to NOT be in ketosis), and 150g carbs, your not supplying your brain enough glucose?
>
> Thanks

I dont believe you need any exogenous glucose for the brain period. Our liver makes the endogenous amounts we need implicitly.

### jonnyh — 2012-06-25T22:25:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-33515>

> **Michael;33632 wrote:**
>
> Unless it's the suggestion they make that keto-adaptation just will not happen unless you get below 50 g of carbohydrate a day, so that there is no smooth transition.
>
>
>
> Is that the issue you were thinking of?

Yes that was the issue I was thinking of, if you look at the link below, look for the 10th post down by Barry, he refers to the same thing:

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1092-Fasted-State-Training-Stories](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1092-Fasted-State-Training-Stories)

Thanks for the link, I'll have a listen when I get the chance.

Cheers

### Michael — 2012-06-25T23:22:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-33531>

> **jonnyh;33652 wrote:** Yes that was the issue I was thinking of, if you look at the link below, look for the 10th post down by Barry, he refers to the same thing:
>
>
>
> [http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1092-Fasted-State-Training-Stories](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1092-Fasted-State-Training-Stories)

Thanks.

It's interesting, isn't it? My understanding is that the brain comes in because it's the limiting factor, since it can only burn two of the three possible energy substrates. If it's running on glucose, it grabs what's around -- which might, for example, have been made endogenously in the liver -- and there can be nothing left for the muscles. This is when cyclists "bonk".

If your brain could run on a mixture of glucose and ketones in any proportion, then it wouldn't grab all the glucose when there wasn't much around, and there'd be more glucose left for the muscles. But it seems that doesn't happen, because production of ketones, while it varies between individuals, doesn't usually ramp up until dietary carbs are not just low but very low (and dietary protein moderate, too).

Mark Sisson mentioned Peter Attia -- an athlete who admits to low carb. (Some athletes are probably doing low carb but keeping it a secret to gain an edge.) I just tried googling his name, and I reckon this could be worth a look:

[http://eatingacademy.com/nutrition/the-interplay-of-exercise-and-ketosis-part-i](http://http://eatingacademy.com/nutrition/the-interplay-of-exercise-and-ketosis-part-i)

**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fallen-fruit-map-eating-with-the-seasons.1983/post-32765>

[https://www.facebook.com/FallenFruit](http://https://www.facebook.com/FallenFruit)

[http://www.fallenfruit.org/index.php/media/maps/](http://http://www.fallenfruit.org/index.php/media/maps/)

international project

### vlynnb — 2012-06-21T21:38:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fallen-fruit-map-eating-with-the-seasons.1983/post-32782>

This looks like a great project.

I have thirty fruit trees on my property so I can't use all the fruit even with non-stop bottling, drying, freezing from late July until Nov. I sell a little to cover costs of maintaining the orchard but I often have a lot of free fruit that I make known to groups that help those in need. I tell them they don't even have to come to my door (to save some people's pride), just come and pick what they need. Guess what? No takers. If I would pick it and take it to their individual doorsteps they would eat it but don't want to put forth any effort to come get it. My grown children know if they come to visit they are welcome to all they want and they help with some of the maintenance work but would never expect me to do all the work for them to enjoy the fruit.

I guess this is a rant but I am not sorry, just a little sad that people won't put forth any effort for themselves.

If any of you are in Idaho and want apples starting in July I would share with you most years. This year I am only going to have about 20 percent of a normal crop because of the strange weather.

# Thread: Naxolone and sugar and wheat addiction
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/naxolone-and-sugar-and-wheat-addiction.1986/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Glamazon — 2012-06-21T21:29:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/naxolone-and-sugar-and-wheat-addiction.1986/post-32779>

I am having such a problem with wheat and sugar addiction I am about to ask if I can get a prescription for naloxone. Both substances seem to have an affinity for the opiod receptors in the brain.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/12055324](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/12055324)

Any natural treatments available? I took wheat and sugar out of the house and out of sight is not the problem, except at work and out and about. This is not some kind of whiny statement. It is about an addictive process I am having a hard time controlling. This brain of mine has such a strong addiction to these substances that there seems to be a type of fugue state that happens when the cravings kick in. Awareness can drop around what I am doing. Ketogenic helps. The only thing that really helps is eating as much saturated fat as possible all the time. I still have lapses.

â€œIn a study conducted at the Psychiatric Institute of the University of South Carolina, wheat-consuming participants given naloxone consumed 33 percent fewer calories at lunch and 23 percent fewer calories at dinner (a total of approximately 400 calories less over the two meals) than participants given a placebo.â€œ William Davis, MD, From Wheat Belly

### nonchalant — 2012-06-22T02:30:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/naxolone-and-sugar-and-wheat-addiction.1986/post-32859>

What are the side effects, Glamazon?

Can you go ahead and carrry snacks until the cravings subside?

### Joann — 2012-06-22T09:01:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/naxolone-and-sugar-and-wheat-addiction.1986/post-32905>

Glam, could it be low blood sugar?

I never had cravings until menopause.....then had cravings for goodies...hormone fluctuations?

### Gracelind — 2012-06-22T12:25:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/naxolone-and-sugar-and-wheat-addiction.1986/post-32910>

Hi Glamazon,

Sept 2009 I was prescribed 4.5mg low dose naltrexone (LDN) and put on GAPS. I lost a pound a day for two weeks--15 pounds total. Then lost another 15 over the next two months. The purpose was to address leaky gut and autoimmune conditions (Sjogren's and myositis) and not specifically for weight loss.

It was remarkably easy to remove all grains, sugars, and my beloved chocolate during that time. Dairy was harder but I dropped it, too. Funny thing, I'd look at something like fancy pastry and it just seemed like just something pretty--a work of art--but it wasn't at all tempting to eat.

I'm sure the LDN was a big part of blunting any potential cravings. Main side effect was very vivid dreams and a bit more difficulty falling asleep at night. It has been surprisingly easy to keep the weight off these last two years, even though I haven't been on LDN for a year now. In my 30s I was pretty well addicted to sugary cookies--now I don't even look twice at cookies, bread, cake and foods of that ilk.

The prescribing doc has LDN capsules made to his order by a compounding pharmacy. It was not as cheap as buying 50mg tabs and dissolving in water, but cost a lot less than other meds.

If you can get a prescription it might be worth a try.

Just one person's experience.

> @Harry you are looking for a problem in the ratio between the RT3 and the Free T3. i.e dividing the Free T3 by the Reverse T3 (Free T3 ÃƒÂ· RT3). For healthy amounts of RT3, The ratio result should be 20 or larger. If it happens to be lower, then you have an issue. If you use the total T3, you are looking for a ratio greater than 10. If lower, you have an issue. The real problem is that the units that rT3 and T3 are measured in are often different! Why I have no idea but it creates a calculation problem. I usually ask my lab to calculate it for me so I dont have too. Then I give the news to the patient.**

High LDL is a sign of a few things.....one is your LDL receptor status......bad genetically or damaged from glycation or lipoxidation or you have pregnenolone steal syndrome for some reason.......the causes of that vary and are substantial. This is why people do consults with doctors.......to find out why they in particular have it. There are no blanket causes.......contrary to what the non doctor bloggers tell you on the internet. This is why internet forums are littered with people who remain clueless why they are broken......and then they blame us. If you ask the people who talk to me or who see me........I cut straight to the heart of the matter so you can see your problem in a crystal clear light. And If I don't know.......I will tell you that too.......Im not afraid of telling you I dont know.....and I also want everyone questioning me too........in fact, I usually mention that in my educational consults.......I give you a blueprint or framework within for you to rebuild your self with your docs help.......and I give you bits of knowledge that you can put together to create your own personal wisdom.......so that you become your own inner guru by adjusting your thinking. Always question and never settle..........that is my dogma.

# Thread: Hunger:  Leptin or Mind Tricks?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hunger-leptin-or-mind-tricks.1990/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AKMan — 2012-06-22T00:58:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hunger-leptin-or-mind-tricks.1990/post-32835>

46yo male here.

Back in the day, I ate breakfast every day. Usually something crappy, like donuts, hashbrowns, biscuits and gravy.

Shortly after doing the reset last summer, I stopped eating breakfast altogether. If I eat before lunch, it's a can of sardines. I have a pretty good appetite for lunch and eat a big salad and more fish or some other meat.

For the past 2 months, I have been fasting one day a week--usually on Thursday. I eat a normal dinner on Wednesday and nothing until noon on Friday, about 42 hours. I am doing this for the puported health benefits of fasting, not to lose weight. I am at my ideal body weight, and have been for 6 mo or so.

On a normal day, I start getting hungry around 1030 and eat my lunch between 11 and 1130. Then, no snacks until dinner at 6, and I'm usually pretty hungry by then.

On my fasting days, I don't experience hunger at all. In fact, the thought of eating kind of turns my stomach.

So, what is going on? On the days I eat normally, is it the leptin telling me I'm hungry? Or just my brain because it's nearing lunch or supper time? What happens to the leptin signals on my fasting days?

The first couple times I tried fasting, I could only make it 20 hours. Now 42hrs is nothing and I could probably go 72 if I wanted.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-08T20:01:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hunger-leptin-or-mind-tricks.1990/post-36212>

You have a couple of things going on. One is the fact you are in an extreme northern environment. This plays a big part on energy needs. Being in such a cold environment and on such short light cycles, should maintain high levels of BAT. You don't need to eat as often to maintain energy levels.

Second is the body only thinks in feast or famine modes. When you don't eat your body goes in reserve mode and assumes food isn't going to be available anytime soon (your external environment plays a huge part in this perception). So when you do eat your body figures it is a one chance deal that it can get energy and it wants to feast.

### chipmeister — 2012-08-17T07:49:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hunger-leptin-or-mind-tricks.1990/post-45309>

Kind of on the same track, I'm finding myself getting hungry later in the evenings, even after eating a big dinner. For example, tonight we had a great sashimi dinner with a big ol' poki salad. I ate like it was my last meal. Then about two hours later I was ravenous. The problem for me is that I know we shouldn't eat within 4 hours of going to bed/sleep.

My question is this: is it normal to get hungry again after only a couple of hours when your letpin is reset? I remember when I was younger and my leptin was probably still good, I could eat 2 dinners and think nothing of it...you know the old "hollow leg". I drank a big water and tried to convince myself that that would be enough...not working so well.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-17T15:07:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hunger-leptin-or-mind-tricks.1990/post-45324>

No it is not normal and means something is amiss.......inflammation likely causing hypothyroidism is an issue maybe......free T3 low?

# Thread: digestion, brain fog, histadine, and the urea cycle
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/digestion-brain-fog-histadine-and-the-urea-cycle.1991/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-06-22T01:09:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/digestion-brain-fog-histadine-and-the-urea-cycle.1991/post-32839>

> [http://curezone.com/forums/fm.asp?i=289770](http://http://curezone.com/forums/fm.asp?i=289770)
>
>
>
> Most of us have varying degrees of brain fog. For me I've pretty much woken up "hung over" every day for the last 3 years or so. On a typical day I would spend the morning in a stupor and only gain my senses around afternoon time. I thought it was the candida producing acetaldehyde (the breakdown product of alcohol) which was causing this.
>
> Big mistake.
>
>
>
> 2 things should have alerted me:
>
> 1\. I was eating a low grain diet - so the candida had nothing to ferment
>
> 2\. If it was acetaldehyde poisoning which was causing the brain fog, why would I only very rarely feel the effects of the alcohol??
>
>
>
> It turns out (I believe) that my brain fog was caused by hyperammonemia (too much ammonia).
>
>
>
> I started taking the amino acid Histidine a couple of weeks ago and immediately noticed the following:
>
> 1\. I would sleep more deeply
>
> 2\. I would wake up with a clear head and not hungover
>
> 3\. I would get borborygmia (stomach rumblings). Lack of borborygmia is one of the symptoms of candida I've had for the longest time.
>
> 4\. Associated with 3 I felt I was producing more stomach acid (though probably still not enough).
>
>
>
> Histidine is most famous for being the precursor to Histamine in the body which most people know is involved in allergic reactions, but Histadine has many functions unrelated to this:
>
>
>
> Histidine is a vital component of gastric juices (this is the original reason I took it)
>
> Histidine is associated with delta level sleep (this is the level of sleep in which the body repairs itself)
>
> Histidine is used to regulate: Copper, Zinc, Iron, Manganese, Molbdenum.
>
> Histidine binds with heavy metals.
>
> Cutler: Elevated histidine in urine is indicative of mercury/copper intoxication.
>
> Histidine deficiency can be caused by a Folate deficiency (which results in excess uriny output of histidine).
>
>
>
>
>
> The Urea cycle is a series of 5 liver enzymes that rid the body of ammonia (by converting it into urea) and is used to metabolise amino acids (the urea is then transported to the kidneys where it is excreted in urine).
>
>
>
> So basically my Urea cycle isn't working properly resulting in too much ammonia = brain fog. There are a number of defects which can occur with the Urea cycle.
>
>
>
> For example, Autism presenting in adulthood (japan) has been linked to OTC deficiency. OTC=Ornithine transcarbamylase is one of the enzymes involved in the Urea cycle, and is actually the most common defect of the urea cycle. Low levels of OTC lead to hyperammonemia. Thats brain fog to you and me. Incidentally OTC can also be damaged by mercury.
>
>
>
> Somehow histidine seems to be counteracting all this. Now acetaldehyde is an exogenous toxin not normally produced by the body. As such there is a specific detoxification pathway (i.e. acetaldehyde dehyrogenase), and there are few things which will speed this up (molybdenum being one of the few). However Ammonia is an exogenous toxin - it is something all of us produce all the time. So the fact that Histidine to a large extent counteracts my brain-fog (especially early morning fog) tells me that the brain-fog is more likely to be caused by urea-cycle dysfuntion and not by acetaldehyde.
>
>
>
> So whats my point? Its certainly not to get people to rush out and buy histidine.
>
>
>
> My point is that brain fog is not caused by acetaldehyde, but instead it is caused by a urea-cycle defect resulting in too much ammonia accumulating in the tissues/brain.
>
>
>
> My recommendation is to have an amino acid profile done and to see which ones you are deficient in, and if you have persistent brain fog (even when on a low fermentable carbs candida diet) to get your urea cycle checked out.
>
>
>
> JV.

I'm having a chicken and egg problem about the hyperammonemea... but the surprise results for the reader seem interesting.

### indigogirl — 2012-06-22T02:02:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/digestion-brain-fog-histadine-and-the-urea-cycle.1991/post-32846>

So were you tested?

I don't sleep well and wake up in a fog most mornings...

### chocolate — 2012-06-22T02:28:03+0300

My kid has the eating delay that is supposed to be from the B6 deficiency caused by probably candida. Not the doesn't want to, just the gut isn't ready for a couple of hours. The folate thing caught my eye and I think we may not have the best kind. The urea cycle is an obsession because of the Vitamin D receptors.

That's why I think the guy has it backwards... but the candida and ammonia thing is new knowledge.... the past year or so.

And the zinc and copper control hunger....

### chocolate — 2012-06-22T03:04:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/digestion-brain-fog-histadine-and-the-urea-cycle.1991/post-32865>

[http://curezone.com/forums/am.asp?i=1689869](http://http://curezone.com/forums/am.asp?i=1689869)

> Ergosterol is present in fungal cell membranes yet absent in animal cell membranes, and is a common target for antifungal drugs. Polyenes drugs such as nystatin and amphotericin B attack ergosterol in the cell walls of Candida, poking holes in the walls, leading to destruction of the yeast cell. Azoles such as fluconazole (Diflucan) and itraconazole (Sporanox) inhibit ergosterol production, as does terbinafine (Lamisil), and this inhibits Candida growth.
>
>
>
> **It has been shown in laboratory experiments that folic acid (folate) is required by Candida to create ergosterol. By disturbing folate metabolism with the antifolate drug pyrimethamine, scientists were able to increase the effectiveness of ergosterol inhibiting antifungals.
>
> [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.g](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.g)**ov/pubmed/17046206
>
>
>
> If denying Candida folate at the cellular level is antifungal, does this mean supplementing folate stimulates Candida growth? Might taking folate supplements interfere with antifungals?
>
>
>
> I could not find any scientific research into this so I decided to do a simple test myself. I half filled a couple of jars with water, then added sugar and baker's yeast. To one jar I added 2 tablets (1000mcg) of folate (crushed and mixed with water first). Over the next 24 hours both mixtures produced strong yeast growth evidenced by bubbling, scum/froth and a strong yeast smell. The folate mixture produced slightly more yeast scum and bubbling.
>
>
>
> After 24 hours I added a 250mg capsule of the antifungal amphotericin B to both samples, and also skimmed the yeast scum from the surface. 12 hours later and only the folate mixture was still bubbling. At a much reduced rate than before the AMB was added, but it was still fermenting, whereas the control mixture was not. The folate mixture also had slightly more yeast scum on top, but there was not a huge difference, so apparently the control mixture had continued to ferment for some time before the AMB fully inhibited yeast growth.
>
>
>
> I did the exact same test with a capsule of B multivitamins, and the results were the same as the control (no bubbling, no more yeast scum).
>
>
>
> I will not make any conclusions from this. I leave it open to discussion. At this point in time I'm just presenting the evidence, and inviting comment.
>
>
>
> Add To Favorites!

The folic acid implies candida.... so getting the histidine in with the copper and zinc makes total sense. I forgot that was the folic acid deficiency. Which no one ever told me.... folic acid just keeps feeding yeast.

### chocolate — 2012-06-22T03:36:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/digestion-brain-fog-histadine-and-the-urea-cycle.1991/post-32879>

[http://www.plospathogens.org/article/info%3Adoi%2F10.1371%2Fjournal.ppat.1000116](http://http://www.plospathogens.org/article/info%3Adoi%2F10.1371%2Fjournal.ppat.1000116)

> Author Summary Top
>
>
>
> It has been estimated that humans contain about 1 kg of microbes, an observation that reflects our coexistence with colonizing microbes such as bacteria and fungi. The fungal species Candida is present as a commensal at mucosal surfaces and on skin. Although it may cause life-threatening infections, such as sepsis, particularly in immunocompromised individuals, it seldom causes disease in normal individuals. In order to control our microbial flora, humans as well as virtually all life forms are armoured with various proteins and peptides that comprise integral parts of our innate immune system. **Here we describe a new component in this system; histidine-rich glycoprotein (HRG), an abundant plasma protein. We show, using a combination of microbiological, biochemical, and biophysical methods, that HRG exerts a potent antifungal activity, which is mediated via a histidine-rich region of the protein, and targets ergosterol-rich membrane structures such as those of Candida. HRG killed Candida both in plasma as well as when incorporated into fibrin clots. In mouse infection models, HRG was protective against systemic infection by Candida, indicating a novel antifungal role of HRG in innate immunity**.

### chocolate — 2012-06-22T22:35:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/digestion-brain-fog-histadine-and-the-urea-cycle.1991/post-33019>

> Polly: Vitamin B1 helps people use sulfur properly. The coenzyme form of it (TTFD or Benfotiamine) might be worth trying.
>
>
>
> If you take supplements in the form of sulfates, don’ t take them with calcium. In Andy Culter’ s book, he said that sulfates in the presence of calcium would form insoluble precipitates (page111).
>
>
>
> In an article at Jeff Clark’ s site, he suggests that if a person is intolerant to sulfur foods, that they should check their level of the amino acid histidine. They may be low on it. This is an excerpt from his article, repeated with permission. [www.CFSN.com](http://http://www.CFSN.com)
>
>
>
> “ Intolerance of sulfur bearing foods like garlic and broccoli along with elevated blood plasma L-cysteine levels indicate inactivation of the L-Histidine and iron dependent enzyme cysteine dioxygenase.
>
>
>
> L-histidine is required by the body to regulate and utilize essential trace minerals such as copper, zinc, iron, manganese and molybdenum. Metals such as zinc, copper and nickel are transported by binding with L-histidine, and such binding appears essential for rapid excretion of excess metal.
>
>
>
> Persons contaminated with heavy metals, suffering from chronic seasonal allergies, or following a low histidine diet are most susceptible to body depletion of adequate histidine stores—creating the possibility of mineral-enzyme deficiencies and dysregulation.”
>
>
>
> (For some information on histidine, see Eric Braverman’ s book, The Healing Nutrients Within. Histidine is found in meat, but there is very little histidine in most cereals, grains, vegetables, fruits and oils.)

### chocolate — 2012-06-23T00:14:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/digestion-brain-fog-histadine-and-the-urea-cycle.1991/post-33038>

[http://www.innvista.com/health/nutrition/amino/histidin.htm](http://http://www.innvista.com/health/nutrition/amino/histidin.htm)

> Large doses of zinc may cause a reduction in the serum histidine levels; whereas, low dosages or prolonged use of zinc has been seen to raise serum histidine and blood histamine. Zinc and manganese deficiencies interfere with the normal metabolism of histidine to histamine. A Vitamin E deficiency also depletes histidine from muscle tissue.
>
>
>
> Histidine is known to be vital in the maintenance of the myelin sheaths surrounding nerves, particularly the auditory nerve and is used to treat some forms of hearing disability. Consequently, where there is a deficiency of histidine, nerve deafness is likely.
>
>
>
>
> Soviet scientists have tested over 25,000 different chemical substances and found that histidine and tryptophan are the only elements that provide significant protection against the effects of radiation.

### chocolate — 2012-06-23T00:42:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/digestion-brain-fog-histadine-and-the-urea-cycle.1991/post-33049>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22568620](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22568620)

> J Am Chem Soc. 2012 May 24. \[Epub ahead of print\]
>
> Thiamin Pyrimidine Biosynthesis in Candida albicans : A Remarkable Reaction between Histidine and Pyridoxal Phosphate.
>
> Lai RY, Huang S, Fenwick MK, Hazra A, Zhang Y, Rajashankar K, Philmus B, Kinsland C, Sanders JM, Ealick SE, Begley TP.
>
> Source
>
>
>
> Department of Chemistry, Texas A&M University , College Station, Texas 77843, United States.
>
> Abstract
>
>
>
> In Saccharomyces cerevisiae , thiamin pyrimidine is formed from histidine and pyridoxal phosphate (PLP). The origin of all of the pyrimidine atoms has been previously determined using labeling studies and suggests that the pyrimidine is formed using remarkable chemistry that is without chemical or biochemical precedent. **Here we report the overexpression of the closely related Candida albicans pyrimidine synthase (THI5p) and the reconstitution and preliminary characterization of the enzymatic activity. A structure of the C. albicans THI5p shows PLP bound at the active site via an imine with Lys62 and His66 in close proximity to the PLP. Our data suggest that His66 of the THI5 protein is the histidine source for pyrimidine formation and that the pyrimidine synthase is a single-turnover enzyme.**

### chocolate — 2012-06-23T00:51:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/digestion-brain-fog-histadine-and-the-urea-cycle.1991/post-33052>

[http://amy.wrytestuff.com/swa482190.htm](http://http://amy.wrytestuff.com/swa482190.htm)

> In chemical terms, minerals need an electrical charge to be absorbed and H.C.L. provides this electrical charge. Magnesium, calcium, iron, zinc, copper, chromium, selenium, manganese, vanadium, molybdenum, cobalt, several other trace minerals, as well as vitamins B6 and B12, are not well absorbed when there is not enough H.C.L. production by the stomach. Malnutrition then results.

# Thread: Leptin RX on vacation?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sem — 2012-06-22T07:57:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-32900>

What do you do when you are following the Leptin Reset and are on vacation? I'm going to be in a hotel for about five days and I am not sure how to do the BAB while there. I am following the AI protocol so don't do eggs. How do I start a big protein breakfast within 30 minutes? What have you done? Any tips and tricks are appreciated!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-06-22T18:46:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-32958>

I pre-cook burgers, bring liverwurst, small cans of salmon, coconut oil, and whatever else I can pack that I would eat. I ask the hotel for a refrigerator and I'm good to go. Oftentimes I can even get to a little store and pick up a salad if I feel I want more...I usually don't. I travel at least once a month and this has worked for me for the past six months. I also just get up and get that food in my stomach immediately upon wakening. Yeah, the food is cold but to me that isn't even a sacrifice as long as I can continue the proper eating.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-22T18:50:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-32960>

Not even sure why this is a question? You can eat the same no matter where you go......good food is there you just have to hunt and gather more.

### maggie — 2012-06-22T19:07:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-32964>

Hi Sem

On a recent trip to a city and in a hotel room with no fridge, I found a Whole Foods and bought cans of herring, tuna, and salmon as well as avocados and raddiccio / other raw greens. Also got some nice liver pate that I put on ice and ate right away because of no fridge. On an earlier kayaking trip I packed ghee, cans of coconut cream and a lot of US Wellness beef jerky in my suitcase since I wouldn't have access to a good store. Breakfast is easy cause you can eat it all up before you have to join the group if you're in one.

### BJK77 — 2012-06-22T22:09:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33011>

I always try to get a hotel room with a kitchenette. If that doesn't work out, take a cooler and just keep it filled with ice. You can get a cooktop relatively cheap on amazon. Yes, it's a bit of a pain and more to pack but so worth it to stick to the program.

Another option is to take or go to a nearby grocery store and get things that store at room temp like canned sardines, jerky, avocados, greens, etc. Many large chain grocery stores (I know Whole Foods, Harris Teeter and a few others) will actually steam shrimp and other seafood for you. I've done that many, many times! Makes for a great, quick dinner and if you plan ahead and have leftovers you can just keep it on ice for breakfast the next morning.

It's a pain, but really not that difficult to stick with it. Just requires some planning.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-22T22:28:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33015>

Well I am going to Disney world for two weeks in July and August and I wont buy one thing in their parks.....not one.

### PrimalPam — 2012-06-22T23:26:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33027>

I just spent 3 days/2 nights in Las Vegas and I lost 2 lbs. while eating amazing food there!

I ate two meals a day, no snacks, no alcohol (I'm doing CT and avoiding all alcohol so it also saved me $$ ! )

Each morning, I went to breakfast early and got:

3 eggs plus a side order of bacon and an order of sausage. I asked them to hold the bread, potatoes, etc, so I wouldn't be tempted. The super high amounts of protein & fat filled me up until around 3 or 4 each day.

For my early evening dinner, here were my meals:

1) Huge cobb salad (I subbed crabmeat for the chicken). I liked this so much, I ate it again at a different restaurant the next day.

2) Sturgeon & asparagus. Yum. Never had sturgeon before.

I was so satisfied on these 2 meals, and it occurred to me as I looked around, how much people eat and how often. And how, on previous trips, I was one of those who was constantly in "vacation" mode: Oh, look...I better eat one of THESE, right now! I'm on vacation!! Instead, I ate my Paleo meals, did my CT in the hotel tub, got a lot of sun, and reaaaally enjoyed myself.

This is the first time in my life (I'm 50!) that I've ever gone on vacation and not come home bloated, miserable, and + 5 lbs.

Now, the best thing to do would be to bring all your own food. Or, find a Whole Foods type-store in your destination city. But if you're traveling with others and that's not as feasible, you can definitely order sensibly.

### Sem — 2012-06-23T02:33:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33075>

Great suggestions! Thank you. I am going to cook up some food and either keep it in the fridge in the room or bring an icebox. My biggest concern was getting breakfast and that much protein into my body in time. I can manage ordering well for any other meals and I'm not really tempted by junk food anymore! We are going to Yosemite but staying at a very nice hotel right outside the park so I should be fine as long as I get some breakfast rather quickly. I was imagining huge room service bills trying to order steak every morning!  I hadn't really thought of precooking and bringing my own meat although it seems so obvious now! Thanks again!

And Disneyland/world really does have the worst food!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-23T02:47:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33080>

I will be chefing it up in Orlando.

### TerrierMom — 2012-06-23T04:56:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33109>

You can find keto food in Disney but as with all food not cooked by yourself, God knows what they've put in it to give it crack-like addiction!!! I'm a frequent Disneyland-goer and know where to find what I can eat...but it ain't optimal (except the seafood buffet at Club 33!)

### Owl — 2012-06-23T08:28:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33122>

> **Jack Kruse;33145 wrote:** Well I am going to Disney world for two weeks in July and August and I wont buy one thing in their parks.....not one.

2 entire weeks at Disney World!!?? What are you going to do there for that long?

### Shijin13 — 2012-06-23T16:27:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33133>

Its easy to travel and eat paleo and stay on the LR. BRING YOUR OWN FOOD... stay in an extended stay hotel that has a kitchen, buy good food at the local organic market...

even if that isn't an option...bring your coconut oil... eat 2-3 spoonfuls at eat meal, and eat the best LR/paleo foods you can find... they may not be optimal..but at least you'll stay on track..

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-23T16:47:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33143>

> **Owl;33254 wrote:** 2 entire weeks at Disney World!!?? What are you going to do there for that long?

relax and swim......go to the ice bar in orlando......and if any of you show up I may optimize you in the sun walrus style on the pool deck? I just need to get away for a time......and right now this would be a good time. This means I will be free a lot for consults......so when I am free I am not rushed for time.......this is why weekend consults rock too.

### PrimalPam — 2012-06-23T17:22:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33152>

While in Vegas, I had a treatment at the Qua Spa at Caesar's. Amongst the many hot tubs, saunas, etc., they had....an arctic room! It was probably below 50F in there. Unfortunately, I was in a hurry, but I did step in to see what it was. Very cool! (pun intended).

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-23T20:07:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33190>

> **PrimalPam;33284 wrote:** While in Vegas, I had a treatment at the Qua Spa at Caesar's. Amongst the many hot tubs, saunas, etc., they had....an arctic room! It was probably below 50F in there. Unfortunately, I was in a hurry, but I did step in to see what it was. Very cool! (pun intended).

OMG thanks for thos post. Doing a week in vegas in august. I wanted to find a cold tub! Was the room just cold or do they have a cold tub?

### PrimalPam — 2012-06-23T20:33:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33194>

> **Cavemam;33322 wrote:** OMG thanks for thos post. Doing a week in vegas in august. I wanted to find a cold tub! Was the room just cold or do they have a cold tub?

The room was freezing cold! There was no tub that I saw. But the room was built to look like a cave, very dark, very peaceful.

I ended up doing my CT that night in my hotel room with bags & bags of hotel ice!

### TerrierMom — 2012-06-23T23:32:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33217>

> **Jack Kruse;33275 wrote:** relax and swim......go to the ice bar in orlando......and if any of you show up I may optimize you in the sun walrus style on the pool deck? I just need to get away for a time......and right now this would be a good time. This means I will be free a lot for consults......so when I am free I am not rushed for time.......this is why weekend consults rock too.

What is this ice bar? Do tell! I'll earmark it for my next trip....

### MartiD — 2012-06-24T16:23:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33269>

I saw lots of advertising for the ice bar while in Vegas. It wasn't at a spa...actual bar completely made of ice. Photos showed people drinking martinis in full fur coats. They must provide the coats because nobody would need a coat out there now...at 108!

I was pleasantly surprised the pool water at hotel was nice and cold. Most of the people we were laying out with complained about it. I thought it felt great!

### MartiD — 2012-06-24T16:25:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33270>

# Thread: Low cost body composition monitors - how useful are they?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cost-body-composition-monitors-how-useful-are-they.2002/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Optimalbound — 2012-06-22T15:49:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cost-body-composition-monitors-how-useful-are-they.2002/post-32926>

I'd like to keep an eye on my changes in bodyfat percentage and body composition in general, and was wondering if products such as [Tanita's line of personal body composition monitors](http://http://www.tanita.com/en/personal/) are accurate enough to be useful for this purpose. Anyone who knows?

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-22T16:30:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cost-body-composition-monitors-how-useful-are-they.2002/post-32929>

> **Optimalbound;33056 wrote:** I'd like to keep an eye on my changes in bodyfat percentage and body composition in general, and was wondering if products such as [Tanita's line of personal body composition monitors](http://http://www.tanita.com/en/personal/) are accurate enough to be useful for this purpose. Anyone who knows?

They are notoriously inaccurate. I own one but don't even use it. I'm planning on getting a DEXA body comp scan. It's the most accurate and only cost $69........

### Ray — 2012-06-22T21:26:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cost-body-composition-monitors-how-useful-are-they.2002/post-32990>

Of the cheap measuring techniques, calipers are the best. Here is a cheap, but effective one for example: [http://www.amazon.com/Accu-Measure-Fitness-3000-Personal-Tester/dp/B000G7YW74/ref=sr\_1\_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1340389338&sr=8-2&keywords=body+fat+caliper](http://http://www.amazon.com/Accu-Measure-Fitness-3000-Personal-Tester/dp/B000G7YW74/ref=sr_1_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1340389338&sr=8-2&keywords=body+fat+caliper)

Here is a site with 6 methods for determining body fat percentage: [http://www.linear-software.com/online.html](http://http://www.linear-software.com/online.html)

I have one of the body-fat measuring scales, but it varies quite a bit depending on how dry the skin on your feet is and your body's hydration level. The calipers are much better.

### Optimalbound — 2012-06-24T23:18:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cost-body-composition-monitors-how-useful-are-they.2002/post-33349>

Thanks, Ray and luvmyzoe! Very useful.

### vlynnb — 2012-06-24T23:56:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cost-body-composition-monitors-how-useful-are-they.2002/post-33356>

What type of facility offers HEXA scans?

# Thread: Wondering if I should try again
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-if-i-should-try-again.2008/>
**Subforum:** HCG Protocol
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DarleenMB — 2012-06-22T23:05:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-if-i-should-try-again.2008/post-33025>

OK I've been skimming through some of the current posts and am now thinking maybe I should try hcg again. When I first tried it 3? years ago it took me 8 months to drop 20 pounds. This was after taking 18 months on the Jenny Craig diet (1200 cal a day of a typical SAD diet) to lose a measly 58 pounds.

I have been keeping to the leptin reset for months now with no success. Just had a blood draw to check for food allergies and will know in a week just what I should be avoiding. I'm positive eggs, nuts and fish will be on the list.

So. Any help or advice would be greatly appreciated. I'm looking up my old ordering info for the hcg but need to know where to get the back water. I think I still have 6 sterile bottles for mixing and a box of syringes left from the last abortive attempts to get this to work.

thanks!

ETA

Of the two places I had bought HcG from one is out of business and on the other I cannot find it listed anywhere. Anyone have any pointers?

### indigogirl — 2012-06-23T00:08:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-if-i-should-try-again.2008/post-33037>

Darleen...you might want to move this to the HCG thread...Colleen Coble is very knowledgeable, she is a moderator on the big HCG Forum and posts on the HCG threads here frequently.

It's very weird that it took 8 months on HCG to lose 20 lbs. I've never heard of anyone losing so slowly on HCG.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-23T00:29:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-if-i-should-try-again.2008/post-33042>

I'm sorry I thought this WAS the hcg forum. didn't know there was a specific thread.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-23T00:30:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-if-i-should-try-again.2008/post-33043>

And there were a LOT of us barely losing at all. I was frequenting the HTA forums then.

### indigogirl — 2012-06-23T04:41:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-if-i-should-try-again.2008/post-33103>

> **DarleenMB;33172 wrote:** I'm sorry I thought this WAS the hcg forum. didn't know there was a specific thread.

Oops...my bad...you're in the right place.

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-23T04:45:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-if-i-should-try-again.2008/post-33108>

> **DarleenMB;33173 wrote:** And there were a LOT of us barely losing at all. I was frequenting the HTA forums then.

Did you pinpoint anything that could have confounded the weight loss? I know there is a TON of "follow pounds and inches!!!!" dogma about, but one thing that does seem to be in play in all the stuff I've read lately (and I read EVERYTHING before deciding to do this) is that those who follow the protacol with the injections seem to lose, and the ones that don't have some variations that are not on the original plan.

I don't for a minute think it's all 100% correct in the WHY's it works, or that there are not hacks you can do to it while it keeps working. . . .so I'm just being curious. I want to understand what might cause someone to not workout on this.

Keep me posted when you start!!!

### Pamela — 2012-06-23T06:15:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-if-i-should-try-again.2008/post-33115>

The HCG protocol needs to be followed to the letter for it to work. Many will substitute or add protein or this or that and I really disagree. I've done the HCG program twice and lost well both times and have been able to maintain by doing the LX Reset. Do not under any circumstances add oil to the HCG protocol. Read the Dr. Simeons manuscript and follow it and you will lose. But no cheats, not a single one at all. Just make the commitment for the 35 days and then do it. Don't do the program if you have a wedding coming up or a vacation. Do it when you have the time and no chance to fall off. Both times I did the program I did it 100% by the Dr. Simeons manuscript. The only thing I did, and this was ok, was to not eat the melba and many days I only ate 1 piece of fruit. I'm not sure it helped much though.

Some months ago some of the online pharmacy's stopped selling HCG so you may need to get it from a Dr. here in the States. It is available but it will cost a bit more. Just don't let a Dr. tell you you can eat broccoli or cauliflower or anything else not on the list of permitted foods.

Hope this helps. Load really well, it will be a huge factor in your success.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-23T18:50:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-if-i-should-try-again.2008/post-33176>

I did follow it exactly and I was using injections. Buying from a us physician is out of the question. The darned stuff 3 years ago was sold for about $15 an amp with a 'suggested retail' of $150 or something like that. It is RIDICULOUS what they charge.

I ordered from a place in Canada which I just found out is now where ADC is selling. I hope I didn't just get taken. I may try another suggestion I found elsewhere. But thanks anyway.

### Owl — 2012-06-23T19:55:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-if-i-should-try-again.2008/post-33188>

There are quite a few places selling hcg that have problems with credit cards and security. Always get a "temporary" card number from your credit card company for ordering, or use a prepaid card. Too many people have been the victims of stolen numbers.

### colleencoble — 2012-06-24T04:27:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-if-i-should-try-again.2008/post-33241>

Try ReliableRX.com. They are a sister company to ADC but DO use the temporary card number. What dose were you on? A few years ago everyone was doing 200 iu and taking the wrong dose plays a BIG role in poor losses. Also you might have an insulin issue and 2 fruits a day is just too many for you. You might need to sub out one fruit for an extra serving of protein. Good luck!

Sorry, I didn't see this until now. I've been at my publishers all week and just got home. I have my new book in my hot little hands.  Been a good week but I've missed you all!

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-24T19:21:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-if-i-should-try-again.2008/post-33312>

Yay on the book, Colleen! And thanks for stopping by.

I ordered from ReliableRxPHARMACY are they the same outfit? never mind I'll just go google it. Just answered my own ? ...yes they are. And no I didn't use a temporary card number. I'd ordered twice before from ADC with nary a hitch. And I check my statement rigorously every month because I never carry a balance. If something hinky shows up I'm on the phone to the cc company STAT.

My dose was in the standard range but never 200 ... actually it might have been a titch too low. I think I was using 125 iu. Fruits were absolutely a problem esp since I was eating those GINORMOUS gala apples that Costco was selling at the time. I think one of them equaled 4 regular size apples. I had the runs for a couple of weeks then was totally constipated after that.

I know, TMI.

I'm waiting on the results of the RAST test (should have them late next week I hope) as I will not begin the hcg until I know what I am allergic to ... I'm pretty sure eggs and nuts and some white fish but I need to know for sure.

I am getting really excited to do this again with the fervent hope that this time it will work. I've been low carbing for 18 months and sticking (mostly) to the leptin rx for 8 months so I think my body is ready. I hope it is. I'm going to stick absolutely to his original plan. 21 days on and then off onto a solid low carb plan. If I need it I will ease back to the reset. I'm going to take this slow and see what happens. If I need to do another round I'll have plenty of supplies. I ordered 5 ampoules 5000 iu each.

FLAB BEGONE!

### colleencoble — 2012-06-24T20:48:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-if-i-should-try-again.2008/post-33323>

Honey, that test isn't going to tell you much. Truly. Even Dr. Kruse says it's a waste of money.  125 might not have been too low. If you had no hunger, then it was fine. Good luck!

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-27T02:09:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-if-i-should-try-again.2008/post-33808>

Thanks for the input but my doc has been specializing in allergies for several decades. I trust him.

And I DID have hunger. I toughed it out for 8 months and still only lost barely 20 pounds. You cannot say I don't have will power.

And I went to check on my order through reliable rx only to find they had doubled it with yet another order... I had started the process because I couldn't find my original order but NEVER COMPLETED IT. So anyone ordering from them be EXTREMELY careful. I've emailed them to cancel the 2nd order. Sure hope somebody sees it in time. I don't need 10 amps of the stuff. 5 will probably be too much as it is.

### colleencoble — 2012-06-29T04:11:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-if-i-should-try-again.2008/post-34310>

You were on hcg for 8 mos? Holy cow, that is not allowed! Your poor body.

Do what you want with the allergy info but it's likely going to show you're allergic to much more than you really or are allergic to things that you aren't. I see it all the time. And as I said, Dr. Kruse says they are useless too.

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-29T04:24:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-if-i-should-try-again.2008/post-34312>

+1 on the allergy testing being useless. I apparently can eat everything I know for sure I'm allergic to.....

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-29T05:52:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-if-i-should-try-again.2008/post-34319>

fwiw on the allergy blood spot test (which is the one mentioned here) it\`s supposed to only pick up foods that you have consumed recently and are reacting to. the food I reacted highest to on the test was duck egg a food I have never eaten

### LisaAPB — 2012-06-29T06:44:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-if-i-should-try-again.2008/post-34324>

Darlene, if by chance you do end up with 10 amps, shoot me a PM. I was going to order some myself but haven't got around to it yet.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-30T06:09:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-if-i-should-try-again.2008/post-34539>

Ok Lisa.

and no I did NOT use the hcg every single day for 8 months. I DID read the protocol. I did several rounds and having reread the protocol realize now I should not have listened to all the "experts" and taken a nice long break between rounds. But it still took that long to get off 20 pounds.

As to the RAST test ... to each their own. As it turns out I tested NOT allergic to any foods. So I now suspect I must have had some kind of bug after all. However, the skin tests for seasonal allergies told another story altogether. Trees, grasses, molds and cats AND dogs. I didn't want to have to drive to town twice a week to get shots so have opted instead for the drops. Sure hope they can at least keep my reactions in the spring and fall down to a low roar.

# Thread: Self Medicating with HC
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/self-medicating-with-hc.2009/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2012-06-23T03:09:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/self-medicating-with-hc.2009/post-33083>

Looking for input on best type and suggested starting dose for HC. Unfortunately my doctor will not budge at all on the HC issue. His concern is that too many patients have become dependent on it and not able to wean off the HC. Based on some feedback I got from Dr. K I think I need to at least give a small dose a try.

I was given this site as a possibility for ordering it without a prescription: [http://www.inhousepharmacy.biz/search.aspx?SearchTerm=hydrocortisone](http://http://www.inhousepharmacy.biz/search.aspx?SearchTerm=hydrocortisone)

For those of you who are on it, which type are you taking? Cream/pills? Have you experienced any side effects? Benefits? Thanks!

### vkiernan — 2012-06-23T05:29:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/self-medicating-with-hc.2009/post-33112>

I started on Isocort and it messed with my stomach (lactose intolerant). Then switched to bio-identical HC and that went much better. Interestingly, after a short bit of time I did pretty well then not so much. I have been on it for 2.5 months and am now trying to wean off of it for some tests to see if my pituitary if functioning properly. 2nd week of down-dosing and I am crashing. I did pretty well the first week but the last couple of days after dropping it further I am not doing so well. I really want CT to work but now not so sure that is enough. I also gained 5 lbs in that time and that is another reason I don't want to be on it. My mood hasn't been great all week either. I started with 32.5 then dropped to 22.5 and now to a total of 10 mg for the day. I wish you luck.

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-23T16:55:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/self-medicating-with-hc.2009/post-33145>

if you are going to self dose with HC you may want to join the Yahoo adrenal fatigue group run by Val (same owner as the RT3 group) they are very experienced at self treat on and off HC

### vkiernan — 2012-06-23T17:45:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/self-medicating-with-hc.2009/post-33155>

Thanks Colleen for the input. I might join just to get more info. I am not self-dosing but I would like more info anyway. Depending how bad I crash, I might go back on it (but a smaller dose if I can) after the tests. I really would like another salivary assay done to see where things are before I treat again though

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-23T18:26:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/self-medicating-with-hc.2009/post-33164>

My saliva test last year revealed my cortisol levels were almost flat lined throughout the day. My doctor prescribed 5mg Cortef.

Within 3 days of taking it I stated to gain weight, probably water weight but it freaked me out. I stopped taking it and the next time I saw my doc, she told me to try taking half a pill. Tried that, but still gained weight, so I stopped taking it altogether.

At my last appointment in May, I told my doc I wasn't taking it anymore and she was okay with that. I have been eating paleo style for a year now, doing the LRx since January and feel so much better anyway, so I don't think I need anymore. I used HATE getting out of bed in the morning and would sleep in to 9-10am on the weekends. I noticed during this last two weeks that I don't feel the need to sleep in on the weekends anymore. In fact, I'm getting up around 6am but lounging in bed until 7am and feel good.

So in short, I think it just takes time to heal those adrenals, but I don't think it's necessary to take HC, but Dr. K can give you the definitive answer.

Grace

### bigknitwit — 2012-06-23T18:38:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/self-medicating-with-hc.2009/post-33168>

If you are crashing, I would recommend going a little slower. On the other hand, I have found CT to be a great step in for tired adrenals, especially if you are just trying to push through and get off the HC so you can do the ASI. Mine are crashing right now due to low thyroid dose, and the CT really obliterates the bulk of the symptoms, at least for a few hours. Meanwhile, I am raising my Armour. I keep the tub filled, and some days I am in there 3-4 times for 20ish minutes (until kids are declaring war downstairs!).

I originally got off too because I was concerned the HC was causing my runaway weight gain. I found out that it wasn't. Inflammatory foods were doing it, and there are a lot of them for me.

> **vkiernan;33243 wrote:** I started on Isocort and it messed with my stomach (lactose intolerant). Then switched to bio-identical HC and that went much better. I did pretty well the first week but the last couple of days after dropping it further I am not doing so well. I really want CT to work but now not so sure that is enough. I also gained 5 lbs in that time and that is another reason I don't want to be on it. My mood hasn't been great all week either. I started with 32.5 then dropped to 22.5 and now to a total of 10 mg for the day. I wish you luck.

### vkiernan — 2012-06-23T19:17:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/self-medicating-with-hc.2009/post-33182>

> **bigknitwit;33300 wrote:** If you are crashing, I would recommend going a little slower. On the other hand, I have found CT to be a great step in for tired adrenals, especially if you are just trying to push through and get off the HC so you can do the ASI. Mine are crashing right now due to low thyroid dose, and the CT really obliterates the bulk of the symptoms, at least for a few hours. Meanwhile, I am raising my Armour. I keep the tub filled, and some days I am in there 3-4 times for 20ish minutes (until kids are declaring war downstairs!).
>
>
>
> I originally got off too because I was concerned the HC was causing my runaway weight gain. I found out that it wasn't. Inflammatory foods were doing it, and there are a lot of them for me.

I believe I have dropped a lb. or 2 this past week since cutting the dose. I'm pretty sure that was it. My experience has been that I have been eating this way for a year and was primal 2 years before that.

How warm does your water get by leaving it in the tub? I would think it would get very warm like 70ish. I suppose that might still be good enough for an evening soak. Do you add ice at that point?

I hear ya on CT. It is helping. I am tired by 9 and in bed by 9:30 usually at the latest and wake between 5 & 6 everyday. Getting out of bed is not a problem at all. Since I have been on DHEA and preg. my sleep is so much better now. CT has probably helped with this too. I am warm all the time now too. I run the air in the car on 60 and it doesn't feel very cold to me. My dh says I am warmer for sure. Something is working. Now if I could only get the mood in line. I'm sure the af is affecting my mood. Seems like now that is my biggest problem. It does not take much to set me off and I have a hard time swinging it back to positive. I know it is hormonal, etc. cuz I am not like this everyday. But, according to Dr. K, gotta work on 1 thing at a time. In my case, I have to check the gut out completely. Then the adrenals, etc.

BJK77 -I would be careful self-dosing and keep a journal. Also, might want to keep a journal and not change anything else for a while. You have to know what is doing what. So don't add supps, change diet, etc. for at least a few weeks to be able to pinpoint any changes happening. I wish I would have realized how much weight I gained but just didn't pay attn. to it and I don't own a scale. You can buy Isocort over the net easily. Read the label though. It is cheap compared to what I pay for Bio-HC. Glad to be going off of it for that reason alone. Ins. won't pay for it either.

### bigknitwit — 2012-06-24T15:15:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/self-medicating-with-hc.2009/post-33266>

> **vkiernan;33314 wrote:** I believe I have dropped a lb. or 2 this past week since cutting the dose. I'm pretty sure that was it. My experience has been that I have been eating this way for a year and was primal 2 years before that.
>
>
>
> How warm does your water get by leaving it in the tub? I would think it would get very warm like 70ish. I suppose that might still be good enough for an evening soak. Do you add ice at that point?

We have well water, so it's about 54 degrees when the tub is first filled. But then if I want it to "keep" for longer, I add the cryopacs. I have lots of them. Large flat square ones about 1.5 inches thick. They cool it a little more of course, and it takes them some time to do so, and they also act a bit like an insulating blanket on top of the water, protecting it from the warmer air. The cryopacs don't effect the temp nearly at quickly as pure ice, but they don't go down the drain at the end, and the insulating thing is useful! By the later afternoon, the water might be 58ish degrees, which isn't optimal, but still works to raise my blood pressure and lower heartrate - basically normalize me. Then I take the kids out to the pool and sit in there. This week it's gotten really warm (pool is 81), but I find even that has a little bit of impact. The cooling vest doesn't seem to do it in any way that I can feel.

# Thread: Have any of you read Ishmael by Daniel Quinn?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-any-of-you-read-ishmael-by-daniel-quinn.2012/>
**Subforum:** Factor X
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### janagram — 2012-06-23T04:06:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-any-of-you-read-ishmael-by-daniel-quinn.2012/post-33097>

This book is very visionary ... using Old Testament stories to show how agriculture was not the way to go.....it's a winner!

### Nate A — 2012-07-08T07:19:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-any-of-you-read-ishmael-by-daniel-quinn.2012/post-36185>

Great, great book. The whole series is worth reading. I read Ishmael right as I was starting Paleo/Primal, and it all just made sense and clicked. I need to re-read it now that it's been two years. I may take some new meaning from it now, as I have learned so much in this time.

May I suggest another book along the lines of Ishmael that I enjoyed even more - it's called "The Gnoll Credo" and is a bit more poignant and cuts to the chase. The author, J. Stanton, does a great job of not only emphasizing the benefits of a paleo-type diet, but ties it all together in to a complete lifestyle. The book really hit me hard. I plan on re-reading here soon. [Click here to read about the book on Amazon.com](http://http://www.amazon.com/Gnoll-Credo-J-Stanton/dp/0982667132) and learn more at [Gnolls.org](http://http://www.gnolls.org/)

### Tizawee — 2012-07-08T16:17:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-any-of-you-read-ishmael-by-daniel-quinn.2012/post-36197>

> **Nate A;36351 wrote:** ...and learn more at [Gnolls.org](http://http://www.gnolls.org/)

Thanks for the link, Nate A. I read the blog entitled "Eat Like A Predator, Not Like Prey" to DH this morning. It really brought some paleo ideas into focus for him: why we now eat so much fish, why I won't spend grocery money on milk or juice (if he wants it, he buys it from his own money), and why we have no more snacks in the house. I liked it so well I shared it on FB and am ordering "The Gnoll Credo" as soon as I have some extra money.

### LinD — 2012-07-08T17:04:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-any-of-you-read-ishmael-by-daniel-quinn.2012/post-36200>

The Gnoll book isn't available for Nook or Kindle.... :-(
