# Thread: Glucose deprivation activates a metabolic and signaling amplification loop leading to
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glucose-deprivation-activates-a-metabolic-and-signaling-amplification-loop-leading-to.2093/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-06-28T23:06:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glucose-deprivation-activates-a-metabolic-and-signaling-amplification-loop-leading-to.2093/post-34229>

Glucose deprivation activates a metabolic and signaling amplification loop leading to cell death

[http://www.nature.com/msb/journal/v8/n1/full/msb201220.html?WT.mc\_id=FBK\_NPG](http://http://www.nature.com/msb/journal/v8/n1/full/msb201220.html?WT.mc_id=FBK_NPG)

> **In cancer cells dependent upon glucose for survival, glucose withdrawal activates a positive feedback loop involving reactive oxygen species (ROS), ROS-mediated inhibition of tyrosine phosphatases, and tyrosine kinase signaling. This loop amplifies ROS to toxic levels, resulting in cell death.**
>
>
>
> In cancer cell lines dependent on glucose for survival, glucose withdrawal induces supra-physiological levels of phospho-tyrosine signaling, even in cells expressing constitutively active tyrosine kinases.
>
> Unbiased, mass spectrometry-based phospho-tyrosine profiling demonstrates that glucose withdrawal induces a unique signature of phospho-tyrosine signaling associated with focal adhesions.
>
> The glucose withdrawal-induced phospho-tyrosine signature results from a positive feedback loop in which reactive oxygen species (ROS) oxidize and inhibit protein tyrosine phosphatases, causing increased tyrosine kinase signaling, thereby inducing further ROS generation until cells undergo ROS-mediated cell death.
>
> The glucose withdrawal-initiated positive feedback loop illustrates the complex, systems-level integration of metabolism and tyrosine kinase signaling in cancer cell homeostasis.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Sources of Seafood for Those Who Live Far From the Sea
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sources-of-seafood-for-those-who-live-far-from-the-sea.2095/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Chrysanthemama — 2012-06-28T23:19:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sources-of-seafood-for-those-who-live-far-from-the-sea.2095/post-34234>

Does anyone have reasonably-priced sources of seafood they could recommend? I am in the mountains of NM, in a one-grocery town, and the quality of seafood here is the pits. I am considering doing bulk purchases of salmon, cod, halibut, and crab from AK or WA, but I thought I would ask to see if anyone is already doing this or something similar, and could make recommendations.

I found companies like this: [http://www.freshseafood.com/fresh-salmon.html](http://http://www.freshseafood.com/fresh-salmon.html)

which look awesome, but I'd also hope for a wholesale source, if possible. For the past two years I've bought a half a grass-fed, high-altitude raised steer, but this year we have permits for elk, so hopefully we'll be successful, and I'm thinking I could invest the grass-fed budget into seafood.

### vlynnb — 2012-06-28T23:30:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sources-of-seafood-for-those-who-live-far-from-the-sea.2095/post-34238>

Good question from my standpoint in the Rocky Mts. also. I will be interested in the responses. I also have the problem of budget in regards to purchasing the seafood. Your plan to hunt Elk has got me thinking. I grew up on deer and elk meat but as an adult I have an aversion to killing. I have always felt if I could buy beef instead that was somehow more acceptable to me. That could come from the incident when I shot my first deer, a little doe, that turned and looked at me with her big, soft, brown eyes just as I fired. I cried and have never hunted since. Maybe now that I can't afford the grass fed beef and good seafood I can justify hunting again and do as you suggest with applying the money saved to a seafood budget.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-29T01:42:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sources-of-seafood-for-those-who-live-far-from-the-sea.2095/post-34276>

Sardines in a can are available everywhere and dirt cheap.

### BJK77 — 2012-06-29T03:19:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sources-of-seafood-for-those-who-live-far-from-the-sea.2095/post-34301>

I have a local buying club I purchase wild Alaskan salmon through and I get some other random types like shrimp, crab, scallops etc at Whole Foods or Trader Joe's, but I've also stocked up on canned sardines, salmon and tuna from amazon. I like the brand Wild Planet - no bpa and good quality, sustainable products. You can get some decent deals too if you do the subscribe and save option: [http://www.amazon.com/s/ref=nb\_sb\_noss\_2?url=search-alias%3Daps&field-keywords=wild+planet](http://http://www.amazon.com/s/ref=nb_sb_noss_2?url=search-alias%3Daps&field-keywords=wild+planet)

### Chrysanthemama — 2012-06-29T18:45:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sources-of-seafood-for-those-who-live-far-from-the-sea.2095/post-34378>

Ahh...sardines. Hadn't even considered them. And oysters, too, I presume. I'm on it!

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-06-30T23:56:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sources-of-seafood-for-those-who-live-far-from-the-sea.2095/post-34682>

Here are my sources for canned and frozen products. Unfortunately, they are for western Canada.

[http://eatkamloops.org/searching-for-seafood/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/searching-for-seafood/)

We also make regular trips to the coast and find local sources. Would this be possible for you to do? If so, you can find amazing sources. We just pick up 10 sockeye salmon from a Native food fishery, frozen prawns loaded with roe, and oysters picked off the beach. I can't begin to tell you how great it is to get the whole fish. You can eat all the "waste" parts of the fish such as: bones, head, roe and organs. Fillets and steaks become almost mundane after eating the waste parts. You can also collect seaweed and sea grass and dry them for later use.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34842=288-native-food-fishery.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34842=289-whole-sockeye-salmon.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34842=290-iced-oysters.jpg)

Have you considered taking up fishing or finding a fisherman friend? In our local lakes around Kamloops we have beautiful trout. I am lucky to have fishermen friends that gift me trout on a regular basis.

### RobH — 2012-09-10T21:40:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sources-of-seafood-for-those-who-live-far-from-the-sea.2095/post-50766>

Do trout count as seafood? Lake water very different than ocean water. I'm just wondering where fresh water fish fit on the Eli-paleo template.

RobH, eating trout for lunch

### nonchalant — 2012-09-10T22:26:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sources-of-seafood-for-those-who-live-far-from-the-sea.2095/post-50772>

I was thinking about this issue for when I move from the city. I think I will be stocking various sorts of canned/frozen seafood available locally, and I will be making periodic trips to the Big City to visit relatives and fill the ice chests. Love that dry ice.

RobH, fresh water fish definitely count, along with crawfish and such. But I wouldn't want them as my only source.

### Jonathin — 2012-09-10T22:39:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sources-of-seafood-for-those-who-live-far-from-the-sea.2095/post-50775>

After searching for seafood options , I discovered THE seafood wholesaler for western Colorado about 45 min away. They sell to all the resorts and high end restaurants and they sell from a small shop to the public 3 days per week. They definitely have a wide range of product. For instance I was able to get a 1/2 gal of refrigerated shelled oysters for $40. I am hoping soon to get a substantial quantity of some fish that is in season, wild-caught, high DHA, etc for a discounted price and stash in one of my freezers like the 1/2 a cow or pig that we usually get. Otherwise I would agree that canned sardines (and canned mackerl) are a good buy, readily available and are not co-mingled with junk ingredients.

### btwimberly — 2012-09-16T06:44:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sources-of-seafood-for-those-who-live-far-from-the-sea.2095/post-51906>

### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-16T14:37:09+0300

### Momma28 — 2012-09-16T16:02:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sources-of-seafood-for-those-who-live-far-from-the-sea.2095/post-51920>

The prices on fish are ridiculous! The only option for our family will have to be high quality fish oils. Dr. K, is their one you like best? Currently using Barleans Salmon oil and Mercola's Krill w/ Evening Primrose. Six kids still at home and one in college keeps the budget pretty tight. We do grassfed beef ( local farmer slaughters for us each year) and pastured chickens and sometimes eggs for our protein. I can get some fish at Sam's Club once in a while, but it doesn't seem to hold the kids and I have to cook so much of it to satisfy them. Everyone eats Paleo so no grains or sugars for any of us. We have done the Specific Carbohydrate Diet since 2007 for a couple of the kids that are on the spectrum and it works out fine for us and is pretty much Paleo style.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-16T17:42:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sources-of-seafood-for-those-who-live-far-from-the-sea.2095/post-51927>

The prices of modern medicine are even more sick. Do what you must. Sardines are dirt cheap! High quality fish oils are not going to help you.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-16T17:46:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sources-of-seafood-for-those-who-live-far-from-the-sea.2095/post-51928>

Here are two comments I made at the blog over a year ago about this issue:

Jack says

My concern is that fish oil supplements are becoming the “proxy” for good dietary choices in our

paleo/primal community (see Wolf's ridiculous fish oil rec's in his book). Once you get your diet repaired and back to ancestral balances of O6/O3 fats, then you can titrate the effective dose of supplemental O3 oils to your desired O6/O3 ratio

based upon your own blood work. This is the context I use for myself and patients.

Jack says:

Im a large fan of obtaining O3 from **food first as you know. But those who paleo with regular grain feed meats need to do something. And same thing with farmed fish……but sardines are dirt cheap. The only people I push fish oil with are the real sick ones. Jack realize who I see in my clinic is not representative of most people on MDA or PH etc…….You guys are way better off than most of my patients. I think y'all forget that context sometime.** Those people who usually have severe pregnenolone steal or seriously overweight need drastic refurbishing. there is no way to avoid the fish oil there. I am very cautious with fish oil in neurodegenerative diseases for the reason mentioned in the blog (AHS 2011 blog). Again this is not who we see on PH or MDA either

We do grassfed beef ( local farmer slaughters for us each year) and pastured chickens and sometimes eggs for our protein. I can get some fish at Sam's Club once in a while, but it doesn't seem to hold the kids and I have to cook so much of it to satisfy them. Everyone eats Paleo so no grains or sugars for any of us. We have done the Specific Carbohydrate Diet since 2007 for a couple of the kids that are on the spectrum and it works out fine for us and is pretty much Paleo style.
>
>
> Jack Kruse;52542 wrote:** The prices of modern medicine are even more sick. Do what you must. Sardines are dirt cheap! High quality fish oils are not going to help you.

Can I try an alternative answer?

Momma28 - if your family's health is great, you are doing just fine. Teach your kids to make good food decisions and hopefully they will live long, healthy lives. If any of you have health issues, examine your diet, light exposure, and seasonal rythms. If none of you have health issues, try to eat seafood several times a week, even if just canned sardines. If you are doing all this and your hormones/labs aren't where you want, then consider adding in way more seafood and wearing the crazy yellow glasses while eating seasonally and getting cold. Good luck and let your labs be your guide.

### Optimalbound — 2012-09-16T19:59:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sources-of-seafood-for-those-who-live-far-from-the-sea.2095/post-51950>

> **jib;52559 wrote:** I was wondering if someone could please translate this from a can of King Oscar cod livers I just received in the mail:
>
>
>
> Ficat de cod - 58%, sare, foi de dafin si piper negru - 42%
>
> Greutatea neta:121g masa neta a componetei solide:60g.
>
> Temperatura de pastrare de la +4C la +25C.
>
> A se consuma de preferinta inainte de data: vezi capacul.
>
>
>
> Thanks much
>
>
>
>
> According to [Google Translate](http://http://translate.google.no/?hl=no&tab=wT#auto/en/Ficat%20de%20cod%20-%2058%25%2C%20sare%2C%20foi%20de%20dafin%20si%20piper%20negru%20-%2042%25%0D%0A%20Greutatea%20neta%3A121g%20masa%20neta%20a%20componetei%20solide%3A60g.%0D%0A%20Temperatura%20de%20pastrare%20de%20la%20%2B4C%20la%20%2B25C.%0D%0A%20A%20se%20consuma%20de%20preferinta%20inainte%20de%20data%3A%20vezi%20capacul.), that's Romanian for: Cod liver - 58%, salt, bay leaf and black pepper - 42%
>
> Net weight: 121g net weight of solid Components: 60g.
>
> Storage temperature from +4 C to +25 C.
>
> To be consumed preferably before date: see lid.

That's rather a lot of bay leaf and pepper.. This sounds like a different product than the cans of King Oscar "Lofot" canned cod liver oil I have in my fridge. The label on that says "Ingredients: Fresh cod liver. Salt" and then goes on to list the contents, per 100g:

Total fats: 60g

Saturated: 10g

Monounsaturated: 7.5g

Polyunsaturated: 22g

of which Omega-3: 7g

Salt: 0.7g

Protein: 7g

Carbs: 0%

I also checked a can of Bornholms (a Danish brand) canned cod liver, which was also contained only liver and salt, but with a bit different numbers:

Total fats: 40g

Saturated: 6.5g

Monounsaturated: 10.9g

Polyunsaturated: 13g

Omega-3: 9.1g

Protein: 8g

Carbs: 1g

(I read that as saying that total polysaturated is 9.1+13g)

A while back, I wrote to King Oscar customer support and asked them about BPA, and they informed me that all their cans are guaranteed not to contain any BPA. I did not ask if that holds true for all markets as well as for Norway. As they seem to have production facilities in many countries, so I guess there could be some variation in terms of compliance.

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-16T23:46:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sources-of-seafood-for-those-who-live-far-from-the-sea.2095/post-51980>

> **btwimberly;52520 wrote:** Jonathin. Can you say more about your Colorado seafood source? I am in Denver and would love to find raw unsalted unprocessed roe.

For Colorado - A couple of the gals on here have mentioned H-mart (I've never been there!).

I have a place here in Longmont, where they will order whatever you want - and they stock quite a bit. It's all sustainable etc. . . but it's not cheap. Although, cheaper than whole foods most of the time.

We have a fun restaurant here that does $1 oysters for happy hour (same price I see them at WF and my fish place). . . so come on up for a happy hour

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-09-16T23:52:59+0300

### Momma28 — 2012-09-19T01:48:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sources-of-seafood-for-those-who-live-far-from-the-sea.2095/post-52564>

> **AKMan;52561 wrote:** Can I try an alternative answer?
>
>
>
> Momma28 - if your family's health is great, you are doing just fine. Teach your kids to make good food decisions and hopefully they will live long, healthy lives. If any of you have health issues, examine your diet, light exposure, and seasonal rythms. If none of you have health issues, try to eat seafood several times a week, even if just canned sardines. If you are doing all this and your hormones/labs aren't where you want, then consider adding in way more seafood and wearing the crazy yellow glasses while eating seasonally and getting cold. Good luck and let your labs be your guide.

Thanks AKman...very encouraging post. A mother beats herself up enough when it comes to her children. I probably fair better than most, though. I have 8 of them and they have never been sickly kids. We stopped vaccines 15 years ago and 5 or the youngest 6 are nonvaxed except for the HepB given in the hospital against wishes. The youngest is addopted and has had 22 before we got him. He is full autistic. Didn't even know his name at 16 months old. It took me a week to change his diet of white bread toast, bananas and commercial milk microwaved in a bottle. No eye contact, no physical affection, just living in his own world. I put him on the Specific Carb Diet He had never had meat and was having minor seizures from lack of dietary fats. He had spastic palsy like movements with his hands also. Seizures stopped in first week with addition of organic butter and other fatty foods such as avocado. He wouldn't eat for three days because it wasn't his diet of choice. He finally gave in after waking up in a full shake from low blood sugar. Then he was ready to eat anything. Now at 23 months he's happy, no more spastic movements, plenty of eye contact and loves affection and attention. He has grown 5 inches in the 7 months I've dhad him, knows his name and is learning all of our names...no easy feat even for an adult. lol I paint him with Lugol's solution and magnesium oil as well. His Vit D3 is optimized and he wears a B12 patch by Vie once a week. I'm already doing everything I can and know to do and I'm still learning. Everybody that saw this little boy when he got here can't believe its the same child. Our other kids never have more than a cold. They had chicken pox once years ago. That's it in 22 years of parenting. They see the pediatrician at one week old and never see him again until I have another baby and I won't be having anymore. I have 12 year olds in my home who have NEVER been on an antibiotic. Never even had an ear infection. So I must be doing something right. I hate fish and have to make myself eat it so I used to rarely make it for the family. But I have made an effort to cook it more often. I have just always added a 15% Lugols Solution I had custom mixed for me from Ecuador...( herbhealers.com, once it became illegal here and I couldn't get it from Walmart pharmacy anymore) ... to fresh squeezed lemonaid or just painted it on their feet and thyroid area. I cured myself of uterine fibroids, fibrocystic breasts, PCOS and a goiter on my thyroid with it as well. Every bit of it gone. It may not be OPTIMAL but it works and is doable.

### caroline — 2012-09-19T02:24:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sources-of-seafood-for-those-who-live-far-from-the-sea.2095/post-52571>

### Momma28 — 2012-09-20T00:20:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sources-of-seafood-for-those-who-live-far-from-the-sea.2095/post-52791>

Hi Caroline,

No, I wasn't raised healthy at all....mother was a single parent, welfare recipient, alcoholic and fed us out of cans or boxes. When I became a mother I wanted to be everything she wasn't. Somehow I just knew home cooking was best and it just must be God that has led me to ditch the vaccines and buck the system. I've had Docs get up in my face and challenge me on the vaccine issues and never backed down because I armed myself with information to fight with. I felt down inside that something was terribly wrong with the system and I am grateful every day that I saved my kids from any further damage. I haven't had a vax since 5 years old. Back then you got four or five of them when you were ready for kindergarten. That was it. Now it's at 36 and counting by 4 years of age. So, to answer your question...it must have been instinct. I new before I married that I would homeschool as well. Been at it 22 years now! ....Thanks

### caroline — 2012-09-20T02:01:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sources-of-seafood-for-those-who-live-far-from-the-sea.2095/post-52822>

Momma 28 - I would guess that you have a very supportive husband/partner as well! I love how you instinctively know what to do and then research and then stand your ground. Isn't it something how you can have had a damaged childhood and rise above it instead of letting it destroy you. There are a lot of amazing stories here.

### Momma28 — 2012-09-21T04:32:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sources-of-seafood-for-those-who-live-far-from-the-sea.2095/post-53101>

Caroline,

Yes, hubby is very supportive. He's a simple man and trusts whatever I do. He's about to be 50 and has the body of an 18 year old, per his yearly physical with the govt. So I'd say he has a reason to trust me. When I met him at 22 he had just been diagnosed with Hypertension and his sugar level was right behind that. He couldn't sit anywhere for 5 minutes without falling asleep... a sure sign of high sugar. All of his family have both of these as well. Of course, he is free from all of it now and his family can't believe how slim he and I stay.

And yes, I agree it is truly a God thing for me to be nothing like my parents. They were very broken, dysfunctional people without a compass or boundaries to live by. God is the one thing that has made my life different than theirs or my siblings. In time I have been able to influence my two younger brothers to come out of the gangster life and drug dealing and become productive citizens. But sadly, my two sisters have not faired as well. The brokenness perpetuates in their lives. We were subjected to sexual abuse and that is something that is very difficult to overcome without a compass. You are very empty and you don't know why...until God shows you why.

And lastly, perhaps because of my past, I will fight to the death for my children's safety. I realized when the first one was born that his only protection in this world was his parents and I am well aware of what can happen when we aren't vigilant. But I never would have thought I would have to protect them from my own govt. My sex therapist told me last year that I had the highest degree of emotional intelligence of any human being she had ever come across. I don't know what that is...I just know what I feel. I guess it works.

# Thread: How can stalls even happen?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-can-stalls-even-happen.2096/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jmathisen — 2012-06-28T23:23:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-can-stalls-even-happen.2096/post-34235>

I've been on LR&CT for 5 weeks. Lost 37 lbs from 302 to 265 pretty steadily. Been stuck at 265 though for several days, and I guess I'm just unsure how that is possible?

I eat pretty much the same thing everyday. 50-60 grams of protein at breakfast, no lunch, 40 grams of protein (almost always sardines/fish) at dinner, and 1 tblspoon of CO at morning and at night. I try to keep carbs under 5g/day, most times zero. Only real carbs I get is from the sauerkraut a few times a week.

Energy is good, muscle tone is good, Sleep isn't as great as I would like (steadily getting worse), but I can't point to anything and say "this is seriously out of whack".

So in total, that has to be what, 1000 calories tops? Even if I've somehow become pretty stingy and burning calories, I find it hard to beleive that I'm not burning at least 1500/day, so even doing nothing else, after a week, I should drop at least one lb, let alone all the shivering after CT and stuff.

But the weight loss has just stopped, like the scale is stuck. Dr. scale yesterday at visit confirmed weight, and matches my scale at home.

I suppose on some level, it's possible I'm building some muscle somewhere I don't see it, but even then, I'd expect then to be losing some inches somewhere, and as near as I can tell, nada.

I don't mind sticking with it for a while longer, because I think I believe what Doc is saying, and everything else has worked, but this is frustrating. Contemplating some HCG or something to try to shake things up. Or maybe carbloading for a few days and starting the LR again.

I don't know, maybe I'm just posting hoping for some "hang in there's".... But I was so jazzed about how well it was going, and all the "CO shreds fat", and "CT blows fat cells away", and I'm doing everything I can, but stalled out...

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-28T23:41:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-can-stalls-even-happen.2096/post-34245>

hormones....and ....inflammation and adaptation.

# Thread: How to explain to my doctor...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-explain-to-my-doctor.2097/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-06-28T23:31:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-explain-to-my-doctor.2097/post-34239>

So, I felt like I had a productive consult with Dr. Kruse last night. I am excited that I was able to reschedule my apt with my PCP today and get it moved up to this coming Tuesday. I have a list of labs that Dr. Kruse suggested, but I'm wondering how I explain this to my doctor... why I want these labs and reasons behind each of them. Or do I just hand him the list? Some of them are easy for me to understand, others... not so much. Here are the labs I want to request:

\- Test for celiac

\- Salivary Cortisol and DHEA

\- HS-CRP

\- Lipid Panel

\- Thyroid Panel (including Free T3 & Free T4)

\- Vitamin D

The ones I'm most concerned about my doctor hesitating on are the salivary cortisol & DHEA as well as the HS-CRP. Suggestions?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-28T23:33:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-explain-to-my-doctor.2097/post-34241>

go to primalmeded.com blog post of 11/8/2011 This primer will help you.

### freshveggies — 2012-06-30T00:45:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-explain-to-my-doctor.2097/post-34464>

The cortsol one can be done at ZRT labs. You do not need a doctors orders and I am not sure of an insurance company that would pay for it anyway. The others are pretty standard and you are paying for it so I don't see why your doc wouldn't order them. Tell them it is for your piece of mind.

### Huck — 2012-08-25T16:11:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-explain-to-my-doctor.2097/post-46998>

I assume you want to use your insurance to pay for labs. I have a high deductible, so I end up paying for my labs anyway.

What I do is order my own labs prior to my doc appointment. That gives me time to research what the labs mean so I can discuss with my doc. If I have my doc draw the blood, I get a call 2 or 3 weeks later from the nurse who doesn't understand what the labs means, so walking in and handing my doc a copy of the labs for discussion has proven to be my best option.

### Lyndra — 2012-08-26T20:10:14+0300

>
>
>
> What I do is order my own labs prior to my doc appointment. That gives me time to research what the labs mean so I can discuss with my doc. If I have my doc draw the blood, I get a call 2 or 3 weeks later from the nurse who doesn't understand what the labs means, so walking in and handing my doc a copy of the labs for discussion has proven to be my best option.

I'm the same way. I'd rather have the labs in hand before my doc appointment so I can do research and come up with thoughts and questions. In addition, doing it that way gives me complete control of who has copies of my labs. My former PCP would have flipped had he seen my "bad" cholesterol panel - the one with HDL of 101 and trigs of 59 but "high" LDL. Some results are best not kept on an official record that may be shared with the insurance company.

### cramernh — 2012-09-25T12:55:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-explain-to-my-doctor.2097/post-54078>

Trisha.. that is exactly what I had to tell the Physician Asst 16 years ago in order to be tested for PCOS and IR. Both sides of the family have significant immediate family medical history for several reproductive/cycle related medical problems. BAM! 12hr Blood work was done for those two issues and my labs lit up like a Christmas Tree... it wasnt pretty.

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-25T21:51:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-explain-to-my-doctor.2097/post-54221>

This is a long over due update, but my doctor agreed to every single test with no reservations. Several, he even remarked, yes I like that test, or that's a good idea. There were also a couple he suggested before I recommended them. There was no convincing, begging, or pleading that I had to do. I'm really liking this new doc, he seems very open to ideas!

### shah78 — 2012-09-25T23:22:55+0300

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-26T00:06:35+0300

# Thread: Kruser Kaught
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kruser-kaught.2098/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-06-28T23:50:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kruser-kaught.2098/post-34252>

damn, I couldn't stop myself with the alternate spelling. Onto my point. . .

Was at Vitamin cottage today, and the lady in line behind me was unloading her basket. It was like a 1000 cans of sardines!!! I looked up and it was Lyndra.

# Thread: One pound of weight loss equals four pounds to your knee joints
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-pound-of-weight-loss-equals-four-pounds-to-your-knee-joints.2100/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-06-29T00:22:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-pound-of-weight-loss-equals-four-pounds-to-your-knee-joints.2100/post-34258>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/15986358](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/15986358)

**Weight loss reduces knee-joint loads in overweight and obese older adults with knee osteoarthritis.

Messier SP, Gutekunst DJ, Davis C, DeVita P.

Source

Snow Biomechanics Laboratory, Department of Health and Exercise Science, Wake Forest University, Winston-Salem, North Carolina 27109, USA. [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted])

Abstract

OBJECTIVE:

To determine the relationship between change in body mass and knee-joint moments and forces during walking in overweight and obese older adults with knee osteoarthritis (OA) following an 18-month clinical trial of diet and exercise.

METHODS:

Data were obtained from 142 sedentary, overweight, and obese older adults with self-reported disability and radiographic evidence of knee OA who underwent 3-dimensional gait analysis. Gait kinetic outcome variables included peak knee-joint forces and peak internal knee-joint moments. Mixed regression models were created to predict followup kinetic values, using followup body mass as the primary explanatory variable. Baseline body mass was used as a covariate, and thus followup body mass was a surrogate measure for change in body mass (i.e., weight loss).

RESULTS:

There was a significant direct association between followup body mass and peak followup values of compressive force (P = 0.001), resultant force (P = 0.002), abduction moment (P = 0.03), and medial rotation moment (P = 0.02). A weight reduction of 9.8 N (1 kg) was associated with reductions of 40.6 N and 38.7 N in compressive and resultant forces, respectively. Thus, each weight-loss unit was associated with an approximately 4-unit reduction in knee-joint forces. In addition, a reduction in body weight of 9.8 N (1 kg) was associated with a 1.4% reduction (0.496 Nm) in knee abduction moment.

CONCLUSION:

**Our results indicate that each pound of weight lost will result in a 4-fold reduction in the load exerted on the knee per step during daily activities. Accumulated over thousands of steps per day, a reduction of this magnitude would appear to be clinically meaningful.**

note the absence of warm adapted rats**

# Thread: How much is a spike?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-a-spike.2101/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### paisley777 — 2012-06-29T00:50:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-a-spike.2101/post-34262>

I've done a few searches and couldn't find the answer to this...

I've seen it said that dairy and whey are ~ok~ for an individual IF it doesn't make their insulin spike.

What is the definition of a 'spike' in insulin?

I have a glucometer and can measure after eating, but how do I judge the results? 5mg/dl = "spike"? 10md/dl? 50? Etc.

Thanks for your time.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-29T01:42:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-a-spike.2101/post-34275>

a 20 point move ..........

### paisley777 — 2012-06-29T06:18:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-a-spike.2101/post-34320>

Thank you for the quick reply!

Cool. I made a keifer (home made) and whey shake for lunch.

I couldn't test every 15 due to work requirements, but pre-shake - 77, 20min - 85, 1hr - 87... then it dropped from there eventually back to 77.

Seemed like a reasonable amount, but I wasn't sure.

Thanks again.

### scotwilson — 2012-07-11T16:29:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-a-spike.2101/post-36813>

# Thread: It's not clear to me how well, HCG and LR can work together...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-not-clear-to-me-how-well-hcg-and-lr-can-work-together.2103/>
**Subforum:** HCG Protocol
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jmathisen — 2012-06-29T01:38:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-not-clear-to-me-how-well-hcg-and-lr-can-work-together.2103/post-34273>

Or maybe I'm confused. I'm thinking about doing HCG in a few weeks, but from reading some info at hcgdietinfo and some other sources, the 2 plans, with the exception of being low in calories, don't really seem to overlap.

So are people eating the LR diet, but taking HCG? Or eating the HCG protocol, taking HCG, not doing LR, and just doing CT?

Part of my issue is that I'm trying to stay basically near-zero carb, as I'm T2D, so LR diet fits well.

But I also don't usually have hunger/appetite issues, I've done 30 day water fasts with no trouble after day 2 or so.

I'm going to stay LR for a few more weeks, but wanted to accumulate some info for what to do after my 8 weeks is up.

Thanks for your time.

### colleencoble — 2012-06-29T04:08:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-not-clear-to-me-how-well-hcg-and-lr-can-work-together.2103/post-34309>

They don't overlap. They are two totally different things. Some who are following Dr. Kruse have tried to eliminate the fruit and eat 200 calories of extra protein in their place and have done fine. Some have also eaten one of their meals at breakfast to try to stay on the same timing schedule as the LR plan.

I personally would love to see someone try CT with NO hcg but follow the hcg diet. I think they might do the same thing but no one has tested it. Some have tried CT with hcg and many of us got hungry so it was like they were fighting each other. I tried doing hcg with added fat but it was a failure.

Good luck, whatever you decide!

### LisaAPB — 2012-06-29T06:57:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-not-clear-to-me-how-well-hcg-and-lr-can-work-together.2103/post-34328>

Are you trying to lose fat? That lack of appetite thing you've got going on should make that fairly easy without hcg.

### jmathisen — 2012-06-29T19:12:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-not-clear-to-me-how-well-hcg-and-lr-can-work-together.2103/post-34390>

Yes, trying to lose some more fat, while becoming LS.

37 lbs of fat came off with LR in about 5 weeks, but I've been stuck at 265 now for 8 days. So I'm wondering if my body has adapted to LR diet, and maybe I need to shake things up a bit and do something different to get things fired up again.

I mean, I could just fast again for a while, but I don't think that will really train my system to be LS and Doctor Kruse doesn't support IF during LR, so I'd like to do whatever keeps me on the path to becoming LS, while still unloading another 70lbs or so.

The other reason was that I'd read somewhere that HCG tends to remove visceral fat, which I carry a lot of. Ice bags on the tummy on the days I don't do full immersion for CT would seem to be targeting the fat just under the skin. Which I don't mind getting rid of, but in terms of long term health, the visceral fat seems to be more dangerous. I guess CT seems to target sq fat in general, (although I do realize that's not it's only goal/benefit).

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-30T01:15:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-not-clear-to-me-how-well-hcg-and-lr-can-work-together.2103/post-34472>

You've been stuck for 8 days? Don't see it as a stall. This is not a magic diet, the scale won't move everyday. You have lost a big amount in only 5 weeks, give you body the chance to adapt.

What is your diet like? Are you having enough fat? Do you CT? Have you done any lab testing? HCG with bad hormones can screw you even more, be careful.

Fat around the waist mean high cortisol...If you are careful with the light and you sleep properly, it will go down, but again, you have to be patient.... Keep reading the blog, you'll find many answers to yours questions.

### colleencoble — 2012-06-30T06:17:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-not-clear-to-me-how-well-hcg-and-lr-can-work-together.2103/post-34540>

I agree, 8 days is nothing, I have 10 day stalls on rounds of hcg. Stalls are perfectly normal. Your body is just shifting fat and water around.

# Thread: Do hormones smell..?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-hormones-smell.2109/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 33
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Inger — 2012-06-29T11:28:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-hormones-smell.2109/post-34331>

I have a coworker that cannot stop telling how very nice he thinks I smell.. it is almost as he would get paralyzed when he is close to me really...

I use a nice perfume just a little in my hair to cover the "mud-scent" I guess my hair must smell like cause I wash it with ghassul. I let him smell my perfum but he said it was not that smell at all.. it was how my body, how I smelled that was so very attractive. That was intresting because I do not shower, I only go for a swim in the river every day, never use soap except a little under my armpits if I feel they smell any.. and use only coconut and oliveoil on my skin.

I never used any hormones in any form. I have the feeling all my "Kruse-lifestyle"  and all my tanning naked have optimized my hormones and that is what my coworker smells.. And no, we have not kissed or anything that close, he just smells it through my clothes and all.. so strange!!!

After I read that tanning certain bodyparts (the ones we cover with swimsuit usually) rises your testosteron up to 200 %... and works as a hormone booster / oprimizer for other hormones too, I use every moment I can to tan those parts. I wish people would know, there is such a powerful remedy for us to take for free.. the sun!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-29T13:55:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-hormones-smell.2109/post-34340>

when someone is attracted to your sent it means they have a dissimilar immune system and that was an early evolutionary signal for homo that they might be a good partner for an offspring.

### Tizawee — 2012-06-29T14:47:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-hormones-smell.2109/post-34341>

Inger, the quick answer to your question is Yes. I have been post-menopausal for 3 years now and began BHRT about a year ago. Now that I've added hormones back into my system, I can tell you without a doubt that they do smell. Even my husband has noticed it.

When I was in my child-bearing years, he said my skin smelled slightly sweet and warm and somehow comforting. He didn't notice that the smell had gone away, I guess because my hormones declined gradually as opposed to suddenly. But he says that I now smell like I did when we first met (13 years ago).

Elin

### Inger — 2012-06-30T10:44:30+0300

If they are too feminine and small I just am not so attracted.. strange. I wonder if some deep female instincts in me wants strong and protecting men that have big strong hands or something.

About the guest blog. Hmm.. I need to think about this because that is not really any amazing story I have to tell, I was not very sick or very fat or anything like that, my max. weight was just 10 pounds more than I weight now! But sure there have been a steady progression into better and better health, into optimal.. To read Jacks Blogs changed everything, even if I lived very healthy before. NowI am waiting for the winter to come.. that never happened in my life before!!! I am SO exited. I have all great options to test it where I live!

I could write just a short story for sure about me how I was and what I did and how it evolved.. if it could be to any help I would be happy to do it! I had bulemia too and that might be helpful for women who suffer from ED to get to know what helped me.

But I so wait for Jacks blogposts.. I do want them all to come sooon.. soon..!!!!! I so want to learn and to know more!!! I hope if I write a guest blog it would not make Jacks posts come any later.. cause that would be sad.

You are all just amazing!!! And KiwiLauren..

### chocolate — 2012-07-29T01:09:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-hormones-smell.2109/post-41725>

not hormones, but a smell article

[http://www.livescience.com/21789-storm-scents-smell-rain.html](http://http://www.livescience.com/21789-storm-scents-smell-rain.html)

> Storm Scents: You Can Smell Oncoming Rain
>
>
>
> When people say they can smell a storm coming, they're right. Weather patterns produce distinctive odors that sensitive noses sniff out. This year's peculiar weather patterns—such as drought in the Midwest and a "super derecho" of thunderstorms earlier this summer—are no exception. In fact, as the rains return after a dryspell, many of these odors are stronger than ever. So, what are the scents of a summer storm? Here's a breakdown of three common odors:
>
>
>
> —Oh, ozone
>
> Before the rain begins, one of the first odors you may notice as winds pick up and clouds roll in is a sweet, pungent zing in your nostrils. That's the sharp, fresh aroma of ozone—a form of oxygen whose name comes from the Greek word ozein (to smell). Tropospheric chemist Louisa Emmons at the National Center for Atmospheric Research explains that ozone emanates from fertilizers and pollutants as well as natural sources. An electrical charge—from lightning or a man-made source such as an electrical generator—splits atmospheric nitrogen and oxygen molecules into separate atoms. Some of these recombine into nitric oxide, and this in turn reacts with other atmospheric chemicals, occasionally producing a molecule made up of three oxygen atoms—ozone, or O3. (Most atmospheric oxygen is made up of two atoms—O2.) The scent of ozone heralds stormy weather because a thunderstorm's downdrafts carry O3 from higher altitudes to nose level.
>
>
>
> —Petrichor potpourri
>
> Once the rains arrive, other odors come with them. Falling water disturbs and displaces odoriferous molecules on surfaces, particularly on dry ones, and carry them into the air. If you happen to be near vegetation, these molecules may come from plants and trees. For city dwellers, these odorants rise up from concrete and asphalt. Some are fragrant, others foul.

# Thread: Bonjour from Paris
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bonjour-from-paris.2110/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Paleodocteur — 2012-06-29T13:12:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bonjour-from-paris.2110/post-34333>

\* I am a conventionally trained French family doctor who decided \*to change the course of his slowly eroding health at age 41.

I started VLCing with BABs a year ago. Within a month I dropped 15 kilos, the knee pain, the lowish mood and the brain fog. I randomly discovered I was wheat sensitive at reintroduction. So I went on with LC fromageo-paleo.\*

\*Through the process I discovered a new passion for understanding human physiology. I am so glad Dr Jack Kruse attempted to catch the attention of the paleo community because that's how I stumbled on his amazing blog a year ago. Nowhere else had I found such depth in \*clinical thinking. When I read the CT series and realized its power I decided that, if I were to use this fascinating tool to help my patients, I had to go through it.\*

So I did.

I got to enjoy the cold bath and cold air. I lost leftover fat and gained muscle volume and strength with little effort. I got tanned. My appetite diminished \*and I got as leptin and carb sensitive as my 3 year old !\*

My little personal journey uphill is quite nice but the implications for my sick patients are overwhelming.

However that's a tough sale to explain on a 15' consultation why and how to go wheat-free/dairy-free, in the home country of baguette and cheese. I keep trying, twicking the message to avoid sounding obtuse, nuts or preachy. I got a couple of good feedbacks on sleep resync and on dairy free. Wheat free appears more like a painful double edge sword, with some patients feeling awesome and some others worse for doing it halfway and probably suffering intermittent withdrawals and flareups. I wish I could discuss these topics with other docs, but so far my colleagues remain trapped in conventional thinking (this year we'll get bonuses to statinize a maximum of people...)\*

\* \*Hopefully I' ll find some other doctors on this forum, but there is already so much to learn from patients own experiences.

### fitness@home — 2012-06-29T13:31:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bonjour-from-paris.2110/post-34334>

Welcome, and I just wanted to say that the world needs MORE doctors like you. Please keep delivering the message to your patients.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-29T13:50:49+0300

A huge welcome from me as well. Your patients are so so very lucky!

# Thread: Developing tolerance on sublingual? Did CT spot icing cause it?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/>
**Subforum:** HCG Protocol
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kalibex — 2012-06-29T16:36:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-34357>

Is that possible? Starting to suspect I am....3 days of a weight plateau, noticeably increased and earlier hunger (had to have an extra protein the past 2 days) ... in the middle of what I wanted to be a long round, but hitting me now just about the 27 day mark... I tried a CT spot icing (once) just about when the hunger began to rise...wondering now if I shot myself in the proverbial foot.

### colleencoble — 2012-07-02T05:38:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-34948>

That's what others of us have noticed. CT on hcg seems to cause more hunger. But I was immersing when I had hunger. Spot CTing might not cause that problem. Let's tweak your dose before we assume anything. What dose are you on? RX sublingual or homeopathic?

### kalibex — 2012-07-02T15:24:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-34980>

Hi, Colleen! Sublingual, Corion, 150 IU. Just started a new vial, have now been stalled 7 days...  Hunger can hit mid-morning (as opposed to being able to wait until lunchtime), and also late evening (so hungry last night, had to have an extra fruit in order to get to sleep).

### colleencoble — 2012-07-02T15:27:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-34982>

Stalls are normal. Corion is very strong. Try skipping your dose this morning. If better by 4, you could take evening's but cut it drastically, to 100 or so. Then tomorrow try 125 2x a day. Good luck! If you're not feeling better by about 4 or 5, let me know.

### kalibex — 2012-07-02T17:30:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-35029>

Thanks; unfortunately already took this am's dose, but can skip tonight's and see how I feel tomorrow am, then reduce the dose as you suggest and see how it goes. Will report out how it's going tomorrow pm.

Your experience and advice is much appreciated.

### kalibex — 2012-07-04T14:02:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-35444>

Great news, Colleen - I've started to lose again, and the hunger's back down again. I never had this sort of titration issue the last (and first) time I did a round, so thanks again so much for your voice of experience!

### colleencoble — 2012-07-05T02:39:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-35561>

I'm so glad you're better!

### kalibex — 2012-07-07T01:15:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-35978>

Alas, I might have to swing back the other way, increase the hcg now - hunger's breaking through again. I guess I'll try going back to the 150 IU dose.

The only other explanation that I can think of is that I'm close to my 'true' weight - which I sincerely hope is not the case...

### colleencoble — 2012-07-07T01:47:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-35988>

You may still be taking too much. It's not uncommon to have to go under 125 and stay there.

### colleencoble — 2012-07-07T01:47:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-35989>

The only way to know for sure is to skip a dose. If much better by late afternoon, you'll know for sure your dose is too high again.

### kalibex — 2012-07-07T02:12:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-36003>

Hunh...then I guess I'll skip tonight, then bump it down 25 more units to make it 100...sure hope I can get the balance right.

### kalibex — 2012-07-07T23:36:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-36137>

Woke early this morning, already hungry...skipped a.m. dose also. Thus far today, eating to hunger, had food at: 6 am, 10:40 am, 1:30 pm - total so far of about a protein and a half, a fruit, and a veg. Napped, just woke and starting to feel hungry (though not quite ravenous yet).

So...continue to titrate down? Or am I now getting too little?

### colleencoble — 2012-07-08T01:28:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-36145>

If the dose is too high, you should feel better through the evening. You can't tell until at least mid afternoon.

### kalibex — 2012-07-08T01:39:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-36147>

Ok; I'll do the new smaller (100 IU) dose starting tonight, and see how I feel tomorrow and the day after. Thanks!

### colleencoble — 2012-07-08T01:52:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-36149>

I wouldn't go down that far. You were doing 150 2x a day, correct? I'd try 125 first.

### kalibex — 2012-07-08T02:07:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-36151>

Except I just got done trying 125 IU at the end of this week...then was starving by mid-afternoon yesterday (ie, I get up kind of early - 6 am-ish, so that lately I'm getting genuinely hungry for lunch by mid-morning, then starving for supper by mid-afternoon).

I could try the 125 IU again and see if I'm ok for the rest of the weekend.

It is true that all this trouble started with a new bottle of Corion...

### colleencoble — 2012-07-08T02:27:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-36155>

Oh okay. I missed that part. Then yes, take it down to 100. Corion is very strong and it's a fresh bottle.

# Thread: The 5 Pillars of Performance
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-5-pillars-of-performance.2114/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Barry — 2012-06-29T19:35:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-5-pillars-of-performance.2114/post-34408>

Through what I've studied, practiced and experienced ... I have identified 5 key factors that govern athletic performance. This excludes exercise. They are factors which have been discussed and mentioned in my threads and in jacks blogs. They are all interrelated. You must have all 5 on order to get optimal performance . And it relates more to endurance performance then strength or speed.

As primordial living is about having "fun" , let's have some ! Give your 5 , in no particular order and if someone gets it right , well, I'll give them a pat on the back.

You can guess too Jack ;-)

### AKMan — 2012-06-30T00:15:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-5-pillars-of-performance.2114/post-34459>

1\. Genetic potential

2\. Proper nutrition

3\. Functional endocrine system

4\. Healthy guts

5\. Good sleep

### Barry — 2012-06-30T12:03:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-5-pillars-of-performance.2114/post-34555>

>
> 2\. Proper nutrition
>
> 3\. Functional endocrine system
>
> 4\. Healthy guts
>
> 5\. Good sleep

Too general. The pillars are specifics . Think adaptations

### AKMan — 2012-06-30T19:01:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-5-pillars-of-performance.2114/post-34622>

1\. Fasted state training

2\. CT

3\. Ketogenic seafood diet

4\. Darkness after sunset

5\. Kerrygold butter in your Bailey's (Bulletproof shooters)

### Barry — 2012-06-30T19:02:39+0300

3 out of 5 !!

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-11T05:24:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-5-pillars-of-performance.2114/post-36736>

Why not light cycles? (Obviously I don't know what you believe 4 & 5 are.) Light cycles/circadian rhythms directly impact not only our health/inflammation/insulin but they are a key hormonal pathway... which ties to optimal levels of T and growth hormone etc. So doesn't getting the melatonin-cortisol axis right directly and hugely impact optimal performance, at least the sort that doesn't kill you young?

### Barry — 2012-07-14T23:24:32+0300

> **johnnyb;37801 wrote:** How about positive mental attitude? A thought can change your DNA. I bet it can also keep one's elite butt moving.

I'll give you that one. But lets call it Epigenetics !! And yes, its huge when it comes to athletes and adaptations. Where you train, how you train, who you train with... besides nutrition.... make a massive difference to training adaptations

1 left !

### Lyndra — 2012-07-15T02:04:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-5-pillars-of-performance.2114/post-37883>

> **Barry;38008 wrote:** I'll give you that one. But lets call it Epigenetics !! And yes, its huge when it comes to athletes and adaptations. Where you train, how you train, who you train with... besides nutrition.... make a massive difference to training adaptations
>
>
>
> 1 left !

In your first post, you said these 5 key factors relate more to endurance performance than strength or speed. Could you elaborate on how the 4 identified so far are endurance-specific, especially #3 & #4? I've been asking myself what is different about endurance performance versus speed & strength and would like to understand your take on the specificity of these pillars to endurance. Thanks!

### Barry — 2012-07-15T17:36:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-5-pillars-of-performance.2114/post-38044>

> **Lyndra;38051 wrote:** In your first post, you said these 5 key factors relate more to endurance performance than strength or speed. Could you elaborate on how the 4 identified so far are endurance-specific, especially #3 & #4? I've been asking myself what is different about endurance performance versus speed & strength and would like to understand your take on the specificity of these pillars to endurance. Thanks!

Lyndra, its all to do with fat/keto adaptation. This is key for endurance, particularly ultra endurance. Read up on RER and how its measured and what it means. All of my pillars lower this.

Speed and Strength performance is not driven by RER. While my pillars still can help these athletes, they have more of an indirect relationship.

### Lyndra — 2012-07-15T20:10:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-5-pillars-of-performance.2114/post-38088>

> **Barry;38008 wrote: I'll give you that one. But lets call it Epigenetics !! And yes, its huge when it comes to athletes and adaptations. Where you train, how you train, who you train with... besides nutrition.... make a massive difference to training adaptations
>
>
>
> 1 left !
>
>
> Barry;38213 wrote:** Lyndra, its all to do with fat/keto adaptation. This is key for endurance, particularly ultra endurance. Read up on RER and how its measured and what it means. All of my pillars lower this.
>
>
>
> Speed and Strength performance is not driven by RER. While my pillars still can help these athletes, they have more of an indirect relationship.

I get how keto-adapting affects endurance performance, but I'm still not following how "where you train, how you train, who you train with" relates to endurance specifically and not speed/strength.

### Barry — 2012-07-15T21:04:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-5-pillars-of-performance.2114/post-38097>

> **Lyndra;38257 wrote:** I get how keto-adapting affects endurance performance, but I'm still not following how "where you train, how you train, who you train with" relates to endurance specifically and not speed/strength.

I never said epigenetics doesn't relate to speed and strength .. Of course it does . What I'm saying is that the 5 pillars combined together apply more specifically to endurance performance.

### Lyndra — 2012-07-15T21:25:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-5-pillars-of-performance.2114/post-38104>

> **Barry;38266 wrote:** I never said epigenetics doesn't relate to speed and strength .. Of course it does . What I'm saying is that the 5 pillars combined together apply more specifically to endurance performance.

I didn't mean to imply you said otherwise. I'm looking forward to #5. Enjoy your time at the TdF.

# Thread: Iron labs  hypothyroid
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/iron-labs-hypothyroid.2115/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lyndra — 2012-06-29T20:03:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/iron-labs-hypothyroid.2115/post-34413>

I did some recent labs and would like help interpreting.

Based on recent thyroid tests, I am hypothyroid. Results from a health fair screening I do every year showed declining hematocrit and iron levels, so I decided to do more iron tests.

Health Fair Labs:

<table style="width: 100%"><tbody><tr><td></td><td>4/22/10</td><td>4/15/11</td><td>4/27/12</td><td>Range</td></tr><tr><td>Hemaglobin</td><td>15.8</td><td>14.6</td><td>14.1</td><td>11.9-15.9</td></tr><tr><td>Hematocrit</td><td>46.5</td><td>43.1</td><td>41.7</td><td>35.0-49.0</td></tr><tr><td>Iron, serum</td><td>137</td><td>129</td><td>98</td><td>40-175</td></tr></tbody></table>

I ordered various iron & B12 tests through Life Extension and just got the results:

<table style="width: 100%"><tbody><tr><td></td><td>6/25/12</td><td>Range</td><td>Notes</td></tr><tr><td>TIBC</td><td>191</td><td>250-450</td><td>Low</td></tr><tr><td>UIBC</td><td>95</td><td>150-375</td><td>Low</td></tr><tr><td>Iron, serum</td><td>95</td><td>35-155</td><td></td></tr><tr><td>Iron saturation</td><td>50</td><td>15-55</td><td></td></tr><tr><td>Vitamin B12</td><td>1113</td><td>211-946</td><td>High</td></tr><tr><td>Folate, serum</td><td>18.1</td><td>&gt; 3.0</td><td></td></tr><tr><td>Methylmalonic acid</td><td>2.2</td><td>1.6-29.7</td><td></td></tr><tr><td>MMA-normalized</td><td>0.7</td><td>0.4-2.5</td><td></td></tr><tr><td>Ferritin, serum</td><td>105</td><td>13-115</td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

The health fair tests had me a bit worried as serum iron & hematocrit were declining, but the LEF tests look good to me. Maybe my iron level was too high and now I'm in a better range? Seems like B12 is too high and I should cut out the supplement.

Any opinions out there?

### Patty Cakes — 2012-06-29T20:14:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/iron-labs-hypothyroid.2115/post-34416>

My B12 was the same way, but I think mine was around 1,400! My doc had me cut my dose in half to every other day. That kept it in the very top of the high range.

I'm curious about your TIBC and UIBC being low. Mine are too and I've been told that it just means that if you had to take iron supps you don't absorb it very well. This was not by my doc so not sure. Your ferritin looks good tho.

# Thread: Craving fat?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-fat.2116/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cookworm — 2012-06-29T20:42:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-fat.2116/post-34425>

Pre-paleo, I always craved fat and sugar. Finally since paleo, my sweet tooth has really calmed down, but I am REALLY craving fat--A LOT!--now. I know that as long as I'm having healthy fats, I shouldn't worry, but I'm wondering if this is a good thing (is my body is learning/being trained to run on fat more now rather than glucose?) or a bad thing? Trying not to be "fat phobic", but I just want to make sure everything is working correctly.

### Shijin13 — 2012-06-29T20:54:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-fat.2116/post-34430>

There are days I will eat 1-2 TBS of Coconut oil w/a meal. There are days I do this with Ghee, Duck Fat and Bacon Fat. if you're body is craving it, add it to your meal. put it over your veggies, your meat

### PrimalPam — 2012-06-30T16:30:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-fat.2116/post-34581>

I've been a sugar-craver my entire life, until adhering strictly to Paleo and I, like you, crave fat at times too. Don't be afraid of it; just eat it WITH your meals.

I've gotten to where I only eat twice a day, and each meal contains a LOT of fat. Breakfast, I make my version of BP Coffee. I add about 2 tbsp. of CO, about 3 tbsp. of CO Milk, and I cook my eggs in 1 tbsp. of CO. Dinner, if it's a salad, I use about 2-3 tbsp. of CO as my dressing. If not, then I eat about 2 tbsp. of CO Manna as my "dessert". Or if I have veggies, I use Ghee. If it's a sweet potato night, nothin' beats Ghee & salt on a sweet potato.

The key with the fat: Don't eat it as a "snack". In fact, this is the first time period in my life I haven't had snacks. When I started the LR Protocol, the hardest thing to wrap my mind around was to eat twice (maybe 3) times a day, period. Now, about 6 months into it, I eat the BAB (with tons of fat!), then I'm good for the rest of the day.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-06-30T23:46:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-fat.2116/post-34680>

I wouldn't worry about it. Fat is good for you especially from an animal or seafood source. Of course, avoid industrial vegetable oils. Ugh. If you are like most North Americans and have been avoiding fat like a good citizen should, you are likely fat deficient. Eat your fill.

### Glamazon — 2012-07-01T05:27:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-fat.2116/post-34741>

Dr. Kruse has said over and over again, fat burners instead of sugar burners. I am going to make some 1/2 mct oil and 1/2 part butter to take with me so I can take a bite now and then when I get cravings. Fat is the only thing that addresses my cravings I have found. I crave it.

### chocolate — 2012-07-01T21:47:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-fat.2116/post-34859>

[http://www.disease-treatment.com/showthread.php?t=69735](http://http://www.disease-treatment.com/showthread.php?t=69735)

> oleic acid in olive oil stimulates **GLP-1 release from cultured mouse
>
> intestinal endocrine L cells via PKC-zeta but independent of MAPK or
>
> PI3K; arachidonic acid but not other long chained fatty acids caused
>
> similar effects \[PMID 17110421\]
> [http://www.glucagon.com/glp1.html](http://http://www.glucagon.com/glp1.html)
>
> The biological activities of GLP-1 include stimulation of glucose-dependent insulin secretion and insulin biosynthesis, inhibition of glucagon secretion and gastric emptying, and inhibition of food intake**. GLP-1 appears to have a number of additional effects in the GI tract and central nervous system, as reviewed in Diabetes 1998 47(2):159-69, Minireview: the glucagon-like peptides. Endocrinology. 2001 Feb;142(2):521-7, Development of glucagon-like peptide-1-based pharmaceuticals as therapeutic agents for the treatment of diabetes. Curr Pharm Des. 2001 Sep;7(14):1399-412. Review and Biological actions and therapeutic potential of the glucagon-like peptides. Gastroenterology. 2002 Feb;122(2):531-44.

I just tripped over this... I love both of these links.

### Dali Dula — 2012-07-01T22:26:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-fat.2116/post-34867>

The challenge is figuring out how to get enough fat into your body. I put fat in and on everything. Grass fed butter, CO, lard, tallow, bacon fat, avocados, olive oil. If i reheat something it gets more fat even if I already cooked it in fat. Drizzle fat over everything. Cook mushrooms in fat. Lacto fermented veg is good drenched in fat. Don't fear the fat. Eat fatty BAB you won't be hungry all day. Eat fatty dinner early you won't be hungry all night. You know you are eating enough fat when you don't get hungry for twelve hours.

### chocolate — 2012-07-01T22:57:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-fat.2116/post-34870>

I agree that the challenge is getting enough fat....I keep falling back to caffeine free tea and fat. I know that what I want and it works.

# Thread: Any ideas on what I can do with some stew meat?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-ideas-on-what-i-can-do-with-some-stew-meat.2117/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-06-30T04:11:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-ideas-on-what-i-can-do-with-some-stew-meat.2117/post-34519>

Most of what I have left of the 1/2 cow we bought is stew meat. I'm trying to use as much of the meat up as possible before reordering. Any ideas on what I can do with it besides the obvious stew. My problem is I am on an AI diet and no FODMAPS. So no onions, garlic, tomatoes, tomato sauce, wine, coconut milk, coconut aminos..... Yep, very limited diet but it is really working for me, so I don't want to make an exception. I would like to cook it in the crockpot too. I do have some chicken stock that I made but not any bone broth right now. I need to make some more of that too.

Any ideas?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-30T04:20:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-ideas-on-what-i-can-do-with-some-stew-meat.2117/post-34522>

I make meat salad out of mine........and eat it for breakfast.

### janagram — 2012-06-30T04:24:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-ideas-on-what-i-can-do-with-some-stew-meat.2117/post-34523>

u could cook it with curry spices

### Melinda — 2012-06-30T04:48:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-ideas-on-what-i-can-do-with-some-stew-meat.2117/post-34529>

You might want to invest in a pressure cooker. I think it comes out with better flavor in the meat. Add whatever things you are allowed and lots of herbs.

### Michael — 2012-06-30T10:07:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-ideas-on-what-i-can-do-with-some-stew-meat.2117/post-34552>

If no onions, how about just carrots & celery & bouquet garni?

You could try dried mushrooms, too. Intense flavor.

You could also try mixing different meats in a stew for more flavor & interest. Lardons of bacon is classic. In France & Spain they do things like poultry or rabbit plus pork or spiced sausage.

### ealachan — 2012-06-30T15:42:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-ideas-on-what-i-can-do-with-some-stew-meat.2117/post-34572>

Do you have access to a meat grinder (or a butcher)? You could grind it up and then just use it for burgers, etc.

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-30T16:47:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-ideas-on-what-i-can-do-with-some-stew-meat.2117/post-34587>

> **Jack Kruse;34681 wrote: I make meat salad out of mine........and eat it for breakfast.
>
>
> janagram;34682 wrote:** u could cook it with curry spices

I like both of these suggestions. I was definitely thinking of it as a breakfast food so I could do something a little different. Since it is such a tough piece of meat, would you simmer it in broth or something to make the meat salad? I found a mixture of spices I liked on chicken wings & it included curry powder. I could definitely use it on the meat. Again, how could I cook it so it would get tender. I have wanted a pressure cooker and have been thinking of buying one, but I already have so many small appliances I thought it would be a bit of overkill!

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-01T16:34:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-ideas-on-what-i-can-do-with-some-stew-meat.2117/post-34776>

Stir fry it with some spices or veggies.

### Glamazon — 2012-07-02T16:04:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-ideas-on-what-i-can-do-with-some-stew-meat.2117/post-34986>

I chop it up even smaller( maybe half size), quick stirfry with some seasonings or do it plain. I keep it in the fridge for snacking and taking to work. Caveat...I have to eat more than three times a day because of a pre-existing medical condition at this time. This is where a consult with Dr. K can make a big difference.

# Thread: So you want to know why FACTOR X matters NOW?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-you-want-to-know-why-factor-x-matters-now.2118/>
**Subforum:** Factor X
**Posts:** 67
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-30T04:18:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-you-want-to-know-why-factor-x-matters-now.2118/post-34521>

Phylogeny: Rewriting evolution:

**

Well, your factor X theory predicts rapid morphological and phylogenetic change in response to rapid, extreme changes in environmental conditions. Petersen's work certainly lends credence to your ideas. Apparently microRNA is highly conserved, found in the oldest, most primitive cell types and increases in incidence with increasing cell complexity. It is able to affect gene expression posttranslationally**. This is a HUGE deal because it blasts apart the dogma of DNA>RNA>protein expression. It also potentially allows for rapid recruitment of non-coding areas of DNA, i.e. our genomes may not be static, but infinitely more variable than we previously believed possible. Maybe your genetic inheritance is not necessarily your fate after all!

Definitions from wikipedia:

miRNAs are post-transcriptional regulators that bind to complementary sequences on target messenger RNA transcripts (mRNAs), usually resulting in translational repression or target degradation and gene silencing.\[1\]\[2\] The human genome may encode over 1000 miRNAs,\[3\]\[4\] which may target about 60% of mammalian genes\[5\]\[6\] and are abundant in many human cell types.\[7\]

miRNA research has revealed multiple roles in negative regulation (transcript degradation and sequestering, translational suppression) and possible involvement in positive regulation (transcriptional and translational activation). By affecting gene regulation, miRNAs are likely to be involved in most biological processes.\[17\]\[18\]\[19\]\[20\]\[21\]\[22\]\[23\] Different sets of expressed miRNAs are found in different cell types and tissues.\[24\]

Aberrant expression of miRNAs has been implicated in numerous disease states, and miRNA-based therapies are under investigation.\[25\]\[26\]\[27\]
