# Thread: The Doctor who Cured HIV.... cool story
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-doctor-who-cured-hiv-cool-story.2207/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-07-04T21:47:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-doctor-who-cured-hiv-cool-story.2207/post-35525>

[http://www.thebodypro.com/content/67555/hiv-frontlines-the-doctor-who-cured-hiv.html?getPage=2](http://http://www.thebodypro.com/content/67555/hiv-frontlines-the-doctor-who-cured-hiv.html?getPage=2)

> When you first thought to do this, this was a shot in the dark, right? Timothy's prognosis was pretty grim. No one had actually attempted this type of treatment before. Did you think, "Might as well give it a shot"?
>
>
>
> Yeah. I would have felt better if I had tested it before. \[Laughs\] I knew it was probably possible. It's not easy to test the same condition in animals, but you could see some effects in animals.
>
>
>
> **In this case, it was clearly a shot in the dark. I studied the literature. I looked in PubMed, up and down, to see if there was anything published on this point: What happens if a patient who is HIV infected changes the CCR5 phenotype? And I found absolutely nothing. It's never been tested before, not even in vitro.
>
>
>
> This was very surprising. It could be that no one really thought about this possibility. And the other explanation could be that they have tested it, it's gone wrong and no one got published. I was a little bit nervous about this point.
>
>
>
> We were a little bit scared that we didn't really know what could happen: You have high selective pressure against the virus, when we changed this immune system to CCR5 depleted cells, and no one really could say how the virus would behave.
>
>
>
>
>
> \[But then we realized that\] the worst thing would be that he changes his tropism to the CXCR4 receptor, and then as long as he takes his antiretroviral medication, there's no harm for the patient. So we got optimistic that we could dare this experiment and that the risks are not too, too big.
>
>
>
> I think it's easy for a lot of us to grasp on to this and say, "Here is the cure. Here is the future. Here is where it's going to happen." I'm guessing you've heard this kind of question a lot: "Is this the path forward?"
>
>
>
> Yeah. If you look at the HIV research, we have no better answers for cure questions than what we have now with the CCR5 receptor, gene therapy. We have approaches in targeting, unmasking and killing, specifically, reservoir cells. But they are all connected with the cure question.
>
>
>
> And the cure question, 10 or 5 years ago, was no real question. If someone said to you, "I want to cure HIV," all you'd have to say is, "You're mad. This is not possible. This is a retrovirus which degrades, and you can't get rid of the genomic material." This was the dogma of this case.
>
> **
>
> The way of thinking about cure has changed a little bit. This makes the process open for alternatives to the current antiretroviral therapy -- it does not have to be only the stem cell approach. There are other approaches derived from this case, which are also promising.
>
>
>
> We've known for a while that the CCR5 receptor is the primary way through which HIV enters a CD4 cell. But there's still so much that we don't really know about the CCR5 receptor. How much have we learned over the past few years?

The story is sooooo interesting and is continued in the link.

### joseph.steve — 2012-07-10T14:47:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-doctor-who-cured-hiv-cool-story.2207/post-36500>

Its good to know about this research but we have to throw light on a factor that people should avoid from all those activities which can be caused of this diseases because as we know that prevention is better than cure.So it means that first make a check and balance for your self because treatment of this diseases is quite difficult.

### chocolate — 2012-07-24T19:37:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-doctor-who-cured-hiv-cool-story.2207/post-40738>

[http://www.punchng.com/education/aids-researchers-discover-new-cell/](http://http://www.punchng.com/education/aids-researchers-discover-new-cell/)

> Researchers at the Institute of Human Virology, University of Maryland, US have identified a new behaviour for the human macrophage that provides new explanations for several features of HIV biology.
>
>
>
> This was contained in a statement signed and made available to the News Agency of Nigeria by the Communications Manager, Institute of Human Virology of Nigeria, Mr Dennis Mordi on Monday.
>
>
>
> Macrophages are white blood cells within tissues.
>
>
>
> It stated that the discovery explains how the virus persists within the body, how inactive infected CD4+ T-cells arise and how the infection leads to depletion of CD4+ T-cells.
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> “We are now trying to determine if this phenomenon is operational in vivo – within the human body.”
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> CD4+cells are specialised cells that are a part of the immune system, while the T-cell is a type of blood cell that protects the body from infection.
>
>
>
> According to the statement, the research team found that the macrophages cultured from human blood can function as “nurse cells” and in this capacity, generate and release newly formed cells.
>
> “The new cells released include a previously unknown small cell, termed “self-renewing monocytoid cell” that is highly susceptible to infection with HIV.
>
>
>
> “This small cell can develop into another nurse macrophage that can, in turn, produce another small cell.
>
>
>
> “This nurse macrophage/small cell developmental cycle can continue in culture for several generations, even during continuous production of HIV.
>
>
>
> “Current anti-HIV drugs cannot inhibit HIV maintained through this process, because they act to prevent new infection. ’’
>
>
>
> It said the researchers emphasised that, although working with HIV led them to recognise nurse macrophage behaviour all of the phenomena observed could be seen in uninfected, as well as HIV-infected macrophage cultures.
>
>
>
> “Amazingly, nurse macrophages can also produce CD4+ T-cells, which are released as resting cells. These cells are a specific subtype of CD4+ T-cells, the subtype preferentially targeted by HIV.’’
>
>
>
> According to the report the researchers observed a dramatic decline in T-cell production in HIV-infected macrophage cultures, as well as release of resting CD4+ T-cells that contained HIV DNA, but were not producing the virus.
>
>
>
> It said that the findings suggested that nurse macrophages might represent a source of latently infected CD4+ T-cells, and the compromise of nurse macrophage production of CD4+ T-cells, brought about by HIV infection, might contribute to the CD4+ T-cell decline that characterises AIDS.
>
>
>
> Dr. Suzanne Gartner, the leader of the Institute’s stem cell research team, noted, “Thus far, the experiments have been performed using macrophages obtained from blood, and then cultured in the laboratory.
>
>
>
> **Dr. Robert Gallo, Director of the Institute of Human Virology adds, “The concept that a cell can be produced within another cell, a mother cell, is new and thought-provoking (at least in human biology) and it makes sense that a virus would exploit this process as a survival strategy.’’**

I am at loss to explain my sexual issues other than to blame it on candida.. but candida works by suppressing normal functioning in the body.. and if it is not affecting my testosterone greatly, what is it doing that is making it so hard?? Thanks

>
>

Thyroid levels, or other hormone levels and people are suffering from this symptom, the hormones are there in the bloodstream, but they are not activating any response" Dr. Truss surmises that because of the presence of candida in the tissue, ****these toxins somehow inhibits the tissues from responding normally to hormones.****

### chocolate — 2012-07-05T05:17:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida-and-hormones.2210/post-35585>

[http://ndnr.com/web-articles/mens-health/pathogenesis-of-candida-albicans-in-andropausal-hormonal-imbalance/](http://http://ndnr.com/web-articles/mens-health/pathogenesis-of-candida-albicans-in-andropausal-hormonal-imbalance/)

> Many researchers are now linking C albicans to illnesses associated with hormonal imbalance. **Candida has an estrogen-binding **protein that displays high affinity for estradiol and estrone and a corticosteroid-binding protein that exhibits high affinity for corticosterone and progesterone.7 When exposed to the host hormone, direct physiological responses have been elicited. Estradiol directly stimulates dimorphic transition from the yeast to the hyphal form. Because the hyphal form is associated with tissue invasion, this may increase the virulence of C albicans. Therefore, the hormonal environment of the host may affect the growth, infectivity, and pathogenicity of this yeast.
>
>
> More research concerning testosterone levels in men with overgrowth of Candida is necessary to firmly establish a causal link. **Judging from preliminary data collected from patients in my clinical practice, Candida antibody levels as measured by an IgG food intolerance panel seem to have some connection to male patients who have been experiencing symptoms of andropause and a host of other chronic illnesses for years.**
>
>
>
> These antibody levels are perhaps more indicative of past exposure to Candida and not necessarily due to current overgrowth. However, using an organic acid test, a practitioner can obtain a great snapshot of the bioterrain located in the gastrointestinal tract. This test can give clues not only about the nature of** the overgrowth of Candida and other pathogenic microorganisms but also about their toxic metabolites, including _oxalates_.** Notably, research has shown that the prostate gland is as susceptible to forming oxalate stones as other tissues that form these stones, including kidneys, bones, joints, lungs, and brain, and high oxalates and their stone formation have been linked to an overgrowth of C albicans in the gastrointestinal tract.9 Serum hormone levels, including total and free testosterone, fractionated estrogens, DHT, cortisol, dehydroepiandrosterone sulfate, and progesterone, should be measured to determine the correlation between these hormone levels and the resultant data from IgG Candida antibody or organic acid testing.

This article is an easy read and very informative.

### chocolate — 2012-07-05T05:38:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida-and-hormones.2210/post-35589>

[http://healing.about.com/od/candida/a/candida\_muran.htm](http://http://healing.about.com/od/candida/a/candida_muran.htm)

> Candida produces an alcohol called ethanol producing an intoxicating effect in the blood if the count level is too high. Ethanol grows rapidly when yeast has a food source like white sugar or white floor products. In severe cases it produces much more than the liver can oxidize and eliminate. **It can produce a false estrogen and make the body think it has enough, which signals the body to cease production. Or send messages to the thyroid, making it think it has enough stopping the production of thyroxin. The cause of this is menstrual problems and hypothyroid problems.
>
>
>
> **Another byproduct is acetaldehyde and it is related to formaldehyde this disrupts collagen production, fatty acid oxidation and blocks normal nerve functions. Basically it interferes with the normal functions of the entire body and is a severe problem. One way to get an overdose of candida in the system is by taking antibiotics and birth control pills, and consuming sugar products. Candida feeds on antibiotics (it is their food source). Other causes: **cortisone**, progesterone suppositories, faulty diets, diet, too much meat, weakened** immune systems, and hi-mercury levels from mercury fillings**.

### chocolate — 2012-07-05T19:33:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida-and-hormones.2210/post-35682>

> **allen001;35759 wrote: Candida doesn't affect the erectile, and it doesn't infect too.
>
>
> [http://www.health-truth.com/213.php](http://http://www.health-truth.com/213.php)
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> **HOW TO STOP MEN FROM GIVING YOU A YEAST INFECTION
>
>
>
> By Michael Biamonte, C.C.N.
>
>
>
> Men can suffer from Candida over growth just as easily as women. The majority of Candida in the body resides in the intestinal tract and the colon. The vaginal area and urinary tract are minor sites of colonization. This is true of men and women alike. The average, healthy male and female have the same amounts of “normal” Candida growth in their respective bodies.
>
>
>
> Men are equally susceptible to Candida overgrowth from antibiotics, stress, steroids, poor diet, drugs, alcohol, genetic predispositions, hydrochloric acid deficiency, and metal toxicity from mercury, copper, iron, etc., just like women.** The difference is that men cannot get those annoying vaginal yeast infections that are the “red flag” and “hallmark” of yeast overgrowth in women. However, they can get penile yeast infections or experience many other symptoms that may never be associated with their Candida overgrowth!
>
>
>
> It is very important to understand that men can have yeast overgrowth in their urinary systems and can implant yeast into a woman’s vaginal area during intercourse or into the mouth during oral sex. However, these same men may not be aware that anything else is wrong with them. It is possible for men to be carriers of yeast and not be aware of it. If a man is suspected of being a yeast carrier and he studies this article fully, he may recognize other symptoms of yeast overgrowth in his body and then make “the yeast connection”.**
>
> Dietary Influences
>
>
>
> “A night out with the boys” can easily encourage Candida overgrowth. High carbohydrate foods like pasta, French fries, and bread can encourage Candida growth.
>
>
>
> Beer can also cause Candida. The maltose level in beer can feed the pre-existing yeast colonies in the intestinal tract and make them spread. Beer can also aggravate nail fungus, jock itch, and skin rashes.

### chocolate — 2012-07-30T03:06:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida-and-hormones.2210/post-41888>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/12851125](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/12851125)

> J Sex Marital Ther. 2003 May-Jun;29(3):207-13.
>
> Treatment of erectile dysfunction with pycnogenol and L-arginine.
>
> Stanislavov R, Nikolova V.
>
> Source
>
>
>
> Seminological Laboratory SBALAG, Maichin Dom, Sofia, Bulgaria. [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted])
>
> Abstract
>
>
>
> Penile erection requires the relaxation of the cavernous smooth muscle, which is triggered by nitric oxide (NO). We investigated the possibility of overcoming erectile dysfunction (ED) by increasing the amounts of endogenous NO. For this purpose, we orally administered Pycnogenol, because it is known to increase production of NO by nitric oxide syntase together with L-arginine as substrate for this enzyme. The study included 40 men, aged 25-45 years, without confirmed organic erectile dysfunction. Throughout the 3-month trial period, patients received 3 ampoules Sargenor a day, a drinkable solution of the dipeptide arginyl aspartate (equivalent to 1.7 g L-arginine per day). During the second month, patients were additionally supplemented with 40 mg Pycnogenol two times per day; during the third month, the daily dosage was increased to three 40-mg Pycnogenol tablets. We obtained a sexual function questionnaire and a sexual activity diary from each patient. **After 1 month of treatment with L-arginine, a statistically nonsignificant number of 2 patients (5%) experienced a normal erection. Treatment with a combination of L-arginine and Pycnogenol for the following month increased the number of men with restored sexual ability to 80%. Finally, after the third month of treatment, 92.5% of the men experienced a normal erection. We conclude that oral administration of L-arginine in combination with Pycnogenol causes a significant improvement in sexual function in men with ED without any side effects.**

This is the same timeline as blood pressure meds, the way I've been reading and comprehending.

### cookworm — 2012-07-05T06:13:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crab-meat.2211/post-35591>

What are some creative ideas I can do with canned crab meat?

### LinD — 2012-07-05T07:06:04+0300

### Tizawee — 2012-07-05T14:59:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crab-meat.2211/post-35608>

Use it like canned tuna for salad. Stuff mushrooms or any kind of fish. Mix it with eggs and/or bacon. Stir fry.

I use allrecipes.com for ideas. There is a section where you can put the ingredients you want to use. There are lots of conventional recipes, and people post their comments including their own variations. Their recipes use conventional ingredients (grain, etc.) but I've found that there's usually a paleo substitute. Or you can use the ideas and some of the ingredients and come up with your own variations.

### PaleoDentist — 2012-07-05T17:31:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crab-meat.2211/post-35626>

Funny you should ask! I just made Paleo crab cakes yesterday!

drain crab meat well. add beaten eggs, seasoning of your choice, I added finely shopped sauted onion, dried chives, parsley, seafood seasoning. the mixture should not be too wet. using a table spoon or a scoop place in pan and make thin patties. kind of like pankakes. fry and enjoy! makes a great breaskfast on the go cold.

### WannaBeOptimal — 2012-07-05T18:06:43+0300

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-05T22:15:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crab-meat.2211/post-35709>

Is this backfin or claw? if its backfin make CRAB CAKES & fry up in grass fed butter or ghee!

### LinD — 2012-07-05T23:34:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crab-meat.2211/post-35717>

1# lump crab meat

2 scallions, thinly sliced

2 TBL Paleo mayonnaise

1/2 tsp red pepper flakes

Juice from 1/2 a lime

Freshly ground pepper

Kosher salt

8 sheets of toasted nori

1 large avocado, pouted, peeled, thinly sliced

2 small Japanese or Persian cucumbers, cut into matchsticks

Handful radish sprouts or micro greens

1\. Combine crab, scallions, mayonnaise, pepper flakes, and lime juice in a large bowl. Mix well & season to taste with salt & pepper.

2\. Cut each piece of nori in half width-wise.

3\. Place the nori shiny side down on the work surface. Put two TBL of crab salad on left side of the nori. The filling should be at a diagonal, running from the top left corner to the bottom center of the nori. Top the crab with a slice of avocado & cucumber and some sprouts.

4\. Fold the bottom left corner over the filling before wrapping the long part of the nori around the crab and vegetables to firm a cone.

5\. Eat immediately before the nori gets soft.

[www.paleomagonline..com](http://http://www.paleomagonline..com)

June/July 2012

# Thread: Lowering RT3 with desiccated thyroid?

**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Luke — 2012-07-05T10:30:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lowering-rt3-with-desiccated-thyroid.2212/post-35598>

My doctor last week gave me a prescription for desiccated thyroid after reading my lab results.

Free T4: 15.0 pmol/L (10.0-19.0)

TSH: 4.43 mIU/L (0.50-4.00)

Free T3: 6.0 pmol/L (3.5-6.5)

Reverse T3: 720 pmol/L (140-1200)

My ratio of T3/RT3 is 8 & im concerned about the T4 in my medication converting to extra RT3 & making things worse. I have no nutritional imbalaces that need to be adressed.

Has anyone seen RT3 problems treated with Armour/Desiccated thyroid alone or do you feel extra T3 is absolutley necessary?

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-05T14:47:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lowering-rt3-with-desiccated-thyroid.2212/post-35606>

there are some that have treated an RT3 problem with NDT but it takes a very, very long time because you have the continuous issue of the incoming t4 converting to Rt3. If I were to try it with NDT I would make sure to take a decent amount of milk thistle and selenium as per Janie over at Stopthethyroidmadness.com

> Using a good liver cleanse/support product, plus Selenium: Since the bulk of RT3 is made in the liver, some have lowered their high RT3 by using a good liver cleanse, especially those with Milk Thistle. You may have to DOUBLE the recommended amount to get the lowered RT3. Milk Thistle can supposedly lower ferritin, so patients are adding iron to their supplementation, or raising what they are already on. Also make sure your Milk Thistle comes from the seeds. Milk Thistle not from the seeds can have an estrogenic effect. Also, there is a lot of research which proves that low selenium can increase RT3. So patients supplement with Selenium to stop that fact.

it\`s definitely faster to do only T3 and treat the concurrent problems that caused the RT3 situation.

your labs suggest you are in a commonwealth(?) country or country other than the USA, by chance are you in Canada and if so where did you get the RT3 done?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-05T15:23:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lowering-rt3-with-desiccated-thyroid.2212/post-35609>

IT wont work.......any NDT still drives T3 and T4 to rT3 if CRH/ACTH/cortisol is out of its rhythm.

### nonchalant — 2012-07-05T17:13:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vacation.2214/post-35622>

Have fun! If you have access to a freezer in your room, some of those blue ice packs would be nice for sleeping. I would love to have well water...

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-07T03:47:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vacation.2214/post-36025>

> **villjamur\_stevenson;35781 wrote:** I'm heading out to hot hot Florida the next ten days. I figure there's no way their cold waters is going to suffice when taking cold showers and baths, like my 53F well water. So I'm packing lots of ziplock bags for ice and hoping for the best. But I might also be taking a mini break from the CT if that doesn't work out.
>
>
>
> Cheers!

When I travelled to Australia (always hotter than where I am in NZ) I arranged for several buckets of ice to be delivered to my room every morning. I'm sure the guy thought I was a drunk or having some sort of 24-hour-a-day champagne party, but the room was minimal with no bathtub. So I brought along big zipock bags, stuffed them full and 'wore' them for an hour each morning. Not exactly a 50degree bath, but pretty good in a pinch. And free.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-07-16T17:46:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vacation.2214/post-38363>

Well, the ice machine didn't run out on me. I iced up every night - I figured if anyone said anything, I'd say I twisted my ankle.

# Thread: Effects of pregnenolone
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effects-of-pregnenolone.2215/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Destiny — 2012-07-05T17:20:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effects-of-pregnenolone.2215/post-35623>

I am going to see my OBGYN in the mid of August since I am supplementing my estrogen and progesterone. I am not great on it so I've decided to start taking 15 mg of pregnenolone in the morning. The idea is to start slow and see since I get heart palpitations a lot.

After two days, I know, it might be a placebo effect, but I feel calmer, almost no heart palpitations, less sluggish and more motivated to do the housework. In addition, my FBG went down to 94 from 104. I also slept better. Is it in my head or is this a result of supplementing with pregnenolone?

It this continues well for a week, I want to add 5mg of DHEA and then 25 mg of 7-Keto. Any experiences with this, or any suggestions?

### BJK77 — 2012-07-06T03:08:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effects-of-pregnenolone.2215/post-35759>

15 mg is a pretty small dose, but you're describing some of the benefits I've noticed from pregnenolone and DHEA though I take much higher doses. I just have a general feeling of well being that I haven't had in a very long time. My sleep has improved, energy increased and I just feel more motivated and excited about life. I'm currently taking 200 mg pregnenolone and 150 mg DHEA split in 2 doses throughout the day.

### Destiny — 2012-07-06T15:35:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effects-of-pregnenolone.2215/post-35830>

My last night was not good. I woke up at 12 and at 5. I felt that my heart pulse was running crazy, not the palpitations, just the heart pumping really fast. I felt very anxious with almost like the heart was in my throat. This morning I checked my heart rate -100 and BP was 117/77. I did a short CT and it went like this - 94 and BP 121/74. My pulse is normally in 80's and BP usually 112/70.

Any ideas?

### bigknitwit — 2012-07-06T17:21:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effects-of-pregnenolone.2215/post-35851>

Take a look at this link that souldanzer posted on the thyroid and ct thread (here: [http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?2214-Thyroid-and-CT&highlight=thyroid](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?2214-Thyroid-and-CT&highlight=thyroid))

I'm still trying to get a drip on things, but it sounds like the extra pregnenelone landed you with excess thyroid hormones in your cells (see description below), which made you feel overstimulated. If your thyroid hormones are not a concern, he suggests taking a smaller dose (or maybe dividing the dose) of pregnenelone, to prevent this kind of over-response. Hope this helps!

[http://www.musclechatroom.com/forum/...l-pregnenolone](http://http://www.musclechatroom.com/forum/...l-pregnenolone)

Irregular heart rate (eg: palpitations) is a symptom of temporarily too low cortisol in relation to thyroid hormones, and it's severity is determined by the relative difference in cortisol and thyroid hormones.

When it's triggered exclusively by an increase in dose of supplementatary preg, then it's due to the following process:

0) Initially resting metabolic rate is very low, so cortisol levels and thyroid hormones are relatively low - but they are balanced to each other.

1) supplementation with preg initially raises preg levels

2) adrenals synthesize preg along the cortisol-production-line into cortisol

3) cells absorb more cortisol and increase their metabolic rate (good)

\[when cells increase their metabolic rate, they consume thyroid hormones faster, and they consume cortisol faster\]

4) cells consume T3 faster, cells consume cortisol faster

5) cells start to invoke their genetic programming which raises their resting metabolic rate, ie: they start to "stockpile" all hormones - especially T4 and cortisol (and they hypothalamus allows serum levels of all hormones to rise) THIS IS THE FUNDAMENTAL GOAL OF THIS EXERCISE

6) but when preg dose is too high for the initial T4 levels, cells won't absorb all of the extra cortisol, and some of that extra cortisol stays temporarily in serum, and the hypothalamus detects this as "too high cortsol" and backs off CRH, which tells the pituitary to back off ACTH, which tells the adrenals to back off cortisol production - by more than the amount of additional cortisol being synthesized by the supplementary preg.

\[ The reason why the hypothalamus over-reacts this way is due to the speed cortisol levels increased. The faster cortisol levels increase, the more harshly the hypothalamus responds by lowering ACTH harshly. Ie: if you had slowed down the rate of absorption of preg, eg: via using transdermal preg, then cortisol would rise more slowly, and the hypothalamus would only react optimally to the excess cortisol \]

7) As a result of the hypothalamus' over-reaction, cortisol levels are now temporarily too low

8) At this time cells are loaded with more T4 and T3, but they've had their cortisol supply reduced, so they have their thyroid hormones in excess of their cortisol.

In Hormone Modulation Therapy 101 I've improved the explanation of this phenomenon as follows:

Tune in to the following symptoms of excess preg, excess cortisol and excess T3, ie:

a) Symptoms of preg-is-in-excess-of-T3 (same as T3-is-too-low-compared-to-preg) are slightly spacey feelings - try reducing preg.

b) Symptoms of T3-is-in-excess-of-cortisol (same as cortisol-is-too-low-compared-to-T3) are awareness of your heart beat / erratic heart beat - this has a complex explanation: it's due to an initial increase in metabolic rate causing an initial boost in cells absorbing both cortisol and thryoid hormones, followed by running low on thyroid hormones, followed by temporary ACTH suppression due to excess cortisol due to excess preg. Result is temporary excess of thyroid hormones in relation to cortisol. Try decreasing preg to reduce the likelihood of ACTH suppression, or switch from oral to TD preg to slow absorption of preg to prevent the excess cortisol.

c) Symptoms of cortisol-is-in-excess-of-T3 (same as T3-is-too-low-compared-to-cortisol) are temporary excess cortisol causing ACTH suppression, causing too low preg, causing drowsiness and / or the yawns - try reducing preg.

### nonchalant — 2012-07-06T17:36:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effects-of-pregnenolone.2215/post-35860>

Thanks for that post, Bigknitwit! Very interesting.

Thank you, Bigknitwit, it makes me feel better. I will try a 7mg dose and go from there.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-06T19:42:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effects-of-pregnenolone.2215/post-35894>

Start slow for sure. When I first started preg (100 mg) I had to lower the dose (50 mg) because the same thing happened to me..... heart palps, racing, and feeling overstimulated. I lowered it and felt fine. Now I take two doses of 50 mg each. I love the preg because it gives me a little energy without any of the negatives. Yesterday I forgot to take it until the afternoon and was tired by late morning. Then within 30 min of taking it I felt great and energized. Actually my "energized" is probably most people's normal feeling! LOL

### Destiny — 2012-07-06T20:01:44+0300

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-06T20:06:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effects-of-pregnenolone.2215/post-35902>

That was probably your cortisol. That is usually how adrenal dysfunction start with high nighttime cortisol.  I can't remember but have you taken a cortisol saliva test to confirm a dysfunction? Plus you have thyroid issues that "zap your vital essence!" I ran around for 3 years completely hypo being tired all the time. I couldn't put my head down on the desk at work for fear I would never get up! Ohhhh....I feel your pain!

But you can slowly raise your preg over time. I don't know what I am taking about but I would think you could raise it a little every 2-3 weeks. I started too high then lowered it and two weeks later I added the second dose back in and was fine.

### janagram — 2012-07-08T19:08:58+0300

### Destiny — 2012-07-09T13:16:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effects-of-pregnenolone.2215/post-36304>

My fasting sugar was 88 this morning! The last time I had it like this was in February when I started BHRT. Then, it started going back to 90's and low 100's.

I think this might be the influence of taking pregnenolone. Since taking it, the fasting sugar has been gradually going down.

### bigknitwit — 2012-07-05T17:37:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sprinkling-of-water-droplet-feeling-on-my-legs.2217/post-35628>

Has anyone experienced anything like this? I felt it last night while walking to the fireworks. I had finished a short evening CT about an hour before. Then I felt it again this morning, again after a short CT session while sitting outside in the sun. I recently started pregnenelone, in addition to my usual DHEA and Armour. Is this some kind of neuropathy? I also do 1-2X weekly B12 (methycobalamin shots). Or is this something to do with all the hormone tinkering going on?

# Thread: Meditation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2012-07-05T17:44:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-35630>

Does anyone have a suggestion for a meditation tutorial? I'm looking for something that focuses on controlled breathing.

### ealachan — 2012-07-05T18:10:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-35641>

> **Nonchalant;35793 wrote:** Does anyone have a suggestion for a meditation tutorial? I'm looking for something that focuses on controlled breathing.

I like John Kabat-Zinn's stuff, personally. I have a CD of his from a book he did on mindfulness and depression, and it has some really helpful guided exercises on it. My therapist gave it to me when I was coming off antidepressants and needed to find a better way to deal with things, and it helped me a lot. I also really like his book, "Where Ever You Go, There You Are". It's not so much step-by-step, "this is how to meditate" stuff, as more examples and things to contemplate, things to try, etc. He's a big believer in the fact that there's no one way to meditate. It's interesting stuff.

### Huck — 2012-07-05T18:52:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-35661>

You might want to check on youtube. Yesterday I downloaded some (free) podcasts on meditation from itunes store. Haven't listened to them yet, so can't recommend any. I also have a qi gong for beginners cd that is good. In fact, I just put it in my computer and was getting ready to do the exercises.

### NickieG — 2012-07-05T22:01:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-35707>

My favorite breathing exercises come from Gay Hendricks. He has a bunch of stuff on Youtube. Also on his website at Hendricks.com.

The Youtube stuff is all free....the website has a bunch of free stuff but the best stuff is on the paid levels.

Happy breathing.

Nickie

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-06T04:58:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-35794>

Huck what Qi Gong CD did you get? Looking for one .....

### Huck — 2012-07-06T14:46:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-35827>

> **Patty Cakes;35957 wrote:** Huck what Qi Gong CD did you get? Looking for one .....

Qi Gong for Beginners with Chris Pei.

### Michael — 2012-07-06T15:14:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-35829>

> **Nonchalant;35793 wrote:** Does anyone have a suggestion for a meditation tutorial? I'm looking for something that focuses on controlled breathing.

You can download a guided meditation using mindfulness of breath from iTunes as a free podcast. The podcaster is called Lisa Dale Miller.

I've used that recording and thought it pretty good.

It's not controlled breathing, the point of mindfulness being not so much to control something as to be aware of it, but it is meditation and it does involve the breath, so it's close to what you're looking for.

### nonchalant — 2012-07-06T16:42:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-35843>

Thanks, everyone, for your suggestions. I'll have a look.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-06T19:14:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-35882>

> **Huck;35991 wrote:** Qi Gong for Beginners with Chris Pei.

Thanks Huck!

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-06T19:59:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-35899>

I use this app on my iphone - seems effective.

[http://www.mentalworkout.com/store/programs/mindfulness-meditation/](http://http://www.mentalworkout.com/store/programs/mindfulness-meditation/)

### nonchalant — 2012-07-10T19:40:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-36592>

I found this breathing video on youtube, it seems to fit with what I'm looking for right now. He explains the reason for pushing out the lower belly while deep breathing, it pulls down the diaphragm with allows even more air into the lungs. Thanks again everyone.

[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fJfFXyAi6T0](http://https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fJfFXyAi6T0)

### allen001 — 2012-07-12T07:16:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-37104>

Yeah you can try deepak chopra too. he is superb in meditation or spiritual pratices.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-07-16T20:25:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-38426>

I was going to sign up for a paid mindfulness course last year through UCLA onlines courses - it was about $300 or so.

But luckily, I missed the sign up deadline by 3 days. I say luckily, because then I found this free site - and I really enjoyed my classes:

[https://sites.google.com/site/mindfulnessonlinecourse/](http://https://sites.google.com/site/mindfulnessonlinecourse/)

### janagram — 2012-07-16T22:41:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-38488>

thanks, villja, for this link. I read and really liked it. Am thinking about doing it.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-07-17T15:36:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-38692>

> **janagram;38663 wrote:** thanks, villja, for this link. I read and really liked it. Am thinking about doing it.

Honestly, going in I was a little worried they would try to sell me something.

Every week I expected it - it never happened. It really gave me a great beginner's education in mindfulness meditation.

I always tried to read on how to do it in the past, and I never got anywhere. The key is just to do it every day and you get into a pattern.

The popularity of this free course is exploding and the membership is way up - so it may not be as personalized as it was when I took it. But I think it would still be worth taking the time and doing it.

### nonchalant — 2012-07-17T15:39:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-38693>

Looks like the next class starts in Sept.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-07-17T17:02:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-38713>

> **Nonchalant;38871 wrote:** Looks like the next class starts in Sept.

I guess that's a change with the increased numbers. When I did this last fall, there was no time to start. You could just join and everyone was on a different week's lesson.

### BJK77 — 2012-07-17T19:19:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-38768>

Thanks for posting this. I've been meaning to start a thread asking how others practice mindfulness. I've tried and struggled with this for so long - can never seem to clear my mind. I think I'll sign up for this class.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-07-17T20:51:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-38802>

> **BJK77;38946 wrote:** Thanks for posting this. I've been meaning to start a thread asking how others practice mindfulness. I've tried and struggled with this for so long - can never seem to clear my mind. I think I'll sign up for this class.

Let me know how it goes. With all of the changes, I wonder how it will be....

But when I took it, the way it worked is you were assigned homework in 1 week increments (a week was loosely 7 to 12 days depending on your speed). There were also 2 or so mp3s you could download that had guided meditations. Once you completed your homework and write up, then you submitted it to the instructor. And after reviewing, you'd get the material for the next week. I was always slow, so I took much longer than 7 days for each course block. But it was one of the better things I've ever done and I'm glad I took the time to stick with it.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Just how bad is bromine/chlorine pools?

**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-how-bad-is-bromine-chlorine-pools.2219/post-35635>

I'm wondering because our pool is cleaned with bromine, and I'm sure it's not good for us. Are there ways to "counter" the bromine absorbed through the skin? # Thread: Travel and Breakfast
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/travel-and-breakfast.2220/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### colleencoble — 2012-07-05T18:21:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/travel-and-breakfast.2220/post-35645>

I'm going to be in a hotel for about 12 days starting next week. The last time I traveled I went ahead and had eggs. I was gone 4 days and by the time I got home, the pain at the base of my thumbs was bad. So I need to figure out something else for breakfast. I'd thought about taking my smoothie blender and making smoothies but I'm not sure I'll have a refrigerator for the spinach and coconut milk I use. Those of you who have autoimmune issues, what do you eat for breakfast when you travel? Funny how a year ago, I had no idea that eggs were inflammatory and that the pain in my thumbs was from eggs. LOL

### MartiD — 2012-07-05T18:37:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/travel-and-breakfast.2220/post-35654>

Most hotels can get you a small refrigerator if you tell them you have medication that has to be cold. Can you cook some beef up before hand to microwave? I took a jar of coconut oil to Vegas to get some fat in my coffee to help with hunger. We did have a restaurant available so I just bacon and egg omlete every morning. I don't have AI so probably can't help much with food options but ask for a frig!

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-05T18:41:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/travel-and-breakfast.2220/post-35656>

Colleen

what about canned fish? easy. couple of cans of sardines, some tuna, salmon etc... Bring you're hand blender - you'll be happy you did... it will make everything easier... what about home-made jerky from grass-fed/finished meat? also pre cook up some bacon - in the oven; throw into a plastic baggie.... easy food on the go!

### colleencoble — 2012-07-05T18:50:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/travel-and-breakfast.2220/post-35660>

Oh both great suggestions, friends! Thanks! I'll be on the road for 6 days total, 3 days there and back, then staying in Orlando for 6 days.

### BJK77 — 2012-07-06T03:07:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/travel-and-breakfast.2220/post-35757>

+1 on the canned fish. I travel frequently for work and have learned to love mashed sardines and avocado. They both travel very easily or can be bought just about anywhere and it makes for a good, filling breakfast.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-06T04:57:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/travel-and-breakfast.2220/post-35793>

Canned Fish-especially salmon

Lox

Cooked hamburgers

Cooked up bacon

Sauerkraut

CO put into a smaller jar

Chocolate bar of your choice

Skewers of meat of choice

I call the hotel ahead and get a fridge reserved if they don't already have one in the room. One time I forgot and that hotel didn't have any so I put my food in a garbage bag full of ice. Sometimes you just have to innovate!!

Then if you can get ot a grocery store pick up sushi, salads, and add to the food.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: More CT goodness...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-ct-goodness.2221/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jmathisen — 2012-07-05T20:52:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-ct-goodness.2221/post-35697>

As T2D, wound healing has been slow. I scratched my leg on a blackberry bush a couple years ago, and it took about 8 months to fully heal.

So been CT'ing for several weeks, and slid into 2nd base at my softball game on the artificial turf (my only exercise, 70 min game, 1x/week), and ripped a big old gash/turfburn in my leg.

A little over 2 weeks later, it's almost completely healed. I attribute the change to CT, because even with the prior injury, I had a good handle on my BG/insulin levels...

Yeehaw!

### NWgirl — 2012-07-06T03:41:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-ct-goodness.2221/post-35779>

congrats... wow that is amazing! Thanks for sharing your successes, really helps us newbies.

Kim

### BJK77 — 2012-07-06T14:17:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-ct-goodness.2221/post-35821>

That's awesome! So good to hear these stories of CT in action

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-07T23:43:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-ct-goodness.2221/post-36141>

Impressive! How long have you been dx with T2 DM? Any lost sensations on feet/toes? Did CT allow you to lower medication?

Congrats on the good work, keep going

### jmathisen — 2012-07-08T03:37:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-ct-goodness.2221/post-36164>

> **AbetterME;36307 wrote:** Impressive! How long have you been dx with T2 DM? Any lost sensations on feet/toes? Did CT allow you to lower medication?
>
>
>
> Congrats on the good work, keep going

Diagnosed several years ago, Tried just metformin, then metformin+actos, but dropped the actos. 2 years ago, started 18u of lantus 1x/day and Apidra/Humalog as-needed for BG control.

Since LR and CT I have stopped all insulin, and cut back metformin to 1000mg 1x/day, BG well under control. BP was down from 140/90 to 110/70 as well.

Just had checkup a few days ago, no issues in extremities.

Only side effect I've noticed so far which is contra to normal results is libido is completely gone.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Coconut oil in homemade mayo?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-in-homemade-mayo.2223/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cookworm — 2012-07-06T01:51:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-in-homemade-mayo.2223/post-35733>

Has anybody tried to use coconut oil to make homemade mayonnaise? Does it turn out/is it good?

### Glamazon — 2012-07-06T02:28:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-in-homemade-mayo.2223/post-35739>

I use MCT oil. Half extra virgin olive oil, the light kind, and half MCT oil. I am not a fan of the flavor of coconut. This tastes great. Very light. I used either some apple cider vinegar or lemon juice to make a basic mayo.

I have also heard of people making baconnaise but I don't know how they keep the bacon grease from being too hot to curdle the egg.

### cookworm — 2012-07-06T03:08:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-in-homemade-mayo.2223/post-35758>

Baconnaise?!?!? I think I may have died and went to heaven!!  So, is the process the same as for regular homemade mayonnaise (just subbing liquified bacon fat/grease for the same amount of oil?)?

### janagram — 2012-07-06T05:12:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-in-homemade-mayo.2223/post-35797>

its most likely gooood. Just let the fat cool as much as you can without hardening....

I made it with coconut oil! I just barely melted it...it came out great! I didn't mind the flavor, it was good.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-07T01:49:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-in-homemade-mayo.2223/post-35991>

I tried the bacon mayo and it turned out really weird with a greasy mouthfeel. It got hard in the fridge and wasn't that good in my opinion. I tried the coconut oil mayo and the taste was definitely off and it also got hard in the fridge. My fave has been with mct oil, or a combo of that with light olive oil. I put my recipe up on my blog, Ive enjoyed it so much!

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-07T03:50:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-in-homemade-mayo.2223/post-36026>

My fav is half CO oil and half macadamia nut oil. Gives it a nutty/buttery vibe.

### Birdy — 2012-07-07T07:21:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-in-homemade-mayo.2223/post-36058>

I like it with pure virgin olive oil. It has an intense olivey flavor, but I really like it that way. I usually add ume plum vinegar and powdered mustard instead of the usual.

### Glamazon — 2012-07-07T07:32:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-in-homemade-mayo.2223/post-36060>

> **cookworm;35921 wrote:** Baconnaise?!?!? I think I may have died and went to heaven!!  So, is the process the same as for regular homemade mayonnaise (just subbing liquified bacon fat/grease for the same amount of oil?)?

yes. make some and let us know.  I wonder if you would do half bacon fat and half oil if it would not get so hard in the fridge. I think we are just looking more for the flavor.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Please help me like CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-me-like-ct.2224/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cookworm — 2012-07-06T03:09:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-me-like-ct.2224/post-35760>

I know there's health benefits, but, I kind of hate CT right now (still a newbie), even in this hot summer weather (I can't even fathom doing this in winter!). I HATE being cold, and I frequently am very cold--I go to bed in layers of clothes and blankets (even hat and mittens if I'm cold enough)...this is killing me. I don't have to \*love\* CT; I'd settle for just liking it. Any encouragement? Does it get easier? I was willing to give up my bad food habits, add exercise, take supplements, but this, I'm having a really bad time with.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-06T03:15:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-me-like-ct.2224/post-35764>

I ct'd today after mowing the lawn, felt refreshing, not cold. Try spot ct'ing with ice packs for a while, or make the tub water a little warmer til you get acclimated. Are you anemic? How are your ferritin levels?

### vkiernan — 2012-07-06T03:44:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-me-like-ct.2224/post-35780>

I started in February and still can't say I "like" it. I do it because I have to. I still shiver afterwards and am COLD for a bit. But, after time I warm up and get over it.

### cookworm — 2012-07-06T03:53:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-me-like-ct.2224/post-35784>

> **incorky;35927 wrote:** Are you anemic? How are your ferritin levels?

This is a good question--I have problems off and on with anemia, but haven't had my levels checked recently (I do take iron supplements daily though). I hadn't thought about the possibility of anemia.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-06T04:17:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-me-like-ct.2224/post-35788>

well your bat mitochondria need iron in order to function well. The iron is a morphological feature of brown fat. Cold intolerance, cold hands/feet is a feature of low iron stores (ferritin).

### jmathisen — 2012-07-09T20:54:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-me-like-ct.2224/post-36372>

I look forward to CT each day. I use the hour to "center", contemplate, read a book, just get some quality "me" time. I find that doing it a couple hours before bed helps. I used to do it right before bed, but I didn't like laying there in bed cold, it seemed to take longer to get to sleep. Now I get out, get the robe on, take care of a few things, and get some movement in so I get warmer before I hit the sack.

Has made it much more tolerable.

### freesia — 2012-07-09T23:42:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-me-like-ct.2224/post-36397>

It does get easier but I think it may take longer for some of us.

I started to like CT when my body started responding with a raised temperature.

It took me 10 weeks to get the kind of response Jack talked about achieving in 2 weeks. I just had to take it slow...detox was intense and that worked against progress if I pushed myself too far too fast. I started at almost 60F and came down slowly....over the past 3 months to 48F. I found reading all the CT blogs useful for motivation.

Like you I really, really HATED being cold. I have bad and very clear memories of times I was cold and miserable, right back from high school days. Other people weren't cold. Now it's mid-winter here I'm rapt to find I'm the one with warmer hands than others!

I don't know if CT is indicated for everyone, but if you really want to do it I'd recommend starting in Summer (maybe get ferritin checked as suggested first) then easing into it as the temperature goes down in Fall.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-10T00:31:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-me-like-ct.2224/post-36405>

You do it regardless of your wants needs or emotions......and watch what happens.

### caroline — 2012-07-11T04:30:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-me-like-ct.2224/post-36725>

> **Jack Kruse;36571 wrote:** You do it regardless of your wants needs or emotions......and watch what happens.

words to live by!

### shilohman — 2012-07-11T05:28:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-me-like-ct.2224/post-36738>

Just ease into it. As suggested above, start with spot CTing, then once you get adapted to that try very short showers and expand from there.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-11T05:34:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-me-like-ct.2224/post-36740>

> **Jack Kruse;36571 wrote:** You do it regardless of your wants needs or emotions......and watch what happens.

I think there must be more to this - this mindset of "just do it" reminds me of the ridiculous chronic cardio times. I did that regardless of my wants, needs, or emotions - 2-3 hrs a day, 6 days a week.

Shouldn't we be more alert to actual outcomes? With CT, it gets to feel so right and good for you - but if not, perhaps thats a clue?

### freesia — 2012-07-11T05:50:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-me-like-ct.2224/post-36746>

Have to admit I _was on a kind of auto-pilot for the first 2 -3 weeks of full baths and that worked best for me.  I already knew_ I didn't like cold and didn't want to invest precious time and energy wrestling with what I knew vs what could be.

...I think I used to run the bath almost as if I was running it for someone else. Then I measured the temp then got right in while focussed firmly on whatever was playing on my laptop - at that time it was UK costume Dramas...  The whole thing could almost have been happening to someone else.

Then when my temperature started heating up while I was running the baths I really made friends with the whole process. Who said I didn't like cold???

### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-11T06:09:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-me-like-ct.2224/post-36748>

> **Cavemam;36906 wrote:** I think there must be more to this - this mindset of "just do it" reminds me of the ridiculous chronic cardio times. I did that regardless of my wants, needs, or emotions - 2-3 hrs a day, 6 days a week.
>
>
>
> Shouldn't we be more alert to actual outcomes? With CT, it gets to feel so right and good for you - but if not, perhaps thats a clue?

outcomes are in how you feel and your labs.......they should be congruent.

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-11T18:36:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-me-like-ct.2224/post-36847>

> **Jack Kruse;36571 wrote: You do it regardless of your wants needs or emotions......and watch what happens.
>
>
> when you're looking at your health - an CT can help fix it - you look at the big picture - what is it you're doing this for - make the decision and execute, you may not like it but you will like the end results.
>
>
>
> Cavemam;36906 wrote:** I think there must be more to this - this mindset of "just do it" reminds me of the ridiculous chronic cardio times. I did that regardless of my wants, needs, or emotions - 2-3 hrs a day, 6 days a week.
>
>
>
> Shouldn't we be more alert to actual outcomes? With CT, it gets to feel so right and good for you - but if not, perhaps thats a clue?

how much of not feeling right is intuition, and how much is CW that's still lurking telling you're effing nuts?

there are times when I don't want to CT - but I do it anyway - it may not be full submersion - but even Spot CT keeps you moving fwd, even if you don't want to do it. There may also be points in your life where CT doesn't fit due to chaos etc. but I've found I'm happier when I CT - crazy huh? maybe its the 1hr I get just sitting in the tub, reading a book, or listening to a podcast etc. its my meditation I guess...


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Behavioral issues after starting CT?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/behavioral-issues-after-starting-ct.2225/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NWgirl — 2012-07-06T03:46:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/behavioral-issues-after-starting-ct.2225/post-35782>

Ok so I am a total newbie to CT baths, have only done two. My first one was full bath 55 degrees for 20 minutes. 2nd one was the next day, same temp for 30 minutes. I really am loving it! It makes me feel good and it's not as bad as I thought it would be.

My problem is I seem to have developed tourette's or something since the few days I've started. I actually said something really rude to my husband, and chewed out one of my daughters for something weird too. My emotions are kind of up and down and now when I start talking to my family they are trying to judge if I'm 'normal' or 'crazy' at that moment.

Could this have anything to do with the CT? I can be emotional with hormones and irritable but this seems different to me. I'm not sure how to deal with these symptoms but I want to keep going, so please don't tell me to slow down!  Did this happen to YOU? If so what did you do?

thanks for your help...

Kim

### vkiernan — 2012-07-06T03:53:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/behavioral-issues-after-starting-ct.2225/post-35785>

Sounds like detox to me. It's happening pretty fast though at least from my own experience. I think it took me at least a week or more before "stuff" started happening. I say keep on a going and get thru it unless you get to bad to tolerate than slow it down some. It is not linear so you may go thru some bad stuff then be ok then go thru some more bad stuff. There are a few threads out there that have a lot of good into them. One of them is symptoms I have not seen before and the other is the benefits of CT or something like that. You should be able to find them under the CT forum. Btw, I kept going. I did go thru a major hormone detox which lasted 2 weeks. It didn't start until a month or so after I started. It was baaaaad and I was a wreck but I have been pretty good since as far as detoxing.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-06T04:07:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/behavioral-issues-after-starting-ct.2225/post-35787>

Someone said this somewhere.... "Too much T is nothing compared to E-rage"... or something like this

### ealachan — 2012-07-06T17:19:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/behavioral-issues-after-starting-ct.2225/post-35849>

> **NWgirl;35945 wrote:** Ok so I am a total newbie to CT baths, have only done two. My first one was full bath 55 degrees for 20 minutes. 2nd one was the next day, same temp for 30 minutes. I really am loving it! It makes me feel good and it's not as bad as I thought it would be.
>
>
>
> My problem is I seem to have developed tourette's or something since the few days I've started. I actually said something really rude to my husband, and chewed out one of my daughters for something weird too. My emotions are kind of up and down and now when I start talking to my family they are trying to judge if I'm 'normal' or 'crazy' at that moment.
>
>
>
> Could this have anything to do with the CT? I can be emotional with hormones and irritable but this seems different to me. I'm not sure how to deal with these symptoms but I want to keep going, so please don't tell me to slow down!  Did this happen to YOU? If so what did you do?
>
>
>
> thanks for your help...
>
>
>
> Kim

YES. Welcome to the Estrogen Rollercoaster. I started supplementing with DIM, which seems to have helped quite a bit. Fight through it...it does eventually get better!

### MartiD — 2012-07-06T18:08:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/behavioral-issues-after-starting-ct.2225/post-35865>

I have noticed that I snap quicker and more intensely. I never really lose my temper but I really snapped at my 17 yr old son the other day after he smarted off to me. Maybe I would have anyway just because it pissed me off but I did notice I was more intense than normal. I think it scared the sh&% out of him so maybe it was a good thing! He hasn't smarted off to me since then! LOL

### jmathisen — 2012-07-08T03:40:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/behavioral-issues-after-starting-ct.2225/post-36165>

Huh, I find almost the exact opposite. Much mellower, and way less stressed. Part of it I think is the forced hour of relaxation during CT reading a book, and just chillin'. (literally and figuratively).

### freesia — 2012-07-08T04:07:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/behavioral-issues-after-starting-ct.2225/post-36168>

DIM helped me through this lovely phase too.

My husband thanks whoever recommended it...

### NWgirl — 2012-07-08T05:37:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/behavioral-issues-after-starting-ct.2225/post-36175>

Freesia... Thanks so much... I ordered some yesterday and I can't wait until it gets here lol. Do you think I should stop until then?

### NickieG — 2012-07-08T18:45:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/behavioral-issues-after-starting-ct.2225/post-36205>

What brand of DIM did you purchase and what dose did you take?

Thanks

Nickie

### ealachan — 2012-07-08T19:54:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/behavioral-issues-after-starting-ct.2225/post-36210>

> **NickieG;36371 wrote:** What brand of DIM did you purchase and what dose did you take?
>
> Thanks
>
> Nickie

I take Source Naturals, which was originally what was in the Jack Recommends section on the website, but I think it's since been changed. I liked the Source Naturals enough that I'll likely re-order it when I run low. The only thing I don't like about it is that it turns my pee kind of pinky-orange.

### freesia — 2012-07-08T23:12:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/behavioral-issues-after-starting-ct.2225/post-36241>

I'm using Source Naturals DIM too. I was using it 2 x per day but I dropped it to once per morning and the E Monster has stayed at bay. Pee is pinkish too. I googled and that's normal.

NWgirl because you're enjoying the baths and you know help is on the way....maybe just push though? That's what I did anyway, and no one was harmed. )

### NickieG — 2012-07-09T08:03:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/behavioral-issues-after-starting-ct.2225/post-36294>

What I have noticed is both anger and sadness cropping up. Things that didn't bother me in the past really irritate me....luckily I can shift my thinking fairly quickly. However, the feelings of deep sadness is what feels most debilitating right now.

I will get some DIM. Thanks you guys..

Nickie

### vkiernan — 2012-07-09T14:26:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/behavioral-issues-after-starting-ct.2225/post-36309>

# Thread: Testosterone increases: Sodium Reabsorption
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/testosterone-increases-sodium-reabsorption.2227/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-07-06T05:03:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/testosterone-increases-sodium-reabsorption.2227/post-35796>

[http://www.hindawi.com/journals/aps/2011/817835/](http://http://www.hindawi.com/journals/aps/2011/817835/) warm adapted rat study

Testosterone Increases: Sodium Reabsorption, Blood Pressure, and Renal Pathology in Female Spontaneously Hypertensive Rats on a High Sodium Diet

AbstractEstrogen (E) and testosterone (T) are important in the sexually dimorphic pattern of blood pressure (BP) development. The goal was to examine the effects of endogenous E and exogenous T in the development of hypertension in female spontaneously hypertensive rats (SHR) on a high sodium diet. Female SHR (N=27, 5-week) were divided into four groups: (1) control (n=8), (2) ovariectomized (OVX, =26), (3) testosterone implants with intact ovaries (T, =6)and (4) ovariectomized + testosterone implants (OVX+T, =7). T was given immediately after OVX and replaced every two weeks and they were fed a 3% NaCl diet. BP was measured weekly and plasma norepinephrine (NE) analyzed by HPLC. OVX+T females exhibited the greatest elevation in BP

(190 Â± 4.0 mmHg) compared to controls at 15 weeks of age

(140 Â± 3.4 mmHg, < .001) and a pattern of hypertension development similar to that of male SHR. Females with T treatment showed evidence of glomerulosclerosis. **In conclusion, T accelerated the development of hypertension similar to the BP pattern observed in males; the presence of ovaries attenuated the T induced increase in BP; T increased renal sodium reabsorption, and T increased glomerulosclerosis.**


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: What's the big deal about liver?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-the-big-deal-about-liver.2230/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jmathisen — 2012-07-06T09:12:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-the-big-deal-about-liver.2230/post-35815>

Looking on nutritiondata.com, except for ridiculously large excess of the RDA for things like copper, beef liver doesn't seem like that extraordinary a meat, compared to all the other choices. Yet I see people pushing it like chiclets in some of the diet discussions.

Is there some super-secret to offal that only the initiates understand? Get a buzz off it? ("Hey dude, just coming down off my liver buzz... Need to score some pate")...

I'll eat it (I hate liver), if I have to, but as wonder-food, it doesn't seem that extraordinary.

Just thinking about it is giving me the creeps. Wonder if I can just eat sardines all day every day and be happy...

What am I missing?

### Michael — 2012-07-06T11:17:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-the-big-deal-about-liver.2230/post-35818>

IIRC, it has at least ten times as much folate as you'll get in any of the vegetables that are (strangely) widely-touted as **the** source of this nutrient.

It's also a good source of iron--so it's important for menstruating women.

But it's probably best known as a good source of vitamin A. Charts you see around, and services like Fitday, sometimes list stuff like carrots as containing A. Well, they don't. They contain beta-carotene, which is a precursor to it, and which, it turns out, some people cannot convert efficiently at all. The World Health Organization keeps finding, to its dismay, that many children in the 3rd world are drastically low in this nutrient, which threatens their eyesight among other problems, and can't get it from what it had assumed (incorrectly) were good sources. (The best of the cheapest hopes to solve that problem right now seems to be palm oil.)

There are probably other nutrients in there that I haven't remembered, and for all I know there may be ones that haven't even been isolated yet.

Suffice to say, liver was usually the hunter's portion. That's to say, in many societies it seems that the man who killed a game animal would be the first to get some of the choice cut - and that usually meant the liver. He'd probably cut it out and eat it a good portion of it raw, there and then. That's shown in _Dances with Wolves_ (and is probably one of the few historically authentic touches in the film). People didn't do that for nothing: they knew what did them good.

If you want more details on why liver - and other organ meats - should be in your diet, I should pop over to the Weston A. Price Foundation website and look up some articles there.

### vkiernan — 2012-07-06T14:18:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-the-big-deal-about-liver.2230/post-35822>

> **jmathisen;35978 wrote:** Is there some super-secret to offal that only the initiates understand? Get a buzz off it? ("Hey dude, just coming down offJust thinking about it is giving me the creeps. Wonder if I can just eat sardines all day every day and be happy...
>
>
>
> What am I missing?

Yep to everything Micheal said but also from what Dr. K. is saying about seafood being top of his food chain, you probably would be fine with sardines. Interesting thought.

### BJK77 — 2012-07-06T14:20:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-the-big-deal-about-liver.2230/post-35823>

It's also a great source of B vitamins. This link has a good chart detailing all the nutrients: [http://www.whfoods.com/genpage.php?tname=foodspice&dbid=129](http://http://www.whfoods.com/genpage.php?tname=foodspice&dbid=129)

### Michael — 2012-07-06T14:29:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-the-big-deal-about-liver.2230/post-35825>

> **vkiernan;35986 wrote:** Yep to everything Micheal said but also from what Dr. K. is saying about seafood being top of his food chain, you probably would be fine with sardines. Interesting thought.

That's an interesting thought.

Do you know Ramiel Nagel's book _Curing Tooth Decay_? He references what Weston Price found with isolated populations in that and says that all the groups that Price found that had "immunity" to tooth decay regularly ate food from one of three groups, seafood, organs from land animals, or raw dairy products; or in some cases people had foods from two of these groups. Obviously, dairy's problematic for many people, but, yeah, get the seafood and the organ meats in, or at least one of the two.

# Thread: T3 / Cytomel making CT more difficult?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/t3-cytomel-making-ct-more-difficult.2238/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis

### BJK77 — 2012-07-06T17:43:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/t3-cytomel-making-ct-more-difficult.2238/post-35861>

I just started taking T3 and am only at 50 gr per day right now, but I'm noticing my CT is getting much more difficult to do. I assumed the opposite would happen, but I'm struggling to stay in my 50-degree tub for my usual 30 minutes and am having a really hard time warming up afterwards too.

I finally felt like I was getting used to CT and would warm up rather quickly, generally in about 30 minutes or so. Now that I'm taking the T3 it seems to be going backwards. Any ideas why?

I can't think of anything else I'm doing new that would have caused this. I've been increasing my DHEA/pregnenolone, but that's been over the course of several weeks and I didn't notice this until a few days after starting the T3.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-06T18:31:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/t3-cytomel-making-ct-more-difficult.2238/post-35873>

That is a sign that CRH or ACTH maybe forcing your T3 to rT3 or it is a sign that your just revving up.....if it does not get better in 10-14 days draw a rT3 and you will know.

### BJK77 — 2012-07-06T21:32:52+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-06T22:09:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/t3-cytomel-making-ct-more-difficult.2238/post-35937>

Read the Hormone 101 blog........mental, emotional, metabolic, physiologic all equal stress......stress = cortisol......cortisol long enough equals LR and this leads to neolithic disease.........when your finished re rereading that then go read why oprah is still fat blog post.........and if you still do not get it........re read it again.

My husband commented this morning that he's been getting hungrier, too. Guess I've noticed it, as well, but careful to not overeat after losing 40# last year.

I told him it was likely due to circadian rhythms/time of year. I was right! He is also wearing the special glasses in the evening like me. Lately, we both forget to put them on at sundown, though, so we are not timely.

(I know.... That's bad.)

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-07T01:27:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hungrier-in-the-summer.2244/post-35982>

### BJK77 — 2012-07-07T04:00:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hungrier-in-the-summer.2244/post-36034>

I've been noticing this as well. Thought it might have been related to my increasing DHEA supplementation, but considering we're in a heatwave right now maybe it is more of a summer thing. I also find I'm wanting more fruits and veggies though I'm really trying to limit them.

### NotAnother30 — 2012-07-07T15:58:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hungrier-in-the-summer.2244/post-36077>

Thanks everyone, I really thought it was just me, whew! Being my first real primal summer, I suppose my body is just getting adjusted. I'm wondering if this has anything to do with it being mango season? With there being more high carb fruits and vegatables around, could the body just naturally crave more? Yesterdays was 3 eggs and sliced steak at 8am then at 11am when my stomach started growling, I grabbed the sweet potatoes from the night before and I wasn't hungry again until about 6pm. Wouldn't more carbs = more work? I would hate to relapse backwards.

### allen001 — 2012-07-11T07:48:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hungrier-in-the-summer.2244/post-36760>

I Think in hot climate the body loses water, salty and nutrients via sweat, or urine this may be the reason for being more hungrier.

# Thread: The 2nd most important thing you need to know about Diet and Exercise
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-2nd-most-important-thing-you-need-to-know-about-diet-and-exercise.2245/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Barry — 2012-07-06T20:36:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-2nd-most-important-thing-you-need-to-know-about-diet-and-exercise.2245/post-35910>

To follow on from my "there ain't no"... something else has come to my attention recently that I think is worth sharing.

I see a lot of questions and comments about "should I work out in the morning or evening" or "should I eat x amount of this food" or "should I run twice a week" ... etc etc

I see a big problem with this approach. The problem is that people are forgetting what the most important decider is when it comes to making individual choices... the most important decider is YOU ! It does not matter what the theory says or what us so called "experts" know... at the end of the day, we are all different, this goes without saying.. and no one dress size fits all. There are some people who thrive on nightshade vegetables and others who are intolerant. There are some who look and feel great after eating 300g of carbs and others who gain weight. There are some who sleep like babies after having a coffee in the evening and others who suffer insomnia. My point is that despite what the science says, we still don't fully understand how the body works. Its way more clever than we realise and its far more in tune with what you need/want compared to any book, blog or forum.

There are 2 important points I need to make here:

1\. I do think that those who listen to their own bodies in order to make decisions need to be healthy, LS, IS, fit, lean etc. If not, then you are not going to receive the right signals. Therefore, you need to follow the theoretical recommendations and modern principles (Jacks work) until you get to a state of self regulation, i.e. healthy.

2\. By listening to the body and allowing it to make the decisions for you, you are empowering the big player in this whole game - Epigenetics ! If you do what you feel is right for you and you enjoy it, then your gene's will express themselves accordingly ;-)

"Beyond the walls of intelligence, life is defined"

Barry

### fitness@home — 2012-07-06T21:00:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progesterone-estrogen-ratio.2246/post-35918>

So, I have my Genova Diagnostics complete hormone test results in hand. Interesting...so much information. Think it will be quite useful in my consult later this month.

It has five different ratios listed on it, but NOT the Progesterone / Estrogen ratio. Have looked online for a formulas on how to convert the results. Let's just say I am mathematically challenged and leave it at that.

Anyone out there care to take a stab at it?

Pregnanediol (24 hr urine) / **15.19 / Reference Range 0.30-4.20 micromol/24 hr

Listing each Estrogen result since I am not sure which is used in the calculation:

Estrone (24 hr urine) / **26.3 / Reference Range 3.0-52.0 mcg/g Creat.

Estradiol (24 hr urine) / 6.9** / Reference Range 1.0-17.0 mcg/g Creat.

Estriol (24 hr urine) / 262.3** / Reference Range 9.0-60.0 mcg/g Creat.

Thanks in advance,

Dawn

conditions. This provides clues about menstrual irregularities, infertility, menopause, fatigue, breast

cancer, and osteoporosis.
