# Thread: Need help what type of lab do I need to do?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-what-type-of-lab-do-i-need-to-do.2276/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mery — 2012-07-08T23:22:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-what-type-of-lab-do-i-need-to-do.2276/post-36242>

**

I stop taking HC about 3 weeks ago because I was not loosing any weight but since starting the LR and CT (19 March) my weight has gone up.

Today I weighed myself and it was a shock to find out that Iâ€™ve put more weight on since I stop taking Hydrocortisone. Iâ€™ve gained nearly 5 pound, I was only taking 15mg. I ask my Dr If HC will make me put on weight and he said its only when one is taking like 40 mg.

I was in a dilemma to stop the LR, although I may not be loosing weight but Iâ€™m able to eat onions, Coconut Oil, bone broth, and garlic which I could not eat when I started the LR. I think my gut is healing. I started taking Siberian Pine nut Oil 1 teaspoon with BAB with colostrums to heal my gut about 7 weeks and it seems to be working.

Today I decided no to have lunch anymore only BAB and small dinner and today probably after the shock I was not hungry, so no lunch and very small dinner with salad. Iâ€™ll do this from now on as I need to loose weigh. Otherwise I have to buy new clothes as the ones Iâ€™ve got are getting to small.

Iâ€™d like to know what test I need to do, to find the state of my hormone as Iâ€™ve not got a clue where to start.** The way my Dr treats me, he ask me to take my pulse and basal temperature in the morning and before going to bed 2 weeks prior to my appointment and this way he knows. When I see him he takes my pulse, blood pressure sitting and standing. I take Armour, adrenal since 2007 and HC since 2010 and progesterone cream since last year. Iâ€™m 57 years old.

Thanks for the help

### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-09T00:15:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-what-type-of-lab-do-i-need-to-do.2276/post-36247>

This is what consults are for. Id ask people's advice here but if your not sure id book a consult with me. I am sure there are plenty of people here who could give you feedback whether it is worth it or not.

### Mery — 2012-07-09T12:51:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-what-type-of-lab-do-i-need-to-do.2276/post-36302>

Thanks Dr Jack what about an email consult done to assess what labs I need based upon my history then i can book a consult, or is it better to have a consult first, thanks

### vkiernan — 2012-07-09T14:20:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-what-type-of-lab-do-i-need-to-do.2276/post-36305>

Mery,

If you book the consult, then you can send him everything you have already like your labs and definitely your history from as far back as you can remember. Include your family in there too if need be. Based on what you send him, when you have your consult, he will guide you for labs, etc. Just know that it is totally worth every penny and you won't waste money going down the wrong path. I remember you have a lot of the things going on like I do, I have had 2 consults now and just trust me on this - you won't be disappointed.

V.

### Mery — 2012-07-10T09:46:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-what-type-of-lab-do-i-need-to-do.2276/post-36484>

Thanks Vkiernan, i'll do that, cheers M

# Thread: Pins and needles?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pins-and-needles.2282/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sem — 2012-07-09T09:12:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pins-and-needles.2282/post-36295>

Hi all - I've been doing the Leptin RX for the past month or so but fell off the wagon the past few days. I've been experiencing really annoying pins and needles or prickly feeling all over my body. I had this a few weeks ago too. Do you think there's any relation or just some other random weird thing going on? Thanks.

### Destiny — 2012-07-09T13:10:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pins-and-needles.2282/post-36303>

> **Sem;36461 wrote:** Hi all - I've been doing the Leptin RX for the past month or so but fell off the wagon the past few days. I've been experiencing really annoying pins and needles or prickly feeling all over my body. I had this a few weeks ago too. Do you think there's any relation or just some other random weird thing going on? Thanks.

Sem, a fried of mine was experiencing the same thing. Dr. K said that she needs to see a doctor. If you search it, it says that it can be many things - neuropathy, diabetes, MS, hypothyroid, etc.

### Sem — 2012-07-10T06:39:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pins-and-needles.2282/post-36473>

Thanks Destiny. My OB/GYN reminded me today that I had a similar complaint before when my hormones were wacky and I had no estrogen. She is going to do a full panel once I get my period and hit day 18. She ordered progesterone so I get my period since its been more than two months! Oy.

### Destiny — 2012-07-10T13:05:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pins-and-needles.2282/post-36493>

> **Sem;36639 wrote:** Thanks Destiny. My OB/GYN reminded me today that I had a similar complaint before when my hormones were wacky and I had no estrogen. She is going to do a full panel once I get my period and hit day 18. She ordered progesterone so I get my period since its been more than two months! Oy.

That is good that you are on it! Let's know how that goes!

### 2bls — 2012-07-12T20:54:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pins-and-needles.2282/post-37233>

interesting that this is mentioned, I have been experiencing something similar; a sort of vibration;humming feeling throughout my body; almost like micro-tremors for about 2 months now. I also have a very hard pulse at times. It's much worse if I'm feeling stressed or upset or really tired. The only thing that is giving me any relief from it at all is to take Holy Basil and Relora as soon as I wake up and again before bed. It doesn't eliminate it completely but at least I can function as I need to. I'm planning to get a full work-up by a Dr. Michael Rahman a Naturopathic Doctor and Microbiologist. I need to save some $$$ first as the consult alone is about $400. In the meantime, I'll stay with the Holy Basil and Relora with maybe some other adrenal support things thrown in. I've sort of concluded its very high Cortisol and or Pregnenolone Steal syndrome...but then again, I could be completely off base.

As far as Leptin RX - its going very well, I have dropped 12 lbs in the last 7 weeks without doing anything but the Leptin RX rules...

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-for-optimal-labs-in-europe.2284/post-36461>

Dr Tierry Herthoghe

Note;

he is third or fourth generation descendant from family of endocrinologists.

He have his own medical school with number of doctors working with/for him.

**He can teach your local doctor how to help you.**

[http://www.hertoghe.eu/](http://http://www.hertoghe.eu/)

[http://www.hertoghe.eu/en/home/](http://http://www.hertoghe.eu/en/home/)

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

\====================================

[http://www.hertoghe.eu/hormone-hotline/](http://http://www.hertoghe.eu/hormone-hotline/)

> Dr Thierry Hertoghe has started a hormone hotline accessible for physicians only. This hormone hotline is accessible through Internet. Its aim is to help beginning or progressing physicians to get advice when necessary; and experienced physicians to advance further in their expertise. Contributors: medical doctors with a large experience in corrective hormone therapies.
>
>
>
>
>
> Types of help provided by the Hormone Hotline:
>
>
>
> basic program
>
>
>
> advanced program
>
>
>
> Basic program: Services
>
>
>
> The following services are provided through this hormone hotline to registered physicians:
>
>
>
> • a precious help under form of medical advice on hormone tests and treatments in case of relative urgency and for difficult cases by Internet (e-mail) and eventually by phone ( maximum of 15 minutes, twice per month) providec by experienced physicians
>
> • lists of basic tests and treatments for an endocrine evaluation
>
> • lists of useful names and addresses of recommended pharmaceutical firms and laboratories for tests in Europe and the USA for various products and tests
>
> • information flyers for patients (many new in project).
>
> • Every month online conferences on Internet (tip of the month video)
>
> • Fee reductions to many anti-aging medical congresses and symposia
>
>
>
> What cannot be provided: prescriptions of medication, medications, online consultation with a patient without his/her physician, selling of products.
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Advanced program: Additional services:
>
>
>
> • Medical training in trained centers: we recommend physicians to come every year in training
>
> • Medical expertise when confronted with medical problems from institutions, medical boards, health care, etc.)
>
> • Computer program that facilitates hormone therapy-related consultations : yearly subscription
>
> • Option: elite seminar of two days with Dr Hertoghe at his office
>
>
>
> Responsibility: The patient’s local doctor remains solely responsible for any test and treatment given to his patient. Nor the hormone hotline, nor any of its contributors accepts any responsibility in any medical test or treatment that may be suggested by a physician after contact with the hormone hotline.
>
>
>
> Registration: A registration to one of the two hormone hotlines is possible for physicians who want to start hormone but need regular help. Further details are obtainable after a physicians has send his coordinates and a short curriculum vitae to e-mail click here for more info

### Pfefferminza — 2012-07-12T18:32:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-for-optimal-labs-in-europe.2284/post-37197>

Here a list of docs trained by Thierry:

Dr. Mutter in Offenbach

[http://www.dr-mutter.de/](http://http://www.dr-mutter.de/)

Heard mixed experiences but the only Hertoghe doc in Germany so far..

Dr. Annette Gadomski

somewhere at Belgian border close to Aachen, heard only good stories so far

Dr. Paolo Accornero

Milano

[http://www.studiopaoloaccornero.com/](http://http://www.studiopaoloaccornero.com/)

Dr. Ascanio Polimeni

Roma + Milano

[http://www.studioapolimeni.com/](http://http://www.studioapolimeni.com/)

There is another one in Perugia but I don't know the name.

Dr. Miguel Martinez Del Campo

London and somewhere in Spain

tel: 0777 5934457

I think he is the only one in the UK.

And then there is of course:

Dr. ThÃ©rÃ¨se Hertoghe

WoluwÃ© Saint Pierre

+32 2 46 30 300

Maybe currently the best because it seems a bit chaotic at Thierry\`s at the moment.. but regular waiting time is 1 year. Consultation costs half compared to Thierry.

Regular blood and urine test with Hertoghes is around €500. I don't think it includes leptin for example.

For testing the fun stuff like rT3, I only know one lab in Germany: [http://www.ganzimmun.de/seiten/start.php](http://http://www.ganzimmun.de/seiten/start.php) Then there is the other one in Belgium already mentioned, where also some of the Hertoghe docs send in their samples from abroad. I wouldn't be surprised if those are the only two good labs in Europe..

Hope that helps!

### Destiny — 2012-07-13T14:04:51+0300

My insurance Aetna has us on a wellness program where they recommend that you have your HS-CRP tested. Anyway, like you, I asked my doctor for it. She was like no problem but the result was that I got back the regular CRP. Of course, I was saying that I needed the HS one not the regular one. Unfortunately, many doctors have no idea there is a difference! Sometimes, I feel that I know more than them at this point. Frustrating ...

# Thread: Help with labs I have and ones to run

### SeaJane — 2012-07-09T17:39:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-labs-i-have-and-ones-to-run.2286/post-36322>

I am new here and am trying to sort out some labs I have and also have a question about ones to run.

First, I am 51 years old, pre-menopausal. I am 5ft 5in and weigh 121lbs. I am gluten and dairy free and have been mostly grain free for 3 years. The last 3 months, I have started some mix of leptin reset, auto-immune diet so I don't snack, eat between 35 -50 grams of protein in the morning, no fruit, no eggs, carbs approx 25 - 30g/day.

I was diagnosed with Graves disease (autoimmune hyperthyroidism) in the fall of 2008. I was on meds approx 6 months before becoming euthryroid. Last summer I was feeling hypothyroid and had labs run. At that time, my FT3 was low (1.8 range 2.3-4.2) although my FT4 was 1.14 (range 0.75-1.76) and TSH was 0.76 (range 0.3 - 2.5). FT4 and TSH were comparable to what mine have been when I was feeling good so only FT3 was low. We re-ran labs including thyroid antibodies last July. The results were FT3 2.0, FT4 1.16, TSH 0.86, and all my anti-bodies were high (in fact higher than when I was diagnosed with Graves) Anti-Thyroglubulin 1559 (

### fitness@home — 2012-07-09T20:48:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-labs-i-have-and-ones-to-run.2286/post-36369>

Jane,

I just had the Genova Diagnostics Complete Hormones test done recently. It is a 24-HR urine collection test and not inexpensive by any means. On the other hand, it gives a LOT of information. Glad I had it done - it will provide a lot of info for my consult. A doctor has to order it for you.

In my early 50s also and working toward getting my hormones to the perfect place for me...

### JanSz — 2012-07-09T22:58:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-labs-i-have-and-ones-to-run.2286/post-36390>

**PSJane;36488 wrote:** I am new here and am trying to sort out some labs I have and also have a question about ones to run.

First, I am 51 years old, pre-menopausal. I am 5ft 5in and weigh 121lbs. I am gluten and dairy free and have been mostly grain free for 3 years. The last 3 months, I have started some mix of leptin reset, auto-immune diet so I don't snack, eat between 35 -50 grams of protein in the morning, no fruit, no eggs, carbs approx 25 - 30g/day.

I was diagnosed with Graves disease (autoimmune hyperthyroidism) in the fall of 2008. I was on meds approx 6 months before becoming euthryroid. Last summer I was feeling hypothyroid and had labs run. At that time, my FT3 was low (1.8 range 2.3-4.2) although my FT4 was 1.14 (range 0.75-1.76) and TSH was 0.76 (range 0.3 - 2.5). FT4 and TSH were comparable to what mine have been when I was feeling good so only FT3 was low. We re-ran labs including thyroid antibodies last July. The results were FT3 2.0, FT4 1.16, TSH 0.86, and all my anti-bodies were high (in fact higher than when I was diagnosed with Graves) Anti-Thyroglubulin 1559 (>Spectracell Comprehensive Nutritional Panel

[http://www.healthremedies.com/intracellular\_vitamin\_analysis\_spectracell.html](http://http://www.healthremedies.com/intracellular_vitamin_analysis_spectracell.html)

\======================

Get this test

Get it either via dr's script or buy it on your own.

Repeat this process 2x/year until all is right, then 1x/year

Essential & Metabolic Fatty Acids Analysis (EMFA) by Genova Diagnostics

[http://www.healthremedies.com/essential\_metabolic\_fatty\_acids\_analysis\_emfa\_genova\_diagnostics.html](http://http://www.healthremedies.com/essential_metabolic_fatty_acids_analysis_emfa_genova_diagnostics.html)

Attention;

test results are ok

test conclusions, often may not be ok

read more on my post #1 in this thread, here:

[http://www.musclechatroom.com/forum/showthread.php?20061-Dr.-Patricia-Kane&p=171942#post171942](http://http://www.musclechatroom.com/forum/showthread.php?20061-Dr.-Patricia-Kane&p=171942#post171942)

start with:

Why 4:1 Ratio Oil?

Why 4:1 Ratio Oil?

[http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/...in-4to1Oil.pdf](http://http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/...in-4to1Oil.pdf)

Goal is (LA/ALA)=(4:1) O6/O3

Goal is (LA/ALA)=(4:1) O6/O3

Highly abbreviated version;

eat fish but not fish oil

eat krill but not krill oil

When you read post #1 including what is under links, you will find your self in very familiar territory of dr Kruse (at least that is how it was meant to be.

\======================

There is more, but you have to start somewhere.

.................

I am not a doctor.

# Thread: Thinking of sharing my very crazy road
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-sharing-my-very-crazy-road.2287/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ealachan — 2012-07-09T17:50:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-sharing-my-very-crazy-road.2287/post-36324>

I would be interested in hearing more of your story, of course...but I think you already know that.

### vlynnb — 2012-07-09T18:37:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-sharing-my-very-crazy-road.2287/post-36333>

Please share your road with us. I have always been mindful of the current conventional wisdom on health and nutrition and my late husband, who was a busy family practice physician who died young of cancer, was of course very interested. I tried to live as healthful a life as possible (more than a little perfectionism imposed on others in the process - one of my children, in all seriousness and with no manipulative motives, told me that he didn't feel loved because I didn't buy cupboards full of junk food like other mothers). And now, what is practical versus optimal? I know Dr. K. has addressed that but I am still struggling. My family thinks I am OCD , which I am not, but they are still disillusioned if I fall off the path to my optimal.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-09T18:39:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-sharing-my-very-crazy-road.2287/post-36334>

I know this is very cliche but if you inspire one person, isn't it worth it? I think so and look forward to reading it myself!

### vlynnb — 2012-07-09T20:08:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-sharing-my-very-crazy-road.2287/post-36358>

Just read this on the How Do We Fix Our Thinking thread (I think). I think it will help me to not ruminate over how I could have done things differently:

From Dr. K.

> Just sitting outside on the dock finishing up the days final consult watching an amazing sunset develop. Seeing this sunset is so dynamic like our lives should be. It doesn’t matter where you are right now, you are nowhere compared to where you might go. No one can go back and start a new beginning at life, but any one of us can start today and make a new ending to our life.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-09T22:21:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-sharing-my-very-crazy-road.2287/post-36384>

Thanks to some good advice, I have left out a LOT of fun life details. . . if I ever do a book, but here is the 1st half nitty gritty with photos.

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?2377-A-fat-kid-s-tale](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?2377-A-fat-kid-s-tale)

# Thread: Sale on steak/patties,  costco
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sale-on-steak-patties-costco.2288/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-07-09T17:58:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sale-on-steak-patties-costco.2288/post-36326>

Hi All,

Saw this today. . .looks like a good deal. I always question how properly raised cattle can come in any quantities to supply a place like Costco, so my little red flag went off a little bit.

I am however going to order some steaks and patties, those little packages thaw quick and will make quick meals easier.

[http://www.costco.com/Browse/Product.aspx?prodid=11757332&whse=BC&topnav=&cm\_sp=RichRelevance-\_-categorypageHorizontalTop-\_-PopularProductsInCategory&cm\_vc=categorypageHorizontalTop|PopularProductsInCategory](http://http://www.costco.com/Browse/Product.aspx?prodid=11757332&whse=BC&topnav=&cm_sp=RichRelevance-_-categorypageHorizontalTop-_-PopularProductsInCategory&cm_vc=categorypageHorizontalTop|PopularProductsInCategory)

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-09T18:02:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sale-on-steak-patties-costco.2288/post-36329>

> **Cavemam;36492 wrote:** Hi All,
>
>
>
> Saw this today. . .looks like a good deal. I always question how properly raised cattle can come in any quantities to supply a place like Costco, so my little red flag went off a little bit.
>
>
>
> I am however going to order some steaks and patties, those little packages thaw quick and will make quick meals easier.
>
>
>
> [http://www.costco.com/Browse/Product.aspx?prodid=11757332&whse=BC&topnav=&cm\_sp=RichRelevance-\_-categorypageHorizontalTop-\_-PopularProductsInCategory&cm\_vc=categorypageHorizontalTop|PopularProductsInCategory](http://http://www.costco.com/Browse/Product.aspx?prodid=11757332&whse=BC&topnav=&cm_sp=RichRelevance-_-categorypageHorizontalTop-_-PopularProductsInCategory&cm_vc=categorypageHorizontalTop|PopularProductsInCategory)

they probably were grass fed - but they probably aren't grass-finished. which is how Costco gets the quantities. of course - maybe they're buying from suppliers similar to US wellness....but I doubt that....

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-09T18:30:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sale-on-steak-patties-costco.2288/post-36332>

Gotta love Google...

[http://sommersorganic.com/default.asp](http://http://sommersorganic.com/default.asp)

Looks like the Sprouts in our area carries this product too.

### kathylu — 2012-07-09T19:02:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sale-on-steak-patties-costco.2288/post-36340>

Great article on how the big beef companies source, raise and finish their meat. It looks like Sommers Organic is ok (beef is grass finished), except for the fact that the country of origin is Argentina and Uruguay. If you are concerned about carbon footprint, this beef travels 9,000 miles to reach us. I prefer to be a locavore, but I can understand the advantages of buying this product. I get my beef from a farmer 20 miles down the road.

[http://santacruzwire.com/index.php/food/24-business/198-buying-beef-in-santa-cruz.html](http://http://santacruzwire.com/index.php/food/24-business/198-buying-beef-in-santa-cruz.html)

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-08T20:24:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sale-on-steak-patties-costco.2288/post-43701>

Reporting back on this. . .we have LOVED these. The steaks are super easy to deal with. They are thin! So what we do is season them and leave them sitting out for about 10 mins while preparing other stuff, then toss em on the hot grill. Because the inside is still cold/frozen it works PERFECT! They are seared yet not overcooked.

The beef patties are similarly easy to deal with. It's nice having a food on hand that can be pulled out of the freezer and prepared right then.

My only complaint is the insane amount of packaging (as is everything at costco) and I hate having things in plastic. . .storing my food in paper and glass is probably too much to ask of them for now. . . so I'll just enjoy the ease until then and not think of the hormonal disruption!

# Thread: The Waiting Game
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Souldanzer — 2012-07-09T21:14:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36374>

\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-09T22:00:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36379>

worry about what you can control... that's all I can say... my job revolves around the mantra of "hurry up and wait" I've been doing that for over 20yrs now. you will always wait on someone else to get you what you need to get things done... its more frustrating when you see the path before you...but others that you need on the path aren't quite there yet.....

### johnnyb — 2012-07-09T22:59:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36391>

...but I'm waiting as fast as I can.

### caroline — 2012-07-10T08:54:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36479>

At least you are taking lots of positive steps and one day soon it will start falling into place. At least you are taking action - I know so many really unhealthy people who leave everything to their Doc and they are getting nowhere. You are moving toward optimum - just not as fast as you want but you will get there if you persist.

> **Souldanzer;36540 wrote:** How do you guys deal with waiting.
>
>
>
> I feel like I'm constantly waiting. Waiting for this test, waiting for that approval, waiting for appointments, waiting for docs to come back from vacation, waiting for someone to call me back,.... being put on hold.
>
>
>
> I'm tired of the waiting. My life's going by while I wait for things to happen. While I sit at home waiting to feel better. I don't feel well enough to do purposeful stuff or maybe I just can't come up with things. I can't even read a friggin' book.
>
>
>
> I know sometimes we have to do the uncomfortable in the now to get to the bigger goal. I feel like I'm missing out  With AN at least I had learned to blame myself.... but this.... nobody left to blame.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-10T17:39:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36551>

> **johnnyb;36557 wrote:** ...but I'm waiting as fast as I can.

I'm trying really hard!!

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-10T17:46:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36553>

\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-10T18:01:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36559>

> **Souldanzer;36719 wrote:** Thx, Shijin.
>
>
>
> I don't know if I agree with that. I don't want to spend my life waiting for things. I want to spend my life doing and accomplishing stuff. And I don't want to be frustrated all the time, either. Part of the problem here is that my life's so limited. Maybe I need to work on removing the limitations. Maybe I need to reconsider my attitude towards rx meds?
>
>
>
> I'm scared of meds and for a reason. Had I taken everything a doc ever prescribed me OMG I'd be in such a mess. I'm afraid if I give in taking meds with the hopes of feeling better and being more able to work on other things than just my health, I'm going to get stuck in the medicated place.... instead of finding true health.

I can see where you're coming from... but sometimes we have to wait for a reason, and that reason may not be clear to us at the time. You can accomplish things that you can control. IMO I think your frustration is coming from the lack of responsiveness you think you should see from the medical community. You know what you want to do and yet you can't do it w/o them, but it takes 6wks to get an appointment to determine if the doc you're seeing is the one to help you implement your plan... yeah its frustrating. Its a scream at the top of your lungs and throw things frustrating. Work on the things YOU CONTROL. Accept that you don't control everything. That's one of the hardest lessons I've ever learned... and I'm still learning it w/DH and his health.

as far as meds - go with your gut on that. Its taken me 10yrs to get to where I'm at regarding the control of my health and finding the right doctors. It doesn't happen overnight - the old mantra sometimes the best things are worth the wait is true. I think we've forgotten the practice of patience in this digital age, of immediacy. we want and expect things to happen as soon as we figure out what we want - but that's not how life works.

hang in there. you'll get there at the right time and place with the right doc.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-10T18:06:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36561>

thing is how much can I really accomplish in the meantime with a hurting head and a non-functioning brain..... get my point? Maybe it needs to be the meds or at least a trial of it then. And that IS under my control.

I'm also wondering how much "ill" I've grown accustomed to. When I tell a new doc I've been having a rather severe headache for 4 years now and am not using pain medication, they kinda look at me like I've lost it. It's become my life.

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-10T18:20:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36567>

> **Souldanzer;36727 wrote:** thing is how much can I really accomplish in the meantime with a hurting head and a non-functioning brain..... get my point? Maybe it needs to be the meds or at least a trial of it then. And that IS under my control.
>
>
>
> I'm also wondering how much "ill" I've grown accustomed to. When I tell a new doc I've been having a rather severe headache for 4 years now and am not using pain medication, they kinda look at me like I've lost it. It's become my life.

How much of that is b/c they expect people to ask for medication to stop the pain. You are so out side their "Normal" patient they're puzzled, intrigued, fascinated. They might even think you're crazy.

I do understand that functioning w/pain is hard - what it comes down to is what is your trade off? some functioning, no functioning, or fully functional. only you can make that decision. and it will be a decision every one here will support you in, even if some don't agree - we're not walking in your shoes and can't make decisions for you... you'll figure it out..and what ever you decide it will be the correct decision for you at that given moment in time.... no one can take that away from you

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-10T19:12:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36578>

Thanks, Shijin. I know I have to make that decision. I'm also becoming super aware of how communication gets missed as I am reading medical records occasionally these days. Every visit pretty much there is a wrong impression of what I am there for and what my goals are.

I just received the records from my second opinion cards and she just got it all wrong. I just don't want to end up on the conveyer belt, ya' know. I've worked so hard to avoid that and feel that if I give in to meds, that's where it's going to take me. And on the other hand, maybe a lifetime of meds would be better than what I have.

I know only I can decide this.... I think I will discuss this with the new neurologist though that apt is 10 days away.

### janagram — 2012-07-10T19:17:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36581>

I like your inquiry...you don't take anything for granted. Keep inquiring into what you want your life to look like, feel like, etc. Right now, you're taking care of yourself, and it's an important job. Wishing you the best!

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-10T20:15:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36600>

Thx, j. yes, sometimes I forget that what I'm doing is important. I get trapped in what I don't have.

### primat — 2012-07-11T13:56:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36774>

I'm also unvoluntarily playing the waiting game, and feel for you.

I can't seem to get access to my brain at all anymore, which is insanely frustrating since I know I'm really not stupid. It's been two months since my MRI came out clean and the endo have failed to call back or contact either me or my regular doctor. Now it's a month vaccation or two. In six months the sick leave payment stops and I can enjoy a 60% cut in income. The added stress of all this is weighing me down in itself. I am back in the saddle again on controlling the binge eating, but right now it feels like it's completely futile.

'Then it comes to be the soothing light at the end of the tunnel is just a freight train coming your way'

/rant

### Dali Dula — 2012-07-11T15:01:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36785>

I don't know your condition or limitations, but while i am waiting for all those frustrating things I put into practice the things I safely can. Start walking if you can handle that. Apply all of JK's protocols if you can and haven't done so. Set goals and knock them down. You don't have to wait to achieve goals even if they are small. Small victories can be inspiring. Do not wait for the rest of the world. Don't let yourself become a victim of the system. I am no fan of rx, but sometimes you do need them as a bridge as long as you are headed in the right direction. Treat yourself well and keep moving. Wish you the best.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-11T19:05:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36859>

> **primat;36940 wrote:** I'm also unvoluntarily playing the waiting game, and feel for you.
>
>
>
> I can't seem to get access to my brain at all anymore, which is insanely frustrating since I know I'm really not stupid. It's been two months since my MRI came out clean and the endo have failed to call back or contact either me or my regular doctor. Now it's a month vaccation or two. In six months the sick leave payment stops and I can enjoy a 60% cut in income. The added stress of all this is weighing me down in itself. I am back in the saddle again on controlling the binge eating, but right now it feels like it's completely futile.
>
>
>
> 'Then it comes to be the soothing light at the end of the tunnel is just a freight train coming your way'
>
>
>
> /rant

When something like this happens I become PITA patient. You've got to call and call again until they react. Or find a different doc. 2 months vacation is not acceptable in my case. They've got patients and they need to make sure they're taken care of while they disappear. Now \*I\* was complaining about my doc being gone for a couple of days  That's different. Speaks to my impatience.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-11T19:16:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36864>

> **Dali Dula;36951 wrote:** I don't know your condition or limitations, but while i am waiting for all those frustrating things I put into practice the things I safely can. Start walking if you can handle that. Apply all of JK's protocols if you can and haven't done so. Set goals and knock them down. You don't have to wait to achieve goals even if they are small. Small victories can be inspiring. Do not wait for the rest of the world. Don't let yourself become a victim of the system. I am no fan of rx, but sometimes you do need them as a bridge as long as you are headed in the right direction. Treat yourself well and keep moving. Wish you the best.

Thanks DD. What you say rings true and that's how I've been approaching things for the last few months. Hardcore CT, eating super clean AI, turning off the lights. I think part of the problem is that I am starting to feel better (really?) and so I want to do more and now I start to feel limited. But looking at it from a different perspective, that would be a good development

It's hard to set goals and knock them down if your brain isn't functioning correctly. My brain has gotten better a bit now and the only change I've made really is supplemental O2 which my docs are telling me I don't need. There's only so much you can fix if your brain isn't getting the nutrition it needs.... know what I mean? I think I might have reached that point.

I got a good night's sleep last night (11hrs! - restarted a small dose of DHEA) and I'm not quite as impatient anymore today. Onto more small victories while I prepare for the bigger ones

### BJK77 — 2012-07-11T19:40:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36874>

Hey Soul,

I can totally relate to what you're saying. Patient has never been a word used to describe me

You're obviously dealing with different challenges than I am, but what's working for me as I wait and wait to get test results, hear back from the doc, or just wait the 6 weeks it takes to get into my doctor is to fine tune my "plan of attack!" I read and re-read Dr. K's blogs that apply to my situation and highlight areas that I need to focus on or that hit home for me.

I'm a bit anal and a total list maker, so I'll jot down what I need to be doing right now (Ct, leptin rx, light cycle, etc) and what my next steps are going to be (additional testing, supplements, etc). It helps me feel like I'm moving forward while I'm waiting and then I find I feel more ready and prepared when I get into the doc next.

Yep, I'm simple minded and you're probably already doing this, but thought I'd put it out there anyway!

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-11T19:48:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36879>

Hey BJ, thanks for the input. My stickies are out of control!!! They're everywhere, electronically on my Mac, all over my coffee table, in my bag, on my fridge, .... Lol. My brain needs a little help  Fortunately, some of my planning skills are slowly reappearing, which is, to say the least, ridiculously helpful.

I'd like to spend quite a bit of time re-reading stuff on the blog but the state of my cognitive function limits that. One reason I'm considering going on meds.

I'm also constantly working on perfecting everything else.... more shellfish, more sun, more cold, more barefooting in the dirt. Now I just have to learn to get off the computer

Btw, I think I found my spunk for life.... now I need to create the body to live it!!

### Lyndra — 2012-07-12T02:50:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-37020>

> **Souldanzer wrote:**
>
>
>
> Btw, I think I found my spunk for life.... now I need to create the body to live it!!

Yay!

Here's a quote I stumbled across that a friend sent me when I was going through a tough time. Maybe some will resonate with you.

_1\. Make no judgements.

2\. Have no expectations.

3\. Give up the need to know why things happen as they do.

4\. Trust that the unscheduled events of our lives are a form of spiritual direction.

5\. Have the courage to make the choices we need to make, and accept what we cannot change.

\- from Anatomy of the Spirit, The Seven Stages of Power and Healing, by Caroline Myss

_

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-12T20:25:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-37224>

# Thread: Results?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/results.2296/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cookworm — 2012-07-09T22:18:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/results.2296/post-36382>

How soon can you expect results doing CT? I ask, because I haven't been doing CT long, but I swear, my thighs seem more muscular, and I haven't changed one thing with exercise (still doing the same things)...maybe I'm crazy???

Will CT also help increase the effectiveness of exercise?

### NWgirl — 2012-07-10T05:50:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/results.2296/post-36464>

Not crazy! At least I hope so... I'm having the same thoughts! I've had only 6 baths and I already feel that I've lost fat. I measured tonight and it says I've lost one inch around my middle. I felt like I was hallucinating, how could this be? Do I want it to work, so it's working? I'm also interested in what some non-newbies have to say on this!

Oh and my achy joint pain has definitely decreased, and my knees are stronger. On the flip side I am having some detox symptoms: body aches and had a couple days of behavioral issues (irritation, sensitive to sound, emotional).

### freesia — 2012-07-10T06:08:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/results.2296/post-36468>

I didn't have significant weight to lose but I lost an inch around my waist and gained muscle in my upper arms. I didn't exercise those muscles there!

Re exercise (not that I do a lot, due to illness) I have noticed I no longer have soreness and stiffness after exertion. I also don't get breathless like I did.

### caroline — 2012-07-10T08:06:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/results.2296/post-36476>

I did CT for about 6 weeks and had very significant improvement in arthritis - almost pain free - then I went to Tibet for a month and had no control over food - so - rice 3 times a day and lots of noodles and some flat breads etc. and very little protein. Now my arthritis is back with a vengeance and I am struggling to get back on track.

I did start to notice some body reshaping and leaning out after awhile too. I think CT is a wonder drug!

### vkiernan — 2012-07-10T14:29:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/results.2296/post-36498>

I believe this is exactly what CT is supposed to do from all of the blogs I have read. I'm still waiting for those kind of effects. Baby steps for some. Happy to hear you guys are rockin' it!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-10T15:23:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/results.2296/post-36519>

vickie the reason why it is slow for you might be from what you posted today......i think you might be turning all your thyroid hormone to rT3. if so the next step is to back off on thyroid meds and address the adrenal until it is back on line and then slowly add back the thyroid meds......you need your doc to help you with this for sure.

### vkiernan — 2012-07-10T15:28:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/results.2296/post-36523>

> **Jack Kruse;36685 wrote:** vickie the reason why it is slow for you might be from what you posted today......i think you might be turning all your thyroid hormone to rT3. if so the next step is to back off on thyroid meds and address the adrenal until it is back on line and then slowly add back the thyroid meds......you need your doc to help you with this for sure.

Thanks. I did ask for the RT3 for sure so I can do my ratio too. It will be a few days as it always takes longer for that result. I really was surprised at the FT3. My dheas was slightly over the range too and my progesterone was higher so all good progress. I will post when I have time to sort it all out. I believe good things are happening but still have some fixing.

### Tizawee — 2012-07-10T16:05:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/results.2296/post-36537>

I was a slow beginner: snorkeling only for the first month (began March 26th). Almost immediately, my sinus issues quit, my face cleared up, and my blackheads disappeared. After two weeks, my fat seemed kinda loosey-goosey and within days I dropped a jeans size.

I've been spot icing (in alot of spots ) for a couple of months now. My arthritis and various other aches/pains have dialed way down.

As Quelsen said, "My broken is not your broken." Your results may differ, but you will begin to see subtle changes as you keep with it.

Hope this helps.

Elin

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-10T17:23:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/results.2296/post-36549>

Elin

said it all, quoting Quelsen. There are days I CRAVE CT. and there are days I DO NOT. for me some are cycle dependent. are there days its hard to put my body in the ice water - YES. but given my other options - this is the best way to fix what I have going on... ignoring it isn't going to help.

I've see changes in body comp doing CT. for me that's a side benefit b/c while I'd love to have the BRTV body - its not the end goal for me. elimination of inflammation, get my Pg:e2 ratio up, bring down the fasting bg/insulin/HbA1C & fix my gut is my primary goal. Will I end up w/the BRTV body - hell yes, but only after I've fixed everything else.

each of us move at the pace our body can handle. the level I'm working at is not the level everyone else is working at. I'd love to Deep CT every day - but can't afford the ice right now (freaking storms have caused a run on ice) I'll do it 2-3x/week until it gets cold - then its a daily thing + Spot CT. do what you can, it will come

### msclman — 2012-07-11T20:08:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/results.2296/post-36891>

its been so hot here in ny that it really feels great to get into the ice bath for me. but then there are other days when i dread it

### Jewelrasal22 — 2012-07-14T21:14:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/results.2296/post-37809>

I haven't tried it also, But after joinong this forum i am thinking about doing CT...........

### PrimalPam — 2012-07-14T22:43:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/results.2296/post-37836>

I've been doing CT 5 x/week @ 52-55F for 40 mins for almost 5 weeks now. I swear, I saw results within 2 weeks. And I've never had any of the detox symptoms.

Now, this all includes adhering to my Paleo nutrition; I haven't stepped on the scale in a couple of weeks, but today, I "shopped" in my own closet and put on clothes I hadn't worn since the last millenium. And I sleep 8 hours...straight! Not even waking up to use the bathroom. My skin conditions have all cleared, and I breathe through my nose so much more easily. I think the airways are less inflamed? So I no longer need those "Breathe Right" strips to sleep.

I had an Upper Endoscopy done 2 years ago due to food getting stuck in my esophagus. She diagnosed it as "oesiniphilic esophagitis" and prescribed Prilosec daily, for the rest of my life. So, I did what the doctor told me, and promptly gained 10 lbs., all in my mid-section. Discontinued the stupid Prilosec, but the GERD was awful, and the food kept getting stuck. Figured it was baby food for me for the rest of my life. One of the great side effects I've had of strict Paleo (no grains!) and CT is...I can swallow all my food. Meat, shrimp, etc., all get chewed up and have ZERO problems going down and GERD is completely gone. Again, less inflammation?

So for me, it's the magic combo of strict Paleo + CT = Optimal.

I feel like I found a magic key...

### NWgirl — 2012-07-15T00:23:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/results.2296/post-37863>

Primalpam... This is amazing thank you for sharing! It really helps someone like me (new to all of this). I was just irritated that I just accidentally ate corn tortilla in a "frittata" of all things and was feeling down until I read your post. Thank you!

Kim

### PrimalPam — 2012-07-15T00:34:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/results.2296/post-37868>

> **NWgirl;38031 wrote:** Primalpam... This is amazing thank you for sharing! It really helps someone like me (new to all of this). I was just irritated that I just accidentally ate corn tortilla in a "frittata" of all things and was feeling down until I read your post. Thank you!
>
>
>
> Kim

Well listen, Kim, it's not like I'm some Paleo/CT saint.  When I started Paleo, I tried Mark Sisson's 80/20 rule, but found that was waaaay too lenient for me. So these days, before I eat something blatantly non-Paleo (yes, I ate one of my Red Velvet July 4th cupcakes), I ask myself "Is this optimal?" Or, WWJD (What would Jack Do?) lol.

When I was trying the 80/20, I'd lose a few lbs, gain 'em right back. Sleep through the night, then have a few toss 'n turn nights. It wasn't until I started more like 98/2 which, for me, is like a short amount of dental floss rather than a big long rope with which to hang myself.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-15T01:05:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/results.2296/post-37874>

80/20 is great for mediocre.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-09T22:19:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-fat-kids-tale.2297/post-36383>

...........................

### bigknitwit — 2012-07-09T22:36:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-fat-kids-tale.2297/post-36386>

I am at a loss for words - but really wanted to thank you for sharing. It's really inspirational for me to listen to someone's story as they work hard to transform themselves. I will stay tuned..

### vlynnb — 2012-07-09T22:41:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-fat-kids-tale.2297/post-36387>

I enjoyed this installment. Keep it up. I have my own battles but not that critical. I see you then like one of the many sad, very overweight young people I see on street corners and in school rooms in my area. I feel so sad when I see them and just wish I could help. I know their weight is impacting all parts of their lives. I can see it in their eyes and in the hopeless looking faces. I am sure your story will help others. Thanks.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-09T22:46:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-fat-kids-tale.2297/post-36388>

> **vlynnb;36553 wrote:** I enjoyed this installment. Keep it up. I have my own battles but not that critical. I see you then like one of the many sad, very overweight young people I see on street corners and in school rooms in my area. I feel so sad when I see them and just wish I could help. I know their weight is impacting all parts of their lives. I can see it in their eyes and in the hopeless looking faces. I am sure your story will help others. Thanks.

It kills me to see the fat kids now, and usually well meaning parents feeding them SAD or worse, some version of a diet. At least they all have company now!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-09T23:43:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-fat-kids-tale.2297/post-36398>

Thanks for taking the time and sharing!

Transformation really means always transforming. It is positive transformation that keeps us at our best. Keep looking inside, change what we can (although maybe not quite to the extent of surgery, yet it worked for you!), do our best at making the desired transformation happen without excuses, and we will become someone even better than we originally set out to be....someone even we didn't think we could become!

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-09T23:47:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-fat-kids-tale.2297/post-36399>

JUST AWESOME!!!! Wtg it's hard work fixing ourselves. Keep on icing!!

### Lyndra — 2012-07-10T00:48:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-fat-kids-tale.2297/post-36406>

I've never had to walk down the path you've had to take but I feel like I can empathize better having read about your life. No point in living in a shell, seeking long life just to get old without having enjoyment. Thanks for sharing.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-10T01:04:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-fat-kids-tale.2297/post-36410>

Everyone wants to transform, but few embrace real change. How do we change? Transform your thoughts and embrace all change as your new and only way of living. The essence of being alive is to live like you truly mean it and transform every last limiting belief you have. You must completely erase your old self when still have no clue what your new self might be. You are a blank canvas and your tools are your brush and your paint. What appears on your canvas today becomes the future self. That self must become aware of its creation. If it fails to realize it, you will only fall back into the rut in which you are today. Your thoughts and beliefs are what formed that rut. Your choices dug that hole. Today's new thoughts will pull you from that rut and allow you to become the artist you want to be for the canvas that is blank in front of you.

The journey between today's current picture of what you are in that rut and who you are painting and reengineering are where the beautiful music of life is and where the dancing of your life really takes place. You have total control of the brush, the paint, the sculpting tools, and the picture you have for you in your mind. The real key thought is stop looking for yourself and begin to create yourself as the artist would. Transforming change requires the thought and belief........and it is followed by a purposeful action. Ideation without proper execution leads to deletion of that masterpiece.

Begin that masterpiece now.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-10T02:58:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-fat-kids-tale.2297/post-36431>

> **Cavemam;36549 wrote:** I had some major demons to slay during that time and I had as many downs as ups, but I couldn’t be the modern-day Cavemam without it.

I love this story you've shared, and the perspective you've shared with it. Optimal belongs in context and it's why each of our paths are different. No one emerges from life unscathed, but the bravery you're talking about here - the kind that transforms vulnerability into strength - is inspiring. Part of what I hear you doing is burning a history of shame... and you emerge from that ash a beautiful phoenix. With an awesome sense of humour (which I'm sure was hard-earned, but is an absolute jewel you've held on to!). Thanks.

### Destiny — 2012-07-10T03:14:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-fat-kids-tale.2297/post-36437>

You are a very brave and inspiring woman.

### caroline — 2012-07-13T15:08:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-fat-kids-tale.2297/post-37460>

Thanks for sharing your story. I was a really fat kid too and it is so hard to erase the scars. It sounds to me tho - in spite of everything - you have had one hell of a ride!! I need to hear more about Italy!

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-13T15:41:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-fat-kids-tale.2297/post-37466>

It's 5am here, and I'm sitting here drinking my morning coffee and reading your story and keep saying "Wow" out loud.

I am shocked at your pictures and how different they are! If I hadn't known it, I would have said they were two different people. I can see the pain on your face in your before photos, and how much you glow in your after photos. Wow.

I started getting chubby around age 12. Was never obese, but felt different when my Mom used to buy my school clothes from the chubby section at Sears. My mother NEVER EVER harassed me about my weight, in fact she was the opposite. She was always telling me how pretty and smart I was, which I think made a HUGE difference for me. It wasn't until I was a teenager and started to get interested in boys that I stated to get self conscious about my chubbiness. I had a very good looking and popular brother, and he would tell me that I would be such a fox if I lost the weight.

So yes, I can relate to the many attempts to lose weight, cause I tried them all too. You did what you thought was right at the time for you, and it gave you some relief from the agony of your tortured soul. Please don't regret it. because I think the biggest change came for you mentally, not physically. Can you reach optimal health? Yes, I think so. Keep up the work on yourself mentally and they physical will follow.

I am going to have my kids read your story, especially my daughter. I get so angry when I hear her perpetuate the attitude that fat people are fat because they are lazy and eat too much. I know she gets that attitude from her father, who is now my ex. (Any wonder why?) I understand the emotional pain they people are experiencing on the inside, and so want to help them, but they are not always ready to hear.

Thanks for sharing your story, you are very brave and inspiring.

Grace

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-16T05:49:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-fat-kids-tale.2297/post-38277>

> **caroline;37627 wrote:** Thanks for sharing your story. I was a really fat kid too and it is so hard to erase the scars. It sounds to me tho - in spite of everything - you have had one hell of a ride!! I need to hear more about Italy!

That one is in person over wine!

### janagram — 2012-07-16T08:40:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-fat-kids-tale.2297/post-38309>

### Sem — 2012-07-10T01:24:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-little-freaked-out-about-cholesterol.2300/post-36415>

I just got some bloodwork back from my doctor. The nurse said my cholesterol is high 222 total and 142 HDL. This is not fasting. I have never had high cholesterol but have been following hcg/paleo/leptin RX the past six months. It must be due to the diet, no? The doctor is mailing me my full blood work results along with a prescription for Crestor. I'm trying to schedule a consult with Dr. Kruse now. I'm assuming most would hold off on the prescription for now? Feeling a little nervous about it all ... I am also having a full hormone panel in a couple of weeks.

The doctor is mailing me my full blood work results along with a prescription for Crestor. I'm trying to schedule a consult with Dr. Kruse now. I'm assuming most would hold off on the prescription for now? Feeling a little nervous about it all ... I am also having a full hormone panel in a couple of weeks.

I don't believe that your total and HDL are bad. Can you post your LDL and triglycerids? I would worry more about those.

### colleencoble — 2012-07-10T01:39:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-little-freaked-out-about-cholesterol.2300/post-36417>

That's not high. LOL sheesh, they're scaring you for no reason. I wouldn't take that Crestor if you paid me good money. Often high cholesterol is from hypothyroidism but I wouldn't call 222 high, especially not with your good cholesterol that high! Until the leptin reset, my total cholesterol was 130 and I'd been trying to get it to raise for literally decades. I was THRILLED when I got it to a little over 200. Too low is much worse than too high. You need cholesterol to make hormones.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-10T01:59:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-little-freaked-out-about-cholesterol.2300/post-36419>

Dr K (and Chris Masterjohn, Chris Kresser and many others) have said many times that total cholesterol numbers are at best meaningless... and typically in the literature the higher your cholesterol the better. High HDL is fine - and seems to point to your gut not being leaky, too. You need to worry about your trigs and your small density LDL, as well as your ratios. My 2cents is definitely do NOT take the script and wait til your consult. I'd also really want to know your hsCRP to give an indication of inflammation. You have to worry about oxidation, not cholesterol numbers per se.

### Sem — 2012-07-10T02:12:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-little-freaked-out-about-cholesterol.2300/post-36421>

I am wondering if I misheard and the other one was my LDL? Would that be bad?

### colleencoble — 2012-07-10T02:41:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-little-freaked-out-about-cholesterol.2300/post-36426>

Your HDL would still be 75 which is pretty good. Really, don't worry, Sem. The main thing to check is your hs-CRP and homocysteine levels. It's inflammation that matters, not cholesterol. Both of my parents have low cholesterol, 130-140 total. And they've both had heart attacks because of the inflammation.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-10T02:41:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-little-freaked-out-about-cholesterol.2300/post-36427>

Here is a gem for you, Sem. (From one of the comments in the Hormones 101 blog, that I am re-reading!)

Low cholesterol is a bad problem. The real reason are these two……when our cholesterol levels drop below 200 we start not having enough LDL cholesterol to perform cell division perfectly all the time. Many physicians are unaware that the mitotic spindle in all mammals need cholesterol for cell division. When you do not have enough your mitotic spindle is not as efficient and it can lead to aneuploidy and chromosomal damage. I personally think this is how many of the genetic diseases we face today are affected by. More bad news…….when humans get aneuploidy and chromosomal damage it leads to cancer…….oncogenesis. Now immediately go and look at any statin trial and go see if people die of cancer more when they take statins……..They do. This was also found in the Framingham heart study. I wrote about it here. [http://jackkruse.com/why-does-heart-disease-really-occur/](http://http://jackkruse.com/why-does-heart-disease-really-occur/)

Second big issue…….all human hormones are made from LDL cholesterol LDL converts to pregnenolone to start the chain of control with T3 and vitamin A as co factors. If your LDL is low, your hormone panels fall……and disease follows. The brain controls its 20 trillion cells in our body using hormones……so if you do not have good hormone control your brain is driving your metabolism with any eye sight. Our profession is blind to these effects but our customers are not. When I draw their labs daily and predict their levels from just looking at their MRI’s they are kind of shocked. They should not be shocked because this is how biology of humans work via an evolutionary paradigm.

### donkjellberg — 2012-07-10T03:35:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-little-freaked-out-about-cholesterol.2300/post-36445>

Total cholesterol between 200 and 246 is associated with the lowest risk of death from all causes. Most of the long-term observational studies substantiate this.

You have now placed yourself at the lowest risk category for all cause mortality. Don't worry. Celebrate!!!

### Sem — 2012-07-10T06:34:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-little-freaked-out-about-cholesterol.2300/post-36472>

It's all so confusing! This guy who took my bloodwork is a cardiologist. Sees everyone in my family. Just started someone else on chooesterol meds too. I don't know what the other numbers are but I am hoping I'll see it all when I get a copy in the mail. The other thing that's confusing is that if my numbers are so good for hormone making, why am I not making my hormones properly? I am not getting my period properly and am having hot flashes, etc. I am 42 but have been dealing with this on and off for seven years! Went to my gyn today and she gave me a prescription for progesterone to being on my period so I can do a hormone panel on day 18. Is the progesterone going to make me crazy? I have had so many bad experiences with prescriptions that I approach all with extreme caution!

Thanks for your responses. I feel better.

### Sem — 2012-07-13T05:47:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-little-freaked-out-about-cholesterol.2300/post-37418>

I also was not fasting. But I just went online and even the Mayo clinic site says the numbers are borderline high. But he still sent me a prescription. Maybe because he treats everyone else in my family for heart disease?

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-13T06:45:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-little-freaked-out-about-cholesterol.2300/post-37423>

> **Sem;37585 wrote:** I also was not fasting. But I just went online and even the Mayo clinic site says the numbers are borderline high. But he still sent me a prescription. Maybe because he treats everyone else in my family for heart disease?

OK, deep breath. Sounds like your testing wasn't optimal. Lipid panels are usually fasting. I think, in order to put it in perspective, you also need to get a thyroid panel (TSH, T3 and T4; and Reverse T3 if they'll let you), and definitely an hsCRP. If possible, you need to add Vitamin D and DHEA. Probably wouldn't hurt to get a Progesterone and Estrogen reading, too. Then you'll have context. As Nonchalant points out, there is likely a underlying reason, but you need to find out what it is (taking a drug to lower the number doesn't treat the underlying reason, is likely unnecessary and is a drug that can kill you!)

As others have said, the total cholesterol isn't a problem (look back at what Dr K and others have written... over 200 is GOOD, not bad).

If it were me, I'd order a bunch more labs (and do lipids fasting, do HbA1c too) and then have a consult with Dr K. But freaking out isn't in your Rx!

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-13T06:46:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-little-freaked-out-about-cholesterol.2300/post-37424>

> **Sem;37585 wrote:** even the Mayo clinic site says the numbers are borderline high

Ah, yes, the Mayo Clinic, that bastion of free-thinking, non-pharmaceutical, open minded medicine

### Sem — 2012-07-13T08:05:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-little-freaked-out-about-cholesterol.2300/post-37434>

> **KiwiLauren;37590 wrote:** OK, deep breath. Sounds like your testing wasn't optimal. Lipid panels are usually fasting. I think, in order to put it in perspective, you also need to get a thyroid panel (TSH, T3 and T4; and Reverse T3 if they'll let you), and definitely an hsCRP. If possible, you need to add Vitamin D and DHEA. Probably wouldn't hurt to get a Progesterone and Estrogen reading, too. Then you'll have context. As Nonchalant points out, there is likely a underlying reason, but you need to find out what it is (taking a drug to lower the number doesn't treat the underlying reason, is likely unnecessary and is a drug that can kill you!)
>
>
>
> As others have said, the total cholesterol isn't a problem (look back at what Dr K and others have written... over 200 is GOOD, not bad).
>
>
>
> If it were me, I'd order a bunch more labs (and do lipids fasting, do HbA1c too) and then have a consult with Dr K. But freaking out isn't in your Rx!

Not in my RX just my DNA!  He did do vitamin D, 25 hydroxy and it's 46 ng/nL. They consider "optimum level" to be 30-80.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-13T22:31:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-little-freaked-out-about-cholesterol.2300/post-37590>

> **Sem;37601 wrote:** Not in my RX just my DNA!  He did do vitamin D, 25 hydroxy and it's 46 ng/nL. They consider "optimum level" to be 30-80.

Vitamin D way too low. Needs to be above the range. If you're not supplementing, you need to start... if you are, probably need to increase your dose. Also, since it's summer where you are, 20 minutes of naked tanning per day is great.

### colleencoble — 2012-07-13T23:04:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-little-freaked-out-about-cholesterol.2300/post-37603>

The first thing you need to get past is thinking your cholesterol being high is the problem for heart disease. It isn't. Cholesterol means NOTHING in the absence of inflammation. if you have inflammation, it doesn't matter if it's high or low, your body will use it to line your arteries. The second thing is the assumption that Mayo clinic or any of those places knows beans about this subject. They are still throwing lipid drugs at people. Truly, I wouldn't take a lipid drug for any amount of money.

# Thread: Acid Reflux/Gerd from exercising hard
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/acid-reflux-gerd-from-exercising-hard.2301/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-07-10T02:36:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/acid-reflux-gerd-from-exercising-hard.2301/post-36424>

Hey everyone. I just started doing Insanity which is a really hard workout. I'm only on the second day, but so far I've been getting some acid reflux while exercising. Which I've never had happen before. The only time I've ever had any reflux is when I was a vegan like 7 yrs ago.. it started with tomato sauce. I still occasionally get it from canned tomato sauce or canned crushed tomatoes. But other than that it has stopped from just eating paleo. I've never experienced it with exercise before though.

I had been doing the reset from Feb '12 - April '12. I moved to the post Leptin plan in April. About mid/end of April I kinda fell off of the leptin plan. But I still stayed paleo/primal. I didn't eat gluten.. and didn't eat horribly, but I did eat some chips or corn and some candy or ice cream.

I've started back on the post leptin plan/strict paleo. I started Insanity on Sunday. I have been exercising around 4-5pm. I've been doing it on an empty stomach and then eating dinner within an hour of exercising. I'm lost as to what could be causing this. But, it's really annoying. I can't find any information that's helpful online so far. Everything is just for normal gerd sufferers and regular doctor advice like take antacids or whatnot. WHich I have never done and will not do. So, looking for some advice here if anyone has any. Thanks.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-10T03:00:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/acid-reflux-gerd-from-exercising-hard.2301/post-36432>

its called a poor cortisol and terrible gastrin circadian cycle........you need to the read the CT blog about 24 hour circadian biology carefully. Your activity will destroy your brain and gut wiring if you continue.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-11T21:12:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/acid-reflux-gerd-from-exercising-hard.2301/post-36907>

The reflux stopped I think. Yesterday it didn't happen. I think maybe I ate something with gluten while out and didn't realize it.

I know you prefer less working out, which I normally prescribe to. I didn't think 60 days of more intense working out would really cause that much damage. I'm not using any weights as it's all body weight exercises. I guess it may be high in the cardio, but other than these 30 min/day workouts I am actually sitting for the most part. I didn't really think that would be excessive for working out. I'm following the post leptin as far as eating. I'm pretty keto even as I'm trying to lose about 28 lbs still. I'm trying to build some muscle while losing fat still with Insanity. After the 60 days I plan to go back to my normal exercise which is some hula hooping and occasional pole dancing.

How is my gastrin circadian cycle messed up when I'm eating like the plan says? I'm eating breakfast within 30 min to an hour of waking. I'm not eating lunch.. if I'm a little hungry I just drink tea with coconut milk in it. Then I work out usually 4-5pm and then eat dinner within an hour after my workout. I'm not eating anything after dinner. I'm getting a minimum of 8 hrs of sleep. Is it just the extra cardio that you think is the issue?

I can't CT in the summer as I don't have any money to buy ice and my water isn't very cold. I do take showers in as cold as my water allows and I do some ice packs that I have for spot CTing.

### vkiernan — 2012-07-12T05:09:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/acid-reflux-gerd-from-exercising-hard.2301/post-37073>

Just curious, why do Insanity if you are just going back to normal routine afterwards? This isn't going to help your weight loss if that's the plan. You are raising your cortisol by doing the intense cardio. If you are LS, then by continuing to eat post LRx will help you lose weight. You can lift heavy to build muscle. Much better plan than wearing yourself out. I know what insanity is and it is exactly that, insane. Not trying to sound mean or anything but raising your cortisol is really the worse thing you can do right now. As for stress, your system won't work right if you are stressed hence the gerd. That is your first clue.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-10T19:03:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-recommendations-on-an-electric-smoker.2304/post-36574>

Little Chief, made in Hood River Oregon

this is a basic smoker, no timers etc and you have to replace the wood chips by hand (although that is very easily done) but works well and is a classic for the hunting community.

### caroline — 2012-07-11T04:02:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-recommendations-on-an-electric-smoker.2304/post-36719>

I use a stovetop one and it is very easy to use and works well

### allen001 — 2012-07-11T07:50:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-recommendations-on-an-electric-smoker.2304/post-36761>

I used E cig its cool, and easy to use and give more smoke to enjoy.
