# Thread: Mike H. Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-h-journal.2311/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mike H. — 2012-07-10T06:40:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-h-journal.2311/post-36474>

Hello all,

My name is Mike Hoban, I am a S&C coach who likes to learn and try new things. I recently came across cold fusion thermogenesis CT, and am very interested in how it will work to increase athletic capability. So I will begin by testing on myself.

I started tonight, by performing 10-12 30 second face dunks in chilled water with some added ice. Unfortunately my thermometer didnt work tonight so I couldn't get any solid numbers. My face is stilll slightly chilly feeling and I am feeling very calm and relaxed as of right now. Looking forward to seeing how this goes. I am open to any and all criticisms and or advice.

thanks

### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-10T15:38:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-h-journal.2311/post-36531>

Cool and welcome.......I think you will be shocked at what happens in S&C.

### Mike H. — 2012-07-11T06:14:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-h-journal.2311/post-36749>

Ok, day 2 is done. I face dunked tonight for a total of 4 minutes of immersion, with chilled tap water and added ice. Worked out this evening just some low volume leg work. Workout went very well, felt very strong and lifts where quick. Some things I noticed today, it seemed as though I had an increased frequency of urination as well as increased amount of hunger. (in a good way.) So far so good,

### Mike H. — 2012-07-12T07:05:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-h-journal.2311/post-37102>

Another easy workout today. 100 kettlebell swings with a 40 lb bell. Dunked for a total 5:30. So far noticing faster recovery, quicker relief from soreness. Right now enjoying the feeling of circulation returning to my face lol.

### nonchalant — 2012-07-12T21:07:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-h-journal.2311/post-37235>

Welcome Mike H! Faster recovery after just a couple days of face dunks? Cool. I didn't exercise when I started CT, so I didn't notice anything for a while. The muscle gains with no exercise came as a complete surprise to me. Probably all the shivering after getting out of the tub?

### Mike H. — 2012-07-13T07:20:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-h-journal.2311/post-37426>

> **Nonchalant;37402 wrote:** Welcome Mike H! Faster recovery after just a couple days of face dunks? Cool. I didn't exercise when I started CT, so I didn't notice anything for a while. The muscle gains with no exercise came as a complete surprise to me. Probably all the shivering after getting out of the tub?

Yes, I am not going to lie, I have used ice baths before to hasten recovery, but this seems different. I am noticing I look and feel leaner, and actually slightly bigger. Hunger is increasing, especially at night, so i am loading up on a lot of protein and essential fatty acids. Still noticing an increase in urination, again in a good way. I feel as though I am carrying less bloat.

As far as today goes, I did a fast shoulder and arm workout. i wont bore you all with the detalis, but I hit a 285 overhead press. My warm-ups felt really light and fast, and I actually got loose quicker. Got to 630 on the face dunks tonight. Jack if you are around please enlighten me on whats going on!?!? Thank you all for reading

### Mike H. — 2012-07-14T22:05:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-h-journal.2311/post-37821>

Took last night off. Got home late from a baseball game and just fell a sleep. Will resume tonight.

### Mike H. — 2012-07-16T06:31:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-h-journal.2311/post-38289>

Reached 7 minutes on the face dunks tonight. Had a good energy systems/strongman workout today. Feeling recovered and good.

### Mike H. — 2012-07-18T07:49:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-h-journal.2311/post-38957>

Hello again. Hit 8 minutes on the face dunks this evening. Had a good workout this evening, 6 sets of incline press supersetted with 6 sets of rows. Felt strong, work had to be cut short due to some conference calls.

### Mike H. — 2012-07-23T07:00:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-h-journal.2311/post-40356>

Ok, I missed a few days of face dunks due to schedule and exhaustion. Today I started my day with a five minute face dunk, and finished my evening with a 10minute dunk session. Tomorrow is leg day, so we shall see how it goes.

### Melinda — 2012-07-23T07:37:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-h-journal.2311/post-40359>

I just listened to the first webinar about deep CT and I am so motivated again to find ways to get icy even in summer. My face will be chilled tomorrow. Thanks for the reminder Mike.

### Mike H. — 2012-07-24T07:10:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-h-journal.2311/post-40604>

Thanks for the reply Melinda. Workout went real well today. Felt strong and fast after a 12 hour day. So far real good. Tried the compression shirt technique tonight. I didnt really feel to much when the ice was on top of the garment. So after 10 mins I placed the ice under the shirt, on top of my tank top t-shirt. Left it there for about another 25 mins. Skin was red, but wasnt overly frozen. Felt good. I'll give this ago for a week or se, then progress to cold water immersion.

### Mike H. — 2012-07-24T18:03:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-h-journal.2311/post-40696>

Did a quick 20 minute energy systems/recovery workout. No real soreness today, and I am noticing some detox effects. Will try Ice on chest again this evening.

### Mike H. — 2012-07-25T07:04:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-h-journal.2311/post-40943>

Had ice on the chest for about 40 mins tonight. Workouts have been going well. I am still experiencing some weird detox symptoms. Feel like I am sweating after removing the ice. Also still experiencing a gastric response after icing. Pretty interesting stuff.

# Thread: Hi all from Kyoto Japan
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all-from-kyoto-japan.2312/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### talkingdog — 2012-07-10T11:18:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all-from-kyoto-japan.2312/post-36489>

Hi. Expat in Japan, low-carbing for 18 months, lost 40 pounds, 20 more to go -- stuck on a weight-loss plateau for the past year. I came here seeking a hormonal solution to this plateau.

Results! At one week into strict application of Leptin Reset I have begun to see the scale move. Amazing.

Now I am on day two of CT. Cold shower I can deal with but I still FEAR the Ice.

### caroline — 2012-07-10T12:58:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all-from-kyoto-japan.2312/post-36492>

Welcome talkingdog and congratulations on your journey to optimal! Cold showers are really hard! most think they are way harder than the full immersion.

### talkingdog — 2012-07-10T15:11:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all-from-kyoto-japan.2312/post-36510>

Fortunately I have a public bath down the street here from me that has a very deep, cold water bath. Next stop.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-10T15:13:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all-from-kyoto-japan.2312/post-36514>

Welcome and make sure you use that bath......

### talkingdog — 2012-07-11T00:11:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all-from-kyoto-japan.2312/post-36639>

Thanks, Jack. I will. And I guess I will finally have the reason to get an icemaker.

I am up earlier than usual this morning and feeling fine.

I used to live next to a buddhist temple here in Kyoto where they had an ice cold mountain waterfall. The young monks would wear loincloths and stand under the blast of cold water and recite sutras every morning. Ancient CT.

### caroline — 2012-07-11T03:11:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all-from-kyoto-japan.2312/post-36707>

That is so interesting - love to know more

### talkingdog — 2012-07-11T06:18:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all-from-kyoto-japan.2312/post-36750>

This is a pretty typical or cliched Buddhist practice.

The legendary warrior Mushashi is said to have done tons of this waterfall training.

### Jack23 — 2012-07-19T03:37:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all-from-kyoto-japan.2312/post-39254>

Yeah I do some research about that tradition and I cant believe

a lot of samurai warrior do waterfall training...awesome..

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

[http://www.themodernman.com/how\_to\_talk\_to\_women.html](http://http://www.themodernman.com/how_to_talk_to_women.html)

### talkingdog — 2012-07-20T08:38:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all-from-kyoto-japan.2312/post-39619>

I also think that if you look into various corners of Japanese culture you will see

cold training. One example is the exposure of elementary school children to cold weather

training in January clad in very scanty garments.

### AmyJ — 2012-07-22T15:51:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all-from-kyoto-japan.2312/post-40209>

Welcome, talkingdog! Glad to hear you jumped out of the starting gate. Like your idea of looking for hormonal solution. That light bulb just clicked for me recently. I'm shifting toward almost all seafood and sea veggies as my diet and as I do the pounds are coming off so easy. I'm curious about the diet there. What do you see people eating on a daily basis? How westernized have they become? Ah, to reverse the clock!

### talkingdog — 2012-08-01T08:54:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all-from-kyoto-japan.2312/post-42226>

Sorry to be late replying. As for the diet here in Japan, it's rather diverse. I think that the younger demographic tends to eat a lot of junky food that's just as bad as SAD. Although there is much less soda pop and no supersizing. Portions are much smaller. Ingredient quality is higher.

As for the type of diet that Jack is promoting, lot's of seafood and sea veggies, that's very Japanese of course. We do tend to eat a bit of fermented soy products such as natto, tofu, miso, and shoyu, as well as a vast array of pickled vegetables. The idea of eliminating these fermented soy products from the diet strikes a raw cultural nerve just as though one were suggesting the elimination of white rice. Heresy.

Somebody told me the other day that researchers had recently discovered that some toxic aspect of the production of miso, shoyu, etc. was involved in the very high stomach and colon cancer numbers here (no. 1 and no. 2 cause of death). I have yet to see the citation for this research.

In general, though, I think people eat a lot healthier in Japan and it shows in the longevity numbers and also just simply in the fact that everybody is so slim. Although middle aged people will pack a little bulge of fat, you seldom see somebody who is morbidly obese.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: FAT - where do you get yours?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-where-do-you-get-yours.2314/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### vkiernan — 2012-07-10T14:16:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-where-do-you-get-yours.2314/post-36496>

I have to do a Metametrics GI Effects test and am not supposed to do coconut oil 3 days prior. My problem is I am not supposed to do dairy and that leaves butter out. How am I supposed to get enough fat in? I have fried up bacon but that only gives me a limited amount to work with. Looking for ideas. Thanks all.

### BJK77 — 2012-07-10T15:11:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-where-do-you-get-yours.2314/post-36511>

How about lard, tallow, or palm oil? All are good to use for sauteeing veggies or just dumping on meat.

### vkiernan — 2012-07-10T15:23:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-where-do-you-get-yours.2314/post-36520>

I will be thrilled to be able to use ghee. I forgot about lard. I actually have some. Got to get some of that red palm oil and try it. Thanks.

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-10T15:30:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-where-do-you-get-yours.2314/post-36525>

> **Jack Kruse;36679 wrote:** or ghee.......

2nd the ghee.... I make my own w/Kerry Gold grass feed butter - I've got the process down pat... [http://www.aayisrecipes.com/2007/05/14/how-to-make-ghee/](http://http://www.aayisrecipes.com/2007/05/14/how-to-make-ghee/)

its pretty easy - and way effing cheaper than buying it at the store. IMO Kerry Gold unsalted grassfed butter makes the best ghee - I've used Organic Valley cultured butter - its good but not as good as KErry Gold ghee.... the kids actually ask for spoonfuls of ghee initially DH was hesitant to let them eat it... I told him - if they're asking for it, there's a reason why - let them eat ghee!

### SeaHorse — 2012-07-10T16:19:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-where-do-you-get-yours.2314/post-36541>

I'm a big duck fat fan...you can buy it in small tubs in the freezer at my natural food store. 1-800-Dartagnan I add it to some of the grassfed hamburger that doesn't have enough fat. YUM

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-10T17:00:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-where-do-you-get-yours.2314/post-36543>

Duck Fat and Ghee! The ghee I use is [http://www.amazon.com/Purity-Farm-Organic-Clarified-13-Ounce/dp/B0046IIPMW/ref=sr\_1\_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1341928759&sr=8-2&keywords=ghee+purity](http://http://www.amazon.com/Purity-Farm-Organic-Clarified-13-Ounce/dp/B0046IIPMW/ref=sr_1_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1341928759&sr=8-2&keywords=ghee+purity). ### vkiernan — 2012-07-11T06:06:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thanks-dr-k-for-all-you-do-for-us.2315/post-36747>

> **Patty Cakes;36710 wrote:** Congrats vkiernan! Wow that is such a change. Can't wait for my new labs soon. Just curious if you tested progesterone and how the Pg/E2 ratio looked?

Patty, I calculated the ratio and it is 55:1 if I did it correctly. I had to convert the progesterone to pg and then convert both to moles. I don't know what this means yet. Have to do further reading. It was an unexpected change for sure. Just waiting to see if it is going to RT3 or not.

What do you know about the ratios? Where is yours at?

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-11T06:29:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thanks-dr-k-for-all-you-do-for-us.2315/post-36752>

You are definitely going in the right direction! The only thing I know about the ratios is this calculator: [http://new.instacalc.com/421](http://http://new.instacalc.com/421) Also the ratio should be 100-500 and I believe Dr. K said it should be closer to 500.

My ratio as of March was a horrible @ .43. I know my estrogen has definitely gone down and progesterone up but need to test....coming soon.

### Destiny — 2012-07-11T13:14:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thanks-dr-k-for-all-you-do-for-us.2315/post-36772>

> **Patty Cakes;36918 wrote:** You are definitely going in the right direction! The only thing I know about the ratios is this calculator: [http://new.instacalc.com/421](http://http://new.instacalc.com/421) Also the ratio should be 100-500 and I believe Dr. K said it should be closer to 500.
>
>
>
> My ratio as of March was a horrible @ .43. I know my estrogen has definitely gone down and progesterone up but need to test....coming soon.

That is a handy calculator ! My ration comes @ 49 that is not a good balance either.

Do I need to increase/lower the progesterone or increase/lower the estradiol?

I supplemented progesterone in small quantities (25 mg/day) without any estrogen for almost two years. I had a salivary test done in January 2012 and the progesterone/estrogen ratio was @ 1,500 since I had almost no estrogen. Then, I started supplementing with estrogen and continued with progesterone as well. It seems that everything is out of whack!

### vkiernan — 2012-07-11T14:24:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thanks-dr-k-for-all-you-do-for-us.2315/post-36778>

>
>

I had a salivary test done in January 2012 and the progesterone/estrogen ratio was @ 1,500 since I had almost no estrogen. Then, I started supplementing with estrogen and continued with progesterone as well. It seems that everything is out of whack!We are pretty close. I am thinking when you have to supplement your hormones that it is not an easy ride and a LOT tweaking goes on. This is probably why a lot of docs don't do it. Too much work which is unfortunate for us.
>
>
>
> Patty Cakes;36918 wrote:** You are definitely going in the right direction! The only thing I know about the ratios is this calculator: [http://new.instacalc.com/421](http://http://new.instacalc.com/421) Also the ratio should be 100-500 and I believe Dr. K said it should be closer to 500.

If that's the ratio, I have a lot of work to do yet then. But, I would imagine once I start supplementing with progesterone, that my ratio will get better. Although it will be still a lot of testing and work. In my last consult, Jack told me to find a good doc for hormones to get my ratio in balance. This will be tough. I also have to find someone who will run more tests to see if I have panhypopititarism.

Let me know how your results are. I think someone out here even has a thread on this stuff. I should go see if I can find it. I would like to know what you are doing to reduce your estrogen.

# Thread: Hiv remedies :
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hiv-remedies.2316/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### joseph.steve — 2012-07-10T14:54:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hiv-remedies.2316/post-36504>

HIV is not curable.We can just prevent our self from those activities and ways which can lead us towards this death.Although doctors are performing a bigger and expansive research for this but eventually prevention is good.I will share two remedies if you have been fallen in this that engage your self in exercise and always be happy because at least it can allow you to avoid from stress.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-10T15:11:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hiv-remedies.2316/post-36512>

I do not know if I buy this any longer. We now have documentation of several thousand people who are completely immune to HIV......the reason is simple and goes back to Brain Gut 2. those people have reshuffled the HIV genome and it has lost its virulence for them very similar to SIV has done to most primates. I think as time goes on that genetic information will be standard in all humans. The humans who are susceptible to HIV will be the ones who have to avoid getting it. Eventually however the surviving species will all become immune to it.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Flight or fight in the water....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flight-or-fight-in-the-water.2321/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Amazonmagic — 2012-07-10T18:35:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flight-or-fight-in-the-water.2321/post-36571>

So I have been on vacation in the Redwood forest for about a week. During our vacation we camped, ate by the fire and bathed in the River. It was wonderful. During our vacation we decided to take a Klamath Jet boat River tour. We had a couple of hours before the tour began and decided to go for an ocean swim. We got to the beach and noticed there were no swimmers. We decided to go swimming in the estuary a few hundred yards away. An estuary is where the mouth of the river meets the ocean. I got in the water and stopped at my knees. Fear filled me and I couldn't understand why. I have been swimming in the Puget Sound since March, why on earth was I so filled with dread. I squatted down and got the water to my neck, swam up and down the shoreline but couldn't get in past my knees. After about 15 minutes, I just HAD to get out of the water. My boyfriend was baffled at my fear, but was ok with us leaving. Well I found out why on the tour. We got on the boat and the first place they took us was the very same estuary I had been swimming in. They proceeded to tell us that this estuary and beach had the highest concentration of GREAT WHITE SHARKS on the west coast and that they actually came into the estuary all the time to eat seals and salmon. OH MY GOD!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! Talk about that natural survival instinct kicking in!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-10T18:37:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flight-or-fight-in-the-water.2321/post-36572>

Always listen to your gut. This is a great lesson that I'm sure will help you "listen" in the future. Thanks for sharing this as it can be a lesson to us all. Love it!

### SeaHorse — 2012-07-10T19:16:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flight-or-fight-in-the-water.2321/post-36579>

Your gut really was keeping you safe wasn't it!

### janagram — 2012-07-10T19:22:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flight-or-fight-in-the-water.2321/post-36583>

You are blessed! Oh how great that you have inner guidance.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-10T19:38:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flight-or-fight-in-the-water.2321/post-36591>

That is quantum biology at work.........another levee in the Quilt.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-10T20:09:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flight-or-fight-in-the-water.2321/post-36596>

My dog taught me this... she's way more sensitive than I am though I do pay attention to this. I have made a U-turn hiking alone in the mountains a number of times (not really safe or recommended to hike alone in the mountains we've got wildlife...). She will take the leash in her mouth and lead me away from whatever is hiding in the bushes. I tried to not listen once.... she threw a fit. Jumping up on me, yapping, tugging the leash (which wasn't attached to her but in my hands!!), laying down flat... she would not move any further into the direction we were heading. I eventually listened to her and we turned away from what was scaring her. She taught me to listen to this. Now I do, even when I'm without her.

We've all officially gone off the deep end  I'm glad I'm not alone down there

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-10T20:25:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flight-or-fight-in-the-water.2321/post-36603>

What's crazy is in our modern world we've been taught to ignore these feelings as BS, instead we rely on digital readouts, spreadsheets, etc. those feelings are not BS. we've forgotten how to listen to our inner-selves, our gut, our intuition, our 6th sense. its there for a reason. Trust your body it knows what its doing.

when I was in labor w/ds about 14hrs in - I had a feeling something was wrong. my MW pushed me to keep going w/the home birth, I asked my birth doula to help me into the shower - and inbtwn contractions I told her point blank - something is wrong, and I need help, I wasn't tired - but intuitively I knew something was wrong. I asked her for help convincing my MW and DH that I needed to go to the hospital - they agreed after they checked my blood pressure - Can you say Danger zone. the boy was happy as a clam and was enjoying labor - my body decided it needed support. I listened to it. I'm 100% positive if I'd ignored it, I wouldn't be here today.

heed the inner-voice, intuition, gut - what ever you want to call it. Its key to our survival.

### nonchalant — 2012-07-11T00:39:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flight-or-fight-in-the-water.2321/post-36658>

> **Jack Kruse;36757 wrote:** That is quantum biology at work.........another levee in the Quilt.

Sorta like Deja Vu? I once read that if you experience deja vu, and then make a real emotional reaction to it, you reinforce it. Like time travel.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-11T02:13:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flight-or-fight-in-the-water.2321/post-36685>

> **Nonchalant;36824 wrote:** Sorta like Deja Vu? I once read that if you experience deja vu, and then make a real emotional reaction to it, you reinforce it. Like time travel.

You can time travel forward, but you can not time travel back.

### nonchalant — 2012-07-11T05:41:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flight-or-fight-in-the-water.2321/post-36744>

> **Cavemam;36851 wrote:** You can time travel forward, but you can not time travel back.

It's not really time travel backward. More like both forward and back at the same time. Like Amazonmagic's emotional response to the tour guide's information was very strong. So her feeling of dread back when she was wading in the water was equally strong.

Ok, this is getting wierd. I'll hush now.

### Amazonmagic — 2012-07-11T16:56:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flight-or-fight-in-the-water.2321/post-36817>

Yeah ya lost me on that, but that's ok

### nonchalant — 2013-03-04T04:45:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flight-or-fight-in-the-water.2321/post-78871>

Jack's QB1 blog post reminded me of this thread.

### SeaHorse — 2013-03-04T14:59:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flight-or-fight-in-the-water.2321/post-78904>

Yes it does, doesn't it. Often our thoughts block the information our bodies are resonating with. Unfortunately, for many people their bodies no longer have the ability to register this kind of information. It's like we get gummed up and all our amazing powers of perception and awareness are dulled down or squashed flat. It's why you can't learn this stuff intellectually...your body has to be able to feel and sense the environment and it can only start to do that when all our cells are cleared or clearing and we are tapped into the sources of energy like the sun and the earth. I think this is when being in nature becomes such an intense source of joy....which seems to be showing up here more and more!

Our brains say "I want to be healthy, or I want to be able to sense energy" but if we stay on the outside of these ideas looking in, then we know we still have work to do. It's living these thoughts that make the difference and actually turn us into finely tuned beings.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-04T15:03:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flight-or-fight-in-the-water.2321/post-78905>

Do you listen to your stream of consciousness every morning? The healthier one gets the more you control the duration of your present........When you live as such, it should be possible to find out when perceiving stops and remembering begins. I have found in my health it has not been possible...but, I try for it elusively. So far in my life, a perception in fact, does not have an end......perceiving just seems to continue on......

# Thread: +++Help Please Osteo+Neuro+locking jaws
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-please-osteo-neuro-locking-jaws.2324/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LilyJ — 2012-07-10T19:33:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-please-osteo-neuro-locking-jaws.2324/post-36588>

I'm hoping some of you have been able to heal your medical issues and can send me in the right direction with labs and supplements. My history ---fully menopausal since 41 (I am 47 currently), history of full body auto-immune reactions incuding iritis, knee's sound like I'm walking on broken glass, osteopenia (neck reading was almost full osteoporosis a couple of years ago), hands are starting to shake a lot (even without coffee). In the last 3 months my jaws are starting to catch and I can't lift my left arm out to the side without discomfort. I have had pneumonia once and pleurisy off and on over the last 4 yrs. I have been LC for 12 years and have managed to gain 30lbs over the last 5 yrs. I am 5'3 165lbs currently. My vitamin D is low but I am unable to take supplements without severe bone pain. I have just started taking magnesium citramate 150-300mg, potassium citrate 200-400mg and Krill oil 1000mg. I take no other medications. Working around the house is really my only form of exercise. Any comments or suggestions would be very appreciated. Thanks!

### LilyJ — 2012-07-10T19:35:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-please-osteo-neuro-locking-jaws.2324/post-36589>

I forgot to mention that my fingers have looked like sausages the last 3 months.

### nonchalant — 2012-07-10T21:59:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-please-osteo-neuro-locking-jaws.2324/post-36621>

Welcome LilyJ! I would say more magnesium, to start with. Magnesium threonate if you can get it, and/or magnesium malate. I take 1000 mg of magnesium daily, but I had to gradually build up to this level. I take a combination of various magnesium chelates, but mostly mg. malate.

You are low carb... Have you eliminated all grains? Especially gluten in your diet? That stuff really attacks the joints.

I like the krill oil you're taking, that should also help.

### LilyJ — 2012-07-10T23:00:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-please-osteo-neuro-locking-jaws.2324/post-36624>

I basically eat beef, a veggy here and there and an apply or blueberries once or twice a week. I eat no grains, rice or potatoes. I eat eggs also but have cut them back recently to see if that would help with the inflammation. I used to be able to eat ground beef for every meal and the inflammation would go away but that hasn't been working for me now. I will look at other forms of magnesium. Thanks Nonchalant

### Joann — 2012-07-10T23:26:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-please-osteo-neuro-locking-jaws.2324/post-36625>

Do you use BHRT? Read Dr K blog on autoimmune ......no eggs, bone broth. also anti-inflammatory supplements like curcumin etc and I think blood tests for your own info to see whats up?

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-11T01:11:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-please-osteo-neuro-locking-jaws.2324/post-36668>

Sounds like autoimmunity and inflammation... and I'd be focussed on the gut (and HUGELY tied to light cycles and their importance). Have you read this blog as a starter? [http://jackkruse.com/could-it-be-the-gut/](http://http://jackkruse.com/could-it-be-the-gut/) You might also want a consult pronto and a lot of testing done with your doc... DHEA levels, hsCRP, vitamin D, thyroid and hormones, etc.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-11T01:14:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-please-osteo-neuro-locking-jaws.2324/post-36671>

[http://jackkruse.com/the-sunshine-of-your-life/](http://http://jackkruse.com/the-sunshine-of-your-life/)

### LilyJ — 2012-07-11T23:15:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-please-osteo-neuro-locking-jaws.2324/post-36948>

I haven't used BHRT. For several years I thought I would never use them and didn't mind the thought of aging gracefully but I feel as though I'm entering a point of no return with the inflammation causing joint destruction and/or some sort of cartilage disorder. I will look into curcumin. I did pick up some K2 and resveratrol today.

### LilyJ — 2012-07-11T23:26:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-please-osteo-neuro-locking-jaws.2324/post-36952>

I haven't read the gut posts but I will do it today. Now that I think about it, in 1990, six months prior to the first autoimmune attack (the dr at that time said the sed rate was worse than someone that was 80 yrs old with severe arthritis) I contracted C Diff after a few days of cefuroxime treatment. I suppose that's when it all started. Thanks.

### nonchalant — 2012-07-12T00:20:01+0300

I suppose that's when it all started. Thanks.

Then when you got C Diff, your genes seized the opportunity, shuffled the deck, and unfortunately you ended up with an AI issue. You might want to consider a consult with Dr. K., as KiwiLauren mentioned. You're at the right place, Lily. Hope you can find relief soon.

### LilyJ — 2012-07-13T02:59:11+0300

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5-garden-weeds-you-a-can-eat.2326/post-36602>

[http://www.livescience.com/21496-5-edible-garden-weeds.html](http://http://www.livescience.com/21496-5-edible-garden-weeds.html)

> Has the heat killed your garden and left nothing but weeds? Then why not eat those weeds?
>
>
>
> Last year at this time we reported on five healthy weeds likely growing in or near your yard: dandelion, plantain, purslane, lamb's-quarters, and stinging nettles. Each one of these is a tasty powerhouse of nutrition. Raw purslane, a rich source of omega-3 fatty acids, offers a perfect tart punch to any smoothie; lamb's-quarters, one of the most nutritionally dense foods known, cooks in seconds in a stir-fry and has a nutty spinach taste.

# Thread: Yellow pee on DHEA?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yellow-pee-on-dhea.2327/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-07-10T21:10:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yellow-pee-on-dhea.2327/post-36616>

I am taking :

no b vitamins outside of meat

k2... natto kinase... maybe I need something else

zinc

selenium

iodide

7 keto

no carnitine for two days

no ala for two days

no marijuana milkshake for two days

added dhea four days ago

ate liver two days in a row

edit: actually I'm taking pantothenic acid also... kind of made from b vitamins.

[http://wikidoc.org/index.php/Human\_urine](http://http://wikidoc.org/index.php/Human_urine)

Colour

**The typical bright yellow colour of urine is caused by the pigment urochrome, but also from the degradation products of **bilirubin and urobilin**.** Clear colors are a sign of hydration and are the preferred colors of urine.

I know I'm drinking a gallon of liquid a day. I don't think its the typical bright yellow pigment urochrome because of all the water. It seems the other two color causers would be detox... Any thoughts or experience would be appreciated.

### chocolate — 2012-07-10T23:34:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yellow-pee-on-dhea.2327/post-36628>

### jmathisen — 2012-07-10T22:14:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/absolutely-loving-the-kippers.2330/post-36623>

I eat sardines quite a bit, and enjoy them, but somebody picked up some kippers for me at the local market. OMG! Delish!

Not as high in the O-3/O-6 as the sardines, but the flavor was awesome!

Something that delish has probably got to be not good for me. I'd eat kippers over chocolate.

### nonchalant — 2012-07-10T23:28:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/absolutely-loving-the-kippers.2330/post-36626>

Thanks for the idea, jmathisen. I used to adore sardines, but since I'm eating seafood every day, I'm starting to get critical of their taste. Perhaps it's the olive oil they are canned in. I'll try the kippers; I've actually purchased a tin of them but they have been languishing in my pantry.

### jmathisen — 2012-07-11T00:35:02+0300

I pour the sardines out on a papertowel, pat 'em "dry", slice 'em, and then sprinkle tabasco sauce on them. Once the oil is gone, they're pretty good.

Also slice 'em, stir 'em in with a bit of spicy hot sausage and some tabasco and eggs and make a scramble. Mucho goodo.

I even found kippers at the 7-11 24 hour market, so I'm really set when I have to work late and forgot to pack protein.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-12T05:07:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-brand-of-dhea-7-keto-dhea-are-you-using-and-like.2331/post-37072>

From what I read it looks like the transdermal makes the DHEA more absorbable:

_Most DHEA taken orally is eliminated as soon as it enters the system from the digestive tract by the first pass effect of the liver. Most of the effective DHEA from oral supplementation is never available to the body to do any good. Very little ends up being available to the body to utilize as a basic building block or pro-hormone._

Most DHEA metabolism occurs in the skin. Dr. Fernand Labrie and a team of Canadian researchers published an important study in February 2008 that showed this. Most DHEA is actually used by the body in the biggest organ we have – the skin, or dermis.

I found this one: [http://store.twist25.com/](http://http://store.twist25.com/)

I want to be able to make my own and I think it's totally doable. The one above has 50mg/cc.

My preg wasn't trashed but everything else. I stared at it and couldn't make sense of it. So this was the explanation. I'm pretty sure my preg probably was trashed at some point in time but is recovering now. That would be good  It also means that I'm still making T3 b/c that's required for conversion of LDL to preg. Which is even better

My heart is affecting my brain b/c there's a hole that allows the old blood coming from the brain to mix with the fresh blood going to the brain. That means my O2 in my blood to my brain is lower than it should be b/c of the "used" blood it's mixed with. Brains need lots of O2 to function well. If they don't get it, neurons go dormant or die. So basically, I have a half dormant brain b/c of low O2 and that part of my brain isn't functioning and making no hormones and such. The old blood that goes back to my brain also has toxins that should not be going back to my brain - and that causes severe headaches. Am I making sense?

What are the side effects of too much preg? I'm currently taking 200 mg total - 100 mg around 6 AM and another 100 mg at 3 PM (same with DHEA).

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-12T17:45:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-brand-of-dhea-7-keto-dhea-are-you-using-and-like.2331/post-37180>

Preg would be heart racing and palps, feeling overstimulated, I think.

The DHEA I think can have the opposite effect - making you fatigued. I'd drop the dose some and see if you do better. Or just skip a day, that's what I do. If I feel better that day, I drop the dose. I'm not sure what you and I are taking is comparable at all to what most people on here are taking b/c it might get absorbed a lot better than swallowing pills.

### Big Bobcat — 2012-07-12T19:40:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-brand-of-dhea-7-keto-dhea-are-you-using-and-like.2331/post-37207>

I've read in other places that some recommend that you cycle on and off of DHEA. I've read that people go 3 weeks on, 1 week off...or something close to that.

Anyone have any experience with this?

What do you think?

### vkiernan — 2012-07-12T20:15:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-brand-of-dhea-7-keto-dhea-are-you-using-and-like.2331/post-37222>

> **BJK77;37330 wrote:** This preg/DHEA stuff has me so confused. I've been supplementing fairly high doses after my consult with Dr. K. I was only taking 50 mg DHEA and he said I could go as high as 3-4 times that amount. I did so fairly quickly and my sleep and energy massively improved, but now I'm back to feeling super fatigued with some serious muscle weakness again . . . just can't figure this all out.
>
>
>
> I'm taking the regular DHEA - micronized, pure powder stuff and I do it sublingually. I know it's converting to T, but I'm not getting any of the negative side effects like chin hair, rage, etc.
>
>
>
> What are the side effects of too much preg? I'm currently taking 200 mg total - 100 mg around 6 AM and another 100 mg at 3 PM (same with DHEA).

Definitely what Soul said about the preg. Try taking less and take it at noon, not 3pm. Kinda late in the day to be stimulated unless you are going out.

Sounds like you need to be tested to see where your levels are at. I am finding out just how important testing is. If my FT3 is correct and my RT3 comes back low, then I seriously have to make some changes. I would not have known this if it wern't for testing. I sure wish it was easier to get docs on board for tests. Why does it have to be so complicated? You can't just fix it magically by taking a pill and then all levels are good. They know this! I am curious too why you are fatigued? Wonder where you are at with the thyroid?

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-12T21:29:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-brand-of-dhea-7-keto-dhea-are-you-using-and-like.2331/post-37243>

> **Souldanzer;37347 wrote:** Preg would be heart racing and palps, feeling overstimulated, I think.

+1 This is what happened to me on too much preg. Work your way up slowly.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-11T00:25:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-looks-body-size.2334/post-36652>

I don't get it. Women keep complaining that their self-worth is all based in looks and weight. But then, why is the first thing most women do is comment on another woman's looks and degree of thinness???

I hated this and hated it and hated it some more when I was putting on weight during recovery. Constant comments on the size of my body. All from women. Men... could care less.

I lost it over it once and a very dear (male) friend told me that he doesn't get it either. Whenever a woman meets a woman they have the need to comment on looks, particularly body weight. When a guy meets a guy the last thing on their mind to say is "You look great. You are so thin today!".

When did it ever become another woman's business what the size of my body is today???

Ready to duck from the flying tomatoes and eggs or whatever is going to come my way. But I'll say it anyways: Women, you are complaining about something that you are creating yourself. Quit doing it and it will go away.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-11T04:36:58+0300

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-11T04:54:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-looks-body-size.2334/post-36730>

What people notice about others, is reflective of how they view themselves. Most women are constantly self evaluating, and in turn evaluating themselves compared to others and expressing on it. I do this too, regretfully. I'm not proud, but it gives me a little schadenfreude thrill when I see someone I didn't like in my past that got fat. Of course, these days that's just about everyone from High School!

Do you not watch what other people eat now? When I ate like the rest of the world, and it wasn't top of mind - I could care less, and never noticed. Now that I've gone down the rabbit hole I can't help but notice what people put in carts, feed their kids, or eat at restaurants. I would never say anything - but god knows I note it in my judgmental little brain.

Over the 4th, I couldn't stop pondering and watching as all the super pale people were slather their kin with sunscreen (and trying to slather my son!).

I don't think this is a failing of women, I think it's human nature. It stands out with women as they are always thinking about their own food/size/body/attractiveness.

I wonder when people are going to de-volve so much they start flinging their poo.

### PrimalPam — 2012-07-14T16:31:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-looks-body-size.2334/post-37744>

Yes it's all about skinny for women. In my friends group, each person always looks at other women (and each other), remarking how "great" she looks, if she's lost weight since the last time we saw each other. One friend is going through a terrible divorce, and she's one of those who simply can't eat under stress, so the already-thin girl has lost another 10 lbs., which, she tells me privately, is not something she's proud of or happy about and how she realizes her nutritional needs are suffering. Yet, all the other friends keep telling her how terrific she looks!! What a nutso world.

I, too, wish I had friends IRL to whom I could talk about this stuff! How great CO is for your skin, how turmeric reduces inflammation, how seafood has all the right nutrients & fats for your brain health. But no, it's all about how many calories this has.

I'm 50, and have had many of the same friends since my 20's, and we are all within about 10-15 lbs. of our 20's weight. I'm actually the only one who gained significant weight (got up to the low 160's from 20's weight of mid-120's). Many in the group are even much thinner now, due to extreme exercising and calorie-control. But I now see their health issues with a magnifying glass: extreme use of prescription drugs, hair loss, vertigo, depression, arthritic conditions. And EVERY SINGLE ONE of my friends takes prescription sleeping pills. We all went on a trip last year, about 8-10 of us, and I was the ONLY ONE who did not take sleeping medication.

The fact that I can sleep for 8 hours straight is the most amazing part of this WOL (way of life) for me. That alone is worth more to me than any pair of skinny jeans (ooooh, but the fact that I can now fit in a lot of my old clothes IS fun too!).

### BJK77 — 2012-07-14T18:06:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-looks-body-size.2334/post-37774>

Yep, have to agree. We women bring this all on ourselves . . . well, with the help of the circus-like media, that is. I wish I could say I was immune to it all, but I definitely have some self image issues. I do find myself comparing my body to others around me, but I'm getting a bit better with it.

DH has always told me I have a majorly distorted body image and he likes me better with a little meat on my bones! I used to cringe at that, but I'm slowly learning to accept and even like my new found curves. If those curves bring me health, they are sooo worth it

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-16T10:57:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-looks-body-size.2334/post-38314>

Wanted to add this to this thread - JK FB inspiration today:

> **Why compare yourself with others? No one in the entire world can do a better job of being you than you.**

Love it & Live it.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-16T11:10:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-looks-body-size.2334/post-38315>

> **Shijin13;37665 wrote:** I can do w/o the xfit. to intense for my workouts. Though I do love my OPI malaga wine nail polish. I may not be able to let that go! I loved living in CO (minus the BS I put up w/in school - but that's what I get for choosing to go to that little piece of heaven at the foot of the Rampart Range)

I yet have to get my first manicure ever. I would destroy it in a day or less me thinks. I will need to find a very special man who doesn't mind me being natural  I fall off shoes with heels. I will wear a dress these days OMG primarily b/c it's comfortable yet during a photo shoot years ago another woman had to give me a speed lesson in sitting and moving in a dress. Hilarious.

I'm not saying these things necessarily are bad in themselves. There's healthy ways to do these things. If you enjoy your nail polish - go for it! If it starts to dominate your life needing to do all those things woman is supposed to do and if there is competition as the underlying drive .... maybe think again. I've tried some feminine stuff on for fit and I feel like a muppet in most of that stuff. It's just not me. So I'm not participating.... not in the skinny madness, not in the nail polish madness, not in the fashion madness (I do some of the fashion in my own special way- for sure not one size fits all!). And I'm just as worthy as the woman next to me who spent three hours in the bathroom fixing herself this morning. I probably did something more interesting during that time

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-16T16:15:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-looks-body-size.2334/post-38344>

Soul - I got into my mani/pedis when I was in the military - lets be honest camoflauge is not attractive on ANYONE - even HOT SF guys. But its really not attractive on women - though it is more attractive on women than a flight suit - which is just a nomex leisure suit!

The jobs I did - I worked my butt off - there's no way make up was an option - I didn't really learn how to apply make up until my late 20's so it looked natural - and not like I painted my face on!

the mani/pedis gave me a sense of girlieness/womanness in a TESTOSTERONE Filled environment (my T included!) it's something I've held on to... but I don't always do it! Its a nice thing to do ever now and again!

either way - you have to find a balance btwn everything... but you can't sacrifice your health for the material....

# Thread: got labs and have high cholesterol and inflammation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/got-labs-and-have-high-cholesterol-and-inflammation.2336/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### janagram — 2012-07-11T00:56:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/got-labs-and-have-high-cholesterol-and-inflammation.2336/post-36661>

OK, I'm 64....my labs show:

high triglycerides, (192)....and lots of small particles of LDL....not good.

Also C-reactive protein is 3.61, high. these were the only worrisome numbers.

Inflammation. What to do?

I'm so afraid it's gonna be....CT!!!!!!!

### nonchalant — 2012-07-11T01:01:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/got-labs-and-have-high-cholesterol-and-inflammation.2336/post-36664>

Janagram, remind me. You do eat animal fats, right? And avoid grains and sugars and seed oils?

### janagram — 2012-07-11T01:13:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/got-labs-and-have-high-cholesterol-and-inflammation.2336/post-36669>

yeah, sure, but......maybe slipping up a bit more than I think...

my functional medicine doc wants me to tighten up the diet, eat more veggies, take ALA and walk daily. (I don;t always)

in summer I slip up more (ice cream)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/got-labs-and-have-high-cholesterol-and-inflammation.2336/post-36673>

### JanSz — 2012-07-11T01:25:15+0300

>
> Inflammation. What to do?
>
> I'm so afraid it's gonna be....CT!!!!!!!

I am 72yo, not a doctor.

Fatty acids and phospholipids are heavily correlated with inflammation.

Check that first.

Second,

do wholesale micronutrient evaluation and follow it up with optimization.

third

iodine/iodide

You may get old trying to find doctor who will write you scripts for this two tests.

Alternatively use your own $$.

Essential & Metabolic Fatty Acids Analysis (EMFA) by Genova Diagnostics

[http://www.healthremedies.com/essential\_metabolic\_fatty\_acids\_analysis\_emfa\_genova\_diagnostics.html](http://http://www.healthremedies.com/essential_metabolic_fatty_acids_analysis_emfa_genova_diagnostics.html)

Spectracell Comprehensive Nutritional Panel

[http://www.healthremedies.com/intracellular\_vitamin\_analysis\_spectracell.html](http://http://www.healthremedies.com/intracellular_vitamin_analysis_spectracell.html)

\=====================

Do both tests 2x/year, follow up with corrections, when good, repeat 1x/year

Spectracell, make corrections to outright defficiencies and close calls.

(EMFA), post results here, I will help you, PM me when ready,

the test is good but the commentary requires adjustments per post #1 here:

[http://www.musclechatroom.com/forum/showthread.php?20061-Dr.-Patricia-Kane&p=171942#post171942](http://http://www.musclechatroom.com/forum/showthread.php?20061-Dr.-Patricia-Kane&p=171942#post171942)

\=======================

Eat, sleep and behave per dr Kruse's advice.

Cliff notes on that on top of above referred post.

...

When you good with the above, return for

Lipids, Steroid & Thyroid Hormone Optimization.

//////////

Specifically triglycerides correlate with:

Trig +76--Niacinamide

Trig +76--vit C

Trig + ---indicate carbohydrate intolerance

Trig +59 chromium

Trig +59 EFA 4:2 oil

Trig +59 Fish Oil

Trig +59 L carnitine

Trig +59 Phosphatidylcholine

Trig +59 selenium

Pay real close attention to what is written in post #1 on fats.

no fish oil, but fish ok

no Krill oil, but krill ok

..

Cholesterol is used to make steroid hormones.

High cholesterol correlates with low Steroid hormones.

Specially DHEAs and Cortisol and also Vit D.

..

# Thread: Thyroid Nodule - anyone knows something about it please chime in!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### youthfuliz — 2012-07-11T02:39:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-36695>

I have two thyroid nodules. I noticed one lump last week and had it checked out. It turned out I have two, a dominant one on the left and a smaller one on the isthmus.

I read intensively online and gathered information from this site and elsewhere, especially the mda iodine thread where Grizz gathered a lot of information on iodine deficiency. I watched the Dr. Brownstein video and read articles. I've been supplementing iodine for 3 days and already feel better. The large nodule shrunk about 50% so I know I'm doing the right thing. Heartbeat was at 55/minute last week, today it's 66. My normal heartbeat is about 66 and it was the first time last week that I ever noticed it dropped to below 60. Taking the co-factor vitamins too, not much detox except for more bowel movements.

Blood test done on last thursday came back today, shows that I have normal thyroid hormone level:

T4: 8 (range: 4.5 - 12)

TSH: 2 (range: 0.4 - 4.5)

Ultrasound results said the nodules are most likely benign. A biopsy is suggested since the nodules are larger than 1cm. I'm thinking whether a biopsy is necessary... it's intrusive, may cause damage to my throid. So maybe supplementing iodine and see in three months? If the nodules shrink more I may not need a biopsy.

Please if you have any experience in this area let me know what you think! Thanks.

### youthfuliz — 2012-07-12T21:11:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-37236>

Decided not to get a biopsy. The nodules are most likely benign. I don't want a biopsy to complicate them. They're shrinking too with the iodine supplement. Will repeat ultrasound in three months.

Iodine is good not just for thyroid, but for your brain, and all sexual organs - breasts, ovaries, prostates... Grizz gathered alot of information on iodine, search his id you'll see information in his signature. Also Dr. Brownstein has books and youtube videos out there for those who are interested.

### AKMan — 2012-07-13T20:24:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-37544>

Let us know how it works out. Sorry no one replied, but what you have is not very common. However, someday someone will do a search for thyroid nodules on this site and hopefully find this post. Please update when you have results!

### LightForTheNight — 2012-07-13T20:45:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-37553>

I've done some research on T. Nodules, and from what I've gathered they are benign in 95%+ of the time. The iodine along with selenium seem to be the best alternatives, and Brownstein and Abraham are definitely leading those fronts. I'd be interested to see if CT has any affect because of its ability to stimulate the thyroid.

### youthfuliz — 2012-07-14T16:03:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-37731>

Thanks Akman.  The information online has been very helpful to me. I'd very glad if one day my experience will help others.

### youthfuliz — 2012-07-14T16:07:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-37733>

> **LightForTheNight;37720 wrote:** I've done some research on T. Nodules, and from what I've gathered they are benign in 95%+ of the time. The iodine along with selenium seem to be the best alternatives, and Brownstein and Abraham are definitely leading those fronts. I'd be interested to see if CT has any affect because of its ability to stimulate the thyroid.

Yeah you're right. iodine/iodide needs selenium to work. Just got my Brownstein books on Iodine and Thyroid. As far as I know nobody has talked about CT and thyroid before Dr. Kruse. We are the n=1.

### youthfuliz — 2012-07-14T16:20:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-37739>

One week update:

Doing well with iodine natural therapy outlined by Dr. Brownstein and others. The nodules have shrunk by maybe 60%. Now they're almost not noticeable if I look in the mirror. When I swallow I still see them moving up and down, but much smaller than before. I have been taking 50mg iodine+iodide from day 2 (on day 1 I ate lots of seaweeds with brazil nuts). The nodules shrunk very quickly at first, then continued to shrink, but at a slowly rate, from day 3 on. Every morning I notice the lump is a bit smaller.  I had almost no detox symptoms from it, except for yellowish urine and stinky sweat. I took 75mg yesterday, am taking 62.5mg today, will go back down to 50mg tomorrow. I'm also taking the cofactors.

### janagram — 2012-07-16T04:11:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-38255>

I have one, but waited to respond as I don't know much about it. I've had it for 20 yrs...I've had it aspirated way back then..benign. It makes me worry a bit about my thyroid but...doc's don't say much.

It's size of walnut.

### youthfuliz — 2012-07-16T18:20:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-38366>

Janagram,

I'm feeling much better with iodine supplementation. My nodules have shrunk to maybe 40% of their original size... I need to repeat the ultrasound to be accurate so that's just my own observation. I do show it to one of my colleagues from day one when I first noticed the lump and she was surprised how fast it is shrinking. I have more energy too and can focus on the important things in life instead of finding excuses to delay doing them. I plan to repeat ultrasound in three months to see clearly how much iodine has helped. Right now my neck looks quite normal, you won't notice my nodules if I don't tell you about them. But when I swallow you still see the lump (much smaller than before) moving up and down. From what I read many people have benign nodules that they don't even know about so it's probably nothing to worry about. But if you want to look into iodine deficiency here're what I find helpful:

[http://joannebrophy.com/programs/iodinetherapy.htm](http://http://joannebrophy.com/programs/iodinetherapy.htm)

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Kd34EJ5E3bI\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;Kd34EJ5E3bI\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Kd34EJ5E3bI\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;Kd34EJ5E3bI)

There're a lot more information online if you do a search. MDA has an iodine thread that's quite helpful too. We also have an iodine thread here if you haven't noticed yet:

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?987-The-iodine-Thread](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?987-The-iodine-Thread)!!!

Doctors usually don't do much about nodules except for making sure they're benign and watch them. Or offer to opearate when they're too large. There're a few iodine literate doctors who treat thyroid problems, and problems with breasts, ovaries and other sexual organs with iodine (and maybe thyroid hormones if necessary).

### janagram — 2012-07-16T18:34:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-38370>

thanks, youthfuliz. Mine is apparently not visible either, but palpable. Thanks for tips about iodine, I have begun taking it. So glad you are getting results.

### Gracelind — 2012-07-17T09:27:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-38669>

Multi-nodular goiter here--5 or 6 nodules (don't have report in front of me right now.) Iodine uptake exam was negative so, I'm told, the nodules are benign. My rheumatologist told me his wife has had the same for twenty years with no apparent ill effects. No one has suggested aspiration.

Incidental finding on a CT for something else 3 years ago showed small calcified thyroid nodule but didn't mention any additional nodules. All of my docs shrugged off that finding.

Discovered the nodules this winter, running my hand up my neck and feeling a lump. We stopped cooking with salt many years ago, so I'm clearly iodine deficient but in the last year I've been adding Celtic Sea salt to food on my plate. Started with a low dose of Lugol's, working up to about 25 mg about the time I found the lump. I was worried that taking the iodine had brought it out. But after some weight loss my doc says it's more noticeable with no fat on my neck to hide it.

So,youthfulize, I'm very interested in yours shrinking with a higher dose of iodine. You mentioned the cofactors. Are you also taking selenium and Vit C? Thanks for posting your experience! This is yet another area where there's a deep divide between CW and alternative treatment.

### youthfuliz — 2012-07-17T14:50:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-38684>

Hi Gracelind,

Thanks for sharing your experience. I'm taking iodoral, 25mg at breakfast, 25mg around 2pm. The co-factors that I take are

1\. celtic salt, 1/2 tsp in water (I do additional salt loading once or twice when I feel need help to detox)

2\. Vitamin C, 3000mg at night

3\. Magnesium malate, 500mg at night

4\. Selenium, currently 200mg + 1 or 2 brazil nut (I should cut this down to 200mg, or 1 or 2 brazil nut, but I just started so I'm taking a little more)

5\. ATP cofactors (this is B2 + B3 in specific ratio for detox)

I also eat plenty of oysters... like every other day  and mushrooms, and I have a recent crush on cilantro. I take other supplements, most what Dr. Kruse suggested in his Top Ten Supplements blog... unfortunately I wasn't taking iodone before last week, which is about the only thing I didn't get out of that list. Also taking Vitamin A or veal liver, D3, Fish oil, Krill oil...etc.

I read about thyroid nodules/goiters online and started to supplement iodine within two days of noticing the lump. The first couple of days I supplemented... the lump shrunk by 50%... it was very obvious to me that iodine was working! And I had more energy, better mood, better focus. So I read more and had more confidence... I was trying to increase to 75mg, but felt a bit jittery so backed down to 50mg. The lump is not noticeable anymore, but palpable, and it's getting smaller each day. I hope in 3 months when I repeat the ultrasound it will be much smaller.

From what I read, thyroid nodules/goiters means iodine deficiency. Which also means the rest of our body needs more iodine too, not just the thyroid. Too bad the CW doctors are ignoring this. But luckily we have easy access to internet to educate ourselves. And I love the fact that great doctors are using the internet to reach us... like Dr. Kruse, like the iodine doctors.

Here is Dr. Kruse' suggestion:

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?2374-Iodine-Deficiency-and-CT](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?2374-Iodine-Deficiency-and-CT)

I'm probably not hypothyriodism... but I did have some symptoms... like laziness, haha, lack of motivation to get things done, not exactly like me. Blood test shows normal TSH and T4.

Are you still taking lugol's? From what I read if iodine supplementation makes the thyroid enlarge, it's because the body was deficient for a long time and when there's iodine the thyroid tries all it could to grab that iodine so it enlarged itself to store up (something like that), and that kind of enlargement will go away, back to normal within days/weeks if you keep supplementing iodine... the thyroid will realize that it doesn't need to hoard eventually. I think that's the reason why iodine supplementation can shrink nodules/goiters... the need for them to be there is gone when you're iodine sufficient. At least I think that's why my lump is shrinking. And I love the new energetic me! My productivity increased a lot in the last several days.

Please keep us posted how you're doing, and I'll update too from time to time. We need to know more about thyroid and iodine.

### SeaHorse — 2012-07-11T17:43:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-neanderthal-hear-me-roar.2338/post-36824>

2.5% for me....darn. I think my husband is pure though....he has a sagittal crest on his skull.. He was a big hit in the anthropology classes in college because..

" It serves as a muscle attachment area for the temparalis muscles that extend up both sides of the head from the mandible (jaw). The presence of a sagittal crest indicates that there are exceptionally strong jaw muscles. Some Australopithecus afarensis and the robust australopithecines (Australopithecus robustus, boisei, and aethiopicus) had sagittal crests. Among the living primates, the most prominent sagittal crests are found on adult male gorillas. Humans do not have them. " The Encyclopedia of Science

So I guess I married a gorilla! What does that make our daughters????

Big Feet

Large Calves

Wide hips

Big Bones

Big (and smarter!) head

Red Hair

Freckles

Blue eyes

Dislike of sports

periodic asbergers like behavior

being creative

introversion

gut permeability

wide bony face

compete lack of green thumb

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-12T02:16:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-neanderthal-hear-me-roar.2338/post-37014>

> **SeaHorse;37039 wrote: This article was posted awhile back on the Factor X thread....pretty mind blowing.
>
>
>
> [www.rdos.net/eng/asperger.htm](http://http://www.rdos.net/eng/asperger.htm)
>
>
> not through yet but wow
>
>
>
> Dog
>
>
>
> Cooperation with wolves were most likely a key to the ability for Neanderthals to survive in the cold Europe. Genetic research shows that dog and wolf parted 135,000 years ago. 77 They also reveal that the dogs ancestor is the European gray wolf. There are wolf remains close to Neanderthals, but not with the pray. This probably means the wolf had a special relation with humans. There are also big similarities between Neanderthal prey and wolves prey. 78 Some people incorrectly believe that dogs must change morph to be dogs and domestic. The main problem with this reasoning is that there is no evidence for a special dog phenotype older than 14,000 years, and analysis of American historical dogs claim they have an Eurasian origin. They must then have been brought with the settlers that crossed the Bering strait at least 15,000 years ago. It's also unlikely that the common dog psychological traits can have evolved in just a few thousands years. It's quite interesting that dogs are sensitive to human cues, 79 and that some humans (probably autistic mainly) are sensitive to dog's cues. This tells us that the original relation between dogs and humans was not as domesticator / domesticated, rather like a symbiotic relation.**

I have an interesting relationship with dogs to say the least.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-12T04:53:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-neanderthal-hear-me-roar.2338/post-37066>

> **Souldanzer;37228 wrote:** I met you and someone must have taught you social skills then. How can you be a real estate agent and an aspie? I would hide in my cave and never come back out
>
>
>
> That site was enlightening, I agree.

I get this question A LOT from anybody who knows me out side of Real Estate - specially friends, I'm the LAST person one would expect to do such a thing. I attribute it to a fascination with the study of human behavior. . . In an aspie type focus on one topic to the Nth degree, I learned how/why people say and do what they do. I made a study of words vs action and interpreting and predicting behavior.

I am terrible in a "party" setting, but one on one - when I have some interest in common, I thrive.

I went into real estate because I love architecture/remodeling/houses etc - but I ended up a good real estate agent because I look at each client like a game to solve. I MUST FIGURE THEM OUT AND FIND THE HOUSE THEY WILL LOVE. Usually our interests bond us. I want to find them a house, they want to find one - win/win.

Interestingly enough I do not get along with other real estate people. I find it so frustrating all the extroversion. They always want to gather and socialize even when nobody has anything in common! I'm always getting scolded by my broker for not coming to X event or participating in X.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-12T05:00:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-neanderthal-hear-me-roar.2338/post-37068>

I s\*ck at the small talk. I'm terrible on the phone or in person as well for that matter. It's even worse having to do it in English now as I didn't grow up learning what to say. I have a few memorized scenarios and I pray I will pick the correct one when I need to meet/talk. I get it wrong all the time  I get stared at a lot. I cannot figure out what the right thing to say.

But I'm great once you know me and I can be me  If you don't mind me going on and on about whatever my interest happens to be at that moment in time  And keep it quiet please. Careful about touching me and don't mind me wearing my clothes inside out. The tags/seams drive me crazy.

I do have quite a sense of humor which apparently is uncharacteristic.

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-12T15:53:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-neanderthal-hear-me-roar.2338/post-37141>

So here's a question.... I wonder what N's Briggs-Meyer's quotient lends towards.

While I appear to be an extrovert - I'm not. I'm not comfortable in crowds, social situations etc. I've learned how to do it, as a matter of survival in my job. but its not my choice. I'm one of those people that prefers to be in the background and make things happen. I'm very good at it. when I was in the military, I had this ability to fad into the background and get things done...but when necessary I'd go toe-to-toe w/the big brass, and I'd usually come out on top, but once it was over I'd fad back into the wall flower again. Its weird confrontation doesn't bother me. I think in a past life I was a warrior of some sort.

I'm highly detail oriented on things I'm interested in, and I have a laser like focus on those things... things I'm not interested in - meh don't get that same level of attention.

on the briggs meyer's quotient I'm an INFP. given that I shouldn't even be in the career field I've chosen, nor should I be good at it, but for some reason I excel in it. I should be an artist or writer - but I'm not.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-12T21:05:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-neanderthal-hear-me-roar.2338/post-37234>

what an interesting thread! i'm @ 3%, (95th percentile)

that pic looks my kindergarten pic, but my nose is smaller and turned up and I have a pointy chin. My hair: just as wild, even today LOL

I have big thick fingers, and very muscular, even before I lifted weights. A big head, hats never fit! Big muscular calves, well defined, even my SHINS. Who has muscular shins?!?! When I was in high school i vividly remember a friend walking behind me up a flight of stairs, and gasp "like, oh my god, teenie! You have these, like, MUSCLES in your legs. I mean, not like a dude, but almost." (yes, say it like a valley girl) I was MORTIFIED. MAN MUSCLES? In my legs? yeesh. There is a pic of me, I NEVER exercised, and I have traps that are visible from the front LOL I always fought my muscular body, I so badly wanted to be LITHE. AY yi yi.

But at the same time as muscular, extremely curvy. Even when I was FORTY lbs less than I am now, in high school, I had an hourglass figure. Cute boobs and a butt. Still do, just a bigger hourglass LOL

I am intro and extroverted. Painfully shy @ times, and then ridiculously confident at others. How is that possible? Hard to fit in. Always felt like the water pistol that squirted jelly...

Cavemam, he is adorable!!

And ditto on the tanning for me too. It's like I'm tanning in between/under my freckles.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-14T17:04:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-neanderthal-hear-me-roar.2338/post-37758>

Here is the key point. It is about mtDNA. This is why they went extinct. My thoughts are tied to leptin because it controls the human placenta. Although modern humans share some nuclear DNA with the extinct Neanderthals, the two species do not share any mitochondrial DNA, which in primates is always maternally transmitted. This observation has prompted the hypothesis that whereas female humans interbreeding with male Neanderthals were able to generate fertile offspring, the progeny of female Neanderthals who mated with male humans were either rare, absent or sterile (in line with Haldane's rule). If mtDNA is not present then you eventually die out.........I think interbreeding is what killed them and the fact that their DNA allowed for impulsivity......so they would have been quite unstable deep into life because of some interesting features unique to them.

### kathylu — 2012-07-14T17:28:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-neanderthal-hear-me-roar.2338/post-37765>

> **Jack Kruse;37925 wrote:** Here is the key point. It is about mtDNA. This is why they went extinct. My thoughts are tied to leptin because it controls the human placenta. Although modern humans share some nuclear DNA with the extinct Neanderthals, the two species do not share any mitochondrial DNA, which in primates is always maternally transmitted. This observation has prompted the hypothesis that whereas female humans interbreeding with male Neanderthals were able to generate fertile offspring, the progeny of female Neanderthals who mated with male humans were either rare, absent or sterile (in line with Haldane's rule). If mtDNA is not present then you eventually die out.........I think interbreeding is what killed them and the fact that their DNA allowed for impulsivity......so they would have been quite unstable deep into life because of some interesting features unique to them.

There's also the Rh neg gene too. If a female neanderthal interbred with a male Cro Magnon, Rh incompatibility would almost ensure no more than one viable offspring. So the population would slowly decline.

The mtDNA theory is intriguing, but it's hard to believe that NO neanderthal females produced a viable offspring with a Cro Magnon. I wonder if anyone has analyzed mtDNA from Basques? From my understanding, they seem to be the population that carries the largest remnants of neanderthal DNA.

### kathylu — 2012-07-15T15:35:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-neanderthal-hear-me-roar.2338/post-38026>

> **Jack Kruse;37970 wrote:** There is not.....because we have been looking and we have found none. This strongly suggests why they died off.

Jack, I'm going to have to maintain agnosticism on this topic especially since the Neanderthal genome was sequenced to only 1.3x coverage using degraded DNA, lots of potential for error and misinterpretation there. And the mtDNA sequencing was performed by using 31 bp PCR amplified fragments out of an estimated 16,000 bp total genome. Enough to identify those unique fragments, but not much else. Degradation is a real problem when sequencing ancient DNA.

I don't think they were that much different than modern humans. They diverged from humans 250,000 (lots of disagreement on that number) years ago while Sub-Saharan Africans and Eurasians diverged 150,000 years ago. If they existed still today they would just be considered another ethnic group, not another species. There would be no surprise if there was interbreeding between two ethnic groups.

Here are a few fun ideas:

[http://www.aoi.com.au/bcw/neanderbasque.htm](http://http://www.aoi.com.au/bcw/neanderbasque.htm)
